title: release-26.2: roachprod: address ibm quota and image deprecation, and optimize clusters list
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166415.

/cc @cockroachdb/release

---

This PR contains four changes for IBM cloud in roachprod:

1. roachprod: use more ibm AZs to reduce capa issues
  In order to avoid impacting IBM customers, roachprod was only using two IBM Cloud regions per our agreement with IBM.
  With the deprecation of s390x in IBM Cloud, we can no longer request for quota increases in these two regions, and as the number of customers decrease, we now can scale the number of regions roachprod uses.
  This patch grows from two regions to six in order to load balance the cluster creations during roachtest operations and reduce quota issues.

2. roachprod: use latest ubuntu 22.04 image in ibm
  Previously, roachprod was using the s390x  Ubuntu 22.04 rev. 5 image in IBM Cloud.
  As this image was deprecated a few months ago and replaced by rev. 7, this patch switches to the latest image.

3. roachprod: fix ibm clusters list
  Previously, listing clusters in IBM was creating two many requests in parallel, leading to very long list/create operations and, sometimes, the API returning HTTP error code 429.
  This patch adds a ctxgroup with a limit fixed to 30 concurrent requests to avoid spamming the IBM API when listing clusters.
  Also, some code path were leading to querying each instance details multiple times. This patch fixes this by only loading instance data when the creation date is unknown. This will reduce the total number of queries per instance from two to one.

4. roachprod: improve clusters cleanup for ibm
  Previously, after a failed cluster creation, roachtest was attempting to destroy the partially created cluster. This process relied on listing the cluster and destroy it via `provider.DestroyCluster()`.
  In IBM, this is subject to failures because of implicit rate limitings, so this patch attempts to call `provider.DeleteCluster()` (by name) in case no clusters were returned during listing.
  This should reduce the number of dangling resources cleaned by the garbage collector.


Epic: none
Release note: none

Release justification:  Test only change
author: golgeek

commits.message:
roachprod: address ibm quota and image deprecation, and optimize clusters list

This PR contains four changes for IBM cloud in roachprod:

1. roachprod: use more ibm AZs to reduce capa issues
  In order to avoid impacting IBM customers, roachprod was only using
  two IBM Cloud regions per our agreement with IBM.
  With the deprecation of s390x in IBM Cloud, we can no longer request
  for quota increases in these two regions, and as the number of
  customers decrease, we now can scale the number of regions roachprod
  uses.
  This patch grows from two regions to six in order to load balance the
  cluster creations during roachtest operations and reduce quota issues.

2. roachprod: use latest ubuntu 22.04 image in ibm
  Previously, roachprod was using the s390x Ubuntu 22.04 rev. 5 image in
  IBM Cloud.
  As this image was deprecated a few months ago and replaced by rev. 7,
  this patch switches to the latest image.

3. roachprod: fix ibm clusters list
  Previously, listing clusters in IBM was creating two many requests in
  parallel, leading to very long list/create operations and, sometimes,
  the API returning HTTP error code 429.
  This patch adds a ctxgroup with a limit fixed to 30 concurrent
  requests to avoid spamming the IBM API when listing clusters.
  Also, some code path were leading to querying each instance details
  multiple times. This patch fixes this by only loading instance data
  when the creation date is unknown. This will reduce the total number
  of queries per instance from two to one.

4. roachprod: improve clusters cleanup for ibm
  Previously, after a failed cluster creation, roachtest was attempting
  to destroy the partially created cluster. This process relied on
  listing the cluster and destroy it via `provider.DestroyCluster()`.
  In IBM, this is subject to failures because of implicit rate
  limitings, so this patch attempts to call `provider.DeleteCluster()`
  (by name) in case no clusters were returned during listing.
  This should reduce the number of dangling resources cleaned by the
  garbage collector.

Epic: none
Release note: None

title: [release-26.1] build: add Trunk merge queue configuration
body:
Backport 2/2 commits from #161230 and 1/1 commits from #161281.

/cc @cockroachdb/release

---

build: add Trunk merge queue configuration

Add trunk.yaml configuration to enable Trunk's merge queue service
as a replacement for the existing bors.toml workflow. The configuration
includes all essential CI checks required for safe merging and enables
merge queue functionality.

Also includes:
- Add trunk-merge/** CI trigger to github-actions-essential-ci.yml (critical for CI to run on trunk branches)
- Move trunk.yaml to .trunk/ directory

Release note (build change): Replaces bors with Trunk merge queue for better performance and reliability. Configuration-only change with no runtime impact - maintains same safety checks while improving CI workflow.

Release justification: Build and CI configuration only - no functional changes.
author: rudrakshsisodia

commits.message:
build: add Trunk merge queue configuration

Add trunk.yaml configuration to enable Trunk's merge queue service
  as a replacement for the existing bors.toml workflow. The configuration
  includes all essential CI checks required for safe merging and enables
  merge queue functionality on the master-trunk branch.

Release note (build change):  Replaces bors with Trunk merge queue for better performance and reliability. Configuration-only change with no runtime impact - maintains same safety checks while improving CI workflow.
ci: add trunk branch on push trigger
ci: Move trunk to .trunk folder

title: release-26.1: roachtest: bump user_login.timeout in follower-reads insufficient-quorum
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166422 on behalf of @tbg.

----

Bump `server.user_login.timeout` from the default 10s to 60s in the
follower-reads insufficient-quorum test variants. After killing the
entire primary region, lease acquisition on system ranges can take ~10s,
racing with the authentication timeout on newly opened SQL connections.

Fixes-24.3 #166376

Release note: None
Epic: none 

----

Release justification:
author: tbg

commits.message:
roachtest: bump user_login.timeout in follower-reads insufficient-quorum

The follower-reads insufficient-quorum test variants kill the entire
primary region (3 nodes via SIGKILL) and then open new SQL connections
to surviving nodes. Authentication on these new connections requires
reading system tables, which blocks until leases transfer from the dead
nodes. This lease acquisition can take ~10s, racing with the default
10s `server.user_login.timeout` and causing flaky authentication
timeouts.

Bump the timeout to 60s for these test variants to give ample room for
lease transfers after a full region kill.

Fixes-24.3 #166376

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: release-26.1: roachtest: fix acceptance/cli/node-status quorum loss
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166418 on behalf of @tbg.

----

## Summary

The `acceptance/cli/node-status` test times out because it assumes `cockroach
node status` works after the cluster has lost quorum on the liveness range.

- Switch from a 3-node to a 5-node cluster. System ranges default to 5x
  replication (`DefaultSystemZoneConfig`), so killing 2 nodes still leaves
  quorum (3/5 alive).
- Create 100 user ranges at default 3x replication; some lose quorum when 2
  nodes die, verifying `node status` works despite partial user-range quorum
  loss.
- Wait for system database and meta ranges to finish upreplicating to 5x
  before killing nodes. Uses `SHOW CLUSTER RANGES` filtered to
  `database_name = 'system' OR start_key LIKE '/Meta%'`. Timeseries ranges
  are intentionally excluded — they inherit `num_replicas=3` from the
  default zone config (not the system zone config) and would never reach 5.

Fixes-25.4: #166351
Epic: none

Release note: None

----

Release justification: Non-production code change (roachtest fix only).
author: tbg

commits.message:
roachtest: fix acceptance/cli/node-status quorum loss

The test previously started 3 nodes, killed 2, and expected `node status`
to still work — assuming it "only accesses gossip info". That assumption is
wrong; `node status` needs the liveness range to have quorum.

Switch to a 5-node cluster. System ranges default to 5x replication, so
with 2 nodes killed (3/5 alive) the liveness range retains quorum. The test
also creates 100 user ranges at the default 3x replication; some of these
may lose quorum when 2 nodes die, verifying that `node status` works even
with partial user-range quorum loss.

Fixes-25.4: #166351

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: catalog/lease: use normal priority for descriptor version fetching
body:
getDescriptorsFromStoreForInterval is used to fetch historical descriptor versions for foreground SQL traffic. Previously, it was using BulkNormalPri, which meant it could be throttled by the elastic CPU limiter. Throttling these requests delays foreground work, which can lead to query timeouts and test failures (like
backup-restore/small-ranges).

This change sets the priority to NormalPri, ensuring these requests are treated as foreground work and not subject to elastic CPU control.

Fixes: #166263

Release note (bug fix): Fixed a bug where descriptor version fetching could be incorrectly throttled by the elastic CPU limiter, potentially leading to increased query latency or timeouts under high CPU load.
author: fqazi

commits.message:
catalog/lease: use normal priority for descriptor version fetching

getDescriptorsFromStoreForInterval is used to fetch historical
descriptor versions for foreground SQL traffic. Previously, it was using
BulkNormalPri, which meant it could be throttled by the elastic CPU
limiter. Throttling these requests delays foreground work, which can
lead to query timeouts and test failures (like
backup-restore/small-ranges).

This change sets the priority to NormalPri, ensuring these requests
are treated as foreground work and not subject to elastic CPU control.

Fixes: #166263

Release note (bug fix): Fixed a bug where descriptor version fetching
could be incorrectly throttled by the elastic CPU limiter, potentially
leading to increased query latency or timeouts under high CPU load.

title: release-26.2: perturbation: rate-limit backfill pre-fill to avoid OOM on target node
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166658 on behalf of @tbg.

----

## Summary

The backfill perturbation test pre-fills ~11M rows at 10KB each with no rate
limit, producing ~18K writes/sec. Because the backfill database is
zone-constrained to place one replica on n12 (the target node), n12's stores
receive 3-4x the follower write load of normal stores. This saturates both
disks, triggers WAL failover thrashing between the two stores, and causes
memtables to grow to ~20GB per store (OOM).

The IO overload score never triggers admission control (max 0.304 vs threshold
1.0) because it's based on L0 shape, not physical disk saturation. While WAL
failover is active (~always in the failing run), IO admission control ignores memtable
build-up. But we've seen failures in which replicas went unavailable due to overload
as well, so disabling WAL failover likely wouldn't stabilize this test either.

This change rate-limits the pre-fill to 8K ops/sec and uses `--max-ops=11000000`
to maintain the same total data volume. The fill will take ~23 minutes instead
of 10, but (hopefully) avoids saturating n12's disks.

See analysis: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/166476#issuecomment-4125828130

Fixes #166476
Fixes-25.2: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/165780
Touches: #136848

## Test plan

- Run `perturbation/full/backfill` and verify n12 no longer OOMs during pre-fill.

----

Release justification:
author: tbg

commits.message:
perturbation: rate-limit backfill pre-fill to avoid OOM on target node

The backfill perturbation test pre-fills the backfill database with 100
concurrent writers at 10KB blocks for 10 minutes (the perturbation
duration). With no rate limit, this produces ~18K writes/sec and ~11M
total inserts. Because the backfill database is zone-constrained to have
one replica on n12 (the target node), n12 ends up with ~55 replicas per
store (1.7x normal), all as followers (leases are constrained off n12).
This results in 6-7K raft commands applied/sec on n12's stores vs ~2K on
normal stores, saturating both disks.

With among-stores WAL failover, when one store's disk saturates, its WAL
fails over to the other store's disk, overloading that disk too. The two
stores enter a coupled overload spiral where both WALs thrash between
disks. Despite this, the IO overload score (based on L0 shape) stays
below 0.304, so admission control never kicks in. Memtables grow to
~20 GB per store as flushes can't keep up, consuming all available RAM
and causing OOM.

This change rate-limits the pre-fill to 8K ops/sec and uses --max-ops
to control total data volume instead of --duration. The original test
completed 11,029,273 ops, so we use 11,000,000. This keeps the same
total data written (~110 GB) while reducing peak write pressure on n12
enough to avoid disk saturation.

Fixes: #166476
Fixes: #136848

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: release-26.2: changefeedccl: fix sink not closed when newEventConsumer fails in Start
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #165753 on behalf of @rharding6373.

----

Previously, in changeAggregator.Start(), the call to newEventConsumer used a multi-value assignment directly into ca.eventConsumer and ca.sink:

    ca.eventConsumer, ca.sink, err = newEventConsumer(...)

When newEventConsumer returned an error, it also returned nil for the sink, which overwrote ca.sink with nil. This meant that when the changeAggregator's close() method ran, the nil check `if ca.sink != nil` would skip closing the sink. The underlying sink (which had already been dialed/connected at line 379) was never closed, leaking the goroutine from the gRPC connection — specifically the fakePubsubServer.Dial goroutine in tests.

This was most visible in enriched envelope tests because creating the enriched event consumer can fail under stress (due to descriptor lookups), and only pubsub's mock sink actually requires Close to prevent goroutine leaks.

The fix uses temporary variables so that ca.sink and ca.eventConsumer are only assigned on the success path, preserving the original sink reference for proper cleanup on error.

Also removes the pubsub skip workaround that was added to maybeForceEnrichedEnvelope in helpers_test.go.

Resolves: #144102
Fixes: #144102                                                                                                                                     
Fixes: #144354                                                                                                                                     
Fixes: #144587                                                                                                                                     
Fixes: #145442
Fixes: #146436
Fixes: #150396
Fixes: #155215
Fixes: #155548

Generated by Claude Code Auto-Solver

Release justification: bug fix for goroutine leak in changefeeds; low risk change
author: rharding6373

reviews.body: LGTM

commits.message:
changefeedccl: fix sink not closed when newEventConsumer fails in Start

Previously, in changeAggregator.Start(), the call to newEventConsumer
used a multi-value assignment directly into ca.eventConsumer and ca.sink:

    ca.eventConsumer, ca.sink, err = newEventConsumer(...)

When newEventConsumer returned an error, it also returned nil for the
sink, which overwrote ca.sink with nil. This meant that when the
changeAggregator's close() method ran, the nil check `if ca.sink != nil`
would skip closing the sink. The underlying sink (which had already been
dialed/connected at line 379) was never closed, leaking the goroutine
from the gRPC connection — specifically the fakePubsubServer.Dial
goroutine in tests.

This was most visible in enriched envelope tests because creating the
enriched event consumer can fail under stress (due to descriptor lookups),
and only pubsub's mock sink actually requires Close to prevent goroutine
leaks.

The fix uses temporary variables so that ca.sink and ca.eventConsumer are
only assigned on the success path, preserving the original sink reference
for proper cleanup on error.

Also removes the pubsub skip workaround that was added to
maybeForceEnrichedEnvelope in helpers_test.go.

Resolves: #144102

Release note: None

Generated by Claude Code Auto-Solver
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: tochar: fix float8 to_char post-decimal digits; fix sign placement for FMS format
body:
### sql: limit float8 to_char post-decimal digits to DBL_DIG 

PostgreSQL's float8_to_char adjusts the post-decimal digit count
(Num.post) to fit within DBL_DIG (15) total significant digits
before generating the overflow '#' fill string. CockroachDB was
not performing this adjustment, causing overflow strings with too
many '#' characters in the decimal portion when the format had
many post-decimal digit specifiers.

Add Float8DecimalToChar which applies the DBL_DIG adjustment
before formatting, matching PostgreSQL's behavior.

Skipping a release note since this seems like a minor bug.

Resolves: #166635

### sql: fix to_char sign placement with FMS and predec space 

The sign-writing condition in numProcessorToChar was checking the
character at the current number position (numberIdx) instead of the
character at the last_relevant position. This caused the sign to be
placed after the digit instead of before it when using FMS format
with values like 0 that have a predecimal space.

For example, `to_char(0, 'FMS9999.999')` produced `0+.` instead of
the correct `+0.`.

Fix by matching PostgreSQL's condition which checks
`*Np->last_relevant == '.'` (the character at the last_relevant
pointer) rather than the character at the current number position.

No release note since this bug seems pretty minor.

fixes #166634

---

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>
author: rafiss

before generating the overflow '#' fill string. CockroachDB was
not performing this adjustment, causing overflow strings with too
many '#' characters in the decimal portion when the format had
many post-decimal digit specifiers.

Add Float8DecimalToChar which applies the DBL_DIG adjustment
before formatting, matching PostgreSQL's behavior.

Resolves: #166635

title: release-26.2: singleflight,stop: fix trace.StartRegion crash with oversized strings
body:
Backport 2/2 commits from #166669 on behalf of @tbg.

----

## Summary

Go's `runtime/trace` crashes with `traceRegion: alloc too large` when
`trace.StartRegion` receives a string longer than ~64KB. This is an upstream
Go bug (missing truncation in `traceStringTable.put`; fix at
https://github.com/golang/go/pull/78348), triggered by CockroachDB's
`singleflight.doCall` which constructs a task name by concatenating
`g.opName + ":" + key`. For range cache lookups, `key` is built from raw
`roachpb.RKey` bytes via `makeLookupRequestKey` and can be hundreds of KB.

Three production crashes were observed on v25.4.5 with string lengths of
96KB, 387KB, and 444KB.

### Commit 1: `singleflight: don't include key in RunTask name`

Drops the key from the task name passed to `RunTask`. The task name is only
used for `trace.StartRegion`; the key is already recorded in the tracing
span via `sp.SetTag`.

### Commit 2: `stop: truncate trace region names to 256 bytes`

Defense in depth: truncates the task name to 256 bytes in `startRegion`
before passing it to `trace.StartRegion`, protecting against any future
caller passing an oversized string.

Epic: none



----

Release justification: fixes a bug that can crash the process when execution tracing is enabled
author: tbg

commits.message:
singleflight: don't include key in RunTask name

The `doCall` method was constructing the task name passed to
`Stopper.RunTask` as `g.opName + ":" + key`. For range cache lookups, the
key is built from raw `roachpb.RKey` bytes via `makeLookupRequestKey` and
can be hundreds of KB. When Go's execution tracer is enabled, this task
name flows through `trace.StartRegion`, which crashes the process with
`traceRegion: alloc too large` for strings exceeding ~64KB (a bug in Go's
`traceStringTable.put` which fails to truncate before inserting into the
trace map).

Three production crashes were observed on v25.4.5 with string lengths
of 96KB, 387KB, and 444KB.

The task name is only used for `trace.StartRegion`; `RunTask` does not
use it for anything else. The key is already recorded in the tracing
span via `sp.SetTag(g.tagName, ...)`, so no observability is lost.
Use `g.opName` alone, which is always a short fixed string.

Release note (bug fix): Fixed a crash (`traceRegion: alloc too large`)
that could occur when Go's execution tracer was enabled and a range
cache lookup used a key longer than ~64KB.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

stop: truncate trace region names to 256 bytes

Defense in depth against an upstream Go runtime bug where
`trace.StartRegion` crashes with `traceRegion: alloc too large` for
strings longer than ~64KB. The root cause is a missing truncation in
`traceStringTable.put`; the fix is upstream at
https://github.com/golang/go/pull/78348.

Truncate the task name to 256 bytes in `startRegion` before passing it
to `trace.StartRegion`. This protects against any caller passing an
oversized string through `RunTask` or `RunTaskWithErr`.

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: release-26.2: leaktest: re-add TLS handshake goroutine leak exclusion
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166085 on behalf of @cockroach-teamcity.

----

The exclusion for `net/http.(*persistConn).addTLS` and
`crypto/tls.(*Conn).handshakeContext` was removed in ecc4d006d93
under the assumption that Go 1.23+ (golang/go#62227) resolved
the underlying goroutine leak. However, the Go fix addressed
the handshake goroutine lifecycle, not the case where
`crypto/x509.loadSystemRoots` blocks on a slow syscall reading
system CA certificates from disk. In CI environments with slow
disk I/O, this read can outlast the HTTP client timeout, leaving
orphaned TLS handshake goroutines that trip the leak detector.

Re-add the exclusion with an updated comment explaining the
actual root cause: environment-dependent slow disk I/O during
system root CA loading, not a Go stdlib bug.

Resolves: #165944

Release note: None

Generated by Claude Code Auto-Solver
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

---

```
 pkg/util/leaktest/leaktest.go | 6 ++++++
 1 file changed, 6 insertions(+)
```

*This PR was auto-generated by [issue-autosolve](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/master/.github/workflows/issue-autosolve.yml) using Claude Code.*
*Please review carefully before approving.*

----

Release justification: flaky test fix
author: blathers-crl

commits.message:
leaktest: re-add TLS handshake goroutine leak exclusion

The exclusion for `net/http.(*persistConn).addTLS` and
`crypto/tls.(*Conn).handshakeContext` was removed in ecc4d006d93
under the assumption that Go 1.23+ (golang/go#62227) resolved
the underlying goroutine leak. However, the Go fix addressed
the handshake goroutine lifecycle, not the case where
`crypto/x509.loadSystemRoots` blocks on a slow syscall reading
system CA certificates from disk. In CI environments with slow
disk I/O, this read can outlast the HTTP client timeout, leaving
orphaned TLS handshake goroutines that trip the leak detector.

Re-add the exclusion with an updated comment explaining the
actual root cause: environment-dependent slow disk I/O during
system root CA loading, not a Go stdlib bug.

Resolves: #165944

Release note: None

Generated by Claude Code Auto-Solver
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: kvserver: fix TestVirtualizedIntentResolution flake
body:
The test writes a transactional intent via `SendWrappedWith` and then immediately calls `executeReadOnlyBatch` to read it. `CanAckBeforeApplication` returns true for transactional intent writes, so the proposal can be ack'd
before the engine batch is applied. This means the read can open a snapshot that doesn't yet contain the intent, causing it to return the original value instead of a `LockConflictError`.

Fix by setting `DisableCanAckBeforeApplication` on the test's store config.
Also add named subtests for easier debugging.

Fixes: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/166600

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>
author: miraradeva

reviews.body: LGTM, thanks! I think this needs backport-26.2.

commits.message:
kvserver: fix TestVirtualizedIntentResolution flake

The test writes a transactional intent via SendWrappedWith and then
immediately calls executeReadOnlyBatch to read it. CanAckBeforeApplication
returns true for transactional intent writes, so the proposal can be ack'd
before the engine batch is applied. This means the read can open a snapshot
that doesn't yet contain the intent, causing it to return the original
value instead of a LockConflictError.

Fix by setting DisableCanAckBeforeApplication on the test's store config.
Also add named subtests for easier debugging.

Fixes: #166600

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: roachtest: fix gopg flake by disabling assertions
body:
The gopg roachtest was failing intermittently due to COPY operations
timing out. The root cause is latch contention from bulk intent
resolution blocking COPY TO operations on a single-node test cluster,
where all user tables share one range. The gopg client has a 10-second
ReadTimeout that gets exceeded when latches are held for several
seconds during intent cleanup.

This change disables runtime assertions for the
gopg test, as the slower assertion-enabled builds widen the timeout
window and make the flake more likely.

Resolves: #166379

commits.message:
roachtest: fix gopg flake by ignoring flaky COPY tests and disabling assertions

The gopg roachtest was failing intermittently due to COPY operations
timing out. The root cause is latch contention from bulk intent
resolution blocking COPY TO operations on a single-node test cluster,
where all user tables share one range. The gopg client has a 10-second
ReadTimeout that gets exceeded when latches are held for several
seconds during intent cleanup.

This change adds `v10.TestBigColumn` and the CopyTo hook tests to the
gopg ignore list, matching the CopyFrom tests already ignored for the
same reason (#160602). It also disables runtime assertions for the
gopg test, as the slower assertion-enabled builds widen the timeout
window and make the flake more likely.

Resolves: #166379

title: release-26.2: sql: fix TestReadCommittedReadTimestampNotSteppedOnCommit for write buffering
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166531 on behalf of @tbg.

----

With a small metamorphic value for
`kv.transaction.write_buffering.max_buffer_size`, the write buffer can
overflow mid-transaction. This triggers a flush that completes all
in-flight writes synchronously, preventing the EndTxn from being a
parallel commit. The test's response filter only records the EndTxn
timestamp when `IsParallelCommit()` is true, so it collects 3 timestamps
instead of 4.

Pin the buffer size to the default (4MB) so the buffer doesn't overflow
during this small transaction.

Also add KV tracing via `SET TRACING` on a dedicated connection, logging
the trace on failure for easier debugging.

Fixes: #166072

----

Release justification: test flake

title: sql: wait for LEARNER replicas to clear before disabling queues in relocate tests
body:
TestRelocateVoters, TestExperimentalRelocateVoters, and
TestExperimentalRelocateNonVoters all share a race condition where
LEARNER replicas from ongoing replication may still exist when the test
disables replicate queues. This freezes the LEARNER in place, causing
subsequent RELOCATE commands to fail with "trying to add to a store
that already has a LEARNER".

The same fix was already applied to TestRelocateNonVoters in e0e333a7be9
(for #161363) — add a testutils.SucceedsSoon loop after
WaitForFullReplication that queries SHOW RANGES WITH DETAILS and waits
until learner_replicas is empty before disabling queues.

This commit applies the identical fix to the three remaining relocate
test functions that were missing it.

Resolves: #166483

title: sql/catalog/lease: fix test hang in TestModificationTimeTxnOrdering
body:
The test spawns two goroutines that call `t.Fatal`/`t.Fatalf` on
error. In Go, calling `t.Fatal` from a non-test goroutine invokes
`runtime.Goexit()`, which terminates the goroutine immediately.
Because `wg.Done()` was placed at the end of each goroutine body
(not deferred), it was skipped when `t.Fatal` triggered, causing
`wg.Wait()` in the main test goroutine to block forever. The test
then hung until the overall timeout killed it.

Move `wg.Done()` to `defer` statements at the top of each goroutine
so it always executes, even when `t.Fatal` calls `runtime.Goexit()`.

Resolves: #166380

Release note: None

Generated by Claude Code Auto-Solver
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

---

 1 file changed, 2 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)
```

commits.message:
sql/catalog/lease: fix test hang in TestModificationTimeTxnOrdering

The test spawns two goroutines that call `t.Fatal`/`t.Fatalf` on
error. In Go, calling `t.Fatal` from a non-test goroutine invokes
`runtime.Goexit()`, which terminates the goroutine immediately.
Because `wg.Done()` was placed at the end of each goroutine body
(not deferred), it was skipped when `t.Fatal` triggered, causing
`wg.Wait()` in the main test goroutine to block forever. The test
then hung until the overall timeout killed it.

Move `wg.Done()` to `defer` statements at the top of each goroutine
so it always executes, even when `t.Fatal` calls `runtime.Goexit()`.

Resolves: #166380

title: singleflight,stop: fix trace.StartRegion crash with oversized strings
body:
## Summary

Go's `runtime/trace` crashes with `traceRegion: alloc too large` when
`trace.StartRegion` receives a string longer than ~64KB. This is an upstream
Go bug (missing truncation in `traceStringTable.put`; fix at
https://github.com/golang/go/pull/78348), triggered by CockroachDB's
`singleflight.doCall` which constructs a task name by concatenating
`g.opName + ":" + key`. For range cache lookups, `key` is built from raw
`roachpb.RKey` bytes via `makeLookupRequestKey` and can be hundreds of KB.

Three production crashes were observed on v25.4.5 with string lengths of
96KB, 387KB, and 444KB.

### Commit 1: `singleflight: don't include key in RunTask name`

Drops the key from the task name passed to `RunTask`. The task name is only
used for `trace.StartRegion`; the key is already recorded in the tracing
span via `sp.SetTag`.

### Commit 2: `stop: truncate trace region names to 256 bytes`

Defense in depth: truncates the task name to 256 bytes in `startRegion`
before passing it to `trace.StartRegion`, protecting against any future
caller passing an oversized string.

Epic: none
author: tbg

reviews.body: /trunk merge

commits.message:
singleflight: don't include key in RunTask name

The `doCall` method was constructing the task name passed to
`Stopper.RunTask` as `g.opName + ":" + key`. For range cache lookups, the
key is built from raw `roachpb.RKey` bytes via `makeLookupRequestKey` and
can be hundreds of KB. When Go's execution tracer is enabled, this task
name flows through `trace.StartRegion`, which crashes the process with
`traceRegion: alloc too large` for strings exceeding ~64KB (a bug in Go's
`traceStringTable.put` which fails to truncate before inserting into the
trace map).

Three production crashes were observed on v25.4.5 with string lengths
of 96KB, 387KB, and 444KB.

The task name is only used for `trace.StartRegion`; `RunTask` does not
use it for anything else. The key is already recorded in the tracing
span via `sp.SetTag(g.tagName, ...)`, so no observability is lost.
Use `g.opName` alone, which is always a short fixed string.

Release note (bug fix): Fixed a crash (`traceRegion: alloc too large`)
that could occur when Go's execution tracer was enabled and a range
cache lookup used a key longer than ~64KB.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

stop: truncate trace region names to 256 bytes

Defense in depth against an upstream Go runtime bug where
`trace.StartRegion` crashes with `traceRegion: alloc too large` for
strings longer than ~64KB. The root cause is a missing truncation in
`traceStringTable.put`; the fix is upstream at
https://github.com/golang/go/pull/78348.

Truncate the task name to 256 bytes in `startRegion` before passing it
to `trace.StartRegion`. This protects against any caller passing an
oversized string through `RunTask` or `RunTaskWithErr`.

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: perturbation: rate-limit backfill pre-fill to avoid OOM on target node
body:
## Summary

The backfill perturbation test pre-fills ~11M rows at 10KB each with no rate
limit, producing ~18K writes/sec. Because the backfill database is
zone-constrained to place one replica on n12 (the target node), n12's stores
receive 3-4x the follower write load of normal stores. This saturates both
disks, triggers WAL failover thrashing between the two stores, and causes
memtables to grow to ~20GB per store (OOM).

The IO overload score never triggers admission control (max 0.304 vs threshold
1.0) because it's based on L0 shape, not physical disk saturation. While WAL
failover is active (~always in the failing run), IO admission control ignores memtable
build-up. But we've seen failures in which replicas went unavailable due to overload
as well, so disabling WAL failover likely wouldn't stabilize this test either.

This change rate-limits the pre-fill to 8K ops/sec and uses `--max-ops=11000000`
to maintain the same total data volume. The fill will take ~23 minutes instead
of 10, but (hopefully) avoids saturating n12's disks.

See analysis: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/166476#issuecomment-4125828130

Fixes-26.2: #166476
Fixes-25.2: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/165780
Touches: #136848

## Test plan

- Run `perturbation/full/backfill` and verify n12 no longer OOMs during pre-fill.
author: tbg

reviews.body: LGTM. Pending q on whether this configuration (--max-rate=8000 and --max-ops=11000000) should be changed for other cluster set up (like 5 nodes 4 vcpu) which may be selected by `perturbation/metamorphic/backfill`. But given perturbation/metamorphic/backfill is skipped, this works for perturbation/full/backfill.

commits.message:
perturbation: rate-limit backfill pre-fill to avoid OOM on target node

The backfill perturbation test pre-fills the backfill database with 100
concurrent writers at 10KB blocks for 10 minutes (the perturbation
duration). With no rate limit, this produces ~18K writes/sec and ~11M
total inserts. Because the backfill database is zone-constrained to have
one replica on n12 (the target node), n12 ends up with ~55 replicas per
store (1.7x normal), all as followers (leases are constrained off n12).
This results in 6-7K raft commands applied/sec on n12's stores vs ~2K on
normal stores, saturating both disks.

With among-stores WAL failover, when one store's disk saturates, its WAL
fails over to the other store's disk, overloading that disk too. The two
stores enter a coupled overload spiral where both WALs thrash between
disks. Despite this, the IO overload score (based on L0 shape) stays
below 0.304, so admission control never kicks in. Memtables grow to
~20 GB per store as flushes can't keep up, consuming all available RAM
and causing OOM.

This change rate-limits the pre-fill to 8K ops/sec and uses --max-ops
to control total data volume instead of --duration. The original test
completed 11,029,273 ops, so we use 11,000,000. This keeps the same
total data written (~110 GB) while reducing peak write pressure on n12
enough to avoid disk saturation.

Fixes: #166476
Fixes: #136848

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: security: fix data race in certificate expiration metric closures
body:
Several certificate metric closures in createMetricsLocked() read CertificateManager fields (nodeClientCert, tenantCert, tenantCACert) directly without acquiring cm.mu, while LoadCertificates() writes these fields under the lock. This data race caused metrics like NodeClientExpiration to not reflect updated values after certificate rotation via SIGHUP.

Add locked getter methods (NodeClientCert, TenantCert, TenantCACert) matching the existing pattern used by CACert, NodeCert, etc., and update the six affected metric closures to use them.

Resolves: #166648

Release note (bug fix): Fixed a data race that could cause certificate expiration metrics (security.certificate.expiration.node-client, security.certificate.expiration.client-tenant,
security.certificate.expiration.ca-client-tenant and their TTL counterparts) to not update after certificate rotation via SIGHUP.
author: aa-joshi

reviews.body: > @aa-joshi made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166655)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on sanchit-CRL).

TFTR! > @aa-joshi made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166655#-:-OoZjtIs4H_heIoeSC50:b-814g8k)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on sanchit-CRL).

commits.message:
security: fix data race in certificate expiration metric closures

Several certificate metric closures in createMetricsLocked() read
CertificateManager fields (nodeClientCert, tenantCert, tenantCACert)
directly without acquiring cm.mu, while LoadCertificates() writes
these fields under the lock. This data race caused metrics like
NodeClientExpiration to not reflect updated values after certificate
rotation via SIGHUP.

Add locked getter methods (NodeClientCert, TenantCert, TenantCACert)
matching the existing pattern used by CACert, NodeCert, etc., and
update the six affected metric closures to use them.

Resolves: #166648

Release note (bug fix): Fixed a data race that could cause
certificate expiration metrics (security.certificate.expiration.node-client,
security.certificate.expiration.client-tenant,
security.certificate.expiration.ca-client-tenant and their TTL
counterparts) to not update after certificate rotation via SIGHUP.

body:
**roachtest/pg_regress: accept the diff in `numeric`**

Here we have a deviation with the `$` sign - the PG has incorrect result, but we match PG 17.7.

**roachtest/pg_regress: accept the diff in `horology`**

We now have support for `to_timestamp` builtin. Note that this commit accepts some deviations from PG (i.e. the diff is larger than we would like) due to two differences:
- we hard-code PG 16.0 version of the regress suite, so the "expected" result is actually wrong
- we behave differently in regards to handling the time zone. Namely, PG defaults to using the server's time zone whereas we default to using UTC. A TODO is left to adjust the file by explicitly setting the UTC timezone in PG too.

title: release-26.2: importer: avoid AC priority inversion for job details update
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166490 on behalf of @rafiss.

----

The initial row count read during IMPORT runs at BulkNormalPri, which is correct for a potentially expensive full table scan. However, the updateDetails call that writes the result to the jobs table was running in the same transaction, meaning it was also subject to bulk admission control queuing. This could cause priority inversion: holding a lock on the jobs table while waiting in an AC queue blocks higher-priority work.

Fix this by passing nil to updateDetails so it runs in its own transaction at default priority. This is safe because the table is offline during IMPORT, so the row count is stable between the two transactions.

part of https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/79212
follow up to https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pull/166067
Release note: None

----

Release justification: avoid potential priority inversion issue for a new 26.2 feature

commits.message:
importer: avoid AC priority inversion for job details update

The initial row count read during IMPORT runs at BulkNormalPri, which
is correct for a potentially expensive full table scan. However, the
updateDetails call that writes the result to the jobs table was running
in the same transaction, meaning it was also subject to bulk admission
control queuing. This could cause priority inversion: holding a lock on
the jobs table while waiting in an AC queue blocks higher-priority work.

Fix this by passing nil to updateDetails so it runs in its own
transaction at default priority. This is safe because the table is
offline during IMPORT, so the row count is stable between the two
transactions.

title: roachtest: fix db-console/mixed-version-endpoints flake
body:
`initializeSchemaAndIDs` is called many times during mixed-version testing due to the randomized test plan. Each call re-ran `workload init tpcc`, causing flakes:
With `--drop`: DROP races with background schema ops from a prior invocation
Without `--drop`: AddSSTable key collisions with older binary versions

Fix: check if TPCC is already initialized via `system.namespace` and skip init on subsequent calls.

Fixes #166500

Release note: None
author: dhartunian

commits.message:
roachtest: fix db-console/mixed-version-endpoints flake

The `initializeSchemaAndIDs` function is called many times during
mixed-version testing due to the randomized test plan. Previously,
each call re-ran `workload init tpcc`, which caused two different
flakes depending on whether `--drop` was used:
- With `--drop`: DROP races with background schema operations from a
  prior invocation.
- Without `--drop`: AddSSTable hits key collisions when older binary
  versions don't handle existing data gracefully.

Fix by checking whether TPCC is already initialized (via
system.namespace) and skipping the init on subsequent calls.

Fixes #166500

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: release-26.2: multiregionccl: retry follower read assertions in regional_by_table test
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166469 on behalf of @shghasemi.

----

Add retry to trace-sql assertions that verify follower reads are served locally from non-leaseholder replicas. Background replicate queue operations can race with the test and temporarily remove a non-voter between wait-for-zone-config-changes passing and the trace-sql assertion running. The replicate queue self-heals the under-replicated range within seconds, so retrying the assertion is sufficient.

Fixes #165420

Release note: None

title: release-26.2: obs/ash: propagate WorkloadType through fetcher, streamer, and tablewriter admission paths
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166466 on behalf of @alyshanjahani-crl.

----

Several DistSQL operator admission paths constructed `admission.WorkInfo` with `WorkloadID` set but `WorkloadType` omitted, causing ASH samples to show `workload_type = 'UNKNOWN'` and job IDs to be hex-encoded instead of decimal. The affected paths are:

- `kv_batch_fetcher` response admission and admission pacer
- `cFetcher` admission pacer
- `kvstreamer` response admission
- `tablewriter` response admission

Thread `workloadType` through the constructors (`NewStreamer`, `NewKVFetcher`, `NewStreamingKVFetcher`, `NewDirectKVBatchFetcher`, `newCFetcherWrapper`) alongside the existing `workloadID` parameter. All callers have access to `EvalCtx.WorkloadType`.

Also extend `TestWorkloadIDPropagation` to assert that `WorkloadType != UNKNOWN` on all admission `WorkInfo` entries captured via the `WorkQueueAdmitInterceptor`, which would have caught this regression.

Fixes: #166463
Release note: None

----

Release justification: Critical fix for ensuring proper attribution in ASH samples.
author: alyshanjahani-crl

commits.message:
obs/ash: propagate WorkloadType through fetcher, streamer, and tablewriter admission paths

Several DistSQL operator admission paths constructed `admission.WorkInfo`
with `WorkloadID` set but `WorkloadType` omitted, causing ASH samples to
show `workload_type = 'UNKNOWN'` and job IDs to be hex-encoded instead
of decimal. The affected paths are:

- `kv_batch_fetcher` response admission and admission pacer
- `cFetcher` admission pacer
- `kvstreamer` response admission
- `tablewriter` response admission

Thread `workloadType` through the constructors (`NewStreamer`,
`NewKVFetcher`, `NewStreamingKVFetcher`, `NewDirectKVBatchFetcher`,
`newCFetcherWrapper`) alongside the existing `workloadID` parameter.
All callers have access to `EvalCtx.WorkloadType`.

Also extend `TestWorkloadIDPropagation` to assert that `WorkloadType !=
UNKNOWN` on all admission `WorkInfo` entries captured via the
`WorkQueueAdmitInterceptor`, which would have caught this regression.

Fixes: #166463

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: release-25.2: sql: add cluster setting to block auto-unlock of schema_locked tables
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166246.

/cc @cockroachdb/release

---

The `schema_locked` storage parameter was designed as a changefeed optimization. The declarative schema changer automatically unlocks schema-locked tables, performs DDL, then re-locks them — meaning `schema_locked` doesn't actually prevent user-initiated DDL.

This commit adds a new cluster setting `sql.schema.auto_unlock.enabled` (default `true`) that controls whether the declarative schema changer is allowed to auto-unlock schema-locked tables. When set to `false`, DDL on schema-locked tables is blocked unless the user manually unlocks the table first via `ALTER TABLE t SET (schema_locked = false)`.

The legacy schema changer already blocks all DDL on schema-locked tables regardless of this setting, so no changes were needed there.

Resolves: #166242

Release note (sql change): Added a new cluster setting `sql.schema.auto_unlock.enabled` that controls whether DDL operations automatically unlock `schema_locked` tables. When set to `false`, DDL on schema-locked tables is blocked unless the user manually unlocks the table first. This allows customers using LDR to enforce `schema_locked` as a hard lock preventing user-initiated DDL. The default is `true`, preserving existing behavior.

Fixes ENGREQ-333
Release justification: low risk, opt-in change requested by customer
author: rafiss

reviews.body: > @fqazi reviewed 5 files and all commit messages, and made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166575#-:-OoVe1DSEyHQDBPq9j9c:bnfp4nl)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on rafiss).

commits.message:
sql: add cluster setting to block auto-unlock of schema_locked tables

The `schema_locked` storage parameter was designed as a changefeed
optimization. The declarative schema changer automatically unlocks
schema-locked tables, performs DDL, then re-locks them — meaning
`schema_locked` doesn't actually prevent user-initiated DDL.

This commit adds a new cluster setting `sql.schema.auto_unlock.enabled`
(default `true`) that controls whether the declarative schema changer
is allowed to auto-unlock schema-locked tables. When set to `false`,
DDL on schema-locked tables is blocked unless the user manually unlocks
the table first via `ALTER TABLE t SET (schema_locked = false)`.

The legacy schema changer already blocks all DDL on schema-locked
tables regardless of this setting, so no changes were needed there.

Resolves: #166242

Release note (sql change): Added a new cluster setting
`sql.schema.auto_unlock.enabled` that controls whether DDL operations
automatically unlock `schema_locked` tables. When set to `false`, DDL
on schema-locked tables is blocked unless the user manually unlocks the
table first. This allows customers using LDR to enforce `schema_locked`
as a hard lock preventing user-initiated DDL. The default is `true`,
preserving existing behavior.

title: release-26.2:  server,grpcutil: fix nil panic in TestDrain with DRPC
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166524 on behalf of @Nukitt.

----

Previously, `grpcutil.IsClosedConnection` would panic with a nil pointer dereference when called with a nil error, because it called `err.Error()` without a nil guard. This was exposed by a race condition specific to DRPC: after server shutdown, the DRPC connection can remain alive (unlike gRPC which tears down immediately via `grpc.Stop()`), allowing `Drain()` to return a nil error during the shutdown window.

cockroachdb/drpc#36 widened this race by introducing a heap allocation (`ClosedError.New`) in the manager's stream cancellation path, slightly delaying `stream.Cancel()` and making it more likely for `CloseSend()` to complete before termination signals are set.

This change adds a nil guard to `IsClosedConnection` and a nil check in `drain_test.go` before calling it.

For more details: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/165377#issuecomment-4116252691

Epic: None
Fixes: #165377
Fixes: #166117 
Release note: None

----

Release justification: Fixes release blockers.
author: blathers-crl

reviews.body: LGTM!

commits.message:
server,grpcutil: fix nil panic in TestDrain with DRPC

Previously, `grpcutil.IsClosedConnection` would panic with a nil pointer
dereference when called with a nil error, because it called `err.Error()`
without a nil guard. This was exposed by a race condition specific to DRPC:
after server shutdown, the DRPC connection can remain alive (unlike gRPC which
tears down immediately via `grpc.Stop()`), allowing `Drain()` to return a nil
error during the shutdown window.

cockroachdb/drpc#36 widened this race by introducing a heap allocation
(`ClosedError.New`) in the manager's stream cancellation path, slightly
delaying `stream.Cancel()` and making it more likely for `CloseSend()` to
complete before termination signals are set.

This change adds a nil guard to `IsClosedConnection` and a nil check in
`drain_test.go` before calling it.

Epic: None
Fixes: #165377
Release note: None

title: sql: fix ALTER FUNCTION RENAME duplicate detection in non-public schemas
body:
Previously, renaming a function or procedure to a name that already existed with the same signature in a non-public schema was incorrectly allowed. This happened because the duplicate check did not set ExplicitSchema on the routine name, causing matchRoutine to search the session search path instead of the specific schema. If the schema was not on the search path, the duplicate was not found and the rename succeeded, leaving two functions with the same name and signature that could not be dropped.

Fix by setting SchemaName and ExplicitSchema on the lookup object.

Fixes #166361

Release note (bug fix): Fixed a bug where ALTER FUNCTION ... RENAME TO and ALTER PROCEDURE ... RENAME TO could create duplicate functions in non-public schemas.
author: shghasemi

reviews.body: > @eric-alton reviewed 3 files and all commit messages, and made 2 comments. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166570#-:-OoWY7w19KrWYbzF9ZvA:bnfp4nl)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on shghasemi). ___ *[`pkg/sql/logictest/testdata/logic_test/procedure_schema_change` line 99 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166570#-OoWW_ma8RWmJoRUNDtL:-OoWW_ma8RWmJoRUNDtM:bbpfj78) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/7f8d9c03ebcae75a9664b5a9b396fb277c1235a1/pkg/sql/logictest/testdata/logic_test/procedure_schema_change#L99)):* > ``` > > # Regression test: renaming a procedure in a non-public schema should > # detect duplicates within that schema. > ``` nit: Consider extending this scenario to confirm we still allow a rename that results in procedure overloading - rename to the same schema and name, but with a different number of arguments. The same comment applies to your new scenario in `udf_schema_change`. It looks like we already have such scenarios for the public schema, but not non-public.

commits.message:
sql: fix ALTER FUNCTION RENAME duplicate detection in non-public schemas

Previously, renaming a function or procedure to a name that already
existed with the same signature in a non-public schema was incorrectly
allowed. This happened because the duplicate check did not set
ExplicitSchema on the routine name, causing matchRoutine to search the
session search path instead of the specific schema. If the schema was
not on the search path, the duplicate was not found and the rename
succeeded, leaving two functions with the same name and signature that
could not be dropped.

Fix by setting SchemaName and ExplicitSchema on the lookup object.

Fixes #166361

Release note (bug fix): Fixed a bug where ALTER FUNCTION ... RENAME TO
and ALTER PROCEDURE ... RENAME TO could create duplicate functions in
non-public schemas.

title: release-26.2: obs/ash: fix tenant isolation for ASH virtual tables in shared-process mode
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166465 on behalf of @alyshanjahani-crl.

----

The ASH virtual table populate functions in crdb_internal.go called ListLocalActiveSessionHistory and ListActiveSessionHistory without propagating the planner's tenant ID into the context. Those handlers rely on roachpb.ClientTenantFromContext to determine the requesting tenant and filter samples accordingly. That context value is normally set by the gRPC auth interceptor, but in shared-process mode the call is in-process so the interceptor never runs, causing filterByTenant to be false and returning all tenants' samples unfiltered.

Fix by wrapping the context with roachpb.ContextWithClientTenant using the planner's Codec.TenantID before calling the handler. This ensures secondary tenants only see their own ASH samples while the system tenant continues to see all samples.

Also add TestASHMultiTenantIsolation which starts two shared-process tenants, generates work on each, and verifies that each tenant only sees its own tenant_id in crdb_internal.node_active_session_history.

Fixes #166456
Release note: None

----

Release justification: Critical fix that prevents tenants in a shared-process environment from seeing each others ASH samples.
author: alyshanjahani-crl

commits.message:
obs/ash: fix tenant isolation for ASH virtual tables in shared-process mode

The ASH virtual table populate functions called
ListLocalActiveSessionHistory and ListActiveSessionHistory without
propagating the planner's tenant ID into the context. Those handlers
rely on roachpb.ClientTenantFromContext to filter samples by tenant,
but that context value is only set by the gRPC auth interceptor which
does not run for in-process calls in shared-process mode. This caused
filterByTenant to be false, leaking all tenants' samples to any
tenant querying the virtual tables.

Fix by setting ContextWithClientTenant from the planner's
Codec.TenantID before calling the handlers.

Fixes #166456

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: tsdump2duck: add CLI tool to convert tsdump.gob to DuckDB SQL
body:
## Summary

- **tsdump2duck**: Add a small CLI tool that reads a CockroachDB `tsdump.gob`
  file (produced by the roachtest harness) and emits SQL to stdout that can be
  piped into DuckDB for interactive exploration of cluster timeseries data.
  Produces two tables: `timeseries(name, source, ts, value)` and
  `store_node_map(store_id, node_id)`.

- **investigate**: Teach the investigation agent about `tsdump2duck`. Adds
  `go run ./pkg/cmd/tsdump2duck` and `duckdb` to the workflow's allowed tools,
  documents usage in the investigation prompt (including DuckDB install,
  schema, example queries, and a pointer to `docs/generated/metrics/metrics.yaml`),
  and adds a duckdb smoke test.

Usage:
```bash
./dev build pkg/cmd/tsdump2duck
./bin/tsdump2duck tsdump.gob | duckdb mydb.duckdb
```

Then explore:
```sql
SELECT DISTINCT name FROM timeseries WHERE name LIKE '%admission%' ORDER BY 1;
SELECT ts, value FROM timeseries
  WHERE name = 'cr.store.storage.wal.failover.switch.count' AND source = '23'
  ORDER BY ts;
```

Epic: none
Release note: None

title: release-26.2: workload: deflake conflict TestRandomWriter
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166438 on behalf of @jeffswenson.

----

This fixes a flake that occurs when the "char" type is included in the
primary index. The "char" type requires parameters to have an explicit
cast to "char", otherwise the type gets inferred as STRING which causes
'\x00' to be interpreted as a string literal instead of a byte literal.
The symptom is that the delete fails to find the row because the "char"
column is truncated to 1 byte on insert, but the WHERE clause compares
against the un-truncated string.

We fix this by adding explicit type casts to the parameter placeholders
(e.g. `$1::"char"`) in both the upsert and delete statements, which
ensures lib/pq values are always decoded using the correct type.

The test was switched over to SucceedsSoon in order to get better error
messages. The ownership of the workload/conflict and workload/random
packages was switched to the CDC team, because these are only used in
LDR tests.

Release note: none
Informs: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/165808

----

Release justification: test only change.

title: release-26.2: server: add cluster setting to disable GC assist
body:
Backport 2/2 commits from #166306.

/cc @cockroachdb/release

---

## Summary

- Adds `pkg/util/gcassist`, a thin wrapper around the forked runtime's
  `SetGCAssistEnabled`/`GCAssistEnabled` with `bazel`/`!bazel` build tags
  (following the `grunning` pattern).
- Adds `server.gc_assist.enabled` cluster setting (bool, `SystemVisible`)
  that dynamically toggles GC assist. Defaults to the runtime's current
  state at startup (respecting `GODEBUG=gcnoassist=1`).
- Adds `sys.gc.assist.enabled` gauge metric (0/1) so the state is
  observable even without the setting being explicitly set.

Fixes: #166040

## Commits

1. **util/gcassist: add package for controlling GC assist** — new package
   with `SetEnabled`/`Enabled` and bazel/non-bazel stubs.
2. **server: add cluster setting and metric for GC assist** — setting
   registration, `SetOnChange` wiring in `startSampleEnvironment`, and
   gauge metric in `RuntimeStatSampler`.

Release justification: default-off change that can help improve foreground latencies.
author: tbg

commits.message:
util/gcassist: add package for controlling GC assist

Add a new utility package that wraps the forked Go runtime's
`runtime.SetGCAssistEnabled` and `runtime.GCAssistEnabled` APIs.
Under Bazel builds (which use CockroachDB's forked runtime), these
delegate to the real runtime functions. Under vanilla Go builds, they
are no-ops (`SetEnabled` does nothing, `Enabled` returns true).

This follows the same `bazel`/`!bazel` build-tag pattern used by
`pkg/util/grunning`.

Informs: #166040

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

server: add cluster setting and metric for GC assist

Add `server.gc_assist.enabled`, a `SystemVisible` bool cluster setting
that dynamically toggles the Go runtime's GC assist via the `gcassist`
package. The default value reflects the runtime's current state at
startup, picking up `GODEBUG=gcnoassist=1` if set.

The setting is applied in `startSampleEnvironment` with a `SetOnChange`
callback so changes take effect immediately.

Also add a `sys.gc.assist.enabled` gauge metric (0 or 1) to
`RuntimeStatSampler`, updated each sampling cycle, so the current
GC assist state is observable via metrics even when the setting has
not been explicitly changed.

Informs: #166040
Release note (ops change): A new cluster setting
`server.gc_assist.enabled` allows dynamically disabling GC assist in
CockroachDB's forked Go runtime. The default follows the
`GODEBUG=gcnoassist` flag. A new metric `sys.gc.assist.enabled`
reports the current state (1 = enabled, 0 = disabled).

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: sql/sqlstats: fix TestTransactionLatencies flake
body:
## Summary

- Replaces the flaky wall-clock comparison (`InDelta` with 5% tolerance) in `TestTransactionLatencies` with an invariant chain: `expectedIdle <= idle <= service <= txnTime`.
- This validates the latency accounting without comparing against external wall-clock time, which varies significantly in CI environments.
- This test is a chronic flake with 5+ prior closed issues, most recently #166479. The latest failure showed a 21% delta.

Fixes #166479

Release note: None
author: dhartunian

reviews.body:
This diff is removing the `InDelta` assertion. I expected it to just modify that line.

~~It's also replacing the wall clock measurement (`totalTxnTime`) which i think is valuable~~

commits.message:
sql/sqlstats: fix TestTransactionLatencies flake

Replace the wall-clock comparison assertion (InDelta with 5% tolerance)
with an invariant chain that is not timing-sensitive. The test now
verifies the relationship: expectedIdle <= idle <= service <= txnTime.
This validates the latency accounting without comparing against external
wall-clock time, which varies significantly in CI environments.

Fixes #166479

Release note: None

title: investigate: analyze heap profiles on OOM failures
body:
## Summary

Teaches the `/investigate` agent to analyze heap profiler artifacts when a node
is OOM-killed:

- Look for `memprof.*.pprof` files in `artifacts.zip` (not `debug.zip`, which
  errors for dead nodes)
- Diff earliest vs latest profiles with `go tool pprof -base` to isolate
  runaway allocation sites
- Cross-reference with `memmonitoring` files to check memory accounting gaps
- Adds `go tool pprof` to the workflow's allowed tools
- Adds a smoke test entry for the new permission

Motivated by [#166476](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/166476)
where the investigation identified the OOM but didn't analyze the heap profiles
that were available in the artifacts.

Release note: None
Epic: none
author: tbg

reviews.body: I trust you checked it works. The wording LGTM.

commits.message:
investigate: analyze heap profiles on OOM failures

Teach the investigate agent to look for heap profiler artifacts in
artifacts.zip when a node is OOM-killed (debug.zip will have errors
since the node is dead). The agent diffs the earliest and latest
memprof pprof snapshots to isolate runaway allocation sites, and
cross-references with memmonitoring files.

Also adds `go tool pprof` to the workflow's allowed tools and a
smoke test entry for it.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: sql: fix TestReadCommittedReadTimestampNotSteppedOnCommit for write buffering
body:
With a small metamorphic value for
`kv.transaction.write_buffering.max_buffer_size`, the write buffer can
overflow mid-transaction. This triggers a flush that completes all
in-flight writes synchronously, preventing the EndTxn from being a
parallel commit. The test's response filter only records the EndTxn
timestamp when `IsParallelCommit()` is true, so it collects 3 timestamps
instead of 4.

Pin the buffer size to the default (4MB) so the buffer doesn't overflow
during this small transaction.

Also add KV tracing via `SET TRACING` on a dedicated connection, logging
the trace on failure for easier debugging.

Fixes: #166072
author: tbg

reviews.body: Thanks for fixing this! > @miraradeva reviewed 1 file and all commit messages, and made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166531#-:-OoVS7zBEKPz_DipQ7K2:b-mws4q9)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on tbg).

commits.message:
sql: fix TestReadCommittedReadTimestampNotSteppedOnCommit for write buffering

With a small metamorphic value for
kv.transaction.write_buffering.max_buffer_size, the write buffer can
overflow mid-transaction. This triggers a flush that completes all
in-flight writes synchronously, preventing the EndTxn from being a
parallel commit. The test's response filter only records the EndTxn
timestamp when IsParallelCommit() is true, so it collects 3 timestamps
instead of 4.

Pin the buffer size to the default (4MB) so the buffer doesn't overflow
during this small transaction.

Also add KV tracing via SET TRACING on a dedicated connection, logging
the trace on failure for easier debugging.

Fixes: #166072

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: release-26.2: mmaprototype: balance CPU on utilization
body:
Backport 5/5 commits from #162182 on behalf of @tbg.

----

1. mmaprototype: remove denom= from load summary logging
2. mmaprototype: add test for uniformly saturated cluster
3. kv/kvserver/allocator: balance CPU on utilization
4. mmaprototype: annotate fractionUsed with * when clamped
5. mmaprototype: extract computeSummaryUpperBound

----

Release justification: mma-only change (not enabled by default).

Epic: CRDB-56265
author: tbg

commits.message:
mmaprototype: remove denom= from load summary logging

Remove the `denom=` field from the load summary log output. This field
logged the effective denominator used by fractionAbove when the
significance floor clamped it. It's noisy and will be replaced by a more
compact annotation in a follow-up commit.

This is a mechanical change — no behavioral impact.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

mmaprototype: add test for uniformly saturated cluster

Add a TestClusterState test where all four nodes are at 95% CPU
utilization. s1-s3 share replicas, s4 has none but is equally
overloaded.

Baseline behavior: all stores are classified as loadNormal despite
being at 95% utilization. Because every store is at the mean, the
relative comparison (fractionAbove ≈ 0) results in loadNormal, and the
old utilization gate (meanUtil*1.1 < fractionUsed) also doesn't fire
since no store is above the mean. The cluster looks "normally loaded"
when it is clearly saturated.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

kv/kvserver/allocator: balance CPU on utilization

Before, the CPU load summary compared absolute cpu-nanos to the mean even when
capacity was known. That caused heterogeneous core counts to converge to equal
absolute usage and left higher-core nodes underutilized. Compare CPU load to
the mean utilization instead. This keeps the existing thresholds and
homogeneous behavior.

Additionally, generalize this utilization-based comparison to all resources
with a known capacity (ByteSize in addition to CPURate), not just CPURate.

Simplify the overload check: since fractionAbove is now utilization-based
whenever capacity is known, the multi-tiered intermediate checks (75%, 1.5x
meanUtil, 1.75x meanUtil) are redundant with the general
meanFractionSlow/meanFractionLow classification. Keep only the >90% absolute
utilization check as a safety net for nearing full capacity.

Test changes fall into the following categories:

1. rebalance_replica.txt: adds an explicit store-load-msg for s2 to give it a
   known capacity. Without this, s2 has UnknownCapacity and the
   utilization-based comparison can't work. The seq numbers bump accordingly.

2. Reason string changes from the removed multi-tiered overload block. The
   old code classified stores through the utilization override (reasons like
   "fractionUsed > 75%", "fractionUsed < 75% and >1.75x meanUtil", etc.).
   Now, the same classification happens through the general fractionAbove
   logic (reason: "load is >10% above mean"). The load summary level
   (overloadSlow, loadNormal, etc.) is unchanged in most cases. This affects
   rebalance_stores_capacity_mismatch, all rebalance_stores_cpu_* tests,
   rebalance_stores_nls_sls_mismatch, and skip_range_invalid_config.

3. rebalance_stores_cpu_replica_refusing_target.txt: all stores are at 100%
   utilization (load=1000, capacity=1000). Previously classified as
   loadNormal (since fractionAbove=0 and the utilization override didn't fire
   because meanUtil*1.1 > fractionUsed). Now correctly classified as
   overloadUrgent via the >90% check. The failure reason changes from
   "not-overloaded" to "no-cand-load" — same outcome (no rebalancing), but
   the classification is more accurate.

4. rebalance_stores_capacity_mismatch.txt: in addition to reason strings,
   some candidate evaluation blocks are removed. These were intermediate
   steps where the old code tried lease transfers between equally-loaded
   stores. With utilization-based fractionAbove, s1 at 90%/83.2% meanUtil
   now classifies as loadNoChange (fractionAbove=0.08) instead of
   overloadSlow, so it correctly skips trying to shed to equally-loaded
   peers earlier.

5. asim simulation testdata (fulldisk, high_write_uniform_cpu,
   skewed_cpu_skewed_write, one_voter_skewed_cpu_skewed_write, etc.): hash
   and metric value changes from different rebalancing decisions. The
   fulldisk test notably shows ByteSize now benefiting from
   utilization-aware balancing.

6. rebalance_stores_cpu_replica_lateral.txt: the test setup is adjusted to
   make this a homogeneous CPU deployment (s3/s4 capacity changed from 1000
   to 500, so n3 with two stores totals 1000 CPU capacity, matching n1 and
   n2). Previously, the utilization override ("fractionUsed < 75%") caused
   s1 and s2 to be incorrectly classified as overloadSlow for CPURate even
   though their CPU utilization was only 10%, because the override didn't
   account for how far from the mean utilization the store actually was. This
   triggered a full lease-shedding cycle from s1 (which failed, since all
   replica-holding stores had the same false-positive overload) before
   finally reaching s3's write-bandwidth overload. Now, with
   utilization-based fractionAbove, s1 and s2 are correctly classified as
   loadNormal (10% utilization vs 10% mean utilization), so only s3 is
   identified as overloaded (on WriteBandwidth). The rebalance moves the
   replica from s3 to s4 directly without the spurious detour through CPU
   lease shedding.

x-ref https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/153777

Release note: None

Epic: CRDB-56265

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

mmaprototype: annotate fractionUsed with * when clamped

When the significance floor clamps the denominator used to compute
fractionAbove, annotate `fractionUsed=x%` as `fractionUsed=x%*` in the
load summary log. This replaces the removed `denom=` field with a more
compact signal that the clamping was active.

Note: `†` (U+2020) was the preferred annotation character but the
cockroachdb/redact package strips non-ASCII characters from safe values,
so `*` is used instead.

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

mmaprototype: extract computeSummaryUpperBound

Factor out the summaryUpperBound computation from loadSummaryForDimension
into a standalone function with documentation explaining how it differs
from significance floor clamping. Both mechanisms prevent unnecessary
shedding at low load, but they address different concerns: clamping
handles statistical significance (small differences / small mean), while
the upper bound handles prioritization (don't bother shedding a
dimension whose absolute utilization is negligible).

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: server,grpcutil: fix nil panic in TestDrain with DRPC
body:
Previously, `grpcutil.IsClosedConnection` would panic with a nil pointer dereference when called with a nil error, because it called `err.Error()` without a nil guard. This was exposed by a race condition specific to DRPC: after server shutdown, the DRPC connection can remain alive (unlike gRPC which tears down immediately via `grpc.Stop()`), allowing `Drain()` to return a nil error during the shutdown window.

cockroachdb/drpc#36 widened this race by introducing a heap allocation (`ClosedError.New`) in the manager's stream cancellation path, slightly delaying `stream.Cancel()` and making it more likely for `CloseSend()` to complete before termination signals are set.

This change adds a nil guard to `IsClosedConnection` and a nil check in `drain_test.go` before calling it.

For more details: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/165377#issuecomment-4116252691

Epic: None
Fixes: #165377
Fixes: #166117 
Release note: None
author: Nukitt

reviews.body: LGTM!
LGTM

commits.message:
server,grpcutil: fix nil panic in TestDrain with DRPC

Previously, `grpcutil.IsClosedConnection` would panic with a nil pointer
dereference when called with a nil error, because it called `err.Error()`
without a nil guard. This was exposed by a race condition specific to DRPC:
after server shutdown, the DRPC connection can remain alive (unlike gRPC which
tears down immediately via `grpc.Stop()`), allowing `Drain()` to return a nil
error during the shutdown window.

cockroachdb/drpc#36 widened this race by introducing a heap allocation
(`ClosedError.New`) in the manager's stream cancellation path, slightly
delaying `stream.Cancel()` and making it more likely for `CloseSend()` to
complete before termination signals are set.

This change adds a nil guard to `IsClosedConnection` and a nil check in
`drain_test.go` before calling it.

Epic: None
Fixes: #165377
Release note: None

title: [release-24.3.29-rc] ci: add trunk branch on push trigger
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #161230.

---

## Summary
Backports the critical missing CI trigger for Trunk merge queue branches to release-24.3.29-rc.

This fixes the issue where Trunk creates `trunk-merge/**` branches but GitHub Actions doesn't run CI on them, causing PRs to timeout.

## Problem Fixed
- Trunk merge queue creates branches but CI doesn't run
- PRs timeout after 80 minutes with no CI activity
- Without this, Trunk won't work properly when multiple PRs are in queue

title: release-25.4: sql: add cluster setting to block auto-unlock of schema_locked tables
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166246.

/cc @cockroachdb/release

---

The `schema_locked` storage parameter was designed as a changefeed optimization. The declarative schema changer automatically unlocks schema-locked tables, performs DDL, then re-locks them — meaning `schema_locked` doesn't actually prevent user-initiated DDL.

This commit adds a new cluster setting `sql.schema.auto_unlock.enabled` (default `true`) that controls whether the declarative schema changer is allowed to auto-unlock schema-locked tables. When set to `false`, DDL on schema-locked tables is blocked unless the user manually unlocks the table first via `ALTER TABLE t SET (schema_locked = false)`.

The legacy schema changer already blocks all DDL on schema-locked tables regardless of this setting, so no changes were needed there.

Resolves: #166242

Release note (sql change): Added a new cluster setting `sql.schema.auto_unlock.enabled` that controls whether DDL operations automatically unlock `schema_locked` tables. When set to `false`, DDL on schema-locked tables is blocked unless the user manually unlocks the table first. This allows customers using LDR to enforce `schema_locked` as a hard lock preventing user-initiated DDL. The default is `true`, preserving existing behavior.

Fixes ENGREQ-333
Release justification: low-risk, opt-in change requested by customer
author: rafiss

commits.message:
sql: add cluster setting to block auto-unlock of schema_locked tables

The `schema_locked` storage parameter was designed as a changefeed
optimization. The declarative schema changer automatically unlocks
schema-locked tables, performs DDL, then re-locks them — meaning
`schema_locked` doesn't actually prevent user-initiated DDL.

This commit adds a new cluster setting `sql.schema.auto_unlock.enabled`
(default `true`) that controls whether the declarative schema changer
is allowed to auto-unlock schema-locked tables. When set to `false`,
DDL on schema-locked tables is blocked unless the user manually unlocks
the table first via `ALTER TABLE t SET (schema_locked = false)`.

The legacy schema changer already blocks all DDL on schema-locked
tables regardless of this setting, so no changes were needed there.

Resolves: #166242

Release note (sql change): Added a new cluster setting
`sql.schema.auto_unlock.enabled` that controls whether DDL operations
automatically unlock `schema_locked` tables. When set to `false`, DDL
on schema-locked tables is blocked unless the user manually unlocks the
table first. This allows customers using LDR to enforce `schema_locked`
as a hard lock preventing user-initiated DDL. The default is `true`,
preserving existing behavior.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: importer: avoid AC priority inversion for job details update
body:
The initial row count read during IMPORT runs at BulkNormalPri, which is correct for a potentially expensive full table scan. However, the updateDetails call that writes the result to the jobs table was running in the same transaction, meaning it was also subject to bulk admission control queuing. This could cause priority inversion: holding a lock on the jobs table while waiting in an AC queue blocks higher-priority work.

Fix this by passing nil to updateDetails so it runs in its own transaction at default priority. This is safe because the table is offline during IMPORT, so the row count is stable between the two transactions.

part of https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/79212
follow up to https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pull/166067
Release note: None
author: rafiss

Previously, bghal (Brendan Gerrity) wrote…
Would it make sense to run `updateDetails()` outside this function scope?
Also wondering: will it be a problem if the details are committed but the detailsWithInitialRowCount transaction rolls back?

> @rafiss made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166490)* status: :shipit: complete! 2 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on bghal, dt, and mw5h). ___ *[`pkg/sql/importer/import_job.go` line 283 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166490#-OoVwuDk4NY6J5jARX84:-OoVzcjDBwHuB91lbI28:bhoqr5q) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/8b8a7c8e807313c852fd4ab991a701ca6265f10c/pkg/sql/importer/import_job.go#L283)):*
