title: release-26.2: roachtest: collect debug zip on cdc latency failure
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166442 on behalf of @DarrylWong.

----

A common pattern in cdc tests is to stick the workload and latency verifier goroutines in an error group and block the test on said error group. One side effect of this is that we may fail the test due to the latency verifier, but continue blocking for the workload to finish. This causes the debug zip taken by the framework on test failure to happen potentially 30+ minutes after the actual latency failure.

This changes the verifier to take its own debug zip on failure.

Release note: none
Epic: none
Fixes: none

----

Release justification: test only change
author: blathers-crl

title: release-26.2: workload/schemachange: exclude hidden columns from policy expressions
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166329 on behalf of @fqazi.

----

This commit updates tableColumnsShuffled to filter out hidden columns when gathering available columns for random policy expression generation.

Previously, the workload generator could generate policies referencing hidden columns (e.g., from hash-sharded indexes). However, the dropColumn error screening logic (which uses information_schema.columns) is blind to hidden columns, leading to false negatives where a column drop was attempted without expecting the policy dependency error.

Since dropColumn already ignores hidden columns when selecting a target to drop, ensuring policies only reference visible columns prevents this class of false-negative dependency bugs.

Fixes: #164725
Epic: None
Release note: None

----

Release justification: test only change

title: multiregionccl: retry follower read assertions in regional_by_table test
body:
Add retry to trace-sql assertions that verify follower reads are served locally from non-leaseholder replicas. Background replicate queue operations can race with the test and temporarily remove a non-voter between wait-for-zone-config-changes passing and the trace-sql assertion running. The replicate queue self-heals the under-replicated range within seconds, so retrying the assertion is sufficient.

Fixes #165420

Release note: None
author: shghasemi

commits.message:
multiregionccl: retry follower read assertions in regional_by_table test

Add retry to trace-sql assertions that verify follower reads are served
locally from non-leaseholder replicas. Background replicate queue
operations can race with the test and temporarily remove a non-voter
between wait-for-zone-config-changes passing and the trace-sql assertion
running. The replicate queue self-heals the under-replicated range within
seconds, so retrying the assertion is sufficient.

Fixes #165420

title: release-26.2: crosscluster: fix mixed-version RangeStats type mismatch
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166349 on behalf of @kev-cao.

----

PR #163427 extracted `StreamEvent.RangeStats` from `streampb` into `rangescanstatspb.RangeStats`. While the wire contents are identical, the nominal type changed, which causes `pbtypes.UnmarshalAny` type checks to fail. In a mixed 26.1/26.2 cluster, nodes pass around both types, blocking the resolved timestamp from progressing.

This commit adds back the `streampb.StreamEvent.RangeStats` message and uses it for marshaling DistSQL progress metadata until the cluster is fully on 26.2. The unmarshal path tries both types for compatibility in either direction.

Fixes: #164640

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

----

Release justification: Fixes bug that blocks PCR jobs during a mixed-version upgrade to 26.2.
author: kev-cao

commits.message:
crosscluster: fix mixed-version RangeStats type mismatch

PR #163427 extracted `StreamEvent.RangeStats` from `streampb` into
`rangescanstatspb.RangeStats`. While the wire contents are identical,
the nominal type changed, which causes `pbtypes.UnmarshalAny` type
checks to fail. In a mixed 26.1/26.2 cluster, nodes pass around both
types, blocking the resolved timestamp from progressing.

This commit adds back the `streampb.StreamEvent.RangeStats` message and
uses it for marshaling DistSQL progress metadata until the cluster is
fully on 26.2. The unmarshal path tries both types for compatibility in
either direction.

Fixes: #164640

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: crosscluster/physical: cancel entire ingest phase on cutover signal
body:
Previously, when a PCR cutover was requested and the source cluster was unreachable, multiple goroutines in startDistIngestion (heartbeat sender, span config stream, replanning, connection refresher) remained blocked on dead TCP connections for up to 15+ minutes. The per-processor checkForCutoverSignal only signals individual ingestion processors via cutoverCh, but the other goroutines sharing the same ctxgroup context were not notified.

This commit adds a top-level cutover watcher in Resume() that polls job progress and cancels the ingestion context when cutover is reached, unblocking all goroutines simultaneously. The per-processor poller is retained for mixed-version compatibility; both detection paths converge on the same maybeRevertToCutoverTimestamp call in Resume().

As part of this restructuring, cutover handling is moved from ingest() to Resume(), the revertToCutoverTimestamp wrapper is removed, and the updateStatus call in ingestWithRetries is removed since the status transition is handled by maybeRevertToCutoverTimestamp directly.

Resolves: #166201

Release note (bug fix): The PCR job now more promptly switches into the cutover phase after a failover is requested, terminating the replication phase more quickly and more reliably than it did previously in cases where componants of the ingestion process may be hung on network errors.
author: dt

commits.message:
crosscluster/physical: cancel entire ingest phase on cutover signal

Previously, when a PCR cutover was requested and the source cluster was
unreachable, multiple goroutines in startDistIngestion (heartbeat sender,
span config stream, replanning, connection refresher) remained blocked on
dead TCP connections for up to 15+ minutes. The per-processor
checkForCutoverSignal only signals individual ingestion processors via
cutoverCh, but the other goroutines sharing the same ctxgroup context
were not notified.

This commit adds a top-level cutover watcher in Resume() that polls job
progress and cancels the ingestion context when cutover is reached,
unblocking all goroutines simultaneously. The per-processor poller is
retained for mixed-version compatibility; both detection paths converge
on the same maybeRevertToCutoverTimestamp call in Resume().

As part of this restructuring, cutover handling is moved from ingest()
to Resume(), the revertToCutoverTimestamp wrapper is removed, and the
updateStatus call in ingestWithRetries is removed since the status
transition is handled by maybeRevertToCutoverTimestamp directly.

Resolves: #166201

Release note (bug fix): The PCR job now more promptly switches into the
cutover phase after a failover is requested, terminating the replication
phase more quickly and more reliably than it did previously in cases
where componants of the ingestion process may be hung on network errors.

title: obs/ash: propagate WorkloadType through fetcher, streamer, and tablewriter admission paths
body:
Several DistSQL operator admission paths constructed `admission.WorkInfo` with `WorkloadID` set but `WorkloadType` omitted, causing ASH samples to show `workload_type = 'UNKNOWN'` and job IDs to be hex-encoded instead of decimal. The affected paths are:

- `kv_batch_fetcher` response admission and admission pacer
- `cFetcher` admission pacer
- `kvstreamer` response admission
- `tablewriter` response admission

Thread `workloadType` through the constructors (`NewStreamer`, `NewKVFetcher`, `NewStreamingKVFetcher`, `NewDirectKVBatchFetcher`, `newCFetcherWrapper`) alongside the existing `workloadID` parameter. All callers have access to `EvalCtx.WorkloadType`.

Also extend `TestWorkloadIDPropagation` to assert that `WorkloadType != UNKNOWN` on all admission `WorkInfo` entries captured via the `WorkQueueAdmitInterceptor`, which would have caught this regression.

Fixes: #166463
Release note: None
author: alyshanjahani-crl

reviews.body: > @yuzefovich reviewed 20 files and all commit messages, and made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166466#-:-OoRv4QeC5JzN1lQX7kl:bnfp4nl)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on annrpom).

commits.message:
obs/ash: propagate WorkloadType through fetcher, streamer, and tablewriter admission paths

Several DistSQL operator admission paths constructed `admission.WorkInfo`
with `WorkloadID` set but `WorkloadType` omitted, causing ASH samples to
show `workload_type = 'UNKNOWN'` and job IDs to be hex-encoded instead
of decimal. The affected paths are:

- `kv_batch_fetcher` response admission and admission pacer
- `cFetcher` admission pacer
- `kvstreamer` response admission
- `tablewriter` response admission

Thread `workloadType` through the constructors (`NewStreamer`,
`NewKVFetcher`, `NewStreamingKVFetcher`, `NewDirectKVBatchFetcher`,
`newCFetcherWrapper`) alongside the existing `workloadID` parameter.
All callers have access to `EvalCtx.WorkloadType`.

Also extend `TestWorkloadIDPropagation` to assert that `WorkloadType !=
UNKNOWN` on all admission `WorkInfo` entries captured via the
`WorkQueueAdmitInterceptor`, which would have caught this regression.

Fixes: #166463

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: obs/ash: fix tenant isolation for ASH virtual tables in shared-process mode
body:
The ASH virtual table populate functions in crdb_internal.go called ListLocalActiveSessionHistory and ListActiveSessionHistory without propagating the planner's tenant ID into the context. Those handlers rely on roachpb.ClientTenantFromContext to determine the requesting tenant and filter samples accordingly. That context value is normally set by the gRPC auth interceptor, but in shared-process mode the call is in-process so the interceptor never runs, causing filterByTenant to be false and returning all tenants' samples unfiltered.

Fix by wrapping the context with roachpb.ContextWithClientTenant using the planner's Codec.TenantID before calling the handler. This ensures secondary tenants only see their own ASH samples while the system tenant continues to see all samples.

Also add TestASHMultiTenantIsolation which starts two shared-process tenants, generates work on each, and verifies that each tenant only sees its own tenant_id in crdb_internal.node_active_session_history.

Fixes #166456
Release note: None
author: alyshanjahani-crl

reviews.body: LGTM; just a testing nit.

commits.message:
obs/ash: fix tenant isolation for ASH virtual tables in shared-process mode

The ASH virtual table populate functions called
ListLocalActiveSessionHistory and ListActiveSessionHistory without
propagating the planner's tenant ID into the context. Those handlers
rely on roachpb.ClientTenantFromContext to filter samples by tenant,
but that context value is only set by the gRPC auth interceptor which
does not run for in-process calls in shared-process mode. This caused
filterByTenant to be false, leaking all tenants' samples to any
tenant querying the virtual tables.

Fix by setting ContextWithClientTenant from the planner's
Codec.TenantID before calling the handlers.

Fixes #166456

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: release-26.2: ccl/multiregionccl: deflake TestMrSystemDatabase
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166337 on behalf of @shghasemi.

----

Previously, automatic stats collection was disabled in TestMrSystemDatabase to prevent a race condition during the multi-region system database conversion. However, disabling the cluster setting doesn't affect the jobs that are already created and running which could cause the race condition. This fix checks for auto stats jobs and wait for them to succeeded before running the multi-region change.

Fixes: #165493

Release note: None

----

Release justification: Test-only change.
author: shghasemi

commits.message:
ccl/multiregionccl: deflake TestMrSystemDatabase

Previously, automatic stats collection was disabled in TestMrSystemDatabase
to prevent a race condition during the multi-region system database conversion.
However, disabling the cluster setting doesn't affect the jobs that are already
created and running which could cause the race condition.
This fix checks for auto stats jobs and wait for them to succeeded before
running the multi-region change.

Fixes: #165493

Release note: None

title: ui: migrate TopRanges to SWR
body:
The Top Ranges page fetches hot range data using a
PaginatedCachedDataReducer, which loops through paginated API
responses and stores them in Redux. This is the legacy data fetching
pattern; other pages have already migrated to SWR.

Add a useHotRanges hook that wraps useSWR with a fetcher that
internally loops all pages of the _status/v2/hotranges endpoint,
accumulates ranges, filters out those with QPS <= 0, and returns the
flattened result. Include unit tests for both the paginated fetcher
and the hook.

-----

The HotRangesPage component uses useSelector/useDispatch to read hot
ranges data from Redux and a useEffect to trigger fetches when the
node filter changes. HotRangesTable is wrapped in a Redux connect HOC
for sort setting persistence via LocalSetting.

Replace the four Redux selectors and the fetch-dispatch effect with a
single useHotRanges(filters.nodeIds) call. Remove the connect HOC
from HotRangesTable and pass sortSetting/onSortChange as props from
the parent, using ephemeral useState with a default of QPS descending.

-----

After the SWR migration, the hot ranges Redux layer is unused.
PaginatedCachedDataReducer and PaginatedCachedDataReducerState have no
remaining consumers.

Delete redux/hotRanges.ts entirely. Remove hotRangesReducerObj,
refreshHotRanges, and clearHotRanges from apiReducers.ts. Delete the
PaginatedCachedDataReducer class, PaginatedCachedDataReducerState
class, and WithPagination interfaces from cachedDataReducer.ts along
with their tests.

Epic: CRDB-58145
Release Note: None
author: jasonlmfong

reviews.body: > @kyle-a-wong made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166457#-:-OoVst0u7imkbfHW3vZQ:bnfp4nl)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on jasonlmfong).

commits.message:
ui: add SWR hook for hot ranges data fetching

The Top Ranges page fetches hot range data using a
PaginatedCachedDataReducer, which loops through paginated API
responses and stores them in Redux.

Add a useHotRanges hook that wraps useSWR with a fetcher that
internally loops all pages of the _status/v2/hotranges endpoint,
accumulates ranges, filters out those with QPS <= 0, and returns the
flattened result. Include unit tests for both the paginated fetcher
and the hook.

Epic: CRDB-58145
Release Note: None

ui: migrate Top Ranges page from Redux to SWR

The HotRangesPage component uses useSelector/useDispatch to read hot
ranges data from Redux and a useEffect to trigger fetches when the
node filter changes. HotRangesTable is wrapped in a Redux connect HOC
for sort setting persistence via LocalSetting.

Replace the four Redux selectors and the fetch-dispatch effect with a
single useHotRanges(filters.nodeIds) call. Remove the connect HOC
from HotRangesTable and pass sortSetting/onSortChange as props from
the parent, using ephemeral useState with a default of QPS descending.

Epic: CRDB-58145
Release Note: None

ui: delete unused Redux artifacts for hot ranges

After the SWR migration, the hot ranges Redux layer is unused.
PaginatedCachedDataReducer and PaginatedCachedDataReducerState have no
remaining consumers.

Delete redux/hotRanges.ts entirely. Remove hotRangesReducerObj,
refreshHotRanges, and clearHotRanges from apiReducers.ts. Delete the
PaginatedCachedDataReducer class, PaginatedCachedDataReducerState
class, and WithPagination interfaces from cachedDataReducer.ts along
with their tests.

Epic: CRDB-58145
Release Note: None

title: [release-25.2] ci: add trunk branch on push trigger
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #161230.

---

## Summary
Backports the critical missing CI trigger for Trunk merge queue branches to release-25.2.

This fixes the issue where Trunk creates `trunk-merge/**` branches but GitHub Actions doesn't run CI on them, causing PRs to timeout.

## Problem Fixed
- Trunk merge queue creates branches but CI doesn't run
- PRs timeout after 80 minutes with no CI activity
- Without this, Trunk won't work properly when multiple PRs are in queue

## Test Plan
After merging:
- Trunk merge queue will properly trigger CI on release-25.2
- Multiple PRs can be tested in parallel without timeout

## Release Note
None

Release justification: Non-production code change. Critical fix for CI/CD infrastructure.

## Related
- Original trunk.yaml backport: #166413
- Same fix for release-24.3: #166445
author: rudrakshsisodia

title: roachtest: collect debug zip on cdc latency failure
body:
A common pattern in cdc tests is to stick the workload and latency verifier goroutines in an error group and block the test on said error group. One side effect of this is that we may fail the test due to the latency verifier, but continue blocking for the workload to finish. This causes the debug zip taken by the framework on test failure to happen potentially 30+ minutes after the actual latency failure.

This changes the verifier to take its own debug zip on failure.

Release note: none
Epic: none
Fixes: none
author: DarrylWong

reviews.body: LGTM

commits.message:
roachtest: collect debug zip on cdc latency failure

A common pattern in cdc tests is to stick the workload
and latency verifier goroutines in an error group and
block the test on said error group. One side effect of
this is that we may fail the test due to the latency
verifier, but continue blocking for the workload to
finish. This causes the debug zip taken by the framework
on test failure to happen potentially 30+ minutes after
the actual latency failure.

This changes the verifier to take its own debug zip on
failure.

title: release-26.2: restore: skip dropped/missing descriptors during restore revert
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166116.

/cc @cockroachdb/release

---

Previously, if a user dropped descriptors (tables, types, functions, schemas) that were created by an online restore while the download job was paused, canceling the download job would get stuck in the reverting state. This happened because both `setDescriptorsOffline` and `dropDescriptors` would fail when trying to look up descriptors that had already been dropped.

Fix this by skipping descriptors that are already dropped or not found.

Fixes: #165789

Release note: None

---

Release justification: Cleanup of online restore on dropped descriptors blocks cleanup and prevents descriptors from being set offline.
author: kev-cao

commits.message:
restore: skip dropped/missing descriptors during restore revert

Previously, if a user dropped descriptors (tables, types, functions,
schemas) that were created by an online restore while the download job
was paused, canceling the download job would get stuck in the reverting
state. This happened because both `setDescriptorsOffline` and
`dropDescriptors` would fail when trying to look up descriptors that
had already been dropped.

Fix this by skipping descriptors that are already dropped or not found.

Fixes: #165789

Release note: None

title: release-26.2: ash: Flush out unattributed code paths
body:
Backport 6/6 commits from #166098.

/cc @cockroachdb/release

---

This PR is the result of running several experiments on roachprod clusters after merging the PRs that instrumented an initial set of system tasks #165731 , and the jobs system #165480.

These experiments uncovered some key operations that were producing samples that were unattributed (no workload id).

The commits in this PR address those gaps.

Release justification: Critical fixes the cover gaps in the ASH feature.
author: alyshanjahani-crl

commits.message:
kvserver: attribute replicate and split queue work to ASH

Add WORKLOAD_ID_REPLICATE_QUEUE and WORKLOAD_ID_SPLIT_QUEUE system
workload IDs, and stamp them on the Go context at each queue's
process() entry point. The context propagates to all transactions
and non-transactional batches created during processing, including
all downstream operations such as replica changes and snapshot
transfers.

This was the root cause of a 46% unattributed gap observed under a
KV overload experiment (512 concurrency, batch=100). Debug logging
identified ~1,200 replicate-queue RPCs and ~39 split-queue RPCs on
a single node sending batches without workload identity.

  Experiment: KV overload (4-node, n2-standard-16)
  Before: 54% attribution (18,396 unattributed / 40,000 samples)
  After:  95.86% attribution — 91% reduction in unattributed samples

Informs: #164381

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

kv: stamp workload identity on non-transactional batch headers

Extract workload info from the Go context onto the batch header in
CrossRangeTxnWrapperSender.Send, so that callers using
NonTransactionalSender().Send() directly (e.g. SSTBatcher for
IMPORT/RESTORE, merge queue) get ASH workload attribution without
needing to populate the header themselves.

This covers the non-transactional path for any operation that has
workload info in its context. For job operations, the jobs registry
calls kv.ContextWithWorkloadInfo(ctx, jobID, ...) before Resume.
For system tasks like merge queue, the workload ID is stamped on
the context at the queue's process() entry point. In both cases,
CrossRangeTxnWrapperSender.Send extracts the workload info from
the context onto the batch header. The WorkloadID == 0 guard
ensures this is a no-op for batches that already carry a workload
ID (e.g. from Txn.Send).

Informs: #164381

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

obs/ash: propagate workload info to elastic CPU admission

Propagate WorkloadID and WorkloadType into admission.WorkInfo structs
across backup ExportRequests, changefeed event processing, changefeed
sink CPU pacing, and bulk CPU pacing. Also inject workload info into
the Go context during DistSQL SetupFlow so that admission control
pacers on remote nodes can extract the workload identity.

Without this fix, all elastic CPU admission time for jobs showed as
unattributed in ASH because the admission pacer's WorkInfo lacked
workload identity, and the Go context on remote DistSQL nodes did
not carry it.

  Experiment: CDC changefeed (4-node, TPC-C --warehouses=10)
  Before: ~52% attribution
  After:  99.07% attribution

  Experiment: Schema change under load (4-node, TPC-C --warehouses=50)
  Before: ~50% attribution
  After:  93.76% attribution

Informs: #164381

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

kvserver: attribute GC queue and rangefeed admission to ASH

Stamp WORKLOAD_ID_GC on the non-transactional GC batch header so
MVCC garbage collection appears as a named system workload in ASH.
Stamp WORKLOAD_ID_RANGEFEED on rangefeed admission requests so
rangefeed processing is attributed.

  GC: confirmed present after a GC pressure experiment that created
  1M MVCC tombstones to force the GC queue to trigger. GC processed
  the tombstones quickly (~seconds), yielding 3 samples — consistent
  with 100ms sampling of a fast-completing operation.

  RANGEFEED: confirmed with 2 kv-elastic-cpu-queue samples during
  a CDC changefeed experiment.

Informs: #164381

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

sql/catalog/lease: attribute descriptor lease reads to ASH

Add WORKLOAD_ID_DESCRIPTOR_LEASE system workload ID and stamp it on
batch headers for descriptor lease KV reads in both
getDescriptorsFromStoreForInterval and getDescriptorsInTxnAtTimestamp.

  Confirmed: 254 DESCRIPTOR_LEASE samples in a backup experiment,
  145 samples during CDC changefeed processing.

Informs: #164381

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

obs/ash: document workload attribution architecture

Document the three workload attribution propagation mechanisms:

1. SQL statements use the kv.Txn as carrier — SetWorkloadInfo is
   called per-statement and Txn.Send auto-stamps all batches.

2. Jobs use the Go context — ContextWithWorkloadInfo at the job
   entry point propagates through DB.NewTxn (transactional) and
   CrossRangeTxnWrapperSender (non-transactional). DistSQL flows
   inject the context on remote nodes via SetupFlow.

3. System tasks use explicit stamping on batch headers or context
   at each queue's process() entry point.

Informs: #164381

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: workload: deflake conflict TestRandomWriter
body:
This fixes a flake that occurs when the "char" type is included in the
primary index. The "char" type requires parameters to have an explicit
cast to "char", otherwise the type gets inferred as STRING which causes
'\x00' to be interpreted as a string literal instead of a byte literal.
The symptom is that the delete fails to find the row because the "char"
column is truncated to 1 byte on insert, but the WHERE clause compares
against the un-truncated string.

We fix this by adding explicit type casts to the parameter placeholders
(e.g. `$1::"char"`) in both the upsert and delete statements, which
ensures lib/pq values are always decoded using the correct type.

The test was switched over to SucceedsSoon in order to get better error
messages. The ownership of the workload/conflict and workload/random
packages was switched to the CDC team, because these are only used in
LDR tests.

Release note: none
Informs: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/165808
author: jeffswenson

reviews.body: LGTM! Thanks for changing the owners. One small request to fix one of the rands, otherwise great.

commits.message:
workload: deflake conflict TestRandomWriter

This fixes a flake that occurs when the "char" type is included in the
primary index. The "char" type requires parameters to have an explicit
cast to "char", otherwise the type gets inferred as STRING which causes
'\x00' to be interpreted as a string literal instead of a byte literal.
The symptom is that the delete fails to find the row because the "char"
column is truncated to 1 byte on insert, but the WHERE clause compares
against the un-truncated string.

We fix this by adding explicit type casts to the parameter placeholders
(e.g. `$1::"char"`) in both the upsert and delete statements, which
ensures lib/pq values are always decoded using the correct type.

The test was switched over to SucceedsSoon in order to get better error
messages. The ownership of the workload/conflict and workload/random
packages was switched to the CDC team, because these are only used in
LDR tests.

Release note: none
Informs: #165808

title: server: supplement missing console keys in Settings endpoint
body:
## Summary

Fixes #165444.

The Settings API endpoint only populated ConsoleKeys (like "version") in the
fallback path when the `crdb_internal.cluster_settings` query failed with
`InsufficientPrivilege`. Users with `MODIFYSQLCLUSTERSETTING` (but not
`VIEWCLUSTERSETTING`) caused the query to succeed with a filtered result
containing only `sql.defaults.*` settings. Since no error occurred, the
ConsoleKeys fallback was never reached, and "version" was missing from the
response — crashing the DB Console Overview page.

This moves the ConsoleKeys supplementation out of the error block so it always
runs. Missing console keys are filled from in-memory setting values without
overwriting keys already present in the response.

Release note (bug fix): Fixed a bug where the DB Console Overview page crashed
for users with MODIFYSQLCLUSTERSETTING and VIEWACTIVITY privileges but without
VIEWCLUSTERSETTING. The Settings API now always includes non-sensitive console
keys like "version" regardless of which privilege tier returned the settings.
author: kyle-a-wong

reviews.body: just have a nit for the test comment

commits.message:
server: supplement missing console keys in Settings endpoint

Previously, the Settings endpoint only populated ConsoleKeys (like
"version") in the fallback path when the crdb_internal.cluster_settings
query failed with InsufficientPrivilege. Users with
MODIFYSQLCLUSTERSETTING (but not VIEWCLUSTERSETTING) caused the query
to succeed with a filtered result containing only sql.defaults.*
settings. Since no error occurred, the ConsoleKeys fallback was never
reached, and "version" was missing from the response. This caused the
DB Console Overview page to crash with a TypeError.

Move the ConsoleKeys supplementation out of the error block so it
always runs after error handling. Missing console keys are filled in
from in-memory setting values without overwriting keys already present
in the response.

Fixes: #165444
Release note (bug fix): Fixed a bug where the DB Console Overview page
crashed for users with MODIFYSQLCLUSTERSETTING and VIEWACTIVITY
privileges but without VIEWCLUSTERSETTING. The Settings API now
always includes non-sensitive console keys like "version" regardless
of which privilege tier returned the settings.

ui: guard against missing version in cluster settings selector

Add optional chaining to selectClusterSettingVersion, consistent with
other selectors in the same file. This provides defense-in-depth
against a missing "version" key in the settings response.

Part of: #165444
Release note: None

title: release-26.2: sql: deflake TestScatterWithOneVoter
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166241 on behalf of @pav-kv.

----

Wait for all stores to be available (not just present) in the store pool before running scatter. Two races existed:

1. `StartCluster.WaitForNStores` uses its own gossip callback worker, independent of the store pool's callback. The store pool may not have processed store descriptors yet when `WaitForNStores` returns.

2. Stores can be transiently marked "suspect" after startup due to node liveness flaps. Suspect stores are excluded from the store list used by the allocator, causing scatter to silently find no rebalance targets.

Fixes-25.4 #165936

----

Release justification: test deflake
author: blathers-crl

commits.message:
sql: deflake TestScatterWithOneVoter

Wait for all stores to be available (not just present) in the store pool
before running scatter. Two races existed:

1. StartCluster's WaitForNStores uses its own gossip callback worker,
   independent of the store pool's callback. The store pool may not have
   processed store descriptors yet when WaitForNStores returns.

2. Stores can be transiently marked "suspect" after startup due to node
   liveness flaps. Suspect stores are excluded from the store list used
   by the allocator, causing scatter to silently find no rebalance
   targets.

Epic: none
Release note: none

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: sql/schemachanger: ensure CREATE TABLE/VIEW attempts to use the DSC
body:
Previously, these statements bypassed the declarative schema changer (DSC) checks entirely because the optimizer directly generated the plan nodes for these statements. To address this, the optimizer will attempt to generate the plan nodes via the declarative schema changer first, then use the legacy schema changer. These are all unimplemented, but this ensures correctness for the unsafe_always option.

Fixes: #147657
Release note: None
author: fqazi

commits.message:
sql/schemachanger: ensure CREATE TABLE/VIEW attempts to use the DSC

Previously, these statements bypassed the declarative schema changer
(DSC) checks entirely because the optimizer directly generated the plan
nodes for these statements. To address this, the optimizer will attempt
to generate the plan nodes via the declarative schema changer first,
then use the legacy schema changer. These are all unimplemented, but
this ensures correctness for the unsafe_always option.

Fixes: #147657
Release note: None

title: roachtest: fix db-console/mixed-version-endpoints flake
body:
Remove `--drop` flag from `workload init tpcc` in `initializeSchemaAndIDs` to prevent races with background operations during mixed-version testing.
Without `--drop`, `workload init tpcc` is idempotent: creates schema only if it doesn't exist, no-op on subsequent calls.

Fixes: #166407

Epic: none
Release note: None
author: dhartunian

commits.message:
roachtest: fix db-console/mixed-version-endpoints flake

The `initializeSchemaAndIDs` function was using `workload init tpcc
--drop` which drops and recreates all TPCC tables on every invocation.
In mixed-version testing, this function is called multiple times across
test steps. The `--drop` flag can race with background operations (e.g.
schema change GC, stats collection) from a prior invocation, causing
"another operation is currently operating on the table" errors.

Remove the `--drop` flag so that `workload init tpcc` is idempotent —
it creates the schema and data only if they don't already exist, and is
a no-op on subsequent calls.

Fixes: #166407

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: release-24.3: cli: deflake/strengthen TestLossOfQuorumRecovery
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166014.

/cc @cockroachdb/release

---

The test flakes when `WaitForFullReplication` returns too early. Under overload, nodes become unavailable and skew replica counter metrics which this function relies on. Don't rely on it too much: wait for the final "interesting" condition to materialize: all ranges are moved from the decommissioned nodes to the new ones.

Fixes #161502

Release justification: test deflake
author: pav-kv

overload, nodes become unavailable and skew replica counter metrics
which this function relies on. Don't rely on it too much: wait for the
final "interesting" condition to materialize: all ranges are moved from
the decommissioned nodes to the new ones.

title: release-25.4: sslocal: fix flaky TestSQLStatsDiscardStatsOnFingerprintLimit
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #161813 on behalf of @kyle-a-wong.

----

The test was failing intermittently because the transaction entry check used inconsistent filtering compared to the statement entry check.

When a fingerprint limit is set, the limit is enforced globally across all fingerprints (internal + user). The statement check correctly used AllowInternal: true to count all fingerprints, but the transaction check did not, causing it to miss internal transactions that consumed limit slots.

This fix makes the transaction checks consistent with statement checks:
- With limit: AllowInternal: true (count all, because limit is global)
- Without limit: No filter (count only user fingerprints)

Fixes: #160851

Release note: None

----

Release justification: test-only change
author: kyle-a-wong

commits.message:
sslocal: fix flaky TestSQLStatsDiscardStatsOnFingerprintLimit

The test was failing intermittently because the transaction entry check
used inconsistent filtering compared to the statement entry check.

When a fingerprint limit is set, the limit is enforced globally across
all fingerprints (internal + user). The statement check correctly used
AllowInternal: true to count all fingerprints, but the transaction check
did not, causing it to miss internal transactions that consumed limit
slots.

This fix makes the transaction checks consistent with statement checks:
- With limit: AllowInternal: true (count all, because limit is global)
- Without limit: No filter (count only user fingerprints)

Fixes: #160851

Release note: None

title: roachtest: bump user_login.timeout in follower-reads insufficient-quorum
body:
Bump `server.user_login.timeout` from the default 10s to 60s in the
follower-reads insufficient-quorum test variants. After killing the
entire primary region, lease acquisition on system ranges can take ~10s,
racing with the authentication timeout on newly opened SQL connections.

Fixes-24.3 #166376

Release note: None
Epic: none
author: tbg

reviews.body: /trunk merge

commits.message:
roachtest: bump user_login.timeout in follower-reads insufficient-quorum

The follower-reads insufficient-quorum test variants kill the entire
primary region (3 nodes via SIGKILL) and then open new SQL connections
to surviving nodes. Authentication on these new connections requires
reading system tables, which blocks until leases transfer from the dead
nodes. This lease acquisition can take ~10s, racing with the default
10s `server.user_login.timeout` and causing flaky authentication
timeouts.

Bump the timeout to 60s for these test variants to give ample room for
lease transfers after a full region kill.

Fixes-24.3 #166376

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: roachtest: enable Go execution traces in kv/restart/nodes=12
body:
Enable continuous Go execution tracing on all nodes in the
`kv/restart/nodes=12` test to aid diagnosis of transient resource
spikes like the one in #166364, where a CGO/memory spike on node 3
caused a TCP timeout but neither the Datadog logs nor pebble logs
revealed the trigger.

The execution tracer captures goroutine scheduling, syscalls, and
GC activity. Traces are written to disk and collected as artifacts
on test failure.

Settings added:
- `obs.execution_tracer.interval` = 10s
- `obs.execution_tracer.duration` = 10s
- `obs.execution_tracer.total_dump_size_limit` = 10 GiB

Closes https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/166364.
author: tbg

title: roachtest: fix acceptance/cli/node-status quorum loss
body:
## Summary

The `acceptance/cli/node-status` test times out because it assumes `cockroach
node status` works after the cluster has lost quorum on the liveness range.

- Switch from a 3-node to a 5-node cluster. System ranges default to 5x
  replication (`DefaultSystemZoneConfig`), so killing 2 nodes still leaves
  quorum (3/5 alive).
- Create 100 user ranges at default 3x replication; some lose quorum when 2
  nodes die, verifying `node status` works despite partial user-range quorum
  loss.
- Wait for system database and meta ranges to finish upreplicating to 5x
  before killing nodes. Uses `SHOW CLUSTER RANGES` filtered to
  `database_name = 'system' OR start_key LIKE '/Meta%'`. Timeseries ranges
  are intentionally excluded — they inherit `num_replicas=3` from the
  default zone config (not the system zone config) and would never reach 5.

Fixes-25.4: #166351
Epic: none

Release note: None
author: tbg

reviews.body: > @arulajmani reviewed 2 files and all commit messages, and made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166418#-:-OoVtxAA59nB-CrQPL4l:bnfp4nl)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on 5hubh4m).

commits.message:
roachtest: fix acceptance/cli/node-status quorum loss

The test previously started 3 nodes, killed 2, and expected `node status`
to still work — assuming it "only accesses gossip info". That assumption is
wrong; `node status` needs the liveness range to have quorum.

Switch to a 5-node cluster. System ranges default to 5x replication, so
with 2 nodes killed (3/5 alive) the liveness range retains quorum. The test
also creates 100 user ranges at the default 3x replication; some of these
may lose quorum when 2 nodes die, verifying that `node status` works even
with partial user-range quorum loss.

Fixes-25.4: #166351

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: roachprod: address ibm quota and image deprecation, and optimize clusters list
body:
This PR contains four changes for IBM cloud in roachprod:

1. roachprod: use more ibm AZs to reduce capa issues
  In order to avoid impacting IBM customers, roachprod was only using two IBM Cloud regions per our agreement with IBM.
  With the deprecation of s390x in IBM Cloud, we can no longer request for quota increases in these two regions, and as the number of customers decrease, we now can scale the number of regions roachprod uses.
  This patch grows from two regions to six in order to load balance the cluster creations during roachtest operations and reduce quota issues.

2. roachprod: use latest ubuntu 22.04 image in ibm
  Previously, roachprod was using the s390x  Ubuntu 22.04 rev. 5 image in IBM Cloud.
  As this image was deprecated a few months ago and replaced by rev. 7, this patch switches to the latest image.

3. roachprod: fix ibm clusters list
  Previously, listing clusters in IBM was creating two many requests in parallel, leading to very long list/create operations and, sometimes, the API returning HTTP error code 429.
  This patch adds a ctxgroup with a limit fixed to 30 concurrent requests to avoid spamming the IBM API when listing clusters.
  Also, some code path were leading to querying each instance details multiple times. This patch fixes this by only loading instance data when the creation date is unknown. This will reduce the total number of queries per instance from two to one.

4. roachprod: improve clusters cleanup for ibm
  Previously, after a failed cluster creation, roachtest was attempting to destroy the partially created cluster. This process relied on listing the cluster and destroy it via `provider.DestroyCluster()`.
  In IBM, this is subject to failures because of implicit rate limitings, so this patch attempts to call `provider.DeleteCluster()` (by name) in case no clusters were returned during listing.
  This should reduce the number of dangling resources cleaned by the garbage collector.


Epic: none
Release note: none
author: golgeek

commits.message:
roachprod: address ibm quota and image deprecation, and optimize clusters list

This PR contains four changes for IBM cloud in roachprod:

1. roachprod: use more ibm AZs to reduce capa issues
  In order to avoid impacting IBM customers, roachprod was only using
  two IBM Cloud regions per our agreement with IBM.
  With the deprecation of s390x in IBM Cloud, we can no longer request
  for quota increases in these two regions, and as the number of
  customers decrease, we now can scale the number of regions roachprod
  uses.
  This patch grows from two regions to six in order to load balance the
  cluster creations during roachtest operations and reduce quota issues.

2. roachprod: use latest ubuntu 22.04 image in ibm
  Previously, roachprod was using the s390x Ubuntu 22.04 rev. 5 image in
  IBM Cloud.
  As this image was deprecated a few months ago and replaced by rev. 7,
  this patch switches to the latest image.

3. roachprod: fix ibm clusters list
  Previously, listing clusters in IBM was creating two many requests in
  parallel, leading to very long list/create operations and, sometimes,
  the API returning HTTP error code 429.
  This patch adds a ctxgroup with a limit fixed to 30 concurrent
  requests to avoid spamming the IBM API when listing clusters.
  Also, some code path were leading to querying each instance details
  multiple times. This patch fixes this by only loading instance data
  when the creation date is unknown. This will reduce the total number
  of queries per instance from two to one.

4. roachprod: improve clusters cleanup for ibm
  Previously, after a failed cluster creation, roachtest was attempting
  to destroy the partially created cluster. This process relied on
  listing the cluster and destroy it via `provider.DestroyCluster()`.
  In IBM, this is subject to failures because of implicit rate
  limitings, so this patch attempts to call `provider.DeleteCluster()`
  (by name) in case no clusters were returned during listing.
  This should reduce the number of dangling resources cleaned by the
  garbage collector.

Epic: none
Release note: None

title: crosscluster: fix mixed-version RangeStats type mismatch
body:
PR #163427 extracted `StreamEvent.RangeStats` from `streampb` into `rangescanstatspb.RangeStats`. While the wire contents are identical, the nominal type changed, which causes `pbtypes.UnmarshalAny` type checks to fail. In a mixed 26.1/26.2 cluster, nodes pass around both types, blocking the resolved timestamp from progressing.

This commit adds back the `streampb.StreamEvent.RangeStats` message and uses it for marshaling DistSQL progress metadata until the cluster is fully on 26.2. The unmarshal path tries both types for compatibility in either direction.

Fixes: #164640

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>
author: kev-cao

reviews.body:
LGTM 

Thank you!
Nice!

commits.message:
crosscluster: fix mixed-version RangeStats type mismatch

PR #163427 extracted `StreamEvent.RangeStats` from `streampb` into
`rangescanstatspb.RangeStats`. While the wire contents are identical,
the nominal type changed, which causes `pbtypes.UnmarshalAny` type
checks to fail. In a mixed 26.1/26.2 cluster, nodes pass around both
types, blocking the resolved timestamp from progressing.

This commit adds back the `streampb.StreamEvent.RangeStats` message and
uses it for marshaling DistSQL progress metadata until the cluster is
fully on 26.2. The unmarshal path tries both types for compatibility in
either direction.

Fixes: #164640

title: release-26.2: sql/importer: fix TestUniqueUUID timeout
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166309 on behalf of @mw5h.

----

## Summary

Disable elastic CPU control in `TestUniqueUUID` to prevent the test from
exceeding the 15m timeout. The test imports 50,000 rows across three
tables with UUID/random primary key defaults.

When the metamorphic constant `import-row-count-validation` selects
`sync` mode, each import synchronously waits for an INSPECT validation
job that scans the imported data. Combined with elastic CPU throttling
(which was recently enabled for import jobs), the cumulative scan time
across the three imports can exceed the test timeout.

This mirrors the fix applied to other import tests in bda9189736f.

Resolves: #166130
Epic: None

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

----

Release justification: Test-only fix.
author: blathers-crl

reviews.body: > @yuzefovich reviewed 1 file and all commit messages. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166347)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on DrewKimball and ZhouXing19).

commits.message:
sql/importer: fix TestUniqueUUID timeout

Disable elastic CPU control in TestUniqueUUID to prevent the test from
exceeding the 15m timeout. The test imports 50,000 rows across three
tables with UUID/random primary key defaults.

When the metamorphic constant `import-row-count-validation` selects
`sync` mode, each import synchronously waits for an INSPECT validation
job that scans the imported data. Combined with elastic CPU throttling
(which was recently enabled for import jobs), the cumulative scan time
across the three imports can exceed the test timeout.

This mirrors the fix applied to other import tests in bda9189736f.

title: ccl/multiregionccl: deflake TestMrSystemDatabase
body:
Previously, automatic stats collection was disabled in TestMrSystemDatabase to prevent a race condition during the multi-region system database conversion. However, disabling the cluster setting doesn't affect the jobs that are already created and running which could cause the race condition. This fix checks for auto stats jobs and wait for them to succeeded before running the multi-region change.

Fixes: #165493

Release note: None
author: shghasemi

reviews.body: @shghasemi Nice fix! : > @fqazi reviewed 2 files and all commit messages, and made 2 comments. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166337#-:-OoPxwS99YFDIrNNFDhl:b9vx9kb)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on shghasemi). ___ *[`pkg/ccl/multiregionccl/multiregion_system_table_test.go` line 79 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166337#-OoPy3Rv2j2YPI6c4e03:-OoPy3Rv2j2YPI6c4e04:b6hiym) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/c2b179285a187cdad928451b452a7f2a3797c0fc/pkg/ccl/multiregionccl/multiregion_system_table_test.go#L79)):* > ```Go > // the setting was disabled. > tDB.Exec(t, `SET CLUSTER SETTING sql.stats.automatic_collection.enabled = false`) > tDB.Exec(t, `SHOW JOBS WHEN COMPLETE (SELECT job_id FROM crdb_internal.jobs WHERE job_type = 'AUTO CREATE STATS' AND status = 'running')`) > ``` TIL about WHEN COMPLETE
nice find!

commits.message:
ccl/multiregionccl: deflake TestMrSystemDatabase

Previously, automatic stats collection was disabled in TestMrSystemDatabase
to prevent a race condition during the multi-region system database conversion.
However, disabling the cluster setting doesn't affect the jobs that are already
created and running which could cause the race condition.
This fix checks for auto stats jobs and wait for them to succeeded before
running the multi-region change.

Fixes: #165493

Release note: None

title: sql: fix TestTrace flake from statement hints spans
body:
## Summary

Fixes a flake in `TestTrace` caused by the statement hints feature introducing
internal query spans that leak into the session trace.

- **Commit 1**: Force `distsql=off` for statement hint queries
  (`CheckForStatementHintsInDB`, `GetStatementHintsFromDB`). These are simple
  point lookups that don't need distributed execution.

- **Commit 2**: Add `get-plan-hints` and `prepare stmt` to the always-optional
  spans list in `TestTrace` to handle the case where the hints cache rangefeed
  hasn't initialized yet and the internal query runs.

The root cause: before the `StatementHintsCache` rangefeed initializes
`hintedHashes` on a node, every statement falls through to querying
`system.statement_hints` via the internal executor. This internal query was
being distributed (due to `ORDER BY` with no stats), producing DistSQL spans
(`/cockroach.sql.distsqlrun.DistSQL/SetupFlow`) that the test didn't expect
when `distsql=off`.

Fixes #166212

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: mw5h

reviews.body: Thanks! > @michae2 reviewed 2 files and all commit messages, and made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166334#-:-OoQd6DuER_bG5DCza_M:b-g2a5fb)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on DrewKimball and ZhouXing19).

commits.message:
sql/hints: force local execution for statement hint queries

Statement hint queries (`CheckForStatementHintsInDB` and
`GetStatementHintsFromDB`) are simple point lookups on
`system.statement_hints` by hash. These should not incur the overhead
of distributed execution. Force `distsql=off` via a session data
override so the internal executor runs them locally.

Previously, when the statement hints cache rangefeed had not yet
initialized on a node, the cache would fall through to querying the
database. On multi-node clusters, the DistSQL planner could decide to
distribute this internal query (e.g. due to `ORDER BY` with no stats),
producing DistSQL-related trace spans that leaked into the session
trace.

Informs #166212

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

sql: add statement hints spans to optional list in TestTrace

Add `get-plan-hints` and `prepare stmt` to the always-optional spans
list in `TestTrace`. Before the statement hints cache rangefeed
initializes, the cache falls through to a database query whose spans
leak into the session trace and cause unexpected span name mismatches.

Fixes #166212

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: scripts: add script to clean up stale bazel output bases
body:
Bazel output bases for workspaces that no longer exist (e.g. deleted git worktrees) or that haven't been used recently can waste significant disk space. An idle workspace typically accumulates enough changes that most of its cached build is invalidated on next use, so the output base isn't worth keeping. Pruning orphaned and stale bases reclaims this space with limited downside, since rebuilds still leverage the remote LRU cache maintained by dev.

Additionally, when using multiple worktrees with similar content, the same build artifacts are often duplicated across output bases. These duplicates can be replaced with copy-on-write links using `fclones`. The script supports this on an opt-in basis: create the directory ~/.cache/bazel-tidy/dedupe and ensure `fclones` is available on PATH.

Release note: none.
Epic: none.

Should we check for thats specific version of fclones? ___ *[`scripts/tidy_bazel_bases.sh` line 140 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166331#-OoBxmPt9ek1ETWkNFJY:-OoBxmPt9ek1ETWkNFJZ:b-2hj2cp) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/a56ae4e29bc0dc6b01c95d636f00c8070ec57cc9/scripts/tidy_bazel_bases.sh#L140)):* > ```Shell > return > fi > > ``` nit: trailing spaces ___ *[`scripts/tidy_bazel_bases.sh` line 163 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166331#-OoBxopDAhGz2zQn9_r_:-OoBnp2EDxR04XYJEWpD:bi90fj0) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/a56ae4e29bc0dc6b01c95d636f00c8070ec57cc9/scripts/tidy_bazel_bases.sh#L163)):* > ```Shell > # bazel binaries and clear the output bases so it stops propagating to output > # files where it becomes uncopyable, but this is less ideal as it means that > # provenance information is no longer t

commits.message:
scripts: add script to clean up stale bazel output bases

Bazel output bases for workspaces that no longer exist (e.g. deleted git
worktrees) or that haven't been used recently can waste significant disk
space. An idle workspace typically accumulates enough changes that most
of its cached build is invalidated on next use, so the output base isn't
worth keeping. Pruning orphaned and stale bases reclaims this space with
limited downside, since rebuilds still leverage the remote LRU cache
maintained by dev.

Additionally, when using multiple worktrees with similar content, the
same build artifacts are often duplicated across output bases. These
duplicates can be replaced with copy-on-write links using `fclones`.
The script supports this on an opt-in basis: create the directory
~/.cache/bazel-tidy/dedupe and ensure `fclones` is available on PATH.

Release note: none.
Epic: none.

title: workload/schemachange: exclude hidden columns from policy expressions
body:
This commit updates tableColumnsShuffled to filter out hidden columns when gathering available columns for random policy expression generation.

Previously, the workload generator could generate policies referencing hidden columns (e.g., from hash-sharded indexes). However, the dropColumn error screening logic (which uses information_schema.columns) is blind to hidden columns, leading to false negatives where a column drop was attempted without expecting the policy dependency error.

Since dropColumn already ignores hidden columns when selecting a target to drop, ensuring policies only reference visible columns prevents this class of false-negative dependency bugs.

Fixes: #164725
Epic: None
Release note: None
author: fqazi

commits.message:
workload/schemachange: exclude hidden columns from policy expressions

when gathering available columns for random policy expression generation.

Previously, the workload generator could generate policies referencing
hidden columns (e.g., from hash-sharded indexes). However, the
dropColumn error screening logic (which uses information_schema.columns)
is blind to hidden columns, leading to false negatives where a column
drop was attempted without expecting the policy dependency error.

Since dropColumn already ignores hidden columns when selecting a
target to drop, ensuring policies only reference visible columns
prevents this class of false-negative dependency bugs.

Fixes: #164725

title: replicastats: removed an unnecessary indirection in `replicaStatsRecord`
body:
The struct `replicaStatsRecord` stores `localityCounts` as a pointer to a map. This causes as extra indirection which is redundant since, in golang, a map is itself a "pointer". This commit adds minor code changes to remove this extra indirection.

Release note: None
Resolves: #123271
author: 5hubh4m

commits.message:
replicastats: removed an unnecessary indirection in `replicaStatsRecord`

The struct `replicaStatsRecord` stores `localityCounts` as a pointer to a map.
This causes as extra indirection which is redundant since, in golang, a map is
itself a "pointer". This commit adds minor code changes to remove this extra
indirection.

Release note: None
Resolves: #123271

title: release-26.2: sql: fix partial index data loss / phantom rows during update
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166123 on behalf of @fqazi.

----

sql: fix partial index data loss / phantom rows during update

This commit fixes a bug on tables with multiple column families where a 
concurrent update that does not overlap with a partial index's column 
family could cause the partial index to write a NULL instead of the 
actual data, or incorrectly add phantom rows to a temporary index 
during a schema change backfill.

This bug was previously masked on default (single-column-family) tables 
because an update to any column causes the optimizer to conservatively 
fetch all columns in that family. However, with multiple column families, 
two normalization rules in the optimizer caused issues:

1. PruneMutationFetchCols: If an update does not change any column
   associated with an index, the optimizer avoids fetching those
   columns. This causes the execution layer to see NULLs for the
   unfetched columns.
2. SimplifyPartialIndexProjections: If an update does not change
   any column associated with a partial index, the optimizer
   simplifies the partial index predicate evaluation to FALSE.
   This causes the execution layer to skip writes to the index.

During a schema change backfill, the execution layer's updater must
unconditionally write complete index entries to temporary (mutating) 
indexes for any concurrent update, even if the index's columns are 
unchanged. This ensures the backfill merger has a complete snapshot 
to correctly reconcile the final index. If columns are pruned (Rule 1) 
or writes are simplified away (Rule 2), the temporary index receives 
incomplete entries (NULLs) or misses the update entirely.

Furthermore, missing columns can lead to phantom rows if the partial 
index predicate evaluates to TRUE when given a NULL value (e.g.,
WHERE val IS NULL).

This change ensures the optimizer always fetches the required
columns and avoids simplifying predicate evaluation if the index
is a mutation index, correctly propagating the full row state to the
execution layer.

Fixes: #166122

Release note (bug fix): Fixed a bug where concurrent updates to a table
using multiple column families during a partial index creation could 
result in data loss, incorrect NULL values, or validation failures in 
the resulting index.


----

Release justification: addresses a bug that can lead to partial indexes that are corrupt or fail to construct in the face of concurrent updates.

reviews.body: > @michae2 reviewed 4 files and all commit messages, and made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166322#-:-OoBj4Ya6scJx9bR5ch0:bnfp4nl)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on yuzefovich).

commits.message:
sql: fix partial index data loss / phantom rows during update

This commit fixes a bug on tables with multiple column families where a
concurrent update that does not overlap with a partial index's column
family could cause the partial index to write a NULL instead of the
actual data, or incorrectly add phantom rows to a temporary index
during a schema change backfill.

This bug was previously masked on default (single-column-family) tables
because an update to any column causes the optimizer to conservatively
fetch all columns in that family. However, with multiple column families,
two normalization rules in the optimizer caused issues:

1. PruneMutationFetchCols: If an update does not change any column
   associated with an index, the optimizer avoids fetching those
   columns. This causes the execution layer to see NULLs for the
   unfetched columns.
2. SimplifyPartialIndexProjections: If an update does not change
   any column associated with a partial index, the optimizer
   simplifies the partial index predicate evaluation to FALSE.
   This causes the execution layer to skip writes to the index.

During a schema change backfill, the execution layer's updater must
unconditionally write complete index entries to temporary (mutating)
indexes for any concurrent update, even if the index's columns are
unchanged. This ensures the backfill merger has a complete snapshot
to correctly reconcile the final index. If columns are pruned (Rule 1)
or writes are simplified away (Rule 2), the temporary index receives
incomplete entries (NULLs) or misses the update entirely.

Furthermore, missing columns can lead to phantom rows if the partial
index predicate evaluates to TRUE when given a NULL value (e.g.,
WHERE val IS NULL).

This change ensures the optimizer always fetches the required
columns and avoids simplifying predicate evaluation if the index
is a mutation index, correctly propagating the full row state to the
execution layer.

Fixes: #166122

Release note (bug fix): Fixed a bug where concurrent updates to a table
using multiple column families during a partial index creation could
result in data loss, incorrect NULL values, or validation failures in
the resulting index.

roachtest: add c2c/disconnect-permanent roachtest

The current c2c/disconnect roachtest only tested that the PCR stream could
advance after a temporary network partition. This patch adds a new partition
invariant that maintains the partition for the duration of the PCR job, to test
that cutover is quick even during a network partition. This patch also modifies
the partition test such that we create a full partition of src and dest nodes
every time.

title: release-26.1: changefeedccl: fix sink not closed when newEventConsumer fails in Start
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #165753.

/cc @cockroachdb/release

---

Previously, in changeAggregator.Start(), the call to newEventConsumer used a multi-value assignment directly into ca.eventConsumer and ca.sink:

    ca.eventConsumer, ca.sink, err = newEventConsumer(...)

When newEventConsumer returned an error, it also returned nil for the sink, which overwrote ca.sink with nil. This meant that when the changeAggregator's close() method ran, the nil check `if ca.sink != nil` would skip closing the sink. The underlying sink (which had already been dialed/connected at line 379) was never closed, leaking the goroutine from the gRPC connection — specifically the fakePubsubServer.Dial goroutine in tests.

This was most visible in enriched envelope tests because creating the enriched event consumer can fail under stress (due to descriptor lookups), and only pubsub's mock sink actually requires Close to prevent goroutine leaks.

The fix uses temporary variables so that ca.sink and ca.eventConsumer are only assigned on the success path, preserving the original sink reference for proper cleanup on error.

Also removes the pubsub skip workaround that was added to maybeForceEnrichedEnvelope in helpers_test.go.

Resolves: #144102
Fixes: #144102                                                                                                                                     
Fixes: #144354

title: release-26.2: sql: pin max_buffer_size in select_for_share_write_buffering test
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166051.

/cc @cockroachdb/release

---

## Summary

Pin `kv.transaction.write_buffering.max_buffer_size` to `2KiB` in the
`select_for_share_write_buffering` logic test, matching what is already done in
`cluster_locks_write_buffering` and other write buffering tests.

Without this, a small metamorphic `max_buffer_size` (e.g. 34 bytes) causes the
write buffer to overflow on the first locking read, permanently disabling write
buffering and its lock durability transformations for the transaction. This makes
the `cluster_locks` assertions fail because locks remain `Replicated` instead of
being transformed to `Unreplicated`.

Fixes: #166019

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Release justification: Test only fix
author: mw5h

> @mw5h made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166315#-:-OoRr9nkEGnT6VmRIAvO:bnfp4nl)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on mw5h).

commits.message:
sql: pin max_buffer_size in select_for_share_write_buffering test

Pin `kv.transaction.write_buffering.max_buffer_size` to `2KiB` in the
`select_for_share_write_buffering` logic test, matching what is already
done in `cluster_locks_write_buffering` and other write buffering tests.

Without this, a small metamorphic `max_buffer_size` (e.g. 34 bytes)
causes the write buffer to overflow on the first locking read, which
sets `flushed = true` and permanently disables write buffering for the
transaction. This prevents the lock durability transformations from
Replicated to Unreplicated, causing the test's `cluster_locks`
assertions to fail.

Fixes: #166019
Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: sql/importer: fix TestUniqueUUID timeout
body:
## Summary

Disable elastic CPU control in `TestUniqueUUID` to prevent the test from
exceeding the 15m timeout. The test imports 50,000 rows across three
tables with UUID/random primary key defaults.

When the metamorphic constant `import-row-count-validation` selects
`sync` mode, each import synchronously waits for an INSPECT validation
job that scans the imported data. Combined with elastic CPU throttling
(which was recently enabled for import jobs), the cumulative scan time
across the three imports can exceed the test timeout.

This mirrors the fix applied to other import tests in bda9189736f.

Resolves: #166130
Epic: None

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: server: add cluster setting to disable GC assist
body:
## Summary

- Adds `pkg/util/gcassist`, a thin wrapper around the forked runtime's
  `SetGCAssistEnabled`/`GCAssistEnabled` with `bazel`/`!bazel` build tags
  (following the `grunning` pattern).
- Adds `server.gc_assist.enabled` cluster setting (bool, `SystemVisible`)
  that dynamically toggles GC assist. Defaults to the runtime's current
  state at startup (respecting `GODEBUG=gcnoassist=1`).
- Adds `sys.gc.assist.enabled` gauge metric (0/1) so the state is
  observable even without the setting being explicitly set.

Fixes: #166040

## Commits

1. **util/gcassist: add package for controlling GC assist** — new package
   with `SetEnabled`/`Enabled` and bazel/non-bazel stubs.
2. **server: add cluster setting and metric for GC assist** — setting
   registration, `SetOnChange` wiring in `startSampleEnvironment`, and
   gauge metric in `RuntimeStatSampler`.
author: tbg

reviews.body: > @arulajmani reviewed 11 files and all commit messages, and made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166306#-:-OoRlYKU45H7s-cK2lA-:bnfp4nl)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on DarrylWong, dhartunian, and srosenberg).

commits.message:
util/gcassist: add package for controlling GC assist

Add a new utility package that wraps the forked Go runtime's
`runtime.SetGCAssistEnabled` and `runtime.GCAssistEnabled` APIs.
Under Bazel builds (which use CockroachDB's forked runtime), these
delegate to the real runtime functions. Under vanilla Go builds, they
are no-ops (`SetEnabled` does nothing, `Enabled` returns true).

This follows the same `bazel`/`!bazel` build-tag pattern used by
`pkg/util/grunning`.

Informs: #166040

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

server: add cluster setting and metric for GC assist

Add `server.gc_assist.enabled`, a `SystemVisible` bool cluster setting
that dynamically toggles the Go runtime's GC assist via the `gcassist`
package. The default value reflects the runtime's current state at
startup, picking up `GODEBUG=gcnoassist=1` if set.

The setting is applied in `startSampleEnvironment` with a `SetOnChange`
callback so changes take effect immediately.

Also add a `sys.gc.assist.enabled` gauge metric (0 or 1) to
`RuntimeStatSampler`, updated each sampling cycle, so the current
GC assist state is observable via metrics even when the setting has
not been explicitly changed.

Informs: #166040
Release note (ops change): A new cluster setting
`server.gc_assist.enabled` allows dynamically disabling GC assist in
CockroachDB's forked Go runtime. The default follows the
`GODEBUG=gcnoassist` flag. A new metric `sys.gc.assist.enabled`
reports the current state (1 = enabled, 0 = disabled).

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: importer: reduce TestImportData runtime to fix timeout
body:
## Summary

Two commits to fix importer test timeouts:

**Commit 1: Parallelize TestImportData subtests and reduce coverage**

`TestImportData` was timing out at the 15-minute limit with ~89 sequential
subtests at ~7-10s each. Two changes:
- Parallelize subtests by replacing the shared `mockRecorder` with a
  `sync.Map` keyed by per-subtest URL paths, giving each subtest its own
  DB connection.
- Only run `save_rejected=true` for test cases that actually verify rejected
  row output (`tc.rejected != ""`).

**Commit 2: Disable elastic CPU control in remaining import tests**

Follow-up to #165443 which disabled elastic CPU control in a few import
tests. Extends the fix to all remaining high-risk tests: TestFailedImport,
TestImportIntoCSVCancel, TestImportDefault, TestUniqueUUID,
TestImportDefaultNextVal, TestImportComputed, TestImportWorkerFailure,
TestImportDelimited, TestImportPgCopy.

Resolves: #166199
Resolves: #166130

Release note: None
author: mw5h

commits.message:
importer: split TestImportData into per-format tests to fix timeout

TestImportData had ~70 sequential subtests, each taking ~7-10s,
totaling ~13+ minutes which regularly exceeded the 15-minute shard
timeout. This also re-enables the test after it was skipped via
skip.WithIssue in 38f856010f3.

Split the monolithic test into separate top-level tests by import
format (TestImportDataCSV, TestImportDataDelimited, TestImportDataPgCopy,
TestImportDataMysqloutMultiple). Each runs independently against its own
server and naturally distributes across test shards. The shared test
table and execution loop are extracted into importDataTests and
runImportDataTests.

Resolves: #166199
Resolves: #166130

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

importer: disable elastic CPU control in remaining import tests

Elastic CPU throttling (bulkio.import.elastic_control.enabled) was
recently enabled by default and can cause import tests to run
significantly slower, pushing them past the 15-minute shard timeout.

Disable it in all remaining import tests that don't already have it
disabled: TestFailedImport, TestImportIntoCSVCancel, TestImportDefault,
TestImportDefaultNextVal, TestImportComputed, TestImportWorkerFailure,
TestImportDelimited, TestImportPgCopy.

Resolves: #166130

Release note: None

title: changefeed: add configurable ProduceBatchMaxBytes for kafka v2 sink
body:
The kafka v2 sink hardcoded ProducerBatchMaxBytes to 256 MiB, which
causes franz-go to form very large batches internally. When multiple
parallel batches are coalesced into a single broker request, this can
trigger spurious MessageTooLarge errors even for messages that would fit
individually.

Add a cluster setting `changefeed.kafka.max_request_size`
(default 256 MiB) and a per-changefeed override via the kafka sink
config's `Flush.MaxRequestSize` field. Lowering this value limits the
size of individual record batches, reducing the chance that coalesced
batches exceed the broker's message size limit.

Fixes: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/165387

Release note (bug fix): Added a new cluster setting
`changefeed.kafka.max_request_size` and a per-changefeed
`Flush.MaxRequestSize` option in the kafka sink config to control the
maximum size of record batches sent to Kafka by the v2 sink. Lowering
this from the default of 256 MiB can prevent spurious message-too-large
errors when multiple batches are coalesced into a single broker request.
author: aerfrei

Overall looks great! I have a few more naming / doc refinement suggestions.

Add a cluster setting `changefeed.kafka.max_request_size`

title: security: removing curve preference of FIPS
body:
* Previously, when running in FIPS mode, we restricted TLS curve preferences to only P-256. This was both redundant and overly restrictive: Go 1.25.5 already filters curves to FIPS-approved values internally (crypto/tls/defaults_fips140.go)
* Remove the explicit curve preference and let Go handle FIPS curve selection natively.
    
Release note (security update): Removed an overly restrictive TLS curve preference that limited FIPS mode to P-256. CockroachDB now uses Go's native FIPS curve selection, improving interoperability with clients that prefer other Go's native FIPS curves.

Epic: [CRDB-61940](https://cockroachlabs.atlassian.net/browse/CRDB-61940)
Fixes: #166494
author: sanchit-CRL

title: sql: add cluster setting to block auto-unlock of schema_locked tables
body:
The `schema_locked` storage parameter was designed as a changefeed optimization. The declarative schema changer automatically unlocks schema-locked tables, performs DDL, then re-locks them — meaning `schema_locked` doesn't actually prevent user-initiated DDL.

This commit adds a new cluster setting `sql.schema.auto_unlock.enabled` (default `true`) that controls whether the declarative schema changer is allowed to auto-unlock schema-locked tables. When set to `false`, DDL on schema-locked tables is blocked unless the user manually unlocks the table first via `ALTER TABLE t SET (schema_locked = false)`.

The legacy schema changer already blocks all DDL on schema-locked tables regardless of this setting, so no changes were needed there.

Resolves: #166242

Release note (sql change): Added a new cluster setting `sql.schema.auto_unlock.enabled` that controls whether DDL operations automatically unlock `schema_locked` tables. When set to `false`, DDL on schema-locked tables is blocked unless the user manually unlocks the table first. This allows customers using LDR to enforce `schema_locked` as a hard lock preventing user-initiated DDL. The default is `true`, preserving existing behavior.

commits.message:
sql: add cluster setting to block auto-unlock of schema_locked tables

The `schema_locked` storage parameter was designed as a changefeed
optimization. The declarative schema changer automatically unlocks
schema-locked tables, performs DDL, then re-locks them — meaning
`schema_locked` doesn't actually prevent user-initiated DDL.

This commit adds a new cluster setting `sql.schema.auto_unlock.enabled`
(default `true`) that controls whether the declarative schema changer
is allowed to auto-unlock schema-locked tables. When set to `false`,
DDL on schema-locked tables is blocked unless the user manually unlocks
the table first via `ALTER TABLE t SET (schema_locked = false)`.

The legacy schema changer already blocks all DDL on schema-locked
tables regardless of this setting, so no changes were needed there.

Resolves: #166242

Release note (sql change): Added a new cluster setting
`sql.schema.auto_unlock.enabled` that controls whether DDL operations
automatically unlock `schema_locked` tables. When set to `false`, DDL
on schema-locked tables is blocked unless the user manually unlocks the
table first. This allows customers using LDR to enforce `schema_locked`
as a hard lock preventing user-initiated DDL. The default is `true`,
preserving existing behavior.

title: sql: deflake TestScatterWithOneVoter
body:
Wait for all stores to be available (not just present) in the store pool before running scatter. Two races existed:

1. `StartCluster.WaitForNStores` uses its own gossip callback worker, independent of the store pool's callback. The store pool may not have processed store descriptors yet when `WaitForNStores` returns.

2. Stores can be transiently marked "suspect" after startup due to node liveness flaps. Suspect stores are excluded from the store list used by the allocator, causing scatter to silently find no rebalance targets.

Fixes-25.4 #165936
author: pav-kv

commits.message:
sql: deflake TestScatterWithOneVoter

Wait for all stores to be available (not just present) in the store pool
before running scatter. Two races existed:

1. StartCluster's WaitForNStores uses its own gossip callback worker,
   independent of the store pool's callback. The store pool may not have
   processed store descriptors yet when WaitForNStores returns.

2. Stores can be transiently marked "suspect" after startup due to node
   liveness flaps. Suspect stores are excluded from the store list used
   by the allocator, causing scatter to silently find no rebalance
   targets.

Epic: none
Release note: none
