title: release-25.4: opt/memo: fix exponential behavior when formatting nested UDFs
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166026 on behalf of @yuzefovich.

----

This commit fixes seemingly exponential behavior when formatting nested UDFs when `ExprFmtHideScalars` flag is not set (which is the case for `EXPLAIN (OPT, TYPES)` and `opt-vv.txt` file of the stmt bundle). This is done by keeping track of "repetitive calls" in addition to already being tracked "recursive calls". Once we've seen a call to a UDF, the UDF's future invocation aren't formatted and are simply replaced with `repetitive-call`.

The impact of the old behavior is that it can easily lead to OOMs. For example, running the regression test for issue 161993 via EXPLAIN ANALYZE (DEBUG) on my laptop consumes 50GiB and doesn't finish within 30s.

Fixes: #166024.

Release note (bug fix): Previously, running `EXPLAIN ANALYZE (DEBUG)` on a query that invokes a UDF with many blocks in some cases could cause OOMs, and this is now fixed.

----

Release justification: low-risk stability bug fix.
author: yuzefovich

reviews.body: > @mw5h made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166240#-:-OoRr-OkB0bwzQzXWqMK:bnfp4nl)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on yuzefovich).

commits.message:
opt/memo: fix exponential behavior when formatting nested UDFs

This commit fixes seemingly exponential behavior when formatting nested
UDFs when `ExprFmtHideScalars` flag is not set (which is the case for
`EXPLAIN (OPT, TYPES)` and `opt-vv.txt` file of the stmt bundle). This
is done by keeping track of "repetitive calls" in addition to already
being tracked "recursive calls". Once we've seen a call to a UDF, the
UDF's future invocation aren't formatted and are simply replaced with
`repetitive-call`.

The impact of the old behavior is that it can easily lead to OOMs. For
example, running the regression test for issue 161993 via EXPLAIN
ANALYZE (DEBUG) on my laptop consumes 50GiB and doesn't finish within
30s.

Release note (bug fix): Previously, running `EXPLAIN ANALYZE (DEBUG)` on
a query that invokes a UDF with many blocks in some cases could cause
OOMs, and this is now fixed.

title: release-26.2: crosscluster/physical: avoid frontier processor deadlock during cutover
body:
Backport 2/2 commits from #166110 on behalf of @msbutler.

----

Informs  #166109

Release note: none

----

Release justification: low risk change to prevent PCR hang during cutover
author: msbutler

commits.message:
crosscluster/physical: avoid frontier processor deadlock during cutover

Informs:  #166109

Release note: none

roachtest: add c2c/disconnect-permanent roachtest

The current c2c/disconnect roachtest only tested that the PCR stream could
advance after a temporary network partition. This patch adds a new partition
invariant that maintains the partition for the duration of the PCR job, to test
that cutover is quick even during a network partition. This patch also modifies
the partition test such that we create a full partition of src and dest nodes
every time.

Informs #166109

Release note: none

title: release-26.2: release: simplify docker login in cloud-only build script
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166200 on behalf of @rail.

----

Remove the separate `gcr_staged_credentials` login for `releases-prod`. The `cockroach-cloud-images` service account now has read access to `releases-prod`, so a single `docker login` suffices for both pulling the staged image and pushing the cloud-only manifest.

Previously, the script performed two `docker login` calls to the same host (`us-docker.pkg.dev`), where the second overwrote the first, causing a 403 Forbidden when `docker buildx imagetools create` tried to pull from `releases-prod`.

Epic: none
Release note: none

----

Release justification: release automation changes
author: rail

commits.message:
release: simplify docker login in cloud-only build script

Remove the separate `gcr_staged_credentials` login for `releases-prod`.
The `cockroach-cloud-images` service account now has read access to
`releases-prod`, so a single `docker login` suffices for both pulling
the staged image and pushing the cloud-only manifest.

Previously, the script performed two `docker login` calls to the same
host (`us-docker.pkg.dev`), where the second overwrote the first,
causing a 403 Forbidden when `docker buildx imagetools create` tried
to pull from `releases-prod`.

Epic: none

title: workflows: pass PR description to autosolve comment handler
body:
The pr-autosolve-comments workflow invokes Claude to address review
feedback, but Claude had no access to the PR description (commit record).
This made it harder to understand the intent behind changes when
addressing review comments.

Save the PR body as `pr_body.txt` in the trigger workflow artifact for all
event types: extracted from the event payload for `pull_request_review` and
`pull_request_review_comment` events, and from the API response for
`issue_comment` events (which already fetches PR details). The handler
copies it into the review context directory and the Claude prompt
documents it.

Release note: None
author: ajstorm

title: sql/privilege: clean up and increase compatibility of new acl functions
body:
Review note: Please review each commit individually to make this easy to review.
See the Claude Code plan [here](https://gist.github.com/rafiss/9537114859d55b91191b1309ffc6b01c).

---

### sql/privilege: sort ACL characters by privilege bit position 

Replace the hardcoded orderedPrivs list with a sort by Kind value,
which corresponds to the privilege bit position. This produces a
deterministic ACL character ordering and also fixes a pre-existing
bug where `make([]string, len(privileges))` created zero-value
entries before appending.

### sql/privilege: remove redundant ValidACLChars constant 

Use ACLCharToPrivName map lookup instead of maintaining a duplicate
list of valid ACL characters.


### sql: update acldefault to reflect actual default privileges 

Update the acldefault builtin to return privilege defaults that match
what CockroachDB actually assigns to newly created objects:

- Include admin and root roles with ALL privileges, matching
  NewCustomSuperuserPrivilegeDescriptor behavior.
- If the owner is distinct from admin/root, include an owner entry too.
- Adjust privilege chars per object type to match CRDB's defaults
  (e.g. tables get "admrtw" not "arwdDxtm").
- Add parameter ('p') defaults with SET and ALTER SYSTEM privileges.
- Do not mark implicit grant options with '*' in the ACL string.
  PostgreSQL documents that "the owner's implicit grant options are not
  marked in the access privileges display", so we follow suit for
  admin, root, and the owner.

Add TestAclDefaultPublicPrivileges to verify that the public role's
privileges from acldefault match the actual defaults on newly created
objects.

### sql: populate ACL columns in pg_catalog tables 

Populate the relacl, nspacl, datacl, proacl, and typacl columns in
pg_catalog tables using a new privilegeDescriptorToACLArray helper.

Only privileges with a PostgreSQL ACL character equivalent are included;
CockroachDB-specific privileges (BACKUP, CHANGEFEED, ZONECONFIG, etc.)
are skipped.

To match PostgreSQL behavior:

- Return NULL when an object's privileges match its defaults, since
  pg_dump compares ACL columns against acldefault() to decide whether
  to emit GRANT/REVOKE statements.
- Strip grant option '*' markers for admin, root, and the owner,
  since their grant options are implicit (as in PostgreSQL for the
  owner/superuser).
- Update acldefault() to use a shared DefaultACLItems function,
  ensuring consistency between ACL column output and acldefault().

Virtual tables and schemas continue to return NULL ACLs.

### sql/privilege: add ACLItem type for structured aclitem construction 

Add a new ACLItem struct that encapsulates PostgreSQL-compatible aclitem
construction (grantee=privchars/grantor format). This replaces ad-hoc
fmt.Sprintf-based ACL string building with a structured type that:

- Implements redact.SafeFormatter (role names are PII, privilege chars
  are safe)
- Provides NewACLItem constructor and ParseACLItem parser with
  round-trip consistency
- Moves DefaultACLItems to return []ACLItem instead of []string
- Adds IsDefaultACL for order-independent default privilege comparison
- Removes ValidateACLItemString (superseded by ParseACLItem) and the
  now-unused skipACLIdentifier helper

### sql: migrate aclitem callers to use ACLItem type 

Update all callers that previously constructed aclitem strings via
fmt.Sprintf to use the structured ACLItem type:

- NewDACLItemFromDString: use ParseACLItem for validation
- acldefault builtin: use DefaultACLItems ([]ACLItem) for
  descriptor-backed types; use NewACLItem for non-descriptor types
  (except PARAMETER which needs raw ACL chars for SET/ALTER SYSTEM)
- privilegeDescriptorToACLArray: use NewACLItem with ALL expansion
  and IsDefaultACL for default detection
- createDefACLItem: accept username.SQLUsername, use NewACLItem

The aclexplode builtin retains manual char iteration because ACL chars
's' (SET) and 'A' (ALTER SYSTEM) have no privilege.Kind equivalent.

### sql: accept privilege.ACLItem in tree.NewDACLItem 

Change `tree.NewDACLItem` to accept a `privilege.ACLItem` instead of a
string. This eliminates the string→parse→validate round-trip at each
call site where a structured ACLItem is already available.

Callers that still need to construct an aclitem datum from a raw string
(pgwire decoding, casts, random datum generation, string literal
resolution) now use `tree.NewDACLItemFromDString`.

Also change `createDefACLItem` in pg_catalog.go to return
`privilege.ACLItem` instead of `string`, since all callers immediately
pass it to `tree.NewDACLItem`.

---

informs #20296
author: rafiss

reviews.body: > @eric-alton reviewed 21 files and all commit messages, and made 6 comments. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166185)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on fqazi, rafiss, and ZhouXing19). ___ *[`-- commits` line 2 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166185#-OoAqut-789pK56BeeEt:-OoAqut-789pK56BeeEu:blexik4):* Outside of the bug fix, the motivation behind this change is unclear. What benefits do we gain from having deterministic ACL ordering? ___ *[`pkg/sql/privilege/kind.go` line 82 at r9](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166185#-OoBBQXd5dnq2ZFFiERH:-OoBBQXd5dnq2ZFFiERI:b-jvipg9) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/778c8806d2b03dcd4fde14cf356e7d3a215066a4/pkg/sql/privilege/kind.go#L82)):* > ```Go > // by acldefault, aclexplode, and makeaclitem. They have no > // corresponding CockroachDB privilege and must never be used in > // bitmask operations (Mask/ToBitField/ListFromBitField). > ``` Can we guard against this in any way? For example, maybe one common code path which performs bitmask operations on these Kind values can check that the value does not exceed `largestKind`. ___ *[`pkg/sql/sem/builtins/pg_builtins.go` line 2630 at r3](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166185#-OoAwQ0_5IvD_TgdayWQ:-OoAwQ0_5IvD_TgdayWR:b-2bb0ef) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/eda4a8244153ae589f064fe99d35dba195d39f55/pkg/sql/sem/builtins/pg_builtins.go#L2630)):* > ```Go > // (matching PostgreSQL behavior for owner defaults). > if def.ownerPrivs != "" { > item := quotedAdmin + "=" + def.ownerPrivs + "/" + quotedOwner > ``` What is the reason for this ordering, admin before public? commits.message:
sql/privilege: sort ACL characters by privilege bit position

Replace the hardcoded orderedPrivs list with a sort by Kind value,
which corresponds to the privilege bit position. This produces a
deterministic ACL character ordering and also fixes a pre-existing
bug where `make([]string, len(privileges))` created zero-value
entries before appending.

The deterministic output helps avoid test churn to update test
expectations whenever the slice ordering changes incidentally.

Epic: none

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

sql/privilege: remove redundant ValidACLChars constant

Use ACLCharToPrivName map lookup instead of maintaining a duplicate
list of valid ACL characters.

Epic: none

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

sql: update acldefault to reflect actual default privileges

Update the acldefault builtin to return privilege defaults that match
what CockroachDB actually assigns to newly created objects:

- Include admin and root roles with ALL privileges, matching
  NewCustomSuperuserPrivilegeDescriptor behavior.
- If the owner is distinct from admin/root, include an owner entry too.
- Adjust privilege chars per object type to match CRDB's defaults
  (e.g. tables get "admrtw" not "arwdDxtm").
- Add parameter ('p') defaults with SET and ALTER SYSTEM privileges.
- Do not mark implicit grant options with '*' in the ACL string.
  PostgreSQL documents that "the owner's implicit grant options are not
  marked in the access privileges display", so we follow suit for
  admin, root, and the owner.

Add TestAclDefaultPublicPrivileges to verify that the public role's
privileges from acldefault match the actual defaults on newly created
objects.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

sql: populate ACL columns in pg_catalog tables

Populate the relacl, nspacl, datacl, proacl, and typacl columns in
pg_catalog tables using a new privilegeDescriptorToACLArray helper.

Only privileges with a PostgreSQL ACL character equivalent are included;
CockroachDB-specific privileges (BACKUP, CHANGEFEED, ZONECONFIG, etc.)
are skipped.

To match PostgreSQL behavior:

- Return NULL when an object's privileges match its defaults, since
  pg_dump compares ACL columns against acldefault() to decide whether
  to emit GRANT/REVOKE statements.
- Strip grant option '*' markers for admin, root, and the owner,
  since their grant options are implicit (as in PostgreSQL for the
  owner/superuser).
- Update acldefault() to use a shared DefaultACLItems function,
  ensuring consistency between ACL column output and acldefault().

Virtual tables and schemas continue to return NULL ACLs.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

sql/privilege: add ACLItem type for structured aclitem construction

Add a new ACLItem struct that encapsulates PostgreSQL-compatible aclitem
construction (grantee=privchars/grantor format). This replaces ad-hoc
fmt.Sprintf-based ACL string building with a structured type that:

- Implements redact.SafeFormatter (role names are PII, privilege chars
  are safe)
- Provides NewACLItem constructor and ParseACLItem parser with
  round-trip consistency
- Moves DefaultACLItems to return []ACLItem instead of []string
- Adds IsDefaultACL for order-independent default privilege comparison
- Removes ValidateACLItemString (superseded by ParseACLItem) and the
  now-unused skipACLIdentifier helper

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

sql: migrate aclitem callers to use ACLItem type

Update all callers that previously constructed aclitem strings via
fmt.Sprintf to use the structured ACLItem type:

- NewDACLItemFromDString: use ParseACLItem for validation
- acldefault builtin: use DefaultACLItems ([]ACLItem) for
  descriptor-backed types; use NewACLItem for non-descriptor types
  (except PARAMETER which needs raw ACL chars for SET/ALTER SYSTEM)
- privilegeDescriptorToACLArray: use NewACLItem with ALL expansion
  and IsDefaultACL for default detection
- createDefACLItem: accept username.SQLUsername, use NewACLItem

The aclexplode builtin retains manual char iteration because ACL chars
's' (SET) and 'A' (ALTER SYSTEM) have no privilege.Kind equivalent.

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

sql: accept privilege.ACLItem in tree.NewDACLItem

Change `tree.NewDACLItem` to accept a `privilege.ACLItem` instead of a
string. This eliminates the string→parse→validate round-trip at each
call site where a structured ACLItem is already available.

Callers that still need to construct an aclitem datum from a raw string
(pgwire decoding, casts, random datum generation, string literal
resolution) now use `tree.NewDACLItemFromDString`.

Also change `createDefACLItem` in pg_catalog.go to return
`privilege.ACLItem` instead of `string`, since all callers immediately
pass it to `tree.NewDACLItem`.

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

sql: include owner's implicit privileges in pg_catalog ACL arrays

When the owner of a database object is a non-root, non-admin user,
their privileges are not explicitly stored in the privilege descriptor
(they are implicit). This caused privilegeDescriptorToACLArray to omit
the owner's entry from the ACL array, leading to two problems:

1. The owner's privilege entry was missing from relacl/nspacl output,
   unlike PostgreSQL which always includes the owner.
2. IsDefaultACL always returned false for non-root-owned objects
   (length mismatch: 2 actual vs 3 expected), so default privileges
   returned a non-NULL ACL array instead of NULL.

Fix this by checking whether the owner appears in privDesc.Users after
the main loop, and if not, adding an entry with the owner's implicit
ALL privileges.

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

docker manifest approach to docker buildx imagetools, consistent
with the changes in 7deab91 for the main release pipeline.

inspect for tag existence checks.

title: cloud/amazon: fix AWS_SKIP_CHECKSUM not suppressing CRC32 on multipart uploads
body:
After the aws-sdk-go-v2 s3 manager upgrade to v1.17.65, the manager's
internal initChecksumAlgorithm() unconditionally defaults ChecksumAlgorithm
to CRC32 when the field is empty. This means setting ChecksumAlgorithm = ""
on the PutObjectInput (which the code did when AWS_SKIP_CHECKSUM=true) no
longer suppresses checksums for multipart uploads — the manager overwrites
the cleared value with CRC32 before the S3 client sees it, bypassing
the RequestChecksumCalculation = WhenRequired configuration.

Fix this by adding smithy-go Initialize middleware via the Uploader's
ClientOptions that clears ChecksumAlgorithm from CreateMultipartUploadInput,
UploadPartInput, CompleteMultipartUploadInput, and PutObjectInput before
the S3 client's checksum middleware processes them. This runs after the
manager's initChecksumAlgorithm but before the client computes checksums,
effectively suppressing all checksum computation on multipart uploads when
AWS_SKIP_CHECKSUM=true.

Resolves: #166090

Release note (bug fix): Fixed a bug where the AWS_SKIP_CHECKSUM=true
storage option did not fully suppress checksums on multipart uploads
to S3-compatible storage. After an internal SDK upgrade, multipart
uploads included CRC32 checksums despite the skip flag, which could
cause errors with S3-compatible services that do not support CRC
checksums.

Generated by Claude Code Auto-Solver
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

---

```

 pkg/cloud/amazon/s3_storage.go      | 29 ++++++++++++++++++
 pkg/cloud/amazon/s3_storage_test.go | 59 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 3 files changed, 92 insertions(+)
```

*This PR was auto-generated by [issue-autosolve](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/master/.github/workflows/issue-autosolve.yml) using Claude Code.*
*Please review carefully before approving.*
author: cockroach-teamcity

commits.message:
cloud/amazon: fix AWS_SKIP_CHECKSUM not suppressing CRC32 on multipart uploads

After the aws-sdk-go-v2 s3 manager upgrade to v1.17.65, the manager's
internal initChecksumAlgorithm() unconditionally defaults ChecksumAlgorithm
to CRC32 when the field is empty. This means setting ChecksumAlgorithm = ""
on the PutObjectInput (which the code did when AWS_SKIP_CHECKSUM=true) no
longer suppresses checksums for multipart uploads — the manager overwrites
the cleared value with CRC32 before the S3 client sees it, bypassing
the RequestChecksumCalculation = WhenRequired configuration.

Fix this by adding smithy-go Initialize middleware via the Uploader's
ClientOptions that clears ChecksumAlgorithm from CreateMultipartUploadInput,
UploadPartInput, CompleteMultipartUploadInput, and PutObjectInput before
the S3 client's checksum middleware processes them. This runs after the
manager's initChecksumAlgorithm but before the client computes checksums,
effectively suppressing all checksum computation on multipart uploads when
AWS_SKIP_CHECKSUM=true.

Resolves: #166090

Release note (bug fix): Fixed a bug where the AWS_SKIP_CHECKSUM=true
storage option did not fully suppress checksums on multipart uploads
to S3-compatible storage. After an internal SDK upgrade, multipart
uploads included CRC32 checksums despite the skip flag, which could
cause errors with S3-compatible services that do not support CRC
checksums.

Generated by Claude Code Auto-Solver
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: release-26.2: stmtdiagnostics: add continuous bundle collection with COUNT-based limiting
body:
Backport 3/3 commits from #162154 on behalf of @isaactwong.

----

## Summary

- Adds `request_id` column and index to `system.statement_diagnostics` via schema migration (`V26_2_StmtDiagnosticsRequestID`), linking bundles to their originating diagnostic request.
- Implements continuous diagnostic bundle collection for requests with `sampling_probability` and `expires_at` set. At insertion time, enforces per-request limits by counting existing bundles (`SELECT COUNT(*) WHERE request_id = ?`) against the `sql.stmt_diagnostics.max_bundles_per_request` cluster setting (default 10).
- During mixed-version upgrades, continuous requests collect without limit enforcement until the migration completes. Pre-migration bundles have NULL `request_id`, so the counter resets to 0 post-migration.

## Commit breakdown

1. **stmtdiagnostics: add request_id schema migration** — Version gate, schema change, migration, golden file updates.
2. **stmtdiagnostics: add continuous bundle collection with COUNT-based limiting** — Registry implementation, cluster setting, tests.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

----

Release justification: missed the branch cut yesterday due to merge conflicts
author: dhartunian

commits.message:
stmtdiagnostics: add request_id schema migration

Add the `request_id` column and index to `system.statement_diagnostics`
via a new cluster version gate `V26_2_StmtDiagnosticsRequestID`. The
`request_id` column links diagnostic bundles to their originating
request, enabling multiple bundles per request for continuous collection.

The migration adds:
- `request_id INT8` column to `system.statement_diagnostics`
- `request_id_idx` index for efficient lookup by request

Resolves: #161001

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

stmtdiagnostics: add continuous bundle collection with COUNT-based limiting

Implement continuous diagnostic bundle collection for requests that have
both `sampling_probability` and `expires_at` set. At bundle insertion
time, enforce the per-request limit by counting existing bundles via
`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM system.statement_diagnostics WHERE request_id = ?`
and comparing against the `sql.stmt_diagnostics.max_bundles_per_request`
cluster setting (default 10). This replaces a previous design that used
an in-memory counter and a `collections_remaining` DB column.

The `max_bundles_per_request` setting now requires a positive integer
(minimum 1); unlimited collection is no longer supported.

During mixed-version rolling upgrades, continuous requests collect
without limit enforcement until the migration completes and `request_id`
becomes available. Pre-migration bundles have NULL `request_id`, so
the post-migration counter effectively resets to 0.

Resolves: #161001

Release note (ops change): Statement diagnostics requests with
sampling_probability and expires_at now collect up to 10 bundles
(configurable via sql.stmt_diagnostics.max_bundles_per_request) instead
of a single bundle. Set the cluster setting to 1 to restore single-bundle
behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

stmtdiagnostics: address code review feedback

Address feedback from dhartunian's review:

- Update copyright year from 2025 to 2026 in new migration file
- Refactor duplicate UPDATE statement in statement_diagnostics.go
  to extract SQL execution after if/else
- Remove unused hasMatchingIndex() function and export
- Test improvements:
  - Remove unnecessary time.Sleep in test
  - Remove redundant count re-query after SucceedsSoon
  - Replace two require calls with single require.Equal
  - Extract SQL execution outside SucceedsSoon loop to prevent
    false positives from accumulated statements (lines 1610, 1887)

Informs: #161001

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: allocator/mmaprototype: add test for non-voter WB rebalancing
body:
## Summary

- Adds a `TestClusterState` datadriven test verifying that MMA rebalances
  `NON_VOTER` replicas across stores to address WriteBandwidth imbalance,
  even when the leaseholder is in a different region.
- Scenario: voters constrained to Region A (via `voter_constraints`),
  non-voters placed in Region B, with a WB imbalance among the Region B
  stores. The leaseholder in Region A detects the remote overload and
  moves the non-voter, preserving the `NON_VOTER` type.
- This scenario had no prior test coverage.

Epic: CRDB-56265
author: tbg

commits.message:
allocator/mmaprototype: add test for non-voter WB rebalancing

Add a TestClusterState datadriven test that verifies MMA rebalances
NON_VOTER replicas across stores to address WriteBandwidth imbalance,
even when the leaseholder is in a different region. This scenario was
previously untested: voters constrained to Region A, non-voters in
Region B, with a WB imbalance among the Region B stores.

The test confirms the leaseholder in Region A detects the remote WB
overload and moves the non-voter from the overloaded store to the
underloaded store, preserving the NON_VOTER replica type.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: sql: disable test tenant for tests using TestingDisableTableLeases
body:
## Summary

Several tests directly manipulate table descriptors via KV writes and rely on
`lease.TestingDisableTableLeases()` to make those changes immediately visible
to the SQL layer. This process-global atomic bool only takes effect within the
test process; when an external process virtual cluster is metamorphically
injected, the external SQL server's lease manager still caches descriptors
normally and never sees the direct KV writes, causing intermittent failures.

This PR disables the default test tenant for all tests that combine
`TestingDisableTableLeases` with direct KV descriptor manipulation:

- `descriptor_mutation_test.go`: 5 tests
- `index_mutation_test.go`: 1 test
- `delete_preserving_index_test.go`: 3 test sites
- `crdb_internal_test.go`: 2 tests

Fixes: #165980

Release note: None

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: mw5h

reviews.body: LGTM, thanks! Just one nit comment about the issue to link. I'm also curious why this test failure happens recently -- seems that this is an old test, and the introduction of `default test tenant / VC injection` is not new either (since 2023, IIUC). Is it purely because the injection of a default tenant is metamorphic, or something changes recently? I asked glean but got nothing convincing. > @ZhouXing19 reviewed 4 files and all commit messages, and made 2 comments. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166108#-:-OoAlOxw6hJwQp8DPSc3:bnmrrve)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on mw5h and yuzefovich). ___ *[`pkg/sql/crdb_internal_test.go` line 217 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166108#-OoAeWR27-bXFhLEJEMa:-OoAeWR27-bXFhLEJEMb:b4p7uj6) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/e86cbebb3e1b03a1a4b9f38b7aaae2dcdb9e5269/pkg/sql/crdb_internal_test.go#L217)):* > ```Go > // This is incompatible with external process virtual clusters where > // the lease disable doesn't take effect. > DefaultTestTenant: base.TestIsForStuffThatShouldWorkWithSecondaryTenantsButDoesntYet(165980), > ``` nit: I'm not sure using the issue number of the original test failure is conventional here (maybe @yuzefovich knows better), but I think we should have it linked to a "root cause" issue, which is about "tests that mutate descriptors via KV + `TestingDisableTableLeases` flaking when an external-process virtual cluster is injected". Also, we might want to add this root issue to #76378?

Great question on the "why now?" — I dug into it and the answer is straightforward.

Before October 2025, the entire `pkg/sql` test package had a package-wide override in `main_test.go` that disabled test tenant injection for all tests:

```go
defer serverutils.TestingSetDefaultTenantSelectionOverride(
    base.TestIsForStuffThatShouldWorkWithSecondaryTenantsButDoesntYet(76378),
)()
```

In `3f64b0eb656` (Oct 22, 2025), @yuzefovich removed that package-wide override and enabled test tenants for `pkg/sql`, individually fixing tests he identified as incompatible. These tests weren't caught in that pass. Since tenant injection is metamorphic, they only fail when the random coin flip injects an external-process tenant, which is why it took months to surface as flaky failures.

Re: the issue number — good point, I'll file a root cause issue for "tests using `TestingDisableTableLeases` + direct KV descriptor manipulation are incompatible with external-process virtual clusters" and link it to #76378, then update the references in this PR.

commits.message:
sql: disable test tenant for tests using TestingDisableTableLeases

Several tests directly manipulate table descriptors via KV writes and
rely on `lease.TestingDisableTableLeases()` to make those changes
immediately visible to the SQL layer. This process-global atomic bool
only takes effect within the test process; when an external process
virtual cluster is metamorphically injected, the external SQL server's
lease manager still caches descriptors normally and never sees the
direct KV writes. This causes intermittent failures where the SQL layer
uses stale descriptors.

The reported failure in TestOperationsWithIndexMutation manifests as a
missing row after UPSERT: the previous subtest's direct KV descriptor
writes (8+ version bumps) leave stale lease state in the external
process, and after the table is dropped and recreated, the first UPSERT
can silently target stale state.

Fix this by setting `DefaultTestTenant` to disable external process
virtual cluster injection in all tests that combine
`TestingDisableTableLeases` with direct KV descriptor manipulation.
Since shared-process mode works fine (same process, same atomic bool),
we use `TestDoesNotWorkWithExternalProcessMode` rather than disabling
all secondary tenant modes.

These tests were exposed when the package-wide tenant injection override
was removed in 3f64b0eb656 (Oct 2025). Since injection is metamorphic,
failures are intermittent.

Fixes: #165980
Informs: #166311

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: release-26.2: workload/schemachange: replace SHOW SCHEMAS and SHOW TYPES with direct catalog queries
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166073 on behalf of @cockroach-teamcity.

----

PR #163975 fixed the schemachange workload's `SHOW TABLES` usage to avoid
a role membership cache race condition where a newly created user appears
in the role list but its membership hasn't been populated yet, causing
`role/user does not exist` errors. However, the same issue remained for
`SHOW SCHEMAS` and `SHOW TYPES` in the `commentOn` function and
`randTypeName` function.

Both `SHOW SCHEMAS` and `SHOW TYPES` internally join with
`pg_catalog.pg_roles`, which triggers `userHasPrivilegeOrRoleOption` for
each role. When the `systemTableWriter` creates a new user concurrently,
the role membership cache can see the user in `system.users` before its
membership is populated, resulting in `NewUndefinedUserError`.

This commit replaces:
- `[SHOW SCHEMAS] WHERE owner != 'node'` with a direct query against
  `information_schema.schemata`, filtering out system schemas by name.
- `[SHOW TYPES]` with direct queries against `pg_catalog.pg_type` joined
  with `pg_catalog.pg_namespace`, avoiding the `pg_roles` join entirely.

This follows the same pattern established by PR #163975 for `SHOW TABLES`.

Resolves: #164906

Release note: None

Generated by Claude Code Auto-Solver
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

---

 1 file changed, 14 insertions(+), 5 deletions(-)
```

*Please review carefully before approving.*

----

Release justification: test only change
author: blathers-crl

commits.message:
workload/schemachange: replace SHOW SCHEMAS and SHOW TYPES with direct catalog queries

PR #163975 fixed the schemachange workload's `SHOW TABLES` usage to avoid
a role membership cache race condition where a newly created user appears
in the role list but its membership hasn't been populated yet, causing
`role/user does not exist` errors. However, the same issue remained for
`SHOW SCHEMAS` and `SHOW TYPES` in the `commentOn` function and
`randTypeName` function.

Both `SHOW SCHEMAS` and `SHOW TYPES` internally join with
`pg_catalog.pg_roles`, which triggers `userHasPrivilegeOrRoleOption` for
each role. When the `systemTableWriter` creates a new user concurrently,
the role membership cache can see the user in `system.users` before its
membership is populated, resulting in `NewUndefinedUserError`.

This commit replaces:
- `[SHOW SCHEMAS] WHERE owner != 'node'` with a direct query against
  `information_schema.schemata`, filtering out system schemas by name.
- `[SHOW TYPES]` with direct queries against `pg_catalog.pg_type` joined
  with `pg_catalog.pg_namespace`, avoiding the `pg_roles` join entirely.

This follows the same pattern established by PR #163975 for `SHOW TABLES`.

Resolves: #164906

title: roachprod: stop cancelling provider contexts on non-fatal list errors
body:
Previously, when a provider returned an error during `ListCloud`, the
error was propagated through the errgroup, which cancelled the shared
context. This killed in-flight work for all other providers -- for
example, every AWS CLI subprocess across all regions got SIGKILL'd,
resulting in `roachprod list` showing no AWS VMs at all even when only
one provider (like Azure) had an issue.

Now, when `BailOnProviderError` is false (the local roachprod path),
provider errors are collected without cancelling the shared context.
Other providers can finish their work, and the collected errors are
still logged as a warning.

The `BailOnProviderError` path (roachprod-centralized) is unchanged
and still fails fast.

Release note: None
author: angeladietz

commits.message:
roachprod: stop cancelling provider contexts on non-fatal list errors

Previously, when a provider returned an error during `ListCloud`, the
error was propagated through the errgroup, which cancelled the shared
context. This killed in-flight work for all other providers -- for
example, every AWS CLI subprocess across all regions got SIGKILL'd,
resulting in `roachprod list` showing no AWS VMs at all even when only
one provider (like Azure) had an issue.

Now, when `BailOnProviderError` is false (the local roachprod path),
provider errors are collected without cancelling the shared context.
Other providers can finish their work, and the collected errors are
still logged as a warning.

The `BailOnProviderError` path (roachprod-centralized) is unchanged
and still fails fast.

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: release-26.2: storeliveness: fix flaky TestTransportClusterSettingToggle
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166076 on behalf of @pav-kv.

----

The test asserted that after toggling the heartbeat smearing setting back to enabled and enqueuing a message, `SendQueueSize` should be 1 (message queued but not yet delivered). This is inherently racy: `EnqueueMessage` signals `sendAllMessages`, which wakes `smearingSenderLoop`, which after ~10ms signals `processQueue`, which after another ~10ms drains the queue and decrements the metric. On a loaded CI machine, this entire pipeline completes before `SucceedsSoon` observes the intermediate queue state, causing the test to spin for 45s and fail with "expected queue size 1, got 0".

Remove the mid-test assertion on transient `SendQueueSize`. The test's purpose is to verify no messages are lost during setting toggles, which is already covered by the final assertions: 0 dropped, 3 sent, 3 received.

Fixes: #165968

----

Release justification: test deflake
author: blathers-crl

commits.message:
storeliveness: fix flaky TestTransportClusterSettingToggle

The test asserted that after toggling the heartbeat smearing setting back
to enabled and enqueuing a message, SendQueueSize should be 1 (message
queued but not yet delivered). This is inherently racy: EnqueueMessage
signals sendAllMessages, which wakes smearingSenderLoop, which after
~10ms signals processQueue, which after another ~10ms drains the queue
and decrements the metric. On a loaded CI machine, this entire pipeline
completes before SucceedsSoon observes the intermediate queue state,
causing the test to spin for 45s and fail with "expected queue size 1,
got 0".

Remove the mid-test assertion on transient SendQueueSize. The test's
purpose is to verify no messages are lost during setting toggles, which
is already covered by the final assertions: 0 dropped, 3 sent, 3
received.

Fixes: #165968

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: release-26.2: logical: fix integration with admission control
body:
Backport 2/2 commits from #165582 on behalf of @jeffswenson.

----

## logical: fix integration with admission control
PR https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pull/161952 added sql cpu accounting. This doesn't play well with LDR
because we are executing a lot of SQL statements inside of a distsql
flow. This breaks two of the invariants expected by https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pull/161952:

1. SQL execution is inside of a distsql flow context, but the
   workload doesn't actually follow the leaseholder. It should be
   treated as "gateway" cpu load.
2. The isession is a connExecutor instance that is not tied to any
   particular goroutine. As a result, the CancelChecker is leaking the
   goroutine handle it requests.

Fix this by creating a dedicated CPU handle per flush worker goroutine
with AtGateway=false and LowPri, and registering the goroutine before
executing SQL. Add a SessionWrapper testing knob and TestLDRCPUHandle
to validate the handle properties.

Release note: none
Fixes: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/164737
Fixes: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/163603

### logical: update TestFlushErrorHandling to use a real FlowCtx

TestFlushErrorHandling previously ran with a nil FlowCtx, which required
defensive nil checks scattered throughout the writer processor. Update
the test to start a test server and wire up a real FlowCtx/ServerConfig
so those nil guards can be removed.

Release note: none
Epic: none

----

Release justification: fixes a GA blocker.
author: blathers-crl

commits.message:
logical: update TestFlushErrorHandling to use a real FlowCtx

TestFlushErrorHandling previously ran with a nil FlowCtx, which required
defensive nil checks scattered throughout the writer processor. Update
the test to start a test server and wire up a real FlowCtx/ServerConfig
so those nil guards can be removed.

Release note: none
Epic: none

logical: fix integration with admission control

PR #161952 added sql cpu accounting. This doesn't play well with LDR
because we are executing a lot of SQL statements inside of a distsql
flow. This breaks two of the invariants expected by #161952:

1. SQL execution is inside of a distsql flow context, but the
   workload doesn't actually follow the leaseholder. It should be
   treated as "gateway" cpu load.
2. We create an unbounded number of goroutines within a single SQL CPU
	 accounting context. This caused the slice of goroutines to grow
	 over time.

This is fixed by creating a dedicated CPU handle per flush worker
goroutine with AtGateway=false and registering the goroutine before
executing SQL.

Release note: none
Fixes: #164737
Fixes: #163603

title: release-26.2: rangescanstats: copy frontier before scanning
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #164773 on behalf of @DarrylWong.

----

For large enough frontiers, we found that the stats poller, which acquires one lock per span, was contending with changefeeds checkpointing, and potentially causing the changefeed to lag.

This change snapshots the frontier before scanning to reduce lock contention.

Informs: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/164417
Informs:  #161039
Epic: none
Release note: none

----

Release justification: low risk fix for performance regression
author: blathers-crl

reviews.body: LGTM

commits.message:
rangescanstats: copy frontier before scanning

For large enough frontiers, we found that the stats poller, which
acquires one lock per span, was contending with changefeeds
checkpointing, and potentially causing the changefeed to lag.

This change snapshots the frontier before scanning to reduce
lock contention.

title: leaktest: re-add TLS handshake goroutine leak exclusion
body:
The exclusion for `net/http.(*persistConn).addTLS` and
`crypto/tls.(*Conn).handshakeContext` was removed in ecc4d006d93
under the assumption that Go 1.23+ (golang/go#62227) resolved
the underlying goroutine leak. However, the Go fix addressed
the handshake goroutine lifecycle, not the case where
`crypto/x509.loadSystemRoots` blocks on a slow syscall reading
system CA certificates from disk. In CI environments with slow
disk I/O, this read can outlast the HTTP client timeout, leaving
orphaned TLS handshake goroutines that trip the leak detector.

Re-add the exclusion with an updated comment explaining the
actual root cause: environment-dependent slow disk I/O during
system root CA loading, not a Go stdlib bug.

Resolves: #165944

Release note: None

Generated by Claude Code Auto-Solver
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

---

 1 file changed, 6 insertions(+)
```

*Please review carefully before approving.*

commits.message:
leaktest: re-add TLS handshake goroutine leak exclusion

The exclusion for `net/http.(*persistConn).addTLS` and
`crypto/tls.(*Conn).handshakeContext` was removed in ecc4d006d93
under the assumption that Go 1.23+ (golang/go#62227) resolved
the underlying goroutine leak. However, the Go fix addressed
the handshake goroutine lifecycle, not the case where
`crypto/x509.loadSystemRoots` blocks on a slow syscall reading
system CA certificates from disk. In CI environments with slow
disk I/O, this read can outlast the HTTP client timeout, leaving
orphaned TLS handshake goroutines that trip the leak detector.

Re-add the exclusion with an updated comment explaining the
actual root cause: environment-dependent slow disk I/O during
system root CA loading, not a Go stdlib bug.

Resolves: #165944

title: release-26.2: sql: fix slow failure detection in schemachange/bulkingest roachtest
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166064 on behalf of @shghasemi.

----

Move monitor creation before the initial workload import so that node failures (e.g. disk stalls) are detected immediately rather than hanging unnoticed. Also propagate the error from the cmdWriteAndRead workload step so the test fails fast instead of running for hours when a node is down.

Informs: #165530

Release note: None

title: release: authenticate git ls-remote with GH_TOKEN to prevent rate limiting
body:
listRemoteBranches() runs `git ls-remote origin` to query remote branches but, unlike other remote operations in this package (pushURL uses GH_TOKEN, clone uses gh CLI), it had no explicit authentication. This causes failures in CI environments where HTTPS credentials are not cached, and unauthenticated requests are subject to GitHub's stricter rate limits.

When GH_TOKEN is set, pass an Authorization header via `git -c http.extraheader=...` so the request is authenticated, working around rate limiting and credential issues.

Release note: None
Epic: None
author: rail

commits.message:
release: authenticate git ls-remote with GH_TOKEN to prevent rate limiting

listRemoteBranches() runs `git ls-remote origin` to query remote
branches but, unlike other remote operations in this package (pushURL
uses GH_TOKEN, clone uses gh CLI), it had no explicit authentication.
This causes failures in CI environments where HTTPS credentials are
not cached, and unauthenticated requests are subject to GitHub's
stricter rate limits.

When GH_TOKEN is set, pass an Authorization header via
`git -c http.extraheader=...` so the request is authenticated,
working around rate limiting and credential issues.

Release note: None
Epic: None

title: kvcoord: re-log "slow range RPC" warning every 10 minutes
body:
Motivated by https://github.com/cockroachlabs/support/issues/3578.

- The "slow range RPC" warning in `sendPartialBatch` was previously logged once
  after 60s and then permanently suppressed (via `tBegin = 0`), making it easy
  to miss RPCs stuck for hours.
- Replace the `tBegin`-zeroing mechanism with a `lastSlowRPCLog` timestamp that
  re-logs the warning every 10 minutes while the RPC remains stuck.
- The `SlowRPCs` metric increment and deferred response log remain first-entry
  only (unchanged behavior).

## Test plan

Verified compilation via `./dev build pkg/kv/kvclient/kvcoord:kvcoord_test`.
It would be too much of a yak shave right now to add actual unit testing of this retry loop, though
that would be good to have someday.
author: tbg

commits.message:
kvcoord: re-log "slow range RPC" warning every 10 minutes

Previously, the "slow range RPC" warning in sendPartialBatch was logged
once after 60s and then suppressed for the lifetime of the RPC by
zeroing tBegin. This made it hard to notice RPCs stuck for hours.

Replace the tBegin-zeroing mechanism with a lastSlowRPCLog timestamp
that gates re-logging at a 10-minute interval. The SlowRPCs metric
increment and deferred response log are still performed only on the
first entry.

Epic: none
Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: cli: deflake/strengthen TestLossOfQuorumRecovery
body:
The test flakes when `WaitForFullReplication` returns too early. Under overload, nodes become unavailable and skew replica counter metrics which this function relies on. Don't rely on it too much: wait for the final "interesting" condition to materialize: all ranges are moved from the decommissioned nodes to the new ones.

Fixes #161502

title: release-24.3: sql: fix TestRelocateNonVoters flake
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #165985 on behalf of @spilchen.

----

The execQueries callback is called sequentially for each tenant on the same testCluster. The callback disables replicate/lease/split queues, but they were never re-enabled between runs. When the secondary tenant's callback ran, queues were already off from the system tenant's run, so WaitForFullReplication and LEARNER cleanup could never complete.

Re-enable all three queues before each execQueries invocation in runTest.

Closes #164791
Release note: none
Epic: none

----

Release justification: test only fix
author: spilchen

but they were never re-enabled between runs. When the secondary tenant's
callback ran, queues were already off from the system tenant's run, so
WaitForFullReplication and LEARNER cleanup could never complete.

Re-enable all three queues before each execQueries invocation in runTest.

Closes #164791

title: sql: add TEMPORARY database privilege and fix temp table privilege checking
body:
## Summary

This PR fixes #165984 — `CREATE TEMP TABLE` incorrectly required `CREATE` privilege on the `public` schema, and CockroachDB was missing PostgreSQL's `TEMPORARY` database privilege.

**Commit 1: Add TEMPORARY privilege and fix temp table privilege checking**
- Adds a new `TEMPORARY` privilege kind (with `TEMP` as a parser alias), valid on database objects.
- Grants `TEMPORARY` to `PUBLIC` by default on newly created databases, matching PostgreSQL.
- Fixes the optbuilder to skip the `CREATE`-on-schema check for temp objects; the execution-time code checks `TEMPORARY` on the database instead.
- Updates logic tests for the new privilege in `SHOW GRANTS ON DATABASE` output.

**Commit 2: Migration to grant TEMPORARY to PUBLIC on existing databases**
- Adds cluster version `V26_2_GrantTemporaryToPublic`.
- Migration iterates all databases and grants `TEMPORARY` to `PUBLIC` where missing.
- Includes a test that simulates pre-upgrade state and verifies the migration.

Fixes: #165984
author: rafiss

reviews.body: > @fqazi partially reviewed 45 files and made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165992#-:-Oo0g9KS7JJK62PKQqRp:bnfp4nl)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on shghasemi).

commits.message:
sql: add TEMPORARY database privilege and fix temp table privilege checking

This commit adds a new TEMPORARY database privilege that controls
whether a user can create temporary tables and views. This aligns
CockroachDB with PostgreSQL's privilege model where TEMPORARY is
a database-level privilege granted to PUBLIC by default.

The privilege check is version-gated on V26_2 so that during
mixed-version clusters the old behavior (checking CREATE on the
schema) is preserved.

Key changes:
- Add TEMPORARY to the privilege kind enum and wire it through the
  privilege system.
- In the optbuilder's resolveSchemaForCreate, check TEMPORARY on
  the database instead of CREATE on the schema for temp objects
  after V26_2.
- Handle TEMPORARY privilege specially in optCatalog.CheckPrivilege
  to resolve against the database descriptor.
- Add logic tests for the new privilege behavior with skipif/onlyif
  for mixed-version configs.

Release note (sql change): Added TEMPORARY database privilege that
controls whether users can create temporary tables and views. This
privilege is granted to the public role by default on new databases,
matching PostgreSQL behavior.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

sql: add migration to grant TEMPORARY to PUBLIC on existing databases

Add a cluster version migration (V26_2_GrantTemporaryToPublic) that
grants the TEMPORARY privilege to the public role on all existing
databases. This ensures that existing databases match the behavior of
newly created databases where PUBLIC has TEMPORARY by default.

The migration iterates over all database descriptors and adds the
TEMPORARY privilege to the public role if it is not already present.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: ui: migrate StatementInsightDetails and TransactionInsightDetails to SWR
body:
The insight details pages currently fetch data via Redux sagas
(statement insights) or imperative useEffect calls (statement details)
and a Redux-dispatched refresh action (transaction details). This
requires boilerplate for actions, reducers, sagas, and selectors.

Add useStmtInsightDetails and useTxnInsightDetails SWR hooks colocated
with their API functions. Both hooks disable revalidateOnFocus and
revalidateOnReconnect since insight data is tied to a specific
execution ID and doesn't change.

-----

The statement insight details page passes insightEventDetails and
insightError as props from Redux state via connect(). The component
also has a fallback useEffect that directly calls getStmtInsightsApi
when the Redux state is empty, managing its own loading/error state
with useState. This results in two parallel data fetching paths.

Replace both with the useStmtInsightDetails SWR hook called directly
in the component. Convert the connected containers in cluster-ui and
db-console from connect(mapState, mapDispatch) to functional
components using useSelector/useDispatch. Remove insightEventDetails
and insightError from the component's props interface. Update tests
to mock the SWR hook instead of spying on the API function.

-----

The transaction insight details page uses a Redux-dispatched
refreshTransactionInsightDetails action with complex retry logic in a
useEffect: a useRef counter tracks fetch attempts up to
MAX_REQ_ATTEMPTS=3, and each render checks whether txnDetails,
statements, and contention info are complete to decide whether to
refetch with selective exclusions.

Replace this with the useTxnInsightDetails SWR hook. The old selective-retry
logic is removed; partial failures are not retried. Compute maxRequestsReached
from the response data completeness (matching the old stmtsComplete and
contentionComplete checks) so the overview tab still shows the "not all
statements displayed" disclaimer and stops loading spinners for
permanently-missing data.
Fix a latent bug in transactionInsightDetailsOverviewTab where statements.find()
would crash when statements is undefined — use optional chaining instead.
Convert connected containers to useSelector/useDispatch.

-----

With the statement and transaction insight details pages now using SWR
hooks for data fetching, the Redux infrastructure that previously
supported them is unused.

Delete statementInsights.selectors.ts and
transactionInsightDetails.selectors.ts. Remove the stmtInsights and
txnInsightDetails reducers from the cluster-ui store, their sagas from
the root saga, and the stmtInsights invalidation from the time scale
change saga. Remove selectStmtInsights from selectors/common.ts. Stop
re-exporting deleted sagas and selectors from index files. Remove
unused selectors from db-console's insightsSelectors.ts, retaining
only selectTxnInsightsByFingerprint and its dependency
selectTransactionInsights.

Delete the now-empty store/insightDetails/transactionInsightDetails/ and
store/insights/statementInsights/ directories, which contained the
disconnected reducer and saga files.

Epic: CRDB-58145
Release note: None
author: jasonlmfong

commits.message:
ui: add SWR hooks for statement and transaction insight details

The insight details pages currently fetch data via Redux sagas
(statement insights) or imperative useEffect calls (statement details)
and a Redux-dispatched refresh action (transaction details). This
requires boilerplate for actions, reducers, sagas, and selectors.

Add useStmtInsightDetails and useTxnInsightDetails SWR hooks colocated
with their API functions. Both hooks disable revalidateOnFocus and
revalidateOnReconnect since insight data is tied to a specific
execution ID and doesn't change.

Partof CRDB-61195
Epic: CRDB-58145
Release note: None

The statement insight details page passes insightEventDetails and
insightError as props from Redux state via connect(). The component
also has a fallback useEffect that directly calls getStmtInsightsApi
when the Redux state is empty, managing its own loading/error state
with useState. This results in two parallel data fetching paths.

Replace both with the useStmtInsightDetails SWR hook called directly
in the component. Convert the connected containers in cluster-ui and
db-console from connect(mapState, mapDispatch) to functional
components using useSelector/useDispatch. Remove insightEventDetails
and insightError from the component's props interface. Update tests
to mock the SWR hook instead of spying on the API function.

Partof CRDB-61195
Epic: CRDB-58145
Release note: None

ui: migrate TransactionInsightDetails to SWR

The transaction insight details page uses a Redux-dispatched
refreshTransactionInsightDetails action with complex retry logic in a
useEffect: a useRef counter tracks fetch attempts up to
MAX_REQ_ATTEMPTS=3, and each render checks whether txnDetails,
statements, and contention info are complete to decide whether to
refetch with selective exclusions.

Replace this with the useTxnInsightDetails SWR hook. The old selective-retry
logic is removed; partial failures are not retried. Compute maxRequestsReached
from the response data completeness (matching the old stmtsComplete and
contentionComplete checks) so the overview tab still shows the "not all
statements displayed" disclaimer and stops loading spinners for
permanently-missing data.
Fix a latent bug in transactionInsightDetailsOverviewTab where statements.find()
would crash when statements is undefined — use optional chaining instead.
Convert connected containers to useSelector/useDispatch.

Partof CRDB-61195
Epic: CRDB-58145
Release note: None

ui: remove dead insight selectors and reducers after SWR migration

With the statement and transaction insight details pages now using SWR
hooks for data fetching, the Redux infrastructure that previously
supported them is unused.

Delete statementInsights.selectors.ts and
transactionInsightDetails.selectors.ts. Remove the stmtInsights and
txnInsightDetails reducers from the cluster-ui store, their sagas from
the root saga, and the stmtInsights invalidation from the time scale
change saga. Remove selectStmtInsights from selectors/common.ts. Stop
re-exporting deleted sagas and selectors from index files. Remove
unused selectors from db-console's insightsSelectors.ts, retaining
only selectTxnInsightsByFingerprint and its dependency
selectTransactionInsights.

Delete the now-empty store/insightDetails/transactionInsightDetails/ and
store/insights/statementInsights/ directories, which contained the
disconnected reducer and saga files.

Part of CRDB-61195
Epic: CRDB-58145

title: storage: add delete to sorted disk map batch writer interface and implement it
body:
We want to use sorted disk map as a temporary storage for transactional
LDR, but SortedDiskMapBatchWriter does not currently support delete. 
We don't want to keep iterating over the same KVs that have been read,
nor do we want it to hog up the disk space, and deleting would solve these
issues.

Resolves: #165967
Release note: None
author: KeithCh

reviews.body: > @sumeerbhola made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165976)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on DrewKimball, jeffswenson, KeithCh, and RaduBerinde). ___ *[`pkg/storage/disk_map.go` line 262 at r4](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165976#-Oo5dDljDt_sGbEmTzbX:-Oo5dDljDt_sGbEmTzbY:b-xqwcz6) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/3dbc4f6ba84d2766cc9c3fcbf92fa8d62a8874e6/pkg/storage/disk_map.go#L262)):* > ```Go > func (b *pebbleMapBatchWriter) Delete(k []byte) error { > if b.makeSequenceStartKey != nil { > startKey := b.makeSequenceStartKey(k) > ``` I don't understand this `makeSequenceStartKey` use case. Can you elaborate? Also, why are we using a `Delete` method on a single key, which suggests a point delete, to do a rangedel? This seems error prone, and a rangedel deserves its own method.
LGTM

commits.message:
storage: add delete to sorted disk map batch writer interface and implement it

We want to use sorted disk map as a temporary storage for transactional
LDR, but SortedDiskMapBatchWriter does not currently support delete.
We don't want to keep iterating over the same KVs that have been read,
nor do we want it to hog up the disk space, and deleting would solve these
issues.

Resolves: #165967
Release note: None

title: kvserver: mv LastReplicaGCTimestamp write to apply time
body:
Previously, `LastReplicaGCTimestamp` was written to the state machine batch during split evaluations. This is inconvenient because it is an unreplicated key and belongs to the `LogEngine`. Move this write to command application time, and write the key to the right engine there.

Closes #157897

title: cli: fix flaky TestZipExcludeRangeInfo by stripping ASH report lines
body:
The ASH report profiler (added in 84fefdbfb33) writes ash_report files to the heap profile directory. When `debug zip` collects heap profiles, it picks up these files non-deterministically depending on whether a goroutine dump triggered an ASH report during the test.

Strip ash_report retrieval lines from the zip test output, following the same pattern used for goroutine_dump, cpuprof, and memprof lines.

Fixes #165940
author: arjunmahishi

reviews.body: LGTM

commits.message:
cli: fix flaky TestZipExcludeRangeInfo by stripping ASH report lines

The ASH report profiler (added in 84fefdbfb33) writes ash_report files
to the heap profile directory. When `debug zip` collects heap profiles,
it picks up these files non-deterministically depending on whether a
goroutine dump triggered an ASH report during the test.

Strip ash_report retrieval lines from the zip test output, following the
same pattern used for goroutine_dump, cpuprof, and memprof lines.

Fixes #165940

title: operations: fix crash in RLS cleanup and signal handling with piped o…
body:
The add-rls-policy cleanup runs in a background goroutine where o.Fatal() panics are not recovered by the runner's defer/recover, crashing the entire process. Use o.Error() instead.

When the operation runner is piped through tee (2>&1 | tee), Ctrl+C kills tee first, breaking the pipe and preventing cleanup from running. Trap SIGINT in the shell script so tee stays alive and cleanup output remains visible.

Operation cleanups can fail if the objects they try to drop were already removed (e.g. table dropped, role already revoked). Use IF EXISTS and CASCADE where appropriate so cleanups succeed even when the target objects no longer exist.

Epic: none
Release note: None
author: shailendra-patel

commits.message:
operations: fix crash in RLS cleanup and signal handling with piped output

The add-rls-policy cleanup runs in a background goroutine where
o.Fatal() panics are not recovered by the runner's defer/recover,
crashing the entire process. Use o.Error() instead.

When the operation runner is piped through tee (2>&1 | tee), Ctrl+C
kills tee first, breaking the pipe and preventing cleanup from running.
Trap SIGINT in the shell script so tee stays alive and cleanup output
remains visible.

Operation cleanups can fail if the objects they try to drop were already
removed (e.g. table dropped, role already revoked). Use IF EXISTS and
CASCADE where appropriate so cleanups succeed even when the target
objects no longer exist.

title: sql/tests: fix stale grammar rule names in TestRandomSyntaxSchemaChangeDatabase
body:
The `TestRandomSyntaxSchemaChangeDatabase` test was referencing grammar
rule names `create_user_stmt`, `drop_user_stmt`, and `alter_user_stmt`
that were renamed to `create_role_stmt`, `drop_role_stmt`, and
`alter_role_stmt` years ago. Since the RSG returns rule names as literal
strings when it can't find a matching production, these three roots
(50% of the test's roots) were generating unparseable SQL like the
literal text "create_user_stmt", wasting half of all test executions on
guaranteed failures.

Under heavy contention (8 goroutines, 5-minute runs in nightly CI),
this reduced the probability of any successful execution enough to
cause consistent `0 successful executions` failures.

Resolves: #165682

Release note: None

title: roachtest: disable post-import INSPECT for large-schema-benchmark
body:
The tpcc/large-schema-benchmark/multiregion=false/tables=40000 test was
failing because the automatic INSPECT job that runs after each IMPORT
was exhausting the memory budget. When importing many tables with high
concurrency, the INSPECT jobs collectively consume too much memory,
causing "memory budget exceeded" errors that prevent IMPORT jobs from
marking as succeeded.

The fix disables the automatic row count validation by setting
`bulkio.import.row_count_validation.mode = 'off'` in the test's cluster
configuration. This prevents the INSPECT job from running after each
IMPORT, avoiding the OOM condition. The row count validation is not
critical for this benchmark's purpose.

Resolves: #164104
Resolves: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/164102

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: workload/schemachange: expect DuplicateColumn error in mixed-version clusters
body:
In v26.1, the declarative schema changer errors with DuplicateColumn (SQLSTATE 42701) when a STORING column overlaps with a primary key column. In v26.2+, this was changed to emit a NOTICE and silently skip the column instead. During mixed-version upgrades from v26.1 to v26.2, the CREATE INDEX statement may be routed to a v26.1 node, producing the old error. The schemachange workload did not expect this error, causing the schemachange/mixed-versions roachtest to fail.

Add pgcode.DuplicateColumn as a potential exec error for createIndex when the cluster version is less than V26_2 and a STORING column is also a PK column.

Fixes: #165684

Release note: None
author: rafiss

In v26.1, the declarative schema changer errors with DuplicateColumn

the column instead. During mixed-version upgrades from v26.1 to v26.2,
the CREATE INDEX statement may be routed to a v26.1 node, producing the
old error. The schemachange workload did not expect this error, causing

title: sql: fix pg_index.indisready to be true for valid indexes
body:
Previously, pg_index.indisready was false for all fully-created
indexes because the logic `isMutation && isWriteOnly` only evaluated
to true for indexes in the write-only mutation state. In PostgreSQL,
indisready is true for all valid indexes; it is only false during
the very early phase of index creation before insert operations are
redirected to the new index.

This caused pg_dump to skip all indexes when dumping a CockroachDB
database, because pg_dump filters with `WHERE ... AND i.indisready`.

Fix the logic to `!isMutation || isWriteOnly` so that:
- Fully-created indexes: indisready = true (matching PostgreSQL)
- Write-only mutation indexes: indisready = true
- Delete-only mutation indexes: indisready = false

Informs: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/20296

Release note (bug fix): Fixed a bug where pg_index.indisready was
incorrectly set to false for all valid indexes. In PostgreSQL,
indisready is true for all fully-created indexes.

commits.message:
sql: fix pg_index.indisready to be true for valid indexes

Previously, pg_index.indisready was false for all fully-created
indexes because the logic `isMutation && isWriteOnly` only evaluated
to true for indexes in the write-only mutation state. In PostgreSQL,
indisready is true for all valid indexes; it is only false during
the very early phase of index creation before insert operations are
redirected to the new index.

This caused pg_dump to skip all indexes when dumping a CockroachDB
database, because pg_dump filters with `WHERE ... AND i.indisready`.

Fix the logic to `!isMutation || isWriteOnly` so that:
- Fully-created indexes: indisready = true (matching PostgreSQL)
- Write-only mutation indexes: indisready = true
- Delete-only mutation indexes: indisready = false

Informs: #20296

Release note (bug fix): Fixed a bug where pg_index.indisready was
incorrectly set to false for all valid indexes. In PostgreSQL,
indisready is true for all fully-created indexes.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

sql: replace hardcoded OIDs with name-based lookups in pg_catalog tests

Several pg_catalog logic tests used hardcoded OIDs in their expected
output (pg_class, pg_attribute, pg_index, pg_rewrite). These OIDs
shift whenever earlier tests create different numbers of objects,
causing spurious test failures unrelated to the code under test.

Replace OID columns with name-based lookups via JOINs to pg_class,
or with stable derived values (e.g. reltype != 0 AS has_reltype).

Informs: #20296

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: roachtest: fix DSC job-compat test for cross-db and version-gated DDLs
body:
The mixed-version job compatibility test for the declarative schema changer had several issues:

1. DDLs referencing functions with fully-qualified names (e.g. CREATE TRIGGER ... EXECUTE FUNCTION testdb.testsc.trg_fn()) failed with "cross-database function references not allowed" when the session database was defaultdb. Fix by switching to testdb before executing these DDLs and during cleanup.

2. CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION was run with the DSC enabled, but the DSC only supports it in v26.2. Fix by running it with DSC off (legacy schema changer) during setup.

3. DROP TRIGGER / CREATE TRIGGER were run with DSC off, but the legacy schema changer does not support them. Fix by running them after enabling the DSC.

4. CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER was in v253DDLs but was only implemented in v26.2. Fix by moving it to v262DDLs.

Fixes: #165507

Release note: None
author: rafiss

reviews.body: > @spilchen made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165868#-:-OnvY2h20v0THr1H0qWe:bnfp4nl)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on shghasemi).

commits.message:
roachtest: fix DSC job-compat test for cross-db and version-gated DDLs

The mixed-version job compatibility test for the declarative schema
changer had several issues:

1. DDLs referencing functions with fully-qualified names (e.g.
   CREATE TRIGGER ... EXECUTE FUNCTION testdb.testsc.trg_fn()) failed
   with "cross-database function references not allowed" when the
   session database was defaultdb. Fix by switching to testdb before
   executing these DDLs and during cleanup.

2. CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION was run with the DSC enabled, but the
   DSC only supports it in v26.2. Fix by running it with DSC off
   (legacy schema changer) during setup.

3. DROP TRIGGER / CREATE TRIGGER were run with DSC off, but the
   legacy schema changer does not support them. Fix by running them
   after enabling the DSC.

4. CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER was in v253DDLs but was only implemented
   in v26.2. Fix by moving it to v262DDLs.

Fixes: #165507

title: obs/ash: expose workload_type in ASH virtual tables
body:
Expose workload_type in the ASH tables and views

Part of: https://cockroachlabs.atlassian.net/browse/CRDB-60684
Release note: None
author: alyshanjahani-crl

commits.message:
obs/ash: expose workload_type in ASH virtual tables

The ASH sampler already tracks WorkloadType internally via the
WorkloadInfo struct, but this field was not surfaced in the ASHSample
struct, the protobuf message, or the virtual tables. Users had no way
to distinguish whether a sampled workload was a statement, job, system
task, or unknown type.

Thread workload_type through all layers: add the field to the Go
ASHSample struct, populate it during sampling from
WorkloadInfo.WorkloadType.String(), add a proto field (field 9), map
it in the status server RPC conversion, and add the column to both
crdb_internal.node_active_session_history and
crdb_internal.cluster_active_session_history as well as their
information_schema views.

Empty strings from old nodes during rolling upgrades are guarded to
"UNKNOWN" at the virtual table layer. No cluster version gate is
needed since this is an additive proto field and virtual table schema
change with no persisted state.

Release note (sql change): Added a `workload_type` column to the
`crdb_internal.node_active_session_history` and
`crdb_internal.cluster_active_session_history` virtual tables, as
well as the corresponding `information_schema` views. The column
exposes the type of workload being sampled, with possible values
"STATEMENT", "JOB", "SYSTEM", or "UNKNOWN".

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: opt: relax recently added requirement to `findExistingColInList`
body:
We just merged 12ad4b6316b97e56f812498a18e7c4cde8a18a20 which fixed overly-permissive check in `findExistingColInList`, but it looks like we made it too restrictive now - hibernate roachtest surfaced a query which started failing. This commit relaxes the requirement of Identical types to Equivalent.

Fixes: #165002.
Release note: None
author: yuzefovich

reviews.body: > @yuzefovich made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165860)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on DrewKimball). ___ *[`pkg/sql/opt/optbuilder/scope.go` line 625 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165860#-OnsjD4L9ByQhhqgzW84:-OnsjD4L9ByQhhqgzW85:b-adxjfb) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/c90d11ca12fdf2f3068fcf01fcc46e5c1038fe1d/pkg/sql/opt/optbuilder/scope.go#L625)):* > ```Go > return col > } > if col.typ.Equivalent(expr.ResolvedType()) && exprStr == col.getExprStr() { > ``` Drew, on the review of #165393, you mentioned adding a cast for equivalent but not identical type - how would I do that? Also, wouldn't it fail the reproduction?

This to fix regressions for now. > @DrewKimball reviewed 4 files and all commit messages, and made 2 comments. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165860#-:-OnzRv2w7d_IMMR0QaxN:ba1a20m)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on yuzefovich). ___ *[`pkg/sql/opt/optbuilder/scope.go` line 625 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165860#-OnsjD4L9ByQhhqgzW84:-OnzRy-pAVR4Zln0mQfe:b-wtkh3w) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/c90d11ca12fdf2f3068fcf01fcc46e5c1038fe1d/pkg/sql/opt/optbuilder/scope.go#L625)):*
Previously, yuzefovich (Yahor Yuzefovich) wrote…
Drew, on the review of #165393, you mentioned adding a cast for equivalent but not identical type - how would I do that? Also, wouldn't it fail the reproduction?
I did a little bit of digging. When all branches have the same type with the same width, Postgres uses that type and width. When one or more branches have a different width, Postgres uses an unspecified/infinite width. We have some logic to kind of do that when building CASE expressions in the opt builder: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/84b0611630e6d7c074470f1841f315a055769c16/pkg/sql/opt/optbuilder/scalar.go#L251 We should probably only be applying this when a branch has a different width, like Postgres, but that's another issue. I think the problem we're running into here is that we don't have the matching "any width" logic during type checking, and so the comparison at this line ends up failing the Identical() check because of the width. TL;DR: type-checker

Given that up until recently we only did a string comparison check, adding type equivalence requirement seems like a clear improvement. TFTR! /trunk merge > @yuzefovich made 2 comments. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165860#-:-Onza1mMBBYo71fgi-Q1:b-w24pbq)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (and 1 stale) (waiting on DrewKimball). ___ *[`pkg/sql/opt/optbuilder/scope.go` line 625 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165860#-OnsjD4L9ByQhhqgzW84:-Onz_xMDDvvn0UwdJa5Z:b-yj226e) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/c90d11ca12fdf2f3068fcf01fcc46e5c1038fe1d/pkg/sql/opt/optbuilder/scope.go#L625)):*
Previously, DrewKimball (Drew Kimball) wrote…
I did a little bit of digging. When all branches have the same type with the same width, Postgres uses that type and width. When one or more branches have a different width, Postgres uses an unspecified/infinite width. We have some logic to kind of do that when building CASE expressions in the opt builder: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/84b0611630e6d7c074470f1841f315a055769c16/pkg/sql/opt/optbuilder/scalar.go#L251 We should probably only be applying this when a branch has a different width, like Postgres, but that's another issue. I think the problem we're running into here is that we don't have the matching "any width" logic during type checking, and so the comparison at this line ends up failing the Identical() check because of the width. TL;DR: type-checker
Thanks for digging in! I left a TODO pointing at this PR for context.

We just merged 12ad4b6316b97e56f812498a18e7c4cde8a18a20 which fixed

title: roachtest: exclude lineitem from import/decommissioned test
body:
The import/decommissioned roachtest has been repeatedly timing out at
the 10-hour limit. The test decommissions one of 4 nodes then imports
a random TPC-H SF-100 dataset. When lineitem (the largest dataset) is
selected, the reduced cluster capacity (~3 active nodes) combined with
metamorphic encryption and expiration leases pushes the import +
fingerprint validation past the timeout.

Exclude lineitem from the dataset pool for this test, keeping the
remaining 5 datasets which complete comfortably within the time budget.

Resolves: #165786

Release note: None
author: mw5h

title: ui: `Canary vs Stable` Statement Times and Plan Distribution bar charts
body: Informs: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/156817 Fixes: #165850 Fixes: #150015 This PR introduce 2 charts that helps differentiate performance of canary and stable table stats for a statement. Both charts live under the `Statement Fingerprint` page, which can be accessed via the `SQL Activity` panel. ### 1. Canary vs Stable Statement Times Similar to the existing `Statement Times`, the new `Canary vs Stable Statement Times` dual-bar chart shows the aggregated planning and execution latency for the statement, but also differentiate the canary and stable exections. Supports the "hovering to show details" and "click legend to isolate" effect. ### 2. Canary vs Stable Plan Distribution Similar to the existing `Plan Distribution Over Time`, the `Canary vs Stable Plan Distribution` dual bar chart shows the aggregated plan distribution for the statement, but also differentiate the canary and stable exections. Also, the plan gist are colored according to their average execution time duration, with the longer the durtion, the darker the plan gist. Supports the "hovering to show details" and "click legend to isolate" effect. Release note (sql ops): Introduce "Canary vs Stable Statement Times" and "Canary vs Stable Plan Distribution" dual-bar charts in the dbconsole's statement fingerprint page, which only shows up when the statement is enrolled in canary stats rollout feature. title: sql: add pg_get_triggerdef builtin
body:
pg_dump calls pg_catalog.pg_get_triggerdef(oid, bool) when reading triggers, which previously failed with "unknown function". Add the builtin with both the 1-arg and 2-arg overloads, joining pg_trigger against crdb_internal.create_trigger_statements to produce the CREATE TRIGGER SQL. Also update pg_get_constraintdef's info string from the generic notUsableInfo placeholder to a real description.

Informs: #20296
