title: txnapply: prototype distributed applier
body:
This patch reworks the txn applier prototype such that multiple appliers can
run while maintaining the same dependent txn and event horizon guarantees.
Inter-applier coordination is now powered by a new DependencyResolver
interface. At a high level, when an applier is working on a txn that depends on
a txn getting applied by a different applier, the waiting applier will send a
Wait() request via the DependencyResolver. When the depending applier finishes
the txn, it will send a Ready message back to the waiting applier.

This approach reflects how we plan to set up a distributed sql flow: each node
will have a txn applier processor that can communicate with every node's
downstream DependencyResolver processor. Each DependencyResolver processor will
have a backchannel to send messages to the collocated txn applier processor.

Distributed appliers no longer track a global resolved time, and instead track
the resolved time of each applier in the flow. So, if an applier is attempting
to apply a txn with an event horizon that is lower than the resolved time of
another applier, the waiting applier will send a WaitHorizon message via the
dependency resolver. When the depending applier's resolved time advances, it
will send a Ready message back to the waiting applier.

Previously, an applier would advance its replicated time as it received
transactions to apply, but now that txn application is distributed, we could
have an idle applier that still needs to advance its resolved time. Thus, this
patch also introduces a new checkpoint event type, which contains the
timestamp-1 of the next txn sent to an applier. When an applier receives a
checkpoint, it can advance its own resolved time after it has applied all
transations older than the checkpoint.

An applier relies on the coordinator sending a checkpoint every time the
coordinator processes a txn whose event horizon exceeds the last checkpoint
timestamp sent. Also note that the coordinator will send the checkpoint to
every applier, and then send the incoming txn. The coordinator may send
intermediate checkpoints to advance the resolved time of an idle applier.

Epic: [CRDB-57649](https://cockroachlabs.atlassian.net/browse/CRDB-57649)

Release note: None
author: msbutler

reviews.body: ty for initial review!
LGTM

commits.message:
txnapply: prototype distributed applier

This patch reworks the txn applier prototype such that multiple appliers can
run while maintaining the same dependent txn and event horizon guarantees.
Inter-applier coordination is now powered by a new DependencyResolver
interface. At a high level, when an applier is working on a txn that depends on
a txn getting applied by a different applier, the waiting applier will send a
Wait() request via the DependencyResolver. When the depending applier finishes
the txn, it will send a Ready message back to the waiting applier.

This approach reflects how we plan to set up a distributed sql flow: each node
will have a txn applier processor that can communicate with every node's
downstream DependencyResolver processor. Each DependencyResolver processor will
have a backchannel to send messages to the collocated txn applier processor.

Distributed appliers no longer track a global resolved time, and instead track
the resolved time of each applier in the flow. So, if an applier is attempting
to apply a txn with an event horizon that is lower than the resolved time of
another applier, the waiting applier will send a WaitHorizon message via the
dependency resolver. When the depending applier's resolved time advances, it
will send a Ready message back to the waiting applier.

Previously, an applier would advance its replicated time as it received
transactions to apply, but now that txn application is distributed, we could
have an idle applier that still needs to advance its resolved time. Thus, this
patch also introduces a new checkpoint event type, which contains the
timestamp-1 of the next txn sent to an applier. When an applier receives a
checkpoint, it can advance its own resolved time after it has applied all
transations older than the checkpoint.

An applier relies on the coordinator sending a checkpoint every time the
coordinator processes a txn whose event horizon exceeds the last checkpoint
timestamp sent. Also note that the coordinator will send the checkpoint to
every applier, and then send the incoming txn. The coordinator may send
intermediate checkpoints to advance the resolved time of an idle applier.

Epic: CRDB-57649

title: ui: migrate statementInsightsView and transactionInsightsView from Redux to SWR
body:
The insights overview page fetches statement and transaction insights
data via Redux thunks, with manual polling managed by useScheduleFunction.

Add useStmtInsights and useTxnInsights SWR hooks that wrap the existing
API functions with automatic caching, deduplication, and polling. These
hooks are not consumed yet.

-----

StatementInsightsView receives its data, loading state, and refresh
function as props from a Redux-connected parent container. It manages
polling manually via useScheduleFunction.

Replace the data/loading/error props with the useStmtInsights SWR hook,
which handles fetching, caching, and polling internally. Remove the
corresponding props from the StateProps and DispatchProps types.

-----

TransactionInsightsView receives its data, loading state, and refresh
function as props from a Redux-connected parent container. It manages
polling manually via useScheduleFunction.

Replace the data/loading/error props with the useTxnInsights SWR hook,
which handles fetching, caching, and polling internally. Remove the
corresponding props from the StateProps and DispatchProps types.

-----

The workload insights page containers in both cluster-ui and db-console
use the connect() HOC with mapStateToProps/mapDispatchToProps to wire up
Redux state and dispatch to the child views.

Replace connect()/withRouter with functional components using
useDispatch/useSelector/useCallback. Since the child views now fetch their
data via SWR, the containers no longer pass data, loading, or refresh props.
Use separate per-tab handler functions to preserve correct analytics
page names for transaction vs statement tabs.

-----

The SWR migration in prior commits made several selectors and local
settings dead code:
- filtersLocalSetting and sortSettingLocalSetting in insightsSelectors.ts
  (db-console) — redefined locally in workloadInsightsPage.tsx, no
  remaining imports from insightsSelectors.
- selectInsightTypes in insightsSelectors.ts (db-console) and
  statementInsights.selectors.ts (cluster-ui) — redefined locally in
  each container component, no remaining imports.

Remove the dead exports and their now-unused imports (defaultFilters,
WorkloadInsightEventFilters, SortSetting, InsightEnumToLabel,
LocalSetting).

Epic: CRDB-58145
Release Note: None
author: jasonlmfong

title: roachtest: increase kv-rangefeed catchup test duration to 35m
body:
The kv-rangefeed catchup test was flaking because the changefeed's effective catch-up rate is ~75% of the theoretical rate (1,500 vs 2,000 events/sec), causing the catch-up to complete ~15 seconds after the 30-minute deadline. Increase the test duration from 30 to 35 minutes to provide comfortable margin.

Fixes: #165817

title: ci: handle issue_comment events in autosolve PR comment addresser
body:
The PR Comment Addresser workflows only triggered on
`pull_request_review_comment` and `pull_request_review` events. Regular
PR conversation comments (`issue_comment`) were silently ignored, so
feedback left as normal PR comments on autosolve PRs was never
addressed (e.g. #165721).

This adds `issue_comment` as a trigger event. Since the `issue_comment`
payload doesn't include PR head repo/ref metadata, the trigger workflow
fetches these via API and saves them as artifact files for the handler
to consume. The head repo check against the expected fork is enforced
in the trigger step to maintain the same security posture.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
author: ajstorm

title: backup: deflake besteffort getBackupFnTelemetry
body:
This besteffort block will occasionally cause a test flake when encountering a retryable error (such as a missing intent) because during tests, the besteffort utility throws a panic rather than logging the error. Under normal operation, ignoring these errors would cause downstream errors which would eventually propagate up to a retry loop. This patch adds a check on the error to see if it is retryable, and ignores the error if it is.

Fixes: #164922, #164505

Release note: None
author: andrew-r-thomas

This patch adds a check on the error to see if it is retryable, and

title: ui: fix TypeError crash in sumNodeStats when liveness data is undefined
body:
When PR #165430 moved sumNodeStats from db-console/src/redux/nodes.ts to cluster-ui/src/nodes/nodeSummaryStats.ts, the guard condition was rewritten from `isArray(nodeStatuses) && !isEmpty(livenessStatusByNodeID)` to `!nodeStatuses?.length || !Object.keys(livenessStatusByNodeID).length`.

These are not equivalent when `livenessStatusByNodeID` is undefined (before the liveness API responds): `isEmpty(undefined)` safely returns true, but `Object.keys(undefined)` throws a `TypeError`. This creates a race condition on the metrics page — if the nodes API response arrives before liveness, `nodeSumsSelector` recomputes and crashes, preventing the page from rendering. A browser refresh works because both responses are cached by then.

Add a null guard before the `Object.keys` call so the function returns the empty result when liveness data hasn't loaded yet.

Fixes #165771
Epic: None
Release Note: None
author: jasonlmfong

commits.message:
ui: fix TypeError crash in sumNodeStats when liveness data is undefined

When PR #165430 moved sumNodeStats from db-console/src/redux/nodes.ts
to cluster-ui/src/nodes/nodeSummaryStats.ts, the guard condition was
rewritten from `isArray(nodeStatuses) && !isEmpty(livenessStatusByNodeID)`
to `!nodeStatuses?.length || !Object.keys(livenessStatusByNodeID).length`.

These are not equivalent when livenessStatusByNodeID is undefined (before
the liveness API responds): isEmpty(undefined) safely returns true, but
Object.keys(undefined) throws a TypeError. This creates a race condition
on the metrics page — if the nodes API response arrives before liveness,
nodeSumsSelector recomputes and crashes, preventing the page from
rendering. A browser refresh works because both responses are cached by
then.

Add a null guard before the Object.keys call so the function returns
the empty result when liveness data hasn't loaded yet.

Fixes #165771
Epic: None
Release Note: None

title: kvstorage: relax StateEngine key assertion
body:
The assertion in the `StateEngine` only allowed `RangeTombstone` accessed as a point key. However, all unreplicated keys <= `RangeTombstone` are in the `StateEngine`. Replica destruction code makes a shortcut to take advantage of this (crosses from replicated to unreplicated keyspace in one op). Relax the assertion so that this is allowed.

Resolves #164351
author: pav-kv

reviews.body: It makes sense to change the validation; are there other places in the code that assign these keys to either engine? > @miraradeva reviewed all commit messages and made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165818#-:-OnrvgYhACif5wtL7lN2:b-o74hcm)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained.

commits.message:
kvstorage: relax StateEngine key assertion

The assertion in the StateEngine only allowed RangeTombstone accessed as
a point key. However, all unreplicated keys <= RangeTombstone are in the
StateEngine. Replica destruction code makes a shortcut to take advantage
of this (crosses from replicated to unreplicated keyspace in one op).
Relax the assertion so that this is allowed.

Epic: none
Release: none

title: roachtest: disable SST batcher elastic control in bulkingest
body:
The elastic=false variant of the bulkingest roachtest was only disabling two of the three elastic CPU control settings relevant to index backfills. While bulkio.index_backfill.elastic_control.enabled and kvadmission.elastic_control_bulk_low_priority.enabled were disabled, the SST batcher's own pacer (bulkio.ingest.sst_batcher_elastic_control.enabled) remained active. Since every index entry flows through the BulkAdder into the SSTBatcher, this meant the bulk of the elastic CPU pacing was still in effect even with elastic=false.

Closes #165339

Release note: None
Epic: None

title: sql: enable write buffering for transactions at weak isolation levels in tests
body:
Make write buffering for weak isolation levels (READ COMMITTED,
REPEATABLE READ) a metamorphic test setting that defaults to false in
production. This enables broader test coverage of write buffering
under weak isolation without changing production behavior.

Epic: none
Release note: None

---

See other commits for some test tweaks and additions. Most commits come from @stevendanna's branch [ssd/bw-weak-iso-test-compat](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/compare/master...stevendanna:cockroach:ssd/bw-weak-iso-test-compat).
author: miraradeva

reviews.body: > @yuzefovich reviewed 8 files and all commit messages, and made 2 comments. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165811#-:-OnrXg5G-_DuJCdhKw3r:bnfp4nl)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on DrewKimball, miraradeva, and stevendanna). ___ *[`-- commits` line 2 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165811#-OnrWW2L8W1YSkkHRHfl:-OnrWW2L8W1YSkkHRHfm:biyd16i):* nit: I'd explicitly mention "in tests" in the commit title.

commits.message:
sql: enable write buffering for transactions at weak isolation levels in tests

Make write buffering for weak isolation levels (READ COMMITTED,
REPEATABLE READ) a metamorphic test setting that defaults to false in
production. This enables broader test coverage of write buffering
under weak isolation without changing production behavior.

Epic: none
Release note: None

sql: fix TestReadCommittedStmtRetry for write buffering

With write buffering enabled for weak isolation levels, KV write
requests (Puts) are decomposed into locking Get requests (to acquire
Exclusive locks) and buffered writes (sent at statement/commit end).
The test's request filter was only intercepting Put requests, causing
it to deadlock when write buffering transformed them.

Update the filter to also intercept locking Get requests with Exclusive
strength, which are the first write-like operations sent under write
buffering. This preserves the test's core assertion: the RC transaction
encounters a WriteTooOldError on key "c" and successfully retries the
statement.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

sql: fix cluster_locks test for write buffering at weak isolation

The cluster_locks test runs under local-read-committed. The root and
testuser connections explicitly disable write buffering, but testuser2
did not, making it sensitive to the default. Add explicit session
variable settings for testuser2 to match the other users, ensuring
the test works regardless of the write buffering default.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

sql: fix select_for_share test for write buffering at weak isolation

The select_for_share test validates FOR SHARE locking semantics:
multiple transactions can hold shared locks, writers block, and
SKIP LOCKED works. It uses crdb_internal.cluster_locks queries as
verification scaffolding, which are sensitive to lock durability
and visibility changes under write buffering.

Explicitly disable write buffering for all users in the test so
that the scaffolding assertions remain stable regardless of the
write buffering default. Lock visibility under write buffering
is covered in a follow-up select_for_share_write_buffering test.

Add a write-buffering variant of the select_for_share test that runs
under weak-iso-level-configs with write buffering and Get/Scan
transforms explicitly enabled. This verifies that FOR SHARE locking
semantics work correctly when write buffering transforms Replicated
locks to Unreplicated, changing their visibility in
crdb_internal.cluster_locks (Unreplicated locks are always visible,
not only during contention).

With write buffering enabled for weak isolation levels, Exclusive locks
acquired by RC and RR transactions have Unreplicated durability instead
of Replicated. Update the expected output in the cross-isolation-level
contention section of the test to reflect this.

title: backup: close restore task channels in runRestore
body:
The channels used to signal between tasks in the restore ctxgroup were
closed by the functions runRestore called (distRestore, sendAddRemoteSSTs)
rather than by runRestore itself. Since runRestore now has multiple
execution paths with early returns — notably the online restore pre-split
path that bypasses distRestore entirely — the channels could be left open,
hanging the consumer goroutines forever.

Move the closes into runRestore so they run regardless of which path is taken.

Fixes #165431

Release note: None
author: dt

commits.message:
backup: close restore task channels in runRestore

runRestore is ultimately responsible for closing the channels that
signal the other tasks in the ctxgroup, but it has multiple possible
execution paths and returns, including early returns that bypass
distRestore. Move the channel closes into runRestore itself via defers
instead of delegating to functions it calls, so the channels are
reliably closed regardless of which path is taken.

Fixes #165431

Release note: None

title: kvserver: add timeout to mma change application
body:
Neither `AdminTransferLease` nor `changeReplicasImpl` enforces an
internal timeout. `changeReplicasImpl` can block waiting for a
snapshot send semaphore permit in `throttleSnapshot`.
`AdminTransferLease` is mostly bounded (~6s retry cap on transfer
rejections), but its outer loop waiting on in-progress lease
extensions has no retry limit and can theoretically cycle indefinitely.

This change wraps both `applyLeaseTransfer` and `applyReplicaChanges`
with `timeutil.RunWithTimeout`, using the same
`makeRateLimitedTimeoutFunc(rebalanceSnapshotRate)` timeout as the
replicate queue and the old store rebalancer.

Note that the same rate-limited formula is used for both lease transfers
and replica changes. This is generous for lease transfers, but
acceptable since the goal is to prevent indefinite blocking rather than
enforce a tight deadline.

Epic: none
Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

commits.message:
kvserver: add timeout to mma change application

Neither `AdminTransferLease` nor `changeReplicasImpl` enforces an
internal timeout. `changeReplicasImpl` can block waiting for a
snapshot send semaphore permit in `throttleSnapshot`.
`AdminTransferLease` is mostly bounded (~6s retry cap on transfer
rejections), but its outer loop waiting on in-progress lease
extensions has no retry limit and can theoretically cycle indefinitely.

This change wraps both `applyLeaseTransfer` and `applyReplicaChanges`
with `timeutil.RunWithTimeout`, using the same
`makeRateLimitedTimeoutFunc(rebalanceSnapshotRate)` timeout as the
replicate queue and the old store rebalancer.

Note that the same rate-limited formula is used for both lease transfers
and replica changes. This is generous for lease transfers, but
acceptable since the goal is to prevent indefinite blocking rather than
enforce a tight deadline.

Epic: none
Release note: None

title: pgwire: use HashString for usernames in auth log calls
body:
Convert usernames in authentication log calls to use redact.HashString() instead of passing them as plain unsafe format arguments. When hash-based redaction is enabled (via log config), these values now produce deterministic 8-character hex hashes instead of being fully redacted to ×, enabling cross-entry correlation in support diagnostics without exposing the actual data.

Also adds TestHashRedactionPerSink which verifies that hash-based redaction respects per-sink settings: a sink with redact=true hashes HashString values while a sink with redact=false shows cleartext.

Epic: CRDB-47199

Release note (ops change): When hash-based redaction is enabled in the logging configuration, usernames in authentication logs now produce deterministic hashes instead of being fully redacted. This allows support engineers to correlate the same user across multiple log entries without seeing the actual values.
author: visheshbardia

reviews.body: Thanks for working on this! The `Warningf` changes are a good start, but I think there's a bigger opportunity here. The 5 `Warningf` call sites are only part of the picture. Every call to `ac.LogAuthOK`, `ac.LogAuthFailed`, and `ac.LogAuthInfof` emits a structured event containing `CommonSessionDetails.User` and `CommonSessionDetails.SystemIdentity` as plain strings. In the proto definition, these fields are not marked as safe-from-redaction: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/1f9236d3e11157184d058403e8bde3ce211bbce7/pkg/util/log/eventpb/session_events.proto#L54-L62 That means in the generated JSON encoder ([`json_encode_generated.go:2349-2370`](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/master/pkg/util/log/eventpb/json_encode_generated.go#L2349-L2370)), these fields are wrapped with `StartMarker()`/`EndMarker()`, so they get fully redacted to `×` in redacted sinks. Similarly, `CommonConnectionDetails.RemoteAddress` is treated as sensitive. If we centralize the hashing at the proto field level, we can fix all of these structured auth events at once, We need that in addition to the individual log call sites you fixed. See inline comment for the suggested approach. > @rafiss made 5 comments. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165804#-:-OnrHph67wYp4KjFh-eH:b-21sz5q)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on aa-joshi, angles-n-daemons, and visheshbardia). ___ *[`pkg/sql/pgwire/auth.go` line 148 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165804#-OnrIb-T7m1_9I1fe6-v:-OnrIb-T7m1_9I1fe6-w:b-pkj744) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/88f772690f64c6e8606d0cdef793c6509b6681d6/pkg/sql/pgwire/auth.go#L148)):* > ```Go > matchedIdentity, err := c.checkClientUsernameMatchesMapping(ctx, ac, behaviors, systemIdentity) > if err != nil { > log.Dev.Warningf(ctx, "unable to map incoming identity %q, or san identities %q to any database user: %+v", systemIdentity

Using hashing for the common fields would make this PR much more valuable for improving the debugging experience and would be worthwhile to include in the 26.2 release. Can you explain more about why you don't want to add it now? It's only about a 20 line code change.

I made a draft version of the change here: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pull/165962/changes/1634179793c378f39164381654e1f933d2b53681

great work on this! this will be really useful for improving the debugging/support experience > @rafiss made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165804#-:-Onwamq11yolvwWN3kq5:b-ba7hz4)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on aa-joshi, angles-n-daemons, and visheshbardia).

Also adds TestHashRedactionPerSink which verifies that hash-based

Epic: CRDB-47199

title: cli,server: add --exclude-log-severities flag to debug zip
body:
Add an `--exclude-log-severities` flag to `debug zip` that filters out
log entries by severity server-side. For large clusters, INFO-level
logs can be extremely voluminous and often aren't needed for debugging.
This flag allows operators to exclude specific severity levels
(e.g. `--exclude-log-severities=INFO`) to reduce both network transfer
and zip file size.

The implementation adds a new `exclude_severities` repeated field to
the `LogFileRequest` protobuf message. The `LogFile()` RPC handler
builds an exclusion set and skips matching entries during the decode
loop. On the CLI side, severity names are validated early in
`runDebugZip` and converted to proto values before being passed in
each per-node `LogFileRequest`.

Rolling upgrade compatibility is maintained without a version gate:
old servers ignore the unknown protobuf field (graceful degradation),
and old clients send an empty field (no filtering).

Epic: none
Fixes: CRDB-61602

Release note (cli change): Added `--exclude-log-severities` flag to
`cockroach debug zip` that filters log entries by severity server-side.
For example, `--exclude-log-severities=INFO` excludes all INFO-level log
entries from the collected log files, which can significantly reduce
zip file size for large clusters. Valid severity names are INFO,
WARNING, ERROR, and FATAL. The flag accepts a comma-delimited list or
can be specified multiple times.

title: changefeedccl: fix sink not closed when newEventConsumer fails in Start
body:
Previously, in changeAggregator.Start(), the call to newEventConsumer used a multi-value assignment directly into ca.eventConsumer and ca.sink:

    ca.eventConsumer, ca.sink, err = newEventConsumer(...)

When newEventConsumer returned an error, it also returned nil for the sink, which overwrote ca.sink with nil. This meant that when the changeAggregator's close() method ran, the nil check `if ca.sink != nil` would skip closing the sink. The underlying sink (which had already been dialed/connected at line 379) was never closed, leaking the goroutine from the gRPC connection — specifically the fakePubsubServer.Dial goroutine in tests.

This was most visible in enriched envelope tests because creating the enriched event consumer can fail under stress (due to descriptor lookups), and only pubsub's mock sink actually requires Close to prevent goroutine leaks.

The fix uses temporary variables so that ca.sink and ca.eventConsumer are only assigned on the success path, preserving the original sink reference for proper cleanup on error.

Also removes the pubsub skip workaround that was added to maybeForceEnrichedEnvelope in helpers_test.go.

Resolves: #144102
Fixes: #144102                                                                                                                                     
Fixes: #144354                                                                                                                                     
Fixes: #144587                                                                                                                                     
Fixes: #145442
Fixes: #146436
Fixes: #150396
Fixes: #155215
Fixes: #155548
Fixes: #159329
Fixes: #159720
Fixes: #163886
Fixes: #165788
Fixes: #158035 
Fixes: #152378

Release note: Fixes a rare, minimal goroutine leak in changefeeds.

Generated by Claude Code Auto-Solver
author: rharding6373

reviews.body:
This is awesome that Claude could resolve this easily and separate the signal from the noise! I remember getting confused by this when we first tried to address it. Exciting to close like 15 issues in one go. 

Point 0: It seems like this PR actually fixes 3 more test failures: #165788, #158035, #152378.

Since this was an issue solved by Claude, I thought it would be only fair to address my points to it first. I think these question are more concise versions of my questions and the answers all square with what I would have thought. Bolding is mine to highlight important points as TL;DR. 

1) Can we write a test to verify this failure mode?

**Yes, and it would be straightforward**. You'd inject an error into the newEventConsumer path (e.g., via a testing knob that forces GetTableSchemaInfo or
  newEnrichedSourceData to fail), then assert that the sink is still properly closed. The test would:
  - Set up a pubsub changefeed with a mock sink that tracks whether Close() was called
  - Inject a failure in newEventConsumer (the enriched envelope path at event_processing.go:110-118 is a natural place)
  - Assert that close() still calls sink.Close()
  - Optionally check for goroutine leaks via leaktest

  **This would be a valuable regression test.** Without it, this exact bug pattern could easily recur if someone refactors the Start() method — the  multi-value-assignment-overwrites-on-error footgun is subtle.

2) Will the test continue to flake with a different failure?

  The answer depends on why newEventConsumer is returning an error. Looking at the code, in enriched envelope mode it calls GetTableSchemaInfo  (event_processing.go:110) and newEnrichedSourceData (event_processing.go:115), which do descriptor lookups. Under stress, those can transiently fail.

  If the descriptor lookup failure is itself a legitimate transient error that would cause the changefeed to retry/restart, then yes — the test could  still flake, but it would fail with a pro
Thanks for this, so exciting to close 15 (!!!) issues

commits.message:
changefeedccl: fix sink not closed when newEventConsumer fails in Start

Previously, in changeAggregator.Start(), the call to newEventConsumer
used a multi-value assignment directly into ca.eventConsumer and ca.sink:

    ca.eventConsumer, ca.sink, err = newEventConsumer(...)

When newEventConsumer returned an error, it also returned nil for the
sink, which overwrote ca.sink with nil. This meant that when the
changeAggregator's close() method ran, the nil check `if ca.sink != nil`
would skip closing the sink. The underlying sink (which had already been
dialed/connected at line 379) was never closed, leaking the goroutine
from the gRPC connection — specifically the fakePubsubServer.Dial
goroutine in tests.

This was most visible in enriched envelope tests because creating the
enriched event consumer can fail under stress (due to descriptor lookups),
and only pubsub's mock sink actually requires Close to prevent goroutine
leaks.

The fix uses temporary variables so that ca.sink and ca.eventConsumer are
only assigned on the success path, preserving the original sink reference
for proper cleanup on error.

Also removes the pubsub skip workaround that was added to
maybeForceEnrichedEnvelope in helpers_test.go.

Resolves: #144102

Release note: None

title: sql: add aclitem type, implement acldefault and makeaclitem, enhance aclexplode
body:
Add a proper `aclitem` type (StringFamily with OID 1033, following the same pattern as `name`) and implement three PostgreSQL-compatible ACL functions:

- `makeaclitem(grantee oid, grantor oid, privileges text, is_grantable bool) → aclitem`: constructs an aclitem string from its components.
- `acldefault(type text, ownerId oid) → aclitem[]`: returns the hard-wired default ACL for a given object type, matching PostgreSQL's defaults for all 13 object type characters.
- `aclexplode(aclitems) → setof record`: previously a stub that always returned no rows. Now parses ACL strings and emits (grantor, grantee, privilege_type, is_grantable) rows. Supports both `text[]` and `aclitem[]` input.

These functions are needed for pg-compatibility with ORMs and tools that inspect PostgreSQL access control lists.

Resolves: #91070
informs: #20296 

Release note (sql change): Added support for the `aclitem` type and the `makeaclitem` and `acldefault` built-in functions for PostgreSQL compatibility. The existing `aclexplode` function, which previously always returned no rows, now correctly parses ACL strings and returns the individual privilege grants they contain.

commits.message:
sql: add aclitem type, implement acldefault and makeaclitem, enhance aclexplode

Add a proper `aclitem` type (StringFamily with OID 1033, following the
same pattern as `name`) and implement three PostgreSQL-compatible ACL
functions:

- `makeaclitem(grantee oid, grantor oid, privileges text, is_grantable
  bool) → aclitem`: constructs an aclitem string from its components.
- `acldefault(type text, ownerId oid) → aclitem[]`: returns the
  hard-wired default ACL for a given object type, matching PostgreSQL's
  defaults for all 13 object type characters.
- `aclexplode(aclitems) → setof record`: previously a stub that always
  returned no rows. Now parses ACL strings and emits (grantor, grantee,
  privilege_type, is_grantable) rows. Supports both `text[]` and
  `aclitem[]` input.

These functions are needed for pg-compatibility with ORMs and tools that
inspect PostgreSQL access control lists.

Role names containing special characters (hyphens, spaces, etc.) are
properly quoted with double quotes in aclitem output via
QuoteACLIdentifier, matching PostgreSQL's putid() behavior. The
aclexplode function correctly unquotes these identifiers when parsing,
and caches role OID lookups to avoid repeated queries.

The aclitem type is integrated into the row encoding (keyside/valueside),
vectorized execution (vec_to_datum, cast), and DatumAlloc infrastructure.
Array containment (@>) works for aclitem arrays. Unterminated quoted
identifiers in aclitem strings are now properly rejected during
validation.

Resolves: #91070

Release note (sql change): Added support for the `aclitem` type and the
`makeaclitem` and `acldefault` built-in functions for PostgreSQL
compatibility. The existing `aclexplode` function, which previously
always returned no rows, now correctly parses ACL strings and returns
the individual privilege grants they contain.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

sql/privilege: fix missing SET and ALTER SYSTEM ACL mappings, add aclitem validation

The ACLCharToPrivName and PrivNameToACLChar maps were missing entries
for 's' (SET) and 'A' (ALTER SYSTEM). This caused two bugs:

1. aclexplode silently dropped SET and ALTER SYSTEM privileges, so
   aclexplode(acldefault('p', oid)) returned 0 rows instead of 2.
2. makeaclitem rejected SET and ALTER SYSTEM as unrecognized privilege
   types.

Fix by adding the missing entries. Also consolidate the three redundant
ACL character mapping tables (privToACL, ACLCharToPrivName,
PrivNameToACLChar) so that ACLCharToPrivName is the single source of
truth and the other two are derived from it in init().

Additionally, add validation to NewDACLItemFromDString so that malformed
aclitem strings are rejected at construction time rather than silently
accepted. Add an aclitem case to StrVal.ResolveAsType for string literal
resolution. Remove the now-redundant validation in eval/cast.go.

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

from prompting on select operations. If it conflicts with a user's
existing, unmerged settings those likely can be added to these project
settings or moved to the local scope (`.claude/settings.local.json`)
where they will take precedence.

title: ttljob: flush progress before job completion to fix lost row counts
body:
The checkpointProgressTracker relied solely on periodic background flushes and the Drained=true metadata flush to persist JobDeletedRowCount to the job record. If the job completed before a periodic flush fired (e.g., job finishes in ~12s with a 30s flush interval) and the Drained=true flush failed or was lost in the DistSQL plumbing, the job record would retain JobDeletedRowCount=0 despite all rows being deleted.

Fix this with two changes:

1. Add flushFinalProgress to the progressTracker interface, called in Resume() after g.Wait() returns. This guarantees the final in-memory cache (with the correct deleted row count) is written to the job record before Resume() returns and the job framework marks it succeeded.

2. Perform an initial flush when the periodic update goroutines start, so a valid baseline RowLevelTTLProgress is always persisted in the job record even if the job fails early.

Fixes #164446
author: fqazi

commits.message:
ttljob: flush progress before job completion to fix lost row counts

The checkpointProgressTracker relied solely on periodic background
flushes and the Drained=true metadata flush to persist JobDeletedRowCount
to the job record. If the job completed before a periodic flush fired
(e.g., job finishes in ~12s with a 30s flush interval) and the
Drained=true flush failed or was lost in the DistSQL plumbing, the job
record would retain JobDeletedRowCount=0 despite all rows being deleted.

Fix this with two changes:

1. Add flushFinalProgress to the progressTracker interface, called in
   Resume() after g.Wait() returns. This guarantees the final in-memory
   cache (with the correct deleted row count) is written to the job
   record before Resume() returns and the job framework marks it
   succeeded.

2. Perform an initial flush when the periodic update goroutines start,
   so a valid baseline RowLevelTTLProgress is always persisted in the
   job record even if the job fails early.

Fixes #164446

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: obs/ash: introduce WorkloadType for ASH workload attribution
body:
The ASH sampler currently always hex-encodes workload IDs, treating them as statement fingerprint IDs. This doesn't work for job IDs or system task IDs, which need different encodings (decimal and name string, respectively). This commit introduces a WorkloadType to distinguish the kind of workload a WorkloadID represents, enabling type-aware encoding throughout the stack.

Specifically this commit:

- Defines WorkloadType (uint8) in the workloadid package with constants WorkloadTypeUnknown, WorkloadTypeStatement, WorkloadTypeJob, and WorkloadTypeSystem.
- Adds a Name() method to WorkloadID for system task name encoding.
- Adds a workload_type field to the BatchRequest proto header and the DistSQL EvalContext proto.
- Plumbs WorkloadType through Txn.SetWorkloadInfo, eval.Context, ash.WorkloadInfo, and admission.WorkInfo.
- Updates the ASH sampler to use a composite (id, type) cache key and choose encoding based on WorkloadType: hex for statements, decimal for jobs, name string for system tasks.
- Updates all existing callers to set WorkloadTypeStatement and forward WorkloadType from proto headers through the KV and DistSQL stacks.

Epic: none
Release note: None
author: alyshanjahani-crl

reviews.body: No blocking comments, just testing, docs, nits. Feel free to merge once it's updated.

title: ui: migrate DataDistribution page from Redux to SWR
body:
Replace Redux-connected data fetching with SWR hooks for the data-distribution page. This is the only consumer of the dataDistribution reducer, so the reducer and its associated API function are deleted entirely.

- Create useDataDistribution SWR hook in cluster-ui wrapping the _admin/v1/data_distribution endpoint with a 1-minute deduping interval
- Replace connect/mapStateToProps/mapDispatchToProps with useNodesSummary() and useDataDistribution() hooks
- Compute locality tree and sorted zone configs via useMemo instead of reselect createSelector
- Remove dataDistributionReducerObj, refreshDataDistribution, and the dataDistribution field from APIReducersState
- Remove getDataDistribution and DataDistributionResponseMessage from db-console's api.ts

Epic: CRDB-48152

Release note: None
author: kyle-a-wong

title: ui: fix transaction insights max API size selector
body:
`selectTransactionInsightsMaxApiReached` was reading from `state.adminUI.stmtInsights` (statement insights) instead of `state.adminUI.txnInsights` (transaction insights). This meant the "not all results are shown" warning was never displayed on the transaction insights page, even when the result set exceeded the max API response size.

Fixes the selector to read from the correct store slice.

Epic: None
Release note: None
author: jasonlmfong

title: obs/ash: add system task workload IDs for internal KV operations
body:
Internal KV operations (heartbeats, intent resolution, lease
acquisition, etc.) create BatchRequest objects without WorkloadID,
producing gaps in ASH samples. This change adds system-level workload
ID constants and instruments internal callsites so that these
operations are attributable.

Specifically:

- Add 11 new WorkloadID constants (IDs 5-15) in pkg/obs/workloadid
  for NODE_LIVENESS, SQL_LIVENESS, TIMESERIES, RAFT_LOG_TRUNCATION,
  TXN_HEARTBEAT, INTENT_RESOLUTION, LEASE_ACQUISITION, MERGE_QUEUE,
  CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PROBE, GC, and RANGEFEED, along with corresponding
  name constants.

- Set WorkloadID on BatchRequests/transactions in: node liveness
  heartbeat, SQL liveness heartbeat, time series poller, raft log
  truncation, transaction heartbeat, intent resolution (push txn +
  batcher configs), lease acquisition, merge queue, and circuit
  breaker probes.

- Add DefaultWorkloadID field to RequestBatcher Config so batched
  operations (GC, intent resolution) propagate workload attribution.

Release note: None

commits.message:
obs/ash: add system task workload IDs for internal KV operations

Internal KV operations (heartbeats, intent resolution, lease
acquisition, etc.) create BatchRequest objects without WorkloadID,
producing gaps in ASH samples. This change adds system-level workload
ID constants and instruments internal callsites so that these
operations are attributable.

Specifically:

- Add 11 new WorkloadID constants (IDs 5-15) in pkg/obs/workloadid
  for NODE_LIVENESS, SQL_LIVENESS, TIMESERIES, RAFT_LOG_TRUNCATION,
  TXN_HEARTBEAT, INTENT_RESOLUTION, LEASE_ACQUISITION, MERGE_QUEUE,
  CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PROBE, GC, and RANGEFEED, along with corresponding
  name constants.

- Set WorkloadID on BatchRequests/transactions in: node liveness
  heartbeat, SQL liveness heartbeat, time series poller, raft log
  truncation, transaction heartbeat, intent resolution (push txn +
  batcher configs), lease acquisition, merge queue, and circuit
  breaker probes.

- Add DefaultWorkloadID field to RequestBatcher Config so batched
  operations (GC, intent resolution) propagate workload attribution.

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

Sets `DevelopmentBranch = false` and updates `version.txt` to `v26.2.0-beta.1` in preparation for the 26.2 beta release.

**⚠️ Do not merge into master.** This PR is drafted against `master` so it can be reviewed before `release-26.2` is cut. Once that branch exists, the base will be re-pointed:
```
gh pr edit <PR> --base release-26.2 --repo cockroachdb/cockroach

- `pkg/clusterversion/cockroach_versions.go` — `DevelopmentBranch = false`
- `pkg/build/version.txt` — `v26.2.0-alpha.2` → `v26.2.0-beta.1`

title: ui: import job profiler API functions directly
body:
Both the cluster-ui connected component and the db-console wrapper passed the same API functions (listExecutionDetailFiles, collectExecutionDetails, getExecutionDetailFile) as props.

Since there's only one implementation, this change imports them directly in JobProfilerView and removes the props.

this simplifies JobDetailsPageConnected and the db-console wrapper to re-exports, and updates tests to use jest.spyOn on the API modules instead of passing mock functions as props.

Epic: None
Release note: None

title: sql: support tableoid system column on virtual tables
body:
Previously, the `tableoid` system column was only supported on regular
tables but not on virtual tables (pg_catalog, information_schema, etc.).
This caused `pg_dump` to fail when connecting to CockroachDB because
pg_dump queries `SELECT x.tableoid, ... FROM pg_extension x ...` as
one of its first introspection queries.

This commit adds `tableoid` support for virtual tables by:

1. Adding the `tableoid` column to `optVirtualTable`'s column list in
   the optimizer catalog, registered as a `cat.System` column (hidden
   by default, like on regular tables).

2. Handling the `tableoid` column in `constructVirtualScan` by rendering
   it as a constant value (the table's descriptor ID) after the virtual
   table scan, since virtual table generators don't produce system
   column values.

3. Including the `tableoid` system column in all indexes' `numCols`
   (both primary and secondary) so the optimizer considers every index
   as covering for tableoid queries. The `Column()` method on
   `optVirtualIndex` is updated to return the tableoid column after all
   stored columns for secondary indexes.

informs https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/20296

Release note (sql change): The `tableoid` system column is now
supported on virtual tables such as those in `pg_catalog` and
`information_schema`. This improves compatibility with PostgreSQL
tools like `pg_dump` that reference `tableoid` in their introspection
queries.

commits.message:
sql: support tableoid system column on virtual tables

Previously, the `tableoid` system column was only supported on regular
tables but not on virtual tables (pg_catalog, information_schema, etc.).
This caused `pg_dump` to fail when connecting to CockroachDB because
pg_dump queries `SELECT x.tableoid, ... FROM pg_extension x ...` as
one of its first introspection queries.

This commit adds `tableoid` support for virtual tables by:

1. Adding the `tableoid` column to `optVirtualTable`'s column list in
   the optimizer catalog, registered as a `cat.System` column (hidden
   by default, like on regular tables).

2. Handling the `tableoid` column in `constructVirtualScan` by rendering
   it as a constant value (the table's descriptor ID) after the virtual
   table scan, since virtual table generators don't produce system
   column values.

3. Including the `tableoid` system column in all indexes' `numCols`
   (both primary and secondary) so the optimizer considers every index
   as covering for tableoid queries. The `Column()` method on
   `optVirtualIndex` is updated to return the tableoid column after all
   stored columns for secondary indexes.

Release note (sql change): The `tableoid` system column is now
supported on virtual tables such as those in `pg_catalog` and
`information_schema`. This improves compatibility with PostgreSQL
tools like `pg_dump` that reference `tableoid` in their introspection
queries.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

sql: fix panic in virtual table lookup join with tableoid

When pg_dump queries pg_attrdef with a join like:
  SELECT a.tableoid, ... FROM unnest('{...}'::oid[]) AS src(tbloid)
  JOIN pg_catalog.pg_attrdef a ON (src.tbloid = a.adrelid)
the virtual table lookup join panics with "index out of range [5]
with length 5". This happens because pushRow iterates lookupCols
which includes the tableoid system column ordinal, but the
looked-up row from the virtual table generator only contains
public columns.

Fix this by detecting the tableoid ordinal in pushRow and producing
its value as a constant from the table descriptor, matching how
constructVirtualScan handles tableoid for regular virtual table
scans.

Informs: #20296

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: kvserver,storage: prefer distinct txns in VIR pre-scan after condensing
body:
**concurrency: fix VIR livelock by persisting toResolve across scans**

Previously, the lock table guard's `toResolve` list of point intent resolutions was cleared on every re-entry to `ScanAndEnqueue`, including for VIR (virtual intent resolution) guards. This caused a livelock: the guard would discover intents, resolve them virtually during evaluation, hit a LockConflictError, re-enter `ScanAndEnqueue` (which cleared `toResolve`), and then rediscover the same intents — looping indefinitely without progress.

This commit fixes the livelock by:
1. Not clearing `toResolve` for VIR guards on re-entry to `ScanAndEnqueue`. VIR results are ephemeral (applied on a temporary pebble batch), so previously resolved intents must be re-resolved on each evaluation attempt.
2. Condensing accumulated point entries into range entries when the list exceeds `condenseOnScanThreshold` (derived from `MaxConflictsPerLockConflictError`, default 5000). This happens inside `ScanAndEnqueue`, after `AddDiscovered` has incorporated newly found intents.

`PrepareForLockConflictRetry` is removed as it is no longer needed. `VirtuallyResolvesIntents` is removed from the `Guard` interface as it is only used internally by the lock table.

Epic: none
Release note: None

---

**kvserver,storage: prefer distinct txns in VIR pre-scan after condensing**

After the lock table guard condenses point intent resolutions into range entries, this commit widens the range resolves to cover the full request span during virtual evaluation, so that all of a transaction's intents are resolved in one pass rather than requiring iterative discovery. Point requests (e.g. Get) whose spans have no EndKey get a synthesized range span for `ResolveIntentRangeRequest`.

In this regime, discovering many intents from the same transaction is wasteful — a single range resolve already covers all of that transaction's intents within the span. This commit adds a `preferDistinctTxns` parameter to `ScanConflictingIntentsForDroppingLatchesEarly`. When set, the pre-scan skips intents from transactions it has already collected, maximizing the number of distinct conflicting transactions discovered within the `maxLockConflicts` budget. This is signaled through the new `HasCondensedIntents()` method on the `Guard` interface.

Epic: none
Release note: None

---

**roachtest: tune backup/intents for VIR**

With virtual intent resolution, the backup/intents roachtest benefits from having fewer, larger transactions rather than many small ones. Increasing perTransactionRowCount from 1,000 to 10,000 (i.e. 100 txns instead of 1,000) means each ExportRequest encounters intents from fewer distinct transactions, which is resolved efficiently by the condensing + ranged VIR path.

VIR is explicitly enabled via cluster setting so the test exercises the preferDistinctTxns pre-scan and ranged virtual resolution.

Part of: [#156800](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/156800)
Release note: None
author: miraradeva

title: build: use docker buildx for multi-arch docker images, consolidate docker builds
body:
Modernize the release docker image pipeline:

1. Move docker image builds from per-platform CI jobs into the docker/manifest jobs. Previously, each per-platform job built a single-arch docker image and pushed it (e.g., amd64-v24.1.0), then the manifest job stitched them together. Now the docker jobs download per-arch tarballs from GCS (with 5 retries) and use `docker buildx build --platform` to build and push proper multi-arch images in a single step. This eliminates orphan single-arch manifests and centralizes docker logic.

2. Update build/deploy/Dockerfile to use `ARG TARGETARCH` so buildx can select the correct per-arch binaries from ${TARGETARCH}/ subdirectories in the build context.

3. Use `docker buildx imagetools create` in publish-staged-cockroach-release.sh to copy multi-arch manifests between registries server-side and to create latest/release-branch tags. The staged image is now referenced as a multi-arch manifest directly (`FROM repo:version`) instead of per-arch tags.

4. Add docker image verification to the build artifacts pipeline (previously only the release pipeline verified images).

5. Remove the binfmt TODO workaround for s390x QEMU on Ubuntu 20.04, since agents have been upgraded to Ubuntu 24.04.

6. Add multi-arch support for Red Hat certified images. Update publish-redhat-release.sh to build and push multi-arch (amd64, arm64, s390x) images using docker buildx. Preflight automatically certifies all architectures in the manifest list.

The per-platform jobs now only build and upload tarballs — no docker credentials or registry interaction needed. Remove --output-directory=artifacts since local tarball copies are no longer consumed by a downstream docker build.

Epic: None
Release note (ops change): Red Hat certified CockroachDB container images are now published as multi-arch manifests supporting linux/amd64, linux/arm64, and linux/s390x. Previously only linux/amd64 was published to the Red Hat registry.
author: rail

reviews.body:
*(made with [/review-crdb](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/master/.claude/skills/review-crdb/SKILL.md))*

Celia's Caveat: 
* I don't have enough context in this area to verify Claude's review. More posting this on Claude's behalf, rather than marking this as "changes requested".

Claude's review:
* Good architectural direction — consolidating multi-arch builds into a single `docker buildx` invocation and removing docker credentials from per-platform jobs is the right call. Three blocking bugs found that will affect real release runs.

## Blocking

1. **`gcp_credentials` undefined in `build-cockroach-release-docker.sh`** — this script defines `gcr_credentials` but the new code uses `gcp_credentials` throughout. Under `set -u` this hard-fails on startup; without it, empty credentials are silently passed to `log_into_gcloud` and `docker_login_gcr`. Will break the first release run with this script.

2. **`$$` never expands in single-quoted trap in `publish-redhat-release.sh`** — the trap body is single-quoted, so `$$` is a literal two-character string. `docker buildx rm "redhat-builder-$$"` targets a builder that doesn't exist; `|| true` hides the failure; the real builder leaks every run.

3. **Both tmpdirs leak in `publish-staged-cockroach-release.sh`** — the EXIT trap only calls `cleanup_buildx; remove_files_on_exit`, neither of which removes `mktemp -d` directories. The script creates two (multi-arch and FIPS Dockerfiles) and neither is cleaned up. The other docker scripts correctly include `rm -rf $tmpdir` in their traps.

## Suggestions

4. **Buildx builder name collision on hard-kill** — if a CI agent is SIGKILL'd, the EXIT trap doesn't run and the builder registration persists in `~/.docker/buildx/`. On the next run, `docker buildx create` fails immediately under `set -e`. Add a defensive `docker buildx rm "release-builder-$$" 2>/dev/null || true` before the `create` in each script.

5. **No retry on `docker buildx build --push

commits.message:
build: use docker buildx for multi-arch docker images, consolidate docker builds

Modernize the release docker image pipeline:

1. Move docker image builds from per-platform CI jobs into the docker/manifest
   jobs. Previously, each per-platform job built a single-arch docker image and
   pushed it (e.g., amd64-v24.1.0), then the manifest job stitched them
   together. Now the docker jobs download per-arch tarballs from GCS (with 5
   retries) and use `docker buildx build --platform` to build and push proper
   multi-arch images in a single step. This eliminates orphan single-arch
   manifests and centralizes docker logic.

2. Update build/deploy/Dockerfile to use `ARG TARGETARCH` so buildx can select
   the correct per-arch binaries from ${TARGETARCH}/ subdirectories in the
   build context.

3. Use `docker buildx imagetools create` in publish-staged-cockroach-release.sh
   to copy multi-arch manifests between registries server-side and to create
   latest/release-branch tags. The staged image is now referenced as a
   multi-arch manifest directly (`FROM repo:version`) instead of per-arch tags.

4. Add docker image verification to the build artifacts pipeline (previously
   only the release pipeline verified images).

5. Remove the binfmt TODO workaround for s390x QEMU on Ubuntu 20.04, since
   agents have been upgraded to Ubuntu 24.04.

6. Add multi-arch support for Red Hat certified images. Update
   publish-redhat-release.sh to build and push multi-arch (amd64, arm64, s390x)
   images using docker buildx. Preflight automatically certifies all
   architectures in the manifest list.

The per-platform jobs now only build and upload tarballs — no docker
credentials or registry interaction needed. Remove --output-directory=artifacts
since local tarball copies are no longer consumed by a downstream docker build.

Epic: None
Release note (ops change): Red Hat certified CockroachDB container images are
now published as multi-arch manifests supporting linux/amd64, linux/arm64, and
linux/s390x. Previously only linux/amd64 was published to the Red Hat registry.

title: sql/stats: add mixed version test for stats delayed deletion
body:
Fixes: #161183

Extend TestTableStatisticsDelayDeleteColumnMigration to exercise the stats cache and SHOW STATISTICS both before and after the V26_2_AddTableStatisticsDelayDeleteColumn migration. The test creates a table with sql_stats_canary_window enabled and verifies that:

- Before migration: SHOW STATISTICS and SHOW STATISTICS USING JSON work correctly when GetTableStatisticsStmt falls back to `false AS "delayDelete"`. The canary delayed deletion path is gated on the version, so delay_delete stays false for all stats even with canary_window set.
- After migration: collecting new stats marks old ones with delay_delete=true (the canary two-phase deletion), while the freshest stats remain delay_delete=false.

Release note: None

title: mmaprototype: emit shedding stores via Infof
body:
Epic: CRDB-55052 
Release note: none

---
**mmaprototype: bump per-range log level to V(3)**

This change bumps per-range and per-candidate log calls from V(2) to
V(3). Store-level log calls (cluster means, shedding store list,
overload status) remain at V(2). With many overloaded stores, the
per-range output dominates the log at V(2), drowning out the
store-level decisions that are more useful for debugging.

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

---
**mmaprototype: emit shedding stores via Infof**

This change replaces the `log.V(2)` guard on the sorted shedding
stores log with a `passObs != nil` check, emitting via `Infof`
during periodic rebalancing passes. This is store-level context
(not per-range), so the volume is low and always useful.

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: vendor: bump cockroachdb/redact to v1.1.8
body:
A test case in
pkg/sql/logictest/testdata/logic_test/builtin_function is modified
due to the minor change in redaction logic.

Previously, the algorithm for redaction used a regular expression
search and replace which would look for the "innermost" set of
redaction markers in a string.

The modified logic now parses linearly from left to right based in the
redaction markers present in the string and will start redaction
immediately from the first marker.

In general, we don't need to worry about this because the `redact`
library escapes nested redaction markers automatically if they occur
inside of user-provided strings. This behavior only makes a difference
if something goes awry in the logging output.

Additionally, fix nested redaction markers in EXPLAIN (OPT, REDACT)
output. The `formatNodeMaybeMarkRedaction` function in `format.go`
writes raw marker bytes directly to a plain `bytes.Buffer`, bypassing
the redact library's nesting/escaping logic. When formatting composite
datums like `DTuple`, `DArray`, and `DOidWrapper`, the outer datum got
wrapped with markers, and then each child datum independently wrote its
own markers to the same buffer — producing nested markers like
`‹(‹1›,)›`. With the old regex-based redact library this worked
because it matched innermost markers first, but the new linear L→R
parser matches the first `‹` with the first `›`, corrupting the output.

The fix excludes `DTuple`, `DArray`, and `DOidWrapper` from outer
marker wrapping in `formatNodeMaybeMarkRedaction`, since their children
are individually wrapped. The tuple/array structure characters (`()`,
`[]`, `,`) are not user data requiring redaction. `DOidWrapper`
delegates formatting to its wrapped datum via `FormatNode`, which
applies its own markers.

A test-build assertion is added to detect nested markers produced by
any future Datum type that delegates to FormatNode internally.

Epic: None
Release note: None
author: dhartunian

I too don't understand why we need the special case for REFCURSOR in there, but I'll investigate that separately. The change > @yuzefovich reviewed 3 files and all commit messages, and made 2 comments. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165718#-:-Onxn8LZAU_7cdotQ8oP:b9ljfmz)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on dhartunian and michae2). ___ *[`pkg/sql/sem/tree/datum.go` line 5761 at r3](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165718#-OnxmEKW0ovbLeRtpZ1d:-OnxmEKW0ovbLeRtpZ1e:b-cxfz5j) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/45f40c435d82f232c9600f97e3784bcd3eb37eb8/pkg/sql/sem/tree/datum.go#L5761)):* > ```Go > wrapped.Format(ctx) > if ctx.flags.HasFlags(FmtMarkRedactionNode) { > ctx.WriteString(string(redact.EndMarker())) > ``` nit: could combine two `if`s into one by using `defer` on the second `WriteString` call.

Re: refcursor special case: I removed the special case code in #166013, and there are a handful of failures, so perhaps not worth digging deeper and your patch is reasonable. > @yuzefovich made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165718#-:-OnyCS4QAIArz3iepkxX:b-ykcgax)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on dhartunian and michae2).

I think we should probably keep it the way it was. ___ *[`pkg/sql/sem/tree/format.go` line 992 at r3](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165718#-Ony8SH1-38hRjuYBT2L:-Ony8SH1-38hRjuYBT2M:b-f76x2j) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/45f40c435d82f232c9600f97e3784bcd3eb37eb8/pkg/sql/sem/tree/format.go#L992)):* > ```Go > bytes.Contains(formatted, redact.EndMarker()) { > panic(errors.AssertionFailedf( > "formatNodeMaybeMarkRedaction: %T produced nested redaction markers; "+ > ``` nit: it would be nice to include %v of v and %s of ctx.Buffer.String() in the message as well

commits.message:
vendor: bump cockroachdb/redact to v1.1.8

A test case in
pkg/sql/logictest/testdata/logic_test/builtin_function is modified
due to the minor change in redaction logic.

Previously, the algorithm for redaction used a regular expression
search and replace which would look for the "innermost" set of
redaction markers in a string.

The modified logic now parses linearly from left to right based in the
redaction markers present in the string and will start redaction
immediately from the first marker.

In general, we don't need to worry about this because the `redact`
library escapes nested redaction markers automatically if they occur
inside of user-provided strings. This behavior only makes a difference
if something goes awry in the logging output.

Additionally, fix nested redaction markers in EXPLAIN (OPT, REDACT)
output. The `formatNodeMaybeMarkRedaction` function in `format.go`
writes raw marker bytes directly to a plain `bytes.Buffer`, bypassing
the redact library's nesting/escaping logic. When formatting composite
datums like `DTuple`, `DArray`, and `DOidWrapper`, the outer datum got
wrapped with markers, and then each child datum independently wrote its
own markers to the same buffer — producing nested markers like
`‹(‹1›,)›`. With the old regex-based redact library this worked
because it matched innermost markers first, but the new linear L→R
parser matches the first `‹` with the first `›`, corrupting the output.

The fix excludes `DTuple`, `DArray`, and `DOidWrapper` from outer
marker wrapping in `formatNodeMaybeMarkRedaction`, since their children
are individually wrapped. The tuple/array structure characters (`()`,
`[]`, `,`) are not user data requiring redaction. `DOidWrapper`
delegates formatting to its wrapped datum via `FormatNode`, which
applies its own markers. The `T_refcursor` branch in
`DOidWrapper.Format` is also updated to use `FormatNode` instead of
calling `Format` directly, ensuring refcursor values are redacted.

A test-build assertion is added to detect nested markers produced by
any future Datum type that delegates to FormatNode internally.

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: ui: migrate locations consumers from Redux to SWR
body:
Migrate all consumers of the locations Redux reducer to the new SWR-based
`useLocations` hook, then remove the reducer entirely.

**Commit 1 — cluster-ui: add SWR-based locations API**
Add a `useLocations` hook wrapping `_admin/v1/locations` via SWR.
Export `buildLocalityTree` and associated types (`LocalityTier`,
`LocalityTree`, `LocationTree`) from cluster-ui so db-console
consumers can replace the private `redux/localities` helpers.

**Commit 2 — ui: migrate nodeCanvasContainer from Redux to SWR**
Replace the Redux `connect` wrapper with `useNodesSummary()`,
`useLocations()`, and `buildLocalityTree()` from cluster-ui.
Filter decommissioned nodes locally before building the locality tree.

**Commit 3 — ui: migrate localities report from Redux to SWR**
Same pattern: replace Redux `connect` with SWR hooks and
`buildLocalityTree`. Add a `getCommissionedNodes` helper for
the decommissioned-node filter.

**Commit 4 — ui: remove locations Redux reducer and selectors**
With all consumers migrated, remove `locationsReducerObj`,
`refreshLocations`, the `selectLocations*` selectors,
`getLocations` API function, and `locations.spec.ts`.

Epic: CRDB-58145
Release note: None
author: kyle-a-wong

> @jasonlmfong reviewed 10 files and all commit messages, and made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165714#-:-OnwxoGYCrSEhxhBR8hC:bnfp4nl)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on kyle-a-wong).

commits.message:
cluster-ui: add SWR-based locations API

Add a new `useLocations` hook that fetches location data via SWR,
replacing the Redux-based `CachedDataReducer` pattern. The hook
returns a pre-computed `locationTree` (keyed by locality tier) for
efficient lookups.

Also export `buildLocalityTree`, which constructs a hierarchical
tree of localities from node statuses, along with the `LocalityTier`,
`LocalityTree`, and `LocationTree` types. These were previously
private to db-console's `redux/localities` module and are now
available to all cluster-ui consumers.

Release note: None

ui: migrate nodeCanvasContainer from Redux to SWR

Replace the Redux `connect` wrapper with direct SWR hooks:
- `useNodesSummary()` for node statuses and liveness data
- `clusterUiApi.useLocations()` for location data
- `buildLocalityTree()` imported from cluster-ui (previously
  duplicated as a private function in `redux/localities.ts`)

The component filters out decommissioned nodes locally before
building the locality tree, matching the previous behavior of
`selectCommissionedNodeStatuses` + `selectLocalityTree`.

Release note: None

ui: migrate localities report from Redux to SWR

Replace Redux `connect` with direct SWR hooks (`useNodesSummary`,
`useLocations`) and `buildLocalityTree` from cluster-ui.

The component now filters decommissioned nodes locally via a
`getCommissionedNodes` helper, matching the prior
`selectLocalityTree` selector behavior.

Release note: None

ui: remove locations Redux reducer and selectors

With both consumers (nodeCanvasContainer, localities report) now
using the SWR-based `useLocations` hook, remove the Redux
infrastructure:

- `locationsReducerObj` and `refreshLocations` from apiReducers
- `selectLocations`, `selectLocationTree`, `selectLocationsRequestStatus`
  from redux/locations (retaining `ILocation` and `LocationTree` types
  still used by `src/util/locations`)
- `getLocations` and associated type aliases from util/api
- `locations.spec.ts` (tests for the removed selectors)

Release note: None
Merge branch 'master' into kwong/migrate-nodecanvas-to-swr

title: sql: add database and enabled columns to SHOW STATEMENT HINTS
body:
#### sql: add database and enabled columns to SHOW STATEMENT HINTS

Add the `database` and `enabled` columns to the output of
SHOW STATEMENT HINTS, matching the columns added to the
system.statement_hints table by the

New column order:

commits.message:
ui: migrate events from Redux CachedDataReducer to SWR

Add a `useEvents` SWR hook in cluster-ui's `eventsApi.ts` that wraps
`getNonRedactedEvents` with `useSwrWithClusterId`, using a 10-second
deduplication interval and disabled revalidateOnFocus.

Convert the `EventBox` and `EventPage` components from Redux-connected
class-style wrappers to plain functional components that call
`clusterUiApi.useEvents()` directly. This removes the `connect()`,
`mapStateToProps`, `mapDispatchToProps`, `withRouter`, and `WithTimezone`
HOC wrappers. The `EventPage` now reads the timezone from
`TimezoneContext` directly and uses `useSelector`/`useDispatch` for the
sort setting local state.

Since these were the only consumers of the events CachedDataReducer,
remove `eventsReducerObj`, `refreshEvents`, and the `events` field from
`APIReducersState` and `combineReducers`. Delete the now-dead
`redux/events.ts` selectors file.

Update the test to mock `clusterUiApi.useEvents` instead of passing
`refreshEvents` as a prop.

title: build: mirror rules_shell dependency to GCS
body:
rules_go declares rules_shell as a transitive dependency via _maybe(), pointing at GitHub. Since we mirror all Bazel dependencies to a GCS bucket, this caused build failures (404) in environments that cannot reach GitHub directly. Pin rules_shell in WORKSPACE before go_rules_dependencies() so the mirrored URL is used instead.

Epic: None
Release note: None
author: rail

commits.message:
build: mirror rules_shell dependency to GCS

rules_go declares rules_shell as a transitive dependency via _maybe(),
pointing at GitHub. Since we mirror all Bazel dependencies to a GCS
bucket, this caused build failures (404) in environments that cannot
reach GitHub directly. Pin rules_shell in WORKSPACE before
go_rules_dependencies() so the mirrored URL is used instead.

Epic: None

title: admission: add a per-tenant token usage metric to CPU time token AC
body:
**admission: move to per-tier per-tenant admission metrics**

AggCounter.AddChild panics on duplicate label values. Both KV work
queues (system_tenant, app_tenant) previously shared the same
AdmittedCountPerTenant and WaitTimeNanosPerTenant AggCounter. Today
GetKVWorkQueue routes each tenant to exactly one tier, so duplicates
can't happen, but it's better not to rely on that routing invariant
for panic safety.

Give each tier its own AggCounter, with tier-suffixed metric names:

admission.cpu_time_tokens.per_tenant.admitted_count.system_tenant
admission.cpu_time_tokens.per_tenant.admitted_count.app_tenant
admission.cpu_time_tokens.per_tenant.wait_time_nanos.system_tenant
admission.cpu_time_tokens.per_tenant.wait_time_nanos.app_tenant

Part of https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/154449

Release note: None.

**admission: add per-tenant token usage metrics**

Add TokensUsedPerTenant and TokensReturnedPerTenant counters, tracked
in adjustTenantUsedLocked. These give per-tenant visibility into CPU
usage: rate(tokens_used) - rate(tokens_returned) yields the net CPU
time token consumption rate per tenant, enabling per-tenant CPU usage
breakdowns.

admission.cpu_time_tokens.per_tenant.tokens_used.system_tenant
admission.cpu_time_tokens.per_tenant.tokens_used.app_tenant
admission.cpu_time_tokens.per_tenant.tokens_returned.system_tenant
admission.cpu_time_tokens.per_tenant.tokens_returned.app_tenant

Part of https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/154449

Release note: None.

title: importer: fix expected row count after ingestWithRetry retries
body:
The row count validation added for IMPORT INTO accounted for retries that re-enter Resume (pause/resume, node restart) but not retries within ingestWithRetry. When a transient error triggers a retry, rows from the successful first attempt persist in KV, but the result only reflects the last attempt. This caused a row count mismatch.

Fix by using the job progress summary as the source of truth for total imported rows, and ensure distImport always flushes the summary before returning.

Fixes: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/165540
Release note: None
Epic: None
author: spilchen

commits.message:
importer: add regression test for row count after ingestWithRetry retry

Add `TestImportIntoRowCountCheckAfterIngestRetry` which verifies that
INSPECT row count validation passes after `ingestWithRetry` retries a
failed `distImport`. The test injects a retryable error (via a new
`afterDistImport` testing knob) after the first successful `distImport`
call, forcing a retry. On retry, all files are at `MaxInt64` resume pos
so 0 new rows are ingested. The test covers both empty-table and
non-empty-table cases.

The `afterDistImport` knob is called after each successful `distImport`
inside `ingestWithRetry`. If it returns an error, that error replaces
the nil result and triggers the retry path.

Epic: None
Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

importer: fix row count validation after ingestWithRetry retries

The expected row count for IMPORT's post-ingest validation was computed
from the return value of the last distImport attempt. This correctly
handled retries that re-entered Resume (e.g. pause/resume), but not
retries within ingestWithRetry, where rows from a prior attempt persist
in KV but aren't reflected in the final attempt's result.

Fix this by reading the total imported row count from the job progress
summary, which accumulates across all attempts. Also add a final
checkpoint persist in distImport to ensure the summary is flushed
before it returns.

Fixes: #165540
Release note: None
Epic: None
