title: sql,stats,server: backend for canary vs stable charts in statement details
body:
Informs: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/156817

Backend support for two upcoming DB Console charts on the Statement Details page (frontend in a follow-up PR):

- `Canary vs stable statement times`: Track canary and stable execution/plan latencies separately in statement statistics via a new `ExperimentStatsInfo` proto. A three-way `StatsRolloutSelection` enum (`Default/Canary/Stable`) replaces the former boolean so that executions are only recorded as canary or stable when the experiment is actually active (`canary_fraction > 0`). `Canary/stable` JSON fields are conditionally encoded to avoid storage overhead when the experiment is off.
- `Canary vs stable plan distribution`: Add `canary_execution_count` and `stable_execution_count` to the
`StatementPlanDistribution` API response.

Release note: None
author: ZhouXing19

reviews.body: I only looked at the sql stats related changes-- just have a question and couple suggestions
the app stats recording makes sense, I'll give it another pass tomorrow
> @ZhouXing19 made 3 comments. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165584)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on kyle-a-wong and michae2).

Overall lgtm, but i didnt look too closely at the `pkg/sql/stats` code, so someone from queries should double check it. The commit history on this PR is a little weird, Why is there the rebase commit in the middle of it, can you rebase this branch on origin/master?
one nit

commits.message:
sql,stats: track canary vs stable stats execution latencies

This is the backend for the "canary vs stable statement times" chart
on the DB Console statement details page, introduced in a follow-up
frontend PR.

Track canary and stable execution latencies separately in statement
statistics using a shared ExperimentStatsInfo proto message type.

An execution is recorded as canary or stable only when both conditions
hold: canaryRollDice() returns Canary or Stable (i.e. the experiment
is active), and CanaryAndStableStatsDiffer is true (i.e. the table's
newest and second-newest stats actually differ).

A three-way StatsRolloutSelection enum (Default/Canary/Stable) on
eval.Context replaces the former boolean, because a boolean cannot
distinguish "experiment off" from "experiment on, using stable stats."
For example, a table may have two generations of stats in its cache
(so CanaryAndStableStatsDiffer is true), but if canary_fraction = 0
the experiment is off cluster-wide. With a boolean we'd have to
record such executions as "stable," polluting experiment metrics with
non-experiment traffic. The Default state prevents that.

The canaryStats and stableStats nested objects are conditionally
encoded in the persisted JSONB column only when their counts are
positive, avoiding storage overhead when the experiment is inactive.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

server: add canary and stable execution counts to plan distribution API

This is the backend for the "canary vs stable plan distribution" chart
on the DB Console statement details page, introduced in a follow-up
frontend PR.

Add canary_execution_count and stable_execution_count to the
StatementPlanDistribution API response. Both are read from the
nested canaryStats/stableStats objects in the JSONB statistics
column, rather than deriving stable as (total - canary).

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

execbuilder: add logictest for canary/stable stats metric recording

Add a `stats_differ_metrics` subtest to the `canary_stats` execbuilder
testdata verifying that canary and stable execution counts are correctly
recorded in `crdb_internal.cluster_statement_statistics`. Tests cover
canary path, stable path, stats outside the canary window, and cached
memo reuse via prepared statements.

Release note: None

title: roachtest: add disk bandwidth AC testing to index-backfill test
body:
## Summary

Repurpose the multi-node `admission-control/index-backfill` roachtest to
properly test disk bandwidth admission control by:

1. **Extract snapshot copy helper** (`roachtestutil.CopySnapshotDataToNodes`):
   copies data from GCE snapshots to CRDB nodes via a temporary disk, avoiding
   the GCE snapshot hydration problem where reads from snapshot-backed disks are
   slow (~50 MB/s, 250ms latency) until fully hydrated.

2. **Fix snapshot hydration**: use the new copy helper in the index-backfill
   test and switch from `ManualOnly` to `Nightly`.

3. **Add cgroup disk bandwidth limiting**: limit disk bandwidth to 128 MiB/s
   via cgroups so disk I/O becomes the bottleneck for admission control.

4. **Add provisioned-bandwidth variant**: register two test variants
   (`provisioned-bandwidth=false` and `provisioned-bandwidth=true`) with
   non-overlapping snapshot prefixes. The `true` variant sets
   `kvadmission.store.provisioned_bandwidth` to match the cgroup limit.

5. **Export metrics to roachperf**: replace `runTPCE` with direct workload
   management via `tpceSpec.run()` and `t.NewGroup`. Export index backfill
   duration, primary key change duration, and mean total disk bandwidth.

Epic: CRDB-47073
Fixes: #165330
author: angeladietz

reviews.body:
Thanks for putting this together -- the extraction of the snapshot copy helper and the metrics export are both welcome additions.

Two blocking issues: the cluster spec is missing `spec.ReuseNone()` (required by the cgroup staller), and the cgroup setup runs before the cluster is started (the systemd cgroup path won't exist yet). See inline comments.

**Commit structure**: Commit 3 (`5441ba253a2`) bundles four changes (extract `runIndexBackfill`, add cgroup limiting, bump timeout, move cluster setting). Consider splitting the mechanical extraction from the semantic additions -- the 327-line diff is hard to review as-is. Similarly, commit 5 (`e626c228a7b`) replaces `runTPCE` with `tpceSpec.run()` *and* adds metrics collection in one shot; separating those would help verify the `runTPCE` replacement is behavior-preserving.

---

commits.message:
roachtestutil: extract snapshot copy helper from single-node index backfill

This extracts the GCE snapshot copy workaround from the single-node
index backfill test into a shared roachtestutil.CopySnapshotDataToNodes
helper. GCE snapshot-backed disks have a hydration problem where reads
are very slow (~50 MB/s with 250ms latency) until GCE fully hydrates
them in the background. The helper works around this by creating
temporary disks from snapshots, rsyncing data to primary disks, then
cleaning up the temporary disks.

The helper processes all CRDB nodes in parallel and includes best-effort
cleanup of temporary disks on failure.

Informs: #165330
Epic: CRDB-47073
Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

roachtest: fix snapshot hydration in index-backfill test, run nightly

This replaces ApplySnapshots with the shared CopySnapshotDataToNodes
helper in the admission-control/index-backfill test. Previously, the
test used ApplySnapshots which relies on GCE snapshot-backed disks
that are very slow (~50 MB/s with 250ms latency) until fully hydrated.
Now, the test copies snapshot data to the primary disk via rsync,
avoiding the hydration problem entirely.

As well, the test is switched from ManualOnly to Nightly so it runs
regularly instead of rotting.

Informs: #165330
Epic: CRDB-47073
Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

roachtest: add cgroup disk bandwidth limiting to index-backfill test

Limit disk bandwidth to 128 MiB/s via cgroups so that disk I/O becomes
the bottleneck for admission control. Without this, pd-ssd disks are
fast enough that admission control has nothing meaningful to manage.

The cgroup staller is set up inside the start callback after c.StartE,
since the cgroup path for the cockroach systemd service only exists
after the service has been created.

Also extract runIndexBackfill to reduce nesting, increase the timeout
to 12h, and move the GC TTL cluster setting from the during callback
into start so it takes effect before the workload begins.

Epic: CRDB-47073
Informs: #165330
Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

roachtest: add provisioned-bandwidth variant to index-backfill test

Register two variants of the multi-node index-backfill test:
one with `provisioned-bandwidth=false` and one with
`provisioned-bandwidth=true`. The provisioned-bandwidth variant
sets `kvadmission.store.provisioned_bandwidth` to 128 MiB/s (matching
the cgroup limit) and allows elastic work to fully utilize it.

Use non-overlapping snapshot prefixes (`index-backfill-tpce-100k-nobw`
and `index-backfill-tpce-100k-bw`) so that ListSnapshots prefix
matching does not return snapshots from the wrong variant.

Epic: CRDB-47073
Informs: #165330
Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

roachtest: export metrics to roachperf from index-backfill test

Replace the `runTPCE` helper with direct workload management using
`tpceSpec.run()` and `t.NewGroup` so that the test can set up its own
prometheus client and stat collector for roachperf export.

Export three metrics:
- `index_backfill_duration`: time to complete CREATE INDEX
- `primary_key_change_duration`: time to complete ALTER PRIMARY KEY
- `mean_total_bandwidth`: average per-node disk bandwidth (read+write)
  during schema changes, comparable to the 128 MiB/s cgroup limit

Collect disk bandwidth samples every minute in a background goroutine
(via `t.Go`) that runs outside the main task group, so `g.Wait()` only
blocks on the workload and schema changes completing.

Epic: CRDB-47073
Informs: #165330
Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

roachtest: add no-disk-limit variant to index-backfill test

This adds a third variant of the multi-node index backfill test that runs
without cgroup disk bandwidth limiting. Previously, both variants used
cgroups to cap disk I/O at 128 MiB/s -- the only difference was whether
admission control knew about the provisioned bandwidth. Now, there are
three variants:

- `no-disk-limit`: no cgroup limiting, no provisioned bandwidth setting
- `provisioned-bandwidth=false`: cgroup limiting, AC unaware of bandwidth
- `provisioned-bandwidth=true`: cgroup limiting, AC aware of bandwidth

The no-disk-limit variant provides a baseline for comparing the effect of
disk bandwidth limiting on index backfill performance.

Epic: CRDB-47073
Fixes: #165330
Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: server: fix TestPlainHTTPServer flake by retrying HTTP GET
body:
`TestPlainHTTPServer` was using `httputil.Get` with the default 3-second
`StandardHTTPTimeout` to fetch the admin URL. Under CI resource
pressure, the request to `/` (which goes through the full
`authenticatedUIHandler` chain) can exceed this timeout, causing
intermittent failures across multiple branches since December 2025.

Wrap the HTTP GET in `testutils.SucceedsSoon` to retry on transient
timeouts, matching the pattern already used in the same test for the
health check request.

Resolves: #165366

Release note: None
author: dhartunian

commits.message:
server: fix TestPlainHTTPServer flake by retrying HTTP GET

The test was using `httputil.Get` with the default 3-second
`StandardHTTPTimeout` to fetch the admin URL. Under CI resource
pressure, the request to `/` (which goes through the full
authenticatedUIHandler chain) can exceed this timeout, causing
intermittent failures.

Wrap the HTTP GET in `testutils.SucceedsSoon` to retry on transient
timeouts, matching the pattern already used in the same test for the
health check request.

Resolves: #165366

Release note: None

title: sql/stats: only record canary/stable experiment metrics when stats actually differ
body:
Informs: #156817

The canary stats system has two distinct concepts:

 1. The dice roll (`canaryRollDice`): picks a rollout *path* — canary (newest stats) or stable (second-newest). Decided once per query before planning; unconditional when `canary_fraction` > 0.

 2. Actual stats divergence: whether the canary and stable statistics for a table are genuinely different. Depends on per-table canary window size, timing, and the existence of two distinct stat generations.

Previously, the dice roll alone determined whether an execution was tagged as canary or stable for experiment metrics. This tagged every execution when `canary_fraction > 0`, even for queries whose tables all use identical stats regardless of path — which will potentially dilute the "canary vs stable" metrics with noise.

---- 

Consider a workload with two query patterns and canary_fraction = 10%:                                   
                                                                                                       
- `SELECT * FROM t1`                    (90% of executions)
- `SELECT * FROM t1 JOIN t2 ...`  (10% of executions)

- t1 has no canary window (canaryWindowSize = 0).
- t2 has a canary window, and its canary and stable stats genuinely
  differ (fresh stats were recently collected).

Out of 1000 executions, the dice roll tags ~100 as "canary" and ~90 as "stable" — regardless of which tables they touch. That means ~90 of those tagged executions are the single-table query on t1, where canary and stable stats are identical. These 90 executions add pure noise to the A/B comparison metric.

Only the ~10 executions that touch t2 (the JOIN query) actually use different stats on the canary vs. stable path and produce a meaningful signal. With canaryAndStableStatsDiffer, the t1-only executions are excluded from experiment metrics, keeping the comparison clean.

----

This commit adds divergence detection during planning: for each table, we check whether its canary and stable stat sets actually differ (`canaryAndStableStatsDiffer`). The result is OR'd across all tables into `eval.Context.CanaryAndStableStatsDiffer`. A follow-up PR (#165257) will use this flag to gate experiment metric recording.

The dice roll still controls which stats the optimizer uses; this change only provides the signal for whether recording the experiment is meaningful.

Release note: None
author: ZhouXing19

commits.message:
sql/stats: add divergence signal for "canary vs stable" metrics

The canary stats system has two distinct concepts:

 1. The dice roll (`canaryRollDice`): picks a rollout *path* — canary
    (newest stats) or stable (second-newest). Decided once per query before
    planning; unconditional when canary_fraction > 0.

 2. Actual stats divergence: whether the canary and stable statistics for
    a table are genuinely different. Depends on per-table canary window
    size, timing, and the existence of two distinct stat generations.

Previously, the dice roll alone determined whether an execution was
tagged as canary or stable for "canary vs stable" metrics. This tagged
every execution when canary_fraction > 0, even for queries whose tables
all use identical stats regardless of path — diluting "canary vs
stable" metrics with noise.

This commit adds divergence detection during planning: for each table,
we check whether its canary and stable stat sets actually differ
(cacheEntry.canaryAndStableStatsDiffer()). The per-table result is
stored on optTable (alongside rawStats) and checked in
execbuilder.Build(), so that reusing cached plan won't lose track of the
stats divergence. If any table in the query has divergent stats, a
PlanFlag (PlanFlagCanaryAndStableStatsDiffer) is set.

The dice roll still controls which stats the optimizer uses; this change
only adds the divergence signal. A follow-up commit will use this flag
to gate "canary vs stable" metric recording so that only executions
with genuinely different stats are counted.

Release note: None

title: roachtest: fix hibernate test flake caused by leaked statement_timeout
body:
Previously, the hibernate roachtest was flaky because Hibernate's `withJdbcTimeout` sets a 1-second `statement_timeout` via a session-level SET, and unlike PostgreSQL, CockroachDB does not revert session-level SET on transaction rollback. This caused the timeout to leak into subsequent tests, which would then fail with "query execution canceled due to statement timeout".

To address this, apply the upstream Hibernate fix (commit 456194394d) as a patch until we upgrade to Hibernate v8.0.0+, which includes the fix natively.

Also remove the `ALTER ROLE ALL SET statement_timeout = '10m'` workaround from `alterZoneConfigAndClusterSettings`. The hibernate test runs as the root user, and `ALTER ROLE ALL` does not apply to the root user, so this setting had no effect.

Fixes: #165544

Release note: None
Epic: None
author: spilchen

Previously, the hibernate roachtest was flaky because Hibernate's
`withJdbcTimeout` sets a 1-second `statement_timeout` via a session-level
SET, and unlike PostgreSQL, CockroachDB does not revert session-level SET
on transaction rollback. This caused the timeout to leak into subsequent
tests, which would then fail with "query execution canceled due to
statement timeout".

To address this, apply the upstream Hibernate fix (commit 456194394d) as
a patch until we upgrade to Hibernate v8.0.0+, which includes the fix
natively.

Also remove the `ALTER ROLE ALL SET statement_timeout = '10m'` workaround
from `alterZoneConfigAndClusterSettings`. The hibernate test runs as the
root user, and `ALTER ROLE ALL` does not apply to the root user, so this
setting had no effect.

Fixes: #165544

title: backfill: migrate SST manifests from job payload to job info keys
body:
Previously, SST manifest metadata for index backfills was stored inline
in proto fields on `BackfillProgress` (declarative schema changer) and
`ResumeSpanList` (legacy schema changer). These fields grow with the
number of SSTs produced during distributed merge, which can cause
availability problems for large schema changes due to oversized job
payload rows.

This commit moves SST manifest storage to job info keys using the
existing `WriteChunkedFileToJobInfo`/`ReadChunkedFileToJobInfo`
infrastructure. The manifests are serialized via a `BulkSSTManifests`
wrapper proto and stored under keys like:
- `~backfill/sst-manifests/<tableID>-<sourceIndexID>.binpb` (declarative)
- `~backfill/sst-manifests/legacy-<mutationIdx>.binpb` (legacy)

Both writes happen atomically in the same txn as the existing job
payload updates, so no additional coordination is needed. The
in-memory `scexec.BackfillProgress` struct retains its `SSTManifests`
field for passing manifests through the system; only the persistence
layer changes.

No version gate is needed because the `sst_manifests` fields on
`BackfillProgress` and `ResumeSpanList` were added in the v26.2
development cycle and have never been released.

Depends on #164978 for the `BulkSSTManifests` wrapper message (added
here as well; resolves naturally on merge).

Resolves: #164675
Resolves: #162541
Epic: CRDB-48845

Release note: None

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

reviews.body: thanks for doing this! commits.message:
backfill: migrate SST manifests from job payload to job info keys

Previously, SST manifest metadata for index backfills was stored inline
in proto fields on BackfillProgress (declarative schema changer) and
ResumeSpanList (legacy schema changer). These fields grow with the
number of SSTs produced during distributed merge, which can cause
availability problems for large schema changes due to oversized job
payload rows.

This commit moves SST manifest storage to job info keys using the
existing WriteChunkedFileToJobInfo/ReadChunkedFileToJobInfo
infrastructure. The manifests are serialized via a BulkSSTManifests
wrapper proto and stored under keys like:
- `~backfill/sst-manifests/<tableID>-<sourceIndexID>.binpb` (declarative)
- `~backfill/sst-manifests/legacy-<mutationIdx>.binpb` (legacy)

Both writes happen atomically in the same txn as the existing job
payload updates, so no additional coordination is needed. The
in-memory scexec.BackfillProgress struct retains its SSTManifests
field for passing manifests through the system; only the persistence
layer changes.

No version gate is needed because the sst_manifests fields on
BackfillProgress and ResumeSpanList were added in the v26.2
development cycle and have never been released.

Depends on #164978 for the BulkSSTManifests wrapper message (added
here as well; resolves naturally on merge).

Resolves: #164675
Epic: CRDB-48845

Release note: None

body:

`state.cachedData.nodes.data` and hide the Network link for
single-node clusters. Replace this with the existing `useNodes()`
SWR hook from cluster-ui, which already fetches and caches the

consumers after this change, remove it.

instead of `useSelector` from `react-redux`, following the same
pattern used by the network page test.

from `state.cachedData.nodes` and `state.cachedData.cluster` to

title: sql: clean up FK back-references during rollback of CREATE TABLE
body:
Previously, when a CREATE TABLE with inline FOREIGN KEY constraints had its schema change job fail permanently, rollbackSchemaChange() would mark the new table as Dropped and queue it for GC, but never remove the InboundFK entries that had been added to the referenced table(s) during the SQL transaction. This left orphaned FK back-references pointing to a descriptor that no longer exists, causing descriptor validation errors like:
```
relation "parent" (209): invalid foreign key backreference: missing table=264: referenced descriptor not found
```

The fix adds FK back-reference cleanup in the ADD-state rollback path of rollbackSchemaChange(), alongside the existing view dependency cleanup. For each outbound FK on the table being rolled back, we remove the corresponding InboundFK from the referenced table. Similarly, for any inbound FKs (other tables referencing this one), we remove the corresponding OutboundFK from the origin table.

This reuses the existing removeFKBackReferenceFromTable() helper already used by dropTableImpl() and maybeReverseMutations(). Errors during cleanup are logged as warnings and skipped (best-effort), matching the pattern used by dropViewDeps().

Closes #165548

Epic: None
Release note (bug fix): Fixed a bug in the legacy schema changer where rolling back a CREATE TABLE with inline FOREIGN KEY constraints could leave orphaned FK back-references on the referenced table, causing descriptor validation errors.

commits.message:
sql: clean up FK back-references during rollback of CREATE TABLE in ADD state

Previously, when a CREATE TABLE with inline FOREIGN KEY constraints had
its schema change job fail permanently, rollbackSchemaChange() would
mark the new table as Dropped and queue it for GC, but never remove the
InboundFK entries that had been added to the referenced table(s) during
the SQL transaction. This left orphaned FK back-references pointing to a
descriptor that no longer exists, causing descriptor validation errors
like:

  relation "parent" (209): invalid foreign key backreference:
  missing table=264: referenced descriptor not found

The fix adds FK back-reference cleanup in the ADD-state rollback path of
rollbackSchemaChange(), alongside the existing view dependency cleanup.
For each outbound FK on the table being rolled back, we remove the
corresponding InboundFK from the referenced table. Similarly, for any
inbound FKs (other tables referencing this one), we remove the
corresponding OutboundFK from the origin table.

This reuses the existing removeFKBackReferenceFromTable() helper already
used by dropTableImpl() and maybeReverseMutations(). Errors during
cleanup are logged as warnings and skipped (best-effort), matching the
pattern used by dropViewDeps().

Epic: None
Release note (bug fix): Fixed a bug in the legacy schema changer where
rolling back a CREATE TABLE with inline FOREIGN KEY constraints could
leave orphaned FK back-references on the referenced table, causing
descriptor validation errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

sql: add regression test for orphaned FK back-references on rollback

Add TestCreateTableFKRollbackCleansBackrefs which verifies that when a
CREATE TABLE with an inline FOREIGN KEY constraint has its async schema
change job fail, the rollback properly cleans up the InboundFK
back-reference on the referenced table.

The test:
1. Creates a parent table.
2. Creates a child table with an inline FK referencing the parent, while
   injecting a permanent error into the child's schema change job via
   the RunBeforeResume testing knob.
3. Waits for the schema change job to fail.
4. Verifies the child table no longer exists (rolled back from ADD
   state).
5. Waits for the GC job to fully delete the child descriptor, so
   that the validation error matches the production error exactly:
   "invalid foreign key backreference: missing table=N: referenced
   descriptor not found".
6. Asserts the parent does not appear in crdb_internal.invalid_objects.
7. Asserts the parent table has no orphaned InboundFKs.

Closes #165551

Epic: None
Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: *: enable DRPC for tests in ttljob package
body:
Refactor the `Dialbacker` interface to be generic (`Dialbacker[Conn rpcConn]`) and introduce protocol-specific adapters (`GRPCDialbackAdapter`, `DRPCDialbackAdapter`) that implement it. This allows `VerifyDialback` to work with both gRPC and DRPC connections without coupling to a specific rpc framework.

The `VerifyDialback` function now accepts a `Dialbacker[Conn]` instead of directly calling gRPC-specific methods on `*Context`. Each adapter delegates to the appropriate dial methods on `*Context` for its protocol. The mock in `rpc_mock_test.go` is updated to be generic as well.

With this in place, re-enable DRPC in the row-level TTL job test helper (`newRowLevelTTLTestJobTestHelper`) by removing the `DefaultDRPCOption: base.TestDRPCDisabled` override that was added as a workaround.

Epic: CRDB-55382
Fixes: #158387
Release note: None

title: stats: fix auto stats rescheduling on ConcurrentCreateStatsError
body:
**stats: ignore ConcurrentCreateStatsError from auto partial jobs**

This commit fixes an oversight from when we introduced auto partial
stats collection USING EXTREMES. In particular, the stats refresher -
whenever it sees `ConcurrentCreateStatsError` - will attempt to
reschedule the stats job. This logic was added for full collections and
wasn't modified after we introduced partial ones. Thus, it became
possible for a _partial_ stats collection due to the "dice roll" force
a _full_ stats collection on concurrent stats error. With default stale
settings, partial jobs are 4x more likely to run, so we could be
collecting full stats more frequently than stale settings dictate.

This commit makes it so that the `ConcurrentCreateStatsError` is simply
ignored if it was in response to a partial stats collection. This seems
ok given that we have high global limit on concurrent partial stats (on
25.4 and prior versions no concurrency limit at all), so most likely
this error was raised due to another auto stats job (either full or
partial) on the same table issued by a different node.

**stats: fix auto stats rescheduling on ConcurrentCreateStatsError**

This commit fixes a bug that was introduced in
d1dfc80906fe027e3b727649481a6fb8ce7e8916 where we adjusted the
`createStatsNode` to swallow `ConcurrentCreateStatsError` (depending on
the cluster setting which was set to `false`, meaning error swallowing
was enabled by default, in 25.3). In particular, we use this error in
the `stats.Refresher` as a marker that the failed stats collection
should be rescheduled, so once the error is swallowed, we lost the
rescheduling behavior.

This commit fixes the issue by moving the responsibility of rescheduling
from the refresher goroutine onto the stats job itself. New
`RescheduleRefresh` API is added to the refresher which is called by the
stats job once it encounters the ConcurrentCreateStatsError.

This patch should be backportable given that in the mixed-version cluster,
including mixed minor patch versions, there might be no change in behavior
(assuming the cluster setting has the default `false` value). This will
depend on which node happened to be the job coordinator - the older one
would keep on swallowing the error, and nothing would change; the newer one
would call `RescheduleRefresh` which will influence scheduling another stats
job on the same node.

Epic: None
Release note: None
author: yuzefovich

reviews.body: Great job figuring this out! I'm not sure about backport. On the one hand, it's definitely a bug. On the other hand, I think it will only manifest as reduced frequency of autostats collections if there are concurrent autostats collections on different tables. I'm not sure how likely that is on a cluster with tens of tables. > @michae2 partially reviewed 10 files and made 2 comments. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165502#-:-OndTH8D0g5zgthYdZbD:brpqr5j)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on yuzefovich). ___ *[`pkg/sql/create_stats.go` line 646 at r4](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165502#-OndRmQVBFTvthgVRhbO:-OndRmQVBFTvthgVRhbP:bnd66gw) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/a66de275cbba8c9984f09b5f616eb2219656565a/pkg/sql/create_stats.go#L646)):* > ```Go > WhereIndexID: n.whereIndexID, > UseLocksTable: useLocksTable, > MustRefresh: n.Options.MustRefresh, > ``` I think we should be able to calculate MustRefresh here ourselves instead of passing it in by repeating the calculation from `maybeRefreshStats`: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/add73d0703f62fc56d39145140b3e0249afc2dfb/pkg/sql/stats/automatic_stats.go#L976-L997 To do that, we'd need to access the current table stats via the stats cache, similar to what we do in `createPartialStatsPlan`: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/84427ff3424990c1c6452473a73c9a0d5aa46ec9/pkg/sql/distsql_plan_stats.go#L309-L313

TFTR! Let's not backport until we see evidence that this behavior is problematic. > @yuzefovich made 2 comments. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165502#-:-OndaXly6zKrHbap2u1L:bq93b8d)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on michae2). ___ *[`pkg/sql/create_stats.go` line 646 at r4](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165502#-OndRmQVBFTvthgVRhbO:-Onda37A5XpaQZARKZel:b-rnveck) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/a66de275cbba8c9984f09b5f616eb2219656565a/pkg/sql/create_stats.go#L646)):*

Indeed, great point, thanks, done.

Also, maybe we should just continue with mustRefresh=true. ___ *[`pkg/sql/stats/automatic_stats.go` line 1110 at r6](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165502#-Onro0jI3DE0ZJZo6Mf6:-Onro0jI3DE0ZJZo6Mf7:b-rvp7pi) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/2b6d6ec96609afc3ef3187c39894013bf494ea18/pkg/sql/stats/automatic_stats.go#L1110)):* > ```Go > > if err := r.refreshStats(ctx, tableID, asOf, isPartial); err != nil { > // ConcurrentCreateStatsError is expected, so simply ignore it. commits.message:
stats: ignore ConcurrentCreateStatsError from auto partial jobs

This commit fixes an oversight from when we introduced auto partial
stats collection USING EXTREMES. In particular, the stats refresher -
whenever it sees `ConcurrentCreateStatsError` - will attempt to
reschedule the stats job. This logic was added for full collections and
wasn't modified after we introduced partial ones. Thus, it became
possible for a _partial_ stats collection due to the "dice roll" force
a _full_ stats collection on concurrent stats error. With default stale
settings, partial jobs are 4x more likely to run, so we could be
collecting full stats more frequently than stale settings dictate.

This commit makes it so that the `ConcurrentCreateStatsError` is simply
ignored if it was in response to a partial stats collection. This seems
ok given that we have high global limit on concurrent partial stats (on
25.4 and prior versions no concurrency limit at all), so most likely
this error was raised due to another auto stats job (either full or
partial) on the same table issued by a different node.

Release note: None

stats: fix auto stats rescheduling on ConcurrentCreateStatsError

This commit fixes a bug that was introduced in
d1dfc80906fe027e3b727649481a6fb8ce7e8916 where we adjusted the
`createStatsNode` to swallow `ConcurrentCreateStatsError` (depending on
the cluster setting which was set to `false`, meaning error swallowing
was enabled by default, in 25.3). In particular, we use this error in
the `stats.Refresher` as a marker that the failed stats collection
should be rescheduled, so once the error is swallowed, we lost the
rescheduling behavior.

This commit fixes the issue by moving the responsibility of rescheduling
from the refresher goroutine onto the stats job itself. New
`RescheduleRefresh` API is added to the refresher which is called by the
stats job once it encounters the ConcurrentCreateStatsError.

This patch should be backportable given that in the mixed-version cluster,
including mixed minor patch versions, there might be no change in behavior
(assuming the cluster setting has the default `false` value). This will
depend on which node happened to be the job coordinator - the older one
would keep on swallowing the error, and nothing would change; the newer one
would call `RescheduleRefresh` which will influence scheduling another stats
job on the same node.

Release note: None

title: ui: migrate nodeOverview and nodeLogs from Redux to SWR
body:
Migrate the nodeOverview and nodeLogs components from Redux/Saga
data fetching to SWR hooks.

## Commits

**ui: add useNodeLogs SWR hook**
Create a `useNodeLogs` SWR hook in cluster-ui that fetches node log
entries from the `_status/logs/{nodeId}` endpoint. This replaces the
Redux CachedDataReducer-based approach, providing automatic caching
with a 10-second deduplication interval and conditional fetching
based on nodeId availability.

**ui: migrate nodeOverview and nodeLogs from Redux to SWR**
Replace Redux `connect()`/`mapStateToProps`/`mapDispatchToProps` with
SWR hooks in the nodeOverview and nodeLogs components.

nodeOverview now uses `useNodesSummary()` from cluster-ui to fetch
nodes and liveness data, deriving the current node from
`nodeStatusByID`. The `withRouter` HOC is replaced with
`useHistory`/`useRouteMatch` hooks.

nodeLogs now uses `useNodesSummary()` for node display names and the
new `useNodeLogs()` hook for log entries. The `withRouter` HOC is
replaced with `useRouteMatch`.

Since nodeLogs was the sole consumer of the logs CachedDataReducer,
remove `logsReducerObj`, `refreshLogs`, and the `logs` field from
`APIReducersState`.

Part-of: CRDB-61192
Epic: CRDB-58145
Release note: None
author: kyle-a-wong

title: jobs,ash: Ash job instrumentation
body:
This PR adds the necessary plumbing to enable ASH instrumentation for jobs in CRDB.

See commits for details.

For more information about Active Session History, see the design doc:
https://docs.google.com/document/d/164fCBKytBGiRDPPMezQDGGiQClVW0wJLAzCEmwb2QKo/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.9ndo02nsmu 

Fixes: #164380
author: alyshanjahani-crl

commits.message:
kv: propagate workload ID via context

Add ContextWithWorkloadInfo and WorkloadInfoFromContext helpers that
allow callers to inject a workload identity into a context.
Transaction creation methods (NewTxn, Txn, TxnWithAdmissionControl,
TxnWithSteppingEnabled) automatically extract this value and call
SetWorkloadInfo on the new transaction. Non-transactional batch sends
(db.Run / db.send) also stamp the workload ID from context onto the
BatchRequest header. This enables transparent ASH workload attribution
without requiring per-use DB copies.

Epic: none
Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

jobs: plumb job identity through context for ASH attribution

Inject the job's ID into the context via kv.ContextWithWorkloadID in
Registry.runJob, before the exec context and resumer are invoked.
This ensures that every kv.DB or InternalDB transaction created
within the job's Resume/OnFailOrCancel automatically carries the
job's workload ID in KV BatchRequest headers, enabling ASH
attribution without requiring per-job DB copies.

Add a WithWorkloadInfo option to NewInternalPlanner that propagates
workload attribution (ID and type) to the planner's EvalContext.
MakeJobExecContext and all job resumers that create their own inner
planners (CREATE STATISTICS, schema changer, database region change
finalizer) use this option so that DistSQL flows carry the job's
workload ID for ASH sampling on remote nodes.

Epic: none
Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: roachtest: create kafka topics manually before consuming
body:
Previously, Kafka topic consumers were started in setupSink before
the changefeed was created without being explicitly created. This
meant consumers could call Partitions() before the topics existed
in Kafka if the changefeed didn't create them in time. While Kafka can
auto-create topics on metadata requests, this didn't always succeed,
likely due to a race with leader, resulting in
  "Request was for a topic or partition that does not exist on this
  broker".
The error was buffered in the monitor's errgroup and only
surfaced at shutdown.

Manually creating the topics ensures this doesn't happen and the test
doesn't flake.

Fixes: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/164590
Fixes: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/162293
Fixes: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/163153
Fixes: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/155512
Fixes: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/160064
Fixes: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/155675

Release note: None
author: aerfrei

reviews.body: LGTM

commits.message:
roachtest: create kafka topics manually before consuming

Previously, Kafka topic consumers were started in setupSink before
the changefeed was created without being explicitly created. This
meant consumers could call Partitions() before the topics existed
in Kafka if the changefeed didn't create them in time. While Kafka can
auto-create topics on metadata requests, this didn't always succeed,
likely due to a race with leader, resulting in
  "Request was for a topic or partition that does not exist on this
  broker".
The error was buffered in the monitor's errgroup and only
surfaced at shutdown.

Manually creating the topics ensures this doesn't happen and the test
doesn't flake.

Fixes: #164590
Fixes: #162293
Fixes: #163153
Fixes: #155512
Fixes: #160064
Fixes: #155675

title: sql/hints: use enabled and database columns
body:
### sql/hints: read enabled and database columns during hint matching

Read the `enabled` and `database` columns from system.statement_hints
when loading hints from the database into the hint cache. Also match on
current database when looking up hints with MaybeGetStatementHints.

Informs: #163522

Release note (sql change): Rewrite-inline-hints rules can now be scoped
to a specific database, and will only apply to matching statements when
the current database also matches. This database can be specified with
an optional third argument to
`information_schema.crdb_rewrite_inline_hints`.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

---

### sql/hints: add enable/disable statement hint builtin

Add `information_schema.crdb_enable_statement_hints` builtin with two
overloads: `(enabled: bool, rowid: int)` and `(enabled: bool,
statement_fingerprint: string)`. Both require REPAIRCLUSTER privilege.

The implementation uses a new `SetHintEnabledInDB` function that issues
a DELETE and an INSERT against `system.statement_hints` to set the
`enabled` column. The Planner interface gains a
`SetStatementHintEnabled` method to expose this to builtins.

The DELETE and INSERT are needed to invalidate the hint cache entry on
every node via the rangefeed on the secondary index of
`system.statement_hints`.

Fixes: #163522

Release note (sql change): This change introduces a new builtin
`information_schema.crdb_enable_statement_hints` which can be used to
enable or disable statement hints by hint ID or by statement
fingerprint.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

commits.message:
kvnemesis: add support for MVCC GC operation

1. Added an explicit MVCC GC operation to KVNemesis. It
works by selecting a random key and looking up its
corresponding range, finding the store that is its
leaseholder, and then directly enqueuing the replica
to that store's MVCC GC queue using `store.Enqueue`.

2. Added a parameter `gcTTLSeconds` to the `kvnemesisTestCfg`
struct to allow setting a different GC TTL than the default.
The function `kvnemesisTestCfg.testClusterArgs` exercises this
parameter to install this into the span config.

3. Finally, added a `TestKVNemesisMVCCGC` that enables the
MVCC GC operation, and lowers the GC TTL so that garbage
collection actually occurs to test its interaction with
other KV operations.

Release note: None

kvnemesis: install a protected timestamp for `Watcher` rangefeed

With MVCC GC operation enabled in KVNemesis, there is a possiblity
that with low GC TTL, GC is performed on data that hasn't been
consumed by the rangefeed of the Watcher, which would cause
us to get stuck in an infinite retry loop after receiving
a rangefeed error. This PR introduces a protected timestamp for
the Watcher rangefeed, to prevent this issue.

Release note: None

title: roachprod-microbench: fix missing warning-level in Slack
body:
The per-benchmark threshold change (#163839) added a magnitude filter to `isRegression`, requiring `abs(delta) >= threshold`. However, the Slack emoji logic still compared against the same threshold to choose between orange diamond (warning) and red triangle (significant), making orange diamonds effectively unreachable.

Fix by using a two-tier system: regressions at half the threshold and above are included in the Slack report with an orange diamond, and regressions at or above the full threshold get a red triangle. For the default 20% threshold this means 10-19.99% gets a warning indicator and
>= 20% gets a significant indicator.

The GitHub issue posting thresholds are unchanged.

Fixes: #165294

Release note: None

title: jobs: store DistSQL diagram URLs as .url files
body:
## Summary

Stacked on #165447.

- Change `addDistSQLDiagram` to write the raw DistSQL diagram URL as a
  `.url` file instead of wrapping it in HTML at write time.
- `ReadExecutionDetailFile` now generates the HTML redirect wrapper on
  the fly when a `.html` file is requested and a corresponding `.url`
  backing file exists, following the same virtual-file pattern used for
  `.binpb` → `.binpb.txt`. Legacy `.html` files continue to be served
  as-is.
- `ListExecutionDetailFiles` maps `.url` files to `.html` virtual
  entries so the UI sees `.html` filenames without changes.

This separation enables future inline viewing of the URL content
without the HTML redirect wrapping.

Epic: None
Release note: None

corresponding `.url` backing file, following the same virtual-file
pattern used for `.binpb` → `.binpb.txt`. Legacy `.html` files written
before this change continue to be served as-is.

title: ui/jobs: fix popup blocker on View button in job profiler
body:
## Summary

- The "View" button in the job profiler's Advanced Debugging tab called
  `window.open()` inside an async `.then()` callback, causing popup
  blockers to block it in every modern browser.
- Fix by calling `window.open("", "_blank")` synchronously in the click
  handler, then setting `newWindow.location.href` after the async fetch
  completes. On failure, close the blank window.

Epic: None
Release note: None
author: dt

commits.message:
ui/jobs: fix popup blocker on View button in job profiler

The "View" button in the job profiler's execution detail files table
called window.open() inside an async .then() callback, which every
modern browser's popup blocker blocks. Fix this by opening the window
synchronously in the click handler and setting its location after the
fetch completes. On failure, close the blank window.

Epic: None
Release note: None

commits.message:
catalog/lease: emit transaction updates asynchronously

Previously, we re-used the existing channel logic for generating
transaction update events. While this was logically correct, it could
lead to test flakes inside our leasing tests, since they intentionally
block the go routine processing events for the range feed . To address
this, patch directly handles the processing the descriptors updates
inside: generateDescriptorTxnInfo. This patch also fixes a bug where new
timestamps could have been published before the version was available
by moving the checkpoint processing into the refresh task.

Fixes: #165162
Fixes: #164822

Release note: None

catalog/lease: deflake TestLeaseManagerLockedTimestampBasic

Update TestLeaseManagerLockedTimestampBasic to use modification time for
new descriptor version detection. This eliminates any timing holes in
this test.

title: ui: migrate overview page components from Redux to SWR
body:
Migrate the three main overview page components (NodesOverview,
ClusterOverview, ClusterVisualization) from Redux/Saga data fetching
to SWR hooks.

## Commits

**ui: migrate nodesOverview from Redux to SWR hooks**
Replace the three Redux `connect()` HOCs (`NodesMainConnected`,
`NodesConnected`, `DecommissionedNodesConnected`) with a single
`NodesMain` component using `useNodesSummary()`. Convert reselect
selectors to plain functions called via `useMemo`.

**ui: fix useNodes/useLiveness SWR hooks for parity with Redux**
Ensure the SWR hooks return the same shape/defaults as the Redux
selectors they replace (empty arrays instead of undefined, proper
loading states).

**ui: move sumNodeStats to cluster-ui for shared use**
Extract `sumNodeStats` and `NodeSummaryStats` from db-console's
`redux/nodes.ts` into `cluster-ui/src/nodes/nodeSummaryStats.ts`
so both Redux selectors and SWR-based components can use them.

**ui: migrate ClusterOverview from Redux to SWR**
Replace `connect()` + `createSelector` with `useNodesSummary()` +
`useMemo(() => sumNodeStats(...))`. Remove the `ClusterSummaryConnected`
wrapper entirely.

**ui: migrate ClusterVisualization from Redux to SWR**
Convert the class component to a functional component. Create a new
`useCluster` SWR hook for the `_admin/v1/cluster` endpoint, replacing
`refreshCluster` dispatch + Redux state. Update tests to mock the
hook instead of passing Redux props.

Part-of: CRDB-61192
Epic: CRDB-58145
Release note: None
author: kyle-a-wong

reviews.body: > @jasonlmfong reviewed 14 files and all commit messages, and made 2 comments. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165430#-:-OnbsrFtAY0FkVrNsu-v:bnfp4nl)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on kyle-a-wong). ___ *[`pkg/ui/workspaces/db-console/src/views/cluster/containers/nodesOverview/index.tsx` line 657 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165430#-OnblLJU09VnP_so5nlS:-OnblLJU09VnP_so5nlT:b-mndf1s) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/f69574da33a5d376eb3e9cc40171c538019a983f/pkg/ui/workspaces/db-console/src/views/cluster/containers/nodesOverview/index.tsx#L657)):* > ```XML > > // Partition nodes into live vs decommissioned, mirroring the logic > // previously in the partitionedStatuses Redux selector. > ``` nit: don't need to mention the old code

commits.message:
ui: migrate nodesOverview from Redux to SWR hooks

Replace the three Redux `connect()` HOCs (`NodesMainConnected`,
`NodesConnected`, `DecommissionedNodesConnected`) with a single
`NodesMain` component that uses the `useNodesSummary()` SWR hook
from cluster-ui for data fetching.

Changes:
- Replace `refreshNodes`/`refreshLiveness` dispatch actions and the
  `useEffect` polling loop with SWR's automatic fetch-on-mount.
- Convert `liveNodesTableDataSelector` and
  `decommissionedNodesTableDataSelector` from reselect selectors
  to plain exported functions (`liveNodesTableData`,
  `decommissionedNodesTableData`), called via `useMemo`.
- Inline the `partitionedStatuses` logic (filtering decommissioned
  nodes) into a `useMemo` inside `NodesMain`.
- Remove `LocalSetting` sort state and the `NodeCategoryListProps`
  interface (sort props were unused by the presentational components).
- Remove imports: `connect`, `createSelector`, `refreshNodes`,
  `refreshLiveness`, `LocalSetting`, `selectNodesSummaryValid`,
  `AdminUIState`, `nodesSummarySelector`, `partitionedStatuses`.
- Update tests to call the new plain functions directly instead of
  using `.resultFunc()` on reselect selectors.

The Redux reducers/actions remain untouched since 20+ other components
still consume them.

Epic: CRDB-58145
Release note: None

ui: fix useNodes/useLiveness SWR hooks for parity with Redux

The `useNodes` hook in cluster-ui had two gaps compared to the Redux
`CachedDataReducer` path:

1. Missing store metrics rollup: The hook returned raw node metrics
   without rolling up store-level metrics (replicas, capacity.used,
   capacity.available, etc.) into the node's top-level metrics map.
   This caused the nodes overview page to show "NaN %" for capacity
   usage and empty replica counts.

2. Missing polling: Neither `useNodes` nor `useLiveness` supported
   polling, so they only fetched on mount. The Redux path polled
   every 10s via useEffect + CachedDataReducer invalidation. Without
   polling, the nodes table showed stale data while the summary bar
   (still Redux-connected) updated correctly.

Fixes:
- Apply `accumulateMetrics()` (already in cluster-ui's util/proto)
  inside `useNodes` via `useMemo` to roll up store metrics.
- Add an optional `refreshInterval` parameter to `useNodes`,
  `useLiveness`, and `useNodesSummary`. Callers opt into polling by
  passing `{ refreshInterval: 10000 }`. The default is no polling,
  so existing consumers (database pages, node selector) are
  unaffected.
- Pass `refreshInterval: 10000` in the nodesOverview consumer.

Epic: CRDB-58145
Release note: None

ui: move sumNodeStats to cluster-ui for shared use

Extract the `sumNodeStats` function and `NodeSummaryStats` type from
`db-console/src/redux/nodes.ts` into `cluster-ui/src/nodes/nodeSummaryStats.ts`
so both Redux selectors and SWR-based components can use the same
implementation. Add `draining` to `NodeSummaryStats.nodeCounts` to
match the db-console version.

The db-console `redux/nodes.ts` re-exports both symbols to keep
existing consumers working without changes.

Epic: CRDB-58145
Release note: None

ui: migrate ClusterOverview from Redux to SWR

Replace the Redux `connect()` pattern in ClusterSummary with the
`useNodesSummary()` SWR hook. The component now computes node sums
via `useMemo` + the shared `sumNodeStats` function instead of the
Redux `nodeSumsSelector`.

Removed: `connect`, `createSelector`, `refreshNodes`,
`refreshLiveness`, `nodeSumsSelector`, `AdminUIState`, and the
`ClusterSummaryConnected` wrapper. SWR handles data fetching
automatically on mount.

Epic: CRDB-58145
Release note: None

ui: migrate ClusterVisualization from Redux to SWR

Create a `useCluster` SWR hook in cluster-ui that fetches cluster
info from `_admin/v1/cluster`, replacing the Redux `refreshCluster`
action and `cachedData.cluster` state.

Convert the `ClusterVisualization` class component to a functional
component and replace Redux `connect(mapStateToProps, { refreshCluster })`
with the `useCluster()` hook. License and enterprise state are now
derived directly from the hook's response data.

Update tests to mock `useCluster` instead of passing Redux props.

Epic: CRDB-58145
Release note: None

title: roachtest/operations: improve cleanup robustness and fix misc issues
body:
Extract cleanup into executeCleanup with its own panic recovery so that a panic during cleanup does not crash the operation worker. Move the dependency check phase after the deferred cleanup setup so that failures during dep checks are properly handled. Also fix a SQL syntax error in the RESTORE job dependency check query, redirect stderr to stdout in the operations run script, and add the attributes/datadog exporter to the workload setup pipeline.

title: sql: add description column to SHOW ALL output
body:
PostgreSQL's SHOW ALL returns three columns: name, setting, description.
CockroachDB's SHOW ALL previously only returned two: variable, value.
Now that all session variables have descriptions, expose them as a third
column to match PostgreSQL's behavior.

This adds a `description` column to the `crdb_internal.session_variables`
virtual table and includes it in the `SHOW ALL` delegate query.

Resolves: #101715

Release note (sql change): `SHOW ALL` now returns a third column,
`description`, containing a human-readable description of each session
variable. This matches the PostgreSQL behavior of `SHOW ALL`.
author: rafiss

CockroachDB's SHOW ALL previously only returned two: variable, value.

column to match PostgreSQL's behavior.

virtual table and includes it in the `SHOW ALL` delegate query.

title: sql/catalog: resolve temp schemas from other sessions by ID
body:
Temporary schemas have no descriptor in system.descriptor — only namespace entries in system.namespace. The by-ID resolution path (lookupTemporary) only checked the current session's data via TemporarySchemaProvider, so temp schemas from other sessions couldn't be resolved by ID even though they could be found by name.

This caused failures when resolving a table from another session's temp schema, then trying to resolve its parent schema by ID. A workaround existed in pg_catalog.go that iterated all schemas to find the match.

Fix this by enhancing the by-ID resolution path to check the catalog reader's cached namespace entries as a fallback when the session-local check returns nil. Also fix IsDescIDKnownToNotExist to not incorrectly report temp schema IDs as non-existent after ScanAll (since they have namespace entries but no descriptors). With these fixes, the pg_catalog.go workaround is no longer needed and is removed.

Resolves: #97822

commits.message:
sql/catalog: resolve temp schemas from other sessions by ID

Temporary schemas have no descriptor in system.descriptor — only
namespace entries in system.namespace. The by-ID resolution path
(lookupTemporary) only checked the current session's data via
TemporarySchemaProvider, so temp schemas from other sessions couldn't
be resolved by ID even though they could be found by name.

This caused failures when resolving a table from another session's
temp schema, then trying to resolve its parent schema by ID. A
workaround existed in pg_catalog.go that iterated all schemas to
find the match.

Fix this by enhancing the by-ID resolution path to check the catalog
reader's cached namespace entries as a fallback when the session-local
check returns nil. Also fix IsDescIDKnownToNotExist to not incorrectly
report temp schema IDs as non-existent after ScanAll (since they have
namespace entries but no descriptors). With these fixes, the
pg_catalog.go workaround is no longer needed and is removed.

Resolves: #97822

Release note (bug fix): Fixed a bug where temporary tables created
in one session could fail to appear in pg_catalog queries from
another session because the parent temporary schema could not be
resolved by ID.

title: mmaprototype: clamp fractionAbove denominator to significance floor
body:
Previously, fractionAbove in loadSummaryForDimension was computed as
(load - mean) / mean. When the mean was small, trivial absolute
differences produced large fractions, triggering unnecessary replica moves.

Now, the denominator is clamped to max(meanLoad, significanceFloor(dim,
capacity)). For dimensions with known capacity, the floor is 5% of
capacity. For dimensions with unknown capacity, we use per-dimension
constants: 2 MiB/s for WriteBandwidth, 100ms/s for CPURate, and 100 MiB
for ByteSize. At high loads where the mean exceeds the floor, the clamp
has no effect.

The floor effectively sets the minimum absolute difference needed to
trigger rebalancing at low loads. Since overloadSlow fires at 10% above
the denominator, the real-world thresholds are 10% of the floor: 200 KiB/s
for WriteBandwidth, 10ms/s for CPURate, and 10 MiB for ByteSize. Anything
smaller is treated as noise.

Epic: [CRDB-55052](https://cockroachlabs.atlassian.net/browse/CRDB-55052)
Informs: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/164539
Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
author: angeladietz

reviews.body: I didn't look at the asim test changes. > @sumeerbhola reviewed 1 file and made 4 comments. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165383#-:-OnZ5z5w2CZ62NODVwaD:brpdjuq)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on angeladietz). ___ *[`pkg/kv/kvserver/allocator/mmaprototype/load.go` line 604 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165383#-OnZ3Qs88eKq_jrp_u3y:-OnZ3Qs88eKq_jrp_u3z:b8hcx02) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/0b39d3851e662bffd0858b586a894041c95f3d77/pkg/kv/kvserver/allocator/mmaprototype/load.go#L604)):* > ```Go > switch dim { > case WriteBandwidth: > return 5 << 20 // 5 MiB/s > ``` 5MiB/s is too high. The WriteBandwidth is what we see at the KV layer, before any of the write amplification. Why not 128KiB/s, akin to what we had before in the flawed logic? ___ *[`pkg/kv/kvserver/allocator/mmaprototype/load.go` line 606 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165383#-OnZ3IIz3bwycTe8X9yS:-OnZ3IIz3bwycTe8X9yT:b-tgy8vz) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/0b39d3851e662bffd0858b586a894041c95f3d77/pkg/kv/kvserver/allocator/mmaprototype/load.go#L606)):* > ```Go > return 5 << 20 // 5 MiB/s > case CPURate: > return 100_000_000 // 100ms/s = 5% of 2 vCPU > ``` It is worth adding a comment that we never expect to take this path for CPURate and ByteSize since they will have a capacity, so this is just defensive. ___ *[`pkg/kv/kvserver/allocator/mmaprototype/load.go` line 675 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165383#-OnZ4FofEddeeQ1mqzqi:-OnZ4FofEddeeQ1mqzqj:b-4omzm) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/0b39d3851e662bffd0858b586a894041c95f3d77/pkg/kv/kvserver/allocator/mmaprototype/load.go#L675)):* > ```Go > summaryUpperBound = loadNormal > }

Previously, sumeerbhola wrote…
5MiB/s is too high. The WriteBandwidth is what we see at the KV layer, before any of the write amplification. Why not 128KiB/s, akin to what we had before in the flawed logic?
It's true that sending (say) 1kb of user data to disk through the LSM can cause 20-30 kb of disk writes (in steady state, over time, as it's compacted down and the LSM has become big under this workload). But a value of 5MiB/s just means that when the mean is below 5MiB/s, we require any store to be 512KiB (10% of the floor) above the (actual) mean to count as overloaded. Examples: mean = 1mb/s, store = 1.4mb/s -> won't count as overloaded (1.5mb/s would) mean = 10kb/s, store = 510kb/s -> overloaded (just barely) [this would need lots of stores obviously] mean = 4mb, store = 4.5mb/s -> just at the overload threshold For actual disk write bandwidth utilization, this might translate to a difference of 12.8MB/s. I'm curious what @sumeerbhola thinks about that but that seems broadly reasonable. Would we really want to move replicas around to correct a 12mb/s disk write bandwidth imbalance when the mean is low, given how choppy disk write usage is anyway (thanks to compactions)? And this is already

> @sumeerbhola made 2 comments. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165383)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (and 1 stale) (waiting on angeladietz and tbg). ___ *[`pkg/kv/kvserver/allocator/mmaprototype/load.go` line 604 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165383#-OnZ3Qs88eKq_jrp_u3y:-Onbe65r65BVgyIXwwNQ:bhwdbuq) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/0b39d3851e662bffd0858b586a894041c95f3d77/pkg/kv/kvserver/allocator/mmaprototype/load.go#L604)):* Thanks for the clarification. > this might translate to a difference of 12.8MB/s So a write-amp of ~25? The mean could be below 5MiB/s and with a write-amp of 25 that translates to disk write bandwidth of 125MiB/s. For default gp3 bandwidth capacity (combined read and write) of 125MiB/s, this can be significant since it is close to the capacity. And it is possible that write amp is higher (though that should be rarer now with value separation) How about dropping this to 2 MiB/s? Then with a write-amp of 25, it corresponds to mean disk write bandwidth of 50MiB/s, and a difference of 5MiB/s will be significant, IIUC. > @tbg made 2 comments. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165383)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (and 1 stale) (waiting on angeladietz, sumeerbhola, and wenyihu6). ___ *[`pkg/kv/kvserver/allocator/mmaprototype/load.go` line 604 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165383#-OnZ3Qs88eKq_jrp_u3y:-OnbrVYX4MBKuYMRN6vg:b-un9hha) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/0b39d3851e662bffd0858b586a894041c95f3d77/pkg/kv/kvserver/allocator/mmaprototype/load.go#L604)):* > So a write-amp of ~25? Yes, this was assuming a write amp of 25. > The mean could be below 5MiB/s and with a write-amp of 25 that translates to disk write bandwidth of 125MiB/s. Well, if the mean is exactly 5mb/s, and the w-amp is 25, then the average physical disk bandwidth used is 125mb/s. But note that there is no more clamping (since we're at the 5mb threshold already). A store would have to hit physical write bandwidth of 25*110%*5 = 137mb/s until it looks overloaded to the allocator. If you're concerned about this, you're not concerned about the clamping but about the 10% threshold - which does not see the write-amp factor. I think the bottom line is, we are again dealing with a logical quantity when things generally "make sense" only when we are dealing with a physical one. Maybe we should do something similar to @wenyihu6's CPU work conversion, and scale up all write bandwidths by the w-amp at the boundary (together with a corresponding change for the load, which could become the actual measured write load or maybe even aggregate throughput of the store's underlying device). Let me know if I'm missing something here, but this seems to be a conversation about modeling write bandwidth at the physical level, not one about the significanceFloor introduced here, where 5mb/s does make sense to me still. I'm not against 2mb/s but don't see why it's better either. ___ *[`pkg/kv/kvserver/asim/tests/t

> @angeladietz made 4 comments. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165383)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (and 1 stale) (waiting on sumeerbhola, tbg, and wenyihu6). ___ *[`pkg/kv/kvserver/allocator/mmaprototype/load.go` line 604 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165383#-OnZ3Qs88eKq_jrp_u3y:-Oncdtmg8NpGir_VqdPK:bbenlc9) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/0b39d3851e662bffd0858b586a894041c95f3d77/pkg/kv/kvserver/allocator/mmaprototype/load.go#L604)):*

Let me know if I'm missing something here, but this seems to be a conversation about modeling write bandwidth at the physical level, not one about the significanceFloor introduced here, where 5mb/s does make sense to me still. I'm not

> @sumeerbhola made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165383)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (and 1 stale) (waiting on angeladietz, tbg, and wenyihu6). ___ *[`pkg/kv/kvserver/allocator/mmaprototype/load.go` line 604 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165383#-OnZ3Qs88eKq_jrp_u3y:-Ond-9dl8ulOZ0PjheF6:bwgv5g7) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/0b39d3851e662bffd0858b586a894041c95f3d77/pkg/kv/kvserver/allocator/mmaprototype/load.go#L604)):* > If you're concerned about this, you're not concerned about the clamping but about the 10% threshold - which does not see the write-amp factor. I am concerned with the clamping and not the 10%. Here is an example. Say the mean is 2MiB/s, and write amp is 25. So the mean disk write bandwidth is 50MiB/s. We have a store at 2.5MiB/s - delta. Its disk write bandwidth is 62.5MiB/s - delta. With the 5MiB/s denominator, it is not considered overloaded. While with the 2MiB/s denominator, it is overloaded. And this hasn't considered the disk read bandwidth, which we are not modeling or controlling, but is a factor in whether the disk is reaching saturation. With customers running with 125MiB/s total bandwidth disks, the difference between 50MiB/s and 62.5MiB/s of write bandwidth is significant -- not just because of read bandwidth, but also because we are using very smoothed values (over 30min?) while we can see bursts at 1s time scales that have an effect on tail latency. So it is a factor to consider when determining a constant or a default value for a cluster setting. I don't think there is any benefit of modeling the write amp when we don't know the provisioned bandwidth.

mmaprototype: clamp fractionAbove denominator to significance floor

Previously, fractionAbove in loadSummaryForDimension was computed as
(load - mean) / mean. When the mean was small, trivial absolute
differences produced large fractions, triggering unnecessary replica moves.

Now, the denominator is clamped to max(meanLoad, significanceFloor(dim,
capacity)). For dimensions with known capacity, the floor is 5% of
capacity. For dimensions with unknown capacity, we use per-dimension
constants: 2 MiB/s for WriteBandwidth, 100ms/s for CPURate, and 100 MiB
for ByteSize. At high loads where the mean exceeds the floor, the clamp
has no effect.

The floor effectively sets the minimum absolute difference needed to
trigger rebalancing at low loads. Since overloadSlow fires at 10% above
the denominator, the real-world thresholds are 10% of the floor: 200 KiB/s
for WriteBandwidth, 10ms/s for CPURate, and 10 MiB for ByteSize. Anything
smaller is treated as noise.

title: obs: instrument Prometheus scrape volume with meta-metrics
body:
Add three new metrics that describe the volume of each `/_status/vars` Prometheus scrape:

- `obs.metric_export.name.count` (Gauge): number of metric families
- `obs.metric_export.line.count` (Gauge): total individual time series
- `obs.metric_export.child.count` (GaugeVec, labeled `metric_name`): per-parent child instance counts for PrometheusIterable metrics

These are updated at the end of each scrape cycle, so values reflect the previous scrape (first scrape reports zeros). The child counting piggybacks on the existing `Each()` iteration in `ScrapeRegistry`—no extra traversal.

The two Gauge metrics are persisted to TSDB; the GaugeVec is Prometheus-only by design. Only the `/_status/vars` endpoint (useStaticLabels=false) updates these metrics.
author: angles-n-daemons

reviews.body: lgtm
Metric additions in roachprod opentelemetery LGTM.

title: kvserver/mmaprototype: add BenchmarkRebalanceStores
body:
## Summary

Reduce per-iteration allocations in the MMA rebalance loop from ~2000 to ~160
allocs/op, primarily by guarding expensive logging and pooling hot slices/maps.

```
name                old time/op    new time/op    delta
RebalanceStores-10     246µs ± 3%     104µs ± 2%  -57.87%  (p=0.000 n=10+10)

name                old alloc/op   new alloc/op   delta
RebalanceStores-10     101kB ± 0%      19kB ± 0%  -81.17%  (p=0.000 n=9+8)

name                old allocs/op  new allocs/op  delta
RebalanceStores-10     1.99k ± 0%     0.16k ± 0%  -91.75%  (p=0.000 n=10+10)
```

### Commit arc

1. **Add BenchmarkRebalanceStores** — microbenchmark for the rebalance loop
   (4 stores, 1000 ranges, 100 hot). Uses `undoPendingChange` to restore state
   between iterations. Baseline: 2013 allocs/op.

2. **Annotate top allocation sites** — TODO comments at each site found via
   memory profiling, guiding the subsequent commits.

3. **Guard logging in sortTargetCandidateSetAndPick** — extract
   `formatCandidatesLog` to a package-level function with an internal
   `ExpensiveLogEnabled` check, removing redundant guards at all 6 call sites.
   2013 → 1689 allocs/op.

4. **Guard top-K debug logging** — wrap the `redact.StringBuilder` +
   `LoadVector` formatting block with `ExpensiveLogEnabled`.
   1689 → 778 allocs/op.

5. **Guard lease-transfer candidate logging** — prevent boxing `candsPL` into
   `interface{}` when verbose logging is off. 778 → 678 allocs/op.

6. **Pool slice and map allocations in rebalance loop** — introduce generic
   `slicePool[T]` and `mapPool[K, V]` wrappers around `sync.Pool`, replacing
   manual pool boilerplate and the `rebalanceEnv.scratch` struct (which had
   aliasing hazards). Five pools cover slices (`storeAndLeasePreference`,
   `StoreID`, `candidateInfo`) and maps (`NodeID→*NodeLoad`,
   `StoreID→struct{}`). `candidatesToMoveLease` accepts a reusable buffer.
   678 → 160 allocs/op.

Epic: CRDB-55052
author: tbg

some nits but lgtm overall, nothing blocking!

commits.message:
kvserver/mmaprototype: add BenchmarkRebalanceStores

Add a microbenchmark for the MMA rebalance loop (rebalanceStores).
The benchmark uses the existing undoPendingChange production code to
reverse mutations after each iteration, plus targeted resets of leaked
fields (lastFailedChange, overload times, meansMemo cache).

Setup: 4 stores, 1000 ranges (100 hot, 900 cold), store 1 overloaded.

    BenchmarkRebalanceStores  100  323k ns/op  102kB/op  2013 allocs/op

Epic: none
Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

kvserver/mmaprototype: annotate top allocation sites in rebalance loop

Add TODO(tbg) comments at each allocation site found via
BenchmarkRebalanceStores memory profiling (4 stores, 1000 ranges,
100 hot). The allocs/op counts are from a 1000-iteration run.

Epic: none
Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

kvserver/mmaprototype: guard logging in sortTargetCandidateSetAndPick

The `formatCandidatesLog` closure and `redact.StringBuilder` were
allocated on every call to `sortTargetCandidateSetAndPick`, even when
verbose logging at level 2 was disabled. Additionally, the standalone
VEventf for discarded candidates boxed multiple arguments into
interfaces unconditionally.

Extract `formatCandidatesLog` to a package-level function and guard all
call sites (plus the standalone VEventf) with `log.ExpensiveLogEnabled`.

    BenchmarkRebalanceStores  1999 -> 1689 allocs/op (-310)

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

kvserver/mmaprototype: guard top-K debug logging with ExpensiveLogEnabled

The top-K debug logging block in `processSheddingStore` builds a
`redact.StringBuilder` and formats `LoadVector` values for every range in
the top-K set on every call, even when verbose logging at level 2 is
disabled. Wrap the entire block with `log.ExpensiveLogEnabled`.

    BenchmarkRebalanceStores  1689 -> 778 allocs/op (-911)

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

kvserver/mmaprototype: guard lease-transfer logging with ExpensiveLogEnabled

The VEventf call logging lease-transfer candidates boxes `candsPL`
(a `storeSet`) to `interface{}`, triggering formatting even when verbose
logging at level 2 is disabled. Guard with `log.ExpensiveLogEnabled`.

    BenchmarkRebalanceStores  778 -> 678 allocs/op (-100)

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

kvserver/mmaprototype: pool slice and map allocations in rebalance loop

Reduce per-iteration allocations by pooling hot slices and maps via generic
`slicePool[T]` and `mapPool[K, V]` wrappers around `sync.Pool`. These handle
the `New` func, type assertion on `get`, and `clear`+reset on `put`.

Five pools are introduced:
- `storeAndLeasePreferencePool`, `storeIDPool`, `candidateInfoPool` for slices
- `nodeLoadMapPool`, `storeIDStructMapPool` for maps

The `rebalanceEnv.scratch` struct (which had aliasing hazards) is removed in
favor of these pools. `candidatesToMoveLease` now accepts a reusable buffer.

678 → 160 allocs/op.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>
