title: sql: partially fix data race in CollationEnvironment
body:
#### sql: partially fix data race in CollationEnvironment

Reset CollationEnv to a zero value in eval.Context.Copy() so that
each copied context gets its own collation cache and buffer.
CollationEnvironment is not safe for concurrent use: the cache map,
the collate.Collator instances (which have mutable internal iter
buffers), and the shared collate.Buffer all race when accessed from
multiple goroutines.

I believe that this fix is unlikely to regress performance for
existing workloads; it only affects places where we copy the
`eval.Context` (such as DistSQL flow setup), in which case we are
doing expensive setup work anyway. In the common case (either no
collated strings, or no remote DistSQL flows), the fix has no
effect and will not increase allocations.

Note that this is only a partial fix that affects cases where
the `eval.Context` is already copied. As a follow-up, we should
audit uses of CollationEnvironment (and similar EvalContext fields)
and come up with a more general solution.

Informs: #110322

Release note: None

#### sql: fix data race in sampler CollationEnvironment

The samplerProcessor and sampleAggregator both use FlowCtx.EvalCtx
directly, without copying. On the gateway node, these processors run
in separate goroutines within the same flow: the sampler processes
rows while the sample aggregator concurrently processes sample rows
arriving from remote nodes. When both encounter collated strings
exceeding the 400-byte sample size limit, both call truncateDatum ->
NewDCollatedString, racing on the shared CollationEnv.

Fix by giving each processor its own CollationEnvironment. This also
narrows the SampleReservoir API to take *tree.CollationEnvironment
instead of *eval.Context, since CollationEnv is all it uses.

Informs: #110322

Release note (bug fix): Fixed a bug existing since before v23.1
that could cause row sampling for table statistics to crash a node
due to a data race when processing a collated string column with
values larger than 400 bytes.
author: DrewKimball

reviews.body: > @yuzefovich reviewed 4 files and all commit messages, and made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165260)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on DrewKimball and mw5h). ___ *[`pkg/sql/sem/eval/context.go` line 621 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165260#-OnJkMQ4CodbR7QyTbM4:-OnJkMQ5D6mQ3TFPUTsW:b-z39165) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/e2189d6d1c4ef653201951f94b204cb643aabc57/pkg/sql/sem/eval/context.go#L621)):* > ```Go > // Copy returns a copy of the EvalCtx that can safely be used concurrently with > // the original. > func (ec *Context) Copy() *Context { > ``` Hm, this fix seems rather fragile to me. As of 40addd2f951ee3791d85e2f4a489e2337663fab8, we only make a copy `eval.Context` when it's explicitly mutated by a processor or an operator, so there is nothing related to concurrency in the requirements. For the reproduction in this PR, where we handle the filter with COLLATE via `filtererProcessor` - we unconditionally copy the eval context, so this fixes this particular reproduction, but I think it'd be possible come up with a repro that this patch doesn't fix.

> @yuzefovich made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165260)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on DrewKimball and mw5h). ___ *[`pkg/sql/sem/eval/context.go` line 621 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165260#-OnJkMQ4CodbR7QyTbM4:-OnJm9gD0iZj5u5Gv8JH:b-72di56) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/e2189d6d1c4ef653201951f94b204cb643aabc57/pkg/sql/sem/eval/context.go#L621)):*
Previously, yuzefovich (Yahor Yuzefovich) wrote…
Hm, this fix seems rather fragile to me. As of 40addd2f951ee3791d85e2f4a489e2337663fab8, we only make a copy `eval.Context` when it's explicitly mutated by a processor or an operator, so there is nothing related to concurrency in the requirements. For the reproduction in this PR, where we handle the filter with COLLATE via `filteredProcessor` - we unconditionally copy the eval context, so this fixes this particular reproduction, but I think it'd be possible come up with a repro that this patch doesn't fix.
Note how both sampler and sampleAggregator processors explicitly use `FlowCtx.EvalCtx` which is shared across the whole flow, so this Copy shouldn't help with the stats collection panic. I wonder whether the backportable mitigation should be modifying both stats processors to store the collation environment explicitly (so each goroutine would definitely get its own copy), and then we can discuss the full fix separately. Thoughts?

> @DrewKimball made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165260)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on mw5h and yuzefovich). ___ *[`pkg/sql/sem/eval/context.go` line 621 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165260#-OnJkMQ4CodbR7QyTbM4:-OnTgQ4X-4yDfSkqNZZ5:b-4136sk) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/f24ede2499c7030575fe0cc6c1e9b563de8c5f17/pkg/sql/sem/eval/context.go#L621)):*
Previously, yuzefovich (Yahor Yuzefovich) wrote…
Note how both sampler and sampleAggregator processors explicitly use `FlowCtx.EvalCtx` which is shared across the whole flow, so this Copy shouldn't help with the stats collection panic. I wonder whether the backportable mitigation should be modifying both stats processors to store the collation environment explicitly (so each goroutine would definitely get its own copy), and then we can discuss the full fix separately. Thoughts?
Really good catch. I've added a commit with a repro for the sampler-specific data race and the fix you suggested. Maybe we backport both and then think about a complete fix/refactor as a follow-up?

Thanks! > @yuzefovich reviewed 5 files and all commit messages, and made 5 comments. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165260#-:-OnTmOli84GNC7NuvjV_:b-ftzx47)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on DrewKimball and mw5h). ___ *[`pkg/sql/sem/eval/context.go` line 621 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165260#-OnJkMQ4CodbR7QyTbM4:-OnTmFMO9dzkis6E6JKG:b-ucpag2) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/f24ede2499c7030575fe0cc6c1e9b563de8c5f17/pkg/sql/sem/eval/context.go#L621)):*
Previously, DrewKimball (Drew Kimball) wrote…
Really good catch. I've added a commit with a repro for the sampler-specific data race and the fix you suggested. Maybe we backport both and then think about a complete fix/refactor as a follow-up?
Yeah, I agree that backporting both makes sense - we're fully solving the problem for stats collections and partially solving it elsewhere. ___ *[`-- commits` line 2 at r2](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165260#-OnTm3iY2ThQGZk78bD7:-OnTm3iY2ThQGZk78bD8:b-fr8e0c):* nit: the commit message in the first commit should be updated indicating that only a subset of the problematic scenarios has been addressed, and that the general problem remains. ___ *[`-- commits` line 18 at r2](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165260#-OnTlncu7yF8mPBKQj0s:-OnTlncv6bwJvMJqIf3H:bcmevcr):* nit: let's not close the issue since we haven't yet solved it completely. ___ *[`-- commits` line 20 at r2](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165260#-OnTluxQ4CNDmakrzLGm:-OnTluxR0_X79Cwx6B-I:b3oxgl4):* nit: perhaps omit the release note from the first commit and only keep the one in the second commit.

title: obs/ash: add ASH instrumentation across KV and SQL execution
body:
This PR implements an initial set of instrumentation for ASH, covering parts of KV and SQL execution.

Benchmarking results with this set of instrumentation are included in the comment below and in the design doc (https://docs.google.com/document/d/164fCBKytBGiRDPPMezQDGGiQClVW0wJLAzCEmwb2QKo/edit?pli=1&tab=t.0)

See commits for details.

Part of: #164381
Part of: #164683
Fixes: #164542
Fixes: #164384

Release note: None
author: alyshanjahani-crl

reviews.body: > @yuzefovich partially reviewed 16 files and made 3 comments. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165259)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on alyshanjahani-crl, angles-n-daemons, dhartunian, DrewKimball, kyle-a-wong, and xxmplus). ___ *[`-- commits` line 31 at r3](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165259#-OnOhRjP4sHWw3Bwy4nX:-OnOhRjP4sHWw3Bwy4nY:bq2ri1p):* IIUC we need to call `SetWorkState` on each important goroutine, right? I think then we're currently missing instrumentation for the connExecutor goroutine that is then used as the "main" goroutine of the gateway flow if we use the vectorized engine. We have a "flow coordinator" as the root operator running on that goroutine, and the processor base covers `colflow.FlowCoordinator`, but we're missing instrumentation if `colflow.BatchFlowCoordinator` is used. In short, we should either instrument `colflow.BatchFlowCoordinator` or `flowinfra.Flow.Run` implementations. ___ *[`-- commits` line 37 at r3](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165259#-OnOfanL2KRyI3QwPbZa:-OnOfanL2KRyI3QwPbZb:b-poa07q):* There are also row-based Outbox (`flowinfra/outbox.go`) and inbox-like handler (a new goroutine in `processInboundStreamHelper` in `flowinfra/inbound.go`). It might be nice to instrument these as well. ___ *[`pkg/sql/flowinfra/flow.go` line 337 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165259#-OnOd-1C-MwX3zmswRIn:-OnOd-1C-MwX3zmswRIo:baf9gky) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/32f686d3cc216eea6b8821aa0f6c1bf523553fb6/pkg/sql/flowinfra/flow.go#L337)):* > ```Go > admissionInfo.WorkloadID = flowCtx.EvalCtx.WorkloadID > admissionInfo.AppNameID = flowCtx.EvalCtx.AppNameID > if flowCtx.NodeID != nil { > ``` nit: you can assume `flowCtx.NodeID` is non-nil. (I think only one test `TestNonVectorizedPanicDoesntHangServer` needs an adjustment.)

LGTM on the KV changes. Re: the benchmarks, I'm surprised you're not using `benchstat`. TBH I'd much rather look at standard output and would encourage you to provide it in that standard format. Attaching the raw benchmark results (as txt files gists for example) is going to be even better as it allows for ad-hoc analysis as well. FYI `benchstat` has a `-cols` argument so it can elegantly compare different benchmarks (ASH vs no ASH) for example. > @tbg reviewed 32 files and all commit messages, and made 6 comments. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165259#-:-OnR_g1D5OEvaaZJ6zZG:bxipk1v)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on alyshanjahani-crl, angles-n-daemons, dhartunian, DrewKimball, kyle-a-wong, and xxmplus). ___ *[`pkg/kv/kvclient/kvcoord/transport.go` line 217 at r2](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165259#-OnRYYzW5yV4UvhCWigT:-OnRYYzW5yV4UvhCWigU:b7a2fia) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/c84cd0a95351eae30c68d7d28cdf6f9b12804b92/pkg/kv/kvclient/kvcoord/transport.go#L217)):* > ```Go > if rpc.IsLocal(iface) { > gt.opts.metrics.LocalSentCount.Inc(1) > cleanup = ash.SetWorkState( > ``` these scopes can be nested, right? Because this one here will be (the call is local, so it'll hit the Replica which will open its own work states). ___ *[`pkg/kv/kvserver/replica_backpressure.go` line 196 at r2](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165259#-OnRYopG1ZZBMlotwif8:-OnRYopG1ZZBMlotwif9:bxcd1wt) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/c84cd0a95351eae30c68d7d28cdf6f9b12804b92/pkg/kv/kvserver/replica_backpressure.go#L196)):* > ```Go > return nil > } > tenantID, _ := roachpb.ClientTenantFromContext(ctx) > ``` It makes sense to restructure this so that the scope is only opened when we actually backpressure, i.e. put this into the `if first { ... ` below. ___ *[`pkg/kv/kvserver/replica_evaluate.go` line 219 at r2](https:

> @sumeerbhola reviewed 1 file and made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165259)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on alyshanjahani-crl, angles-n-daemons, dhartunian, DrewKimball, kyle-a-wong, and xxmplus). ___ *[`pkg/kv/kvserver/kvadmission/kvadmission.go` line 327 at r4](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165259#-OnS5O3I6QXB9XtdR93t:-OnS5O3I6QXB9XtdR93u:b-zi9pq4) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/21cd9ee50810ee836420c30137b2f04371dae04c/pkg/kv/kvserver/kvadmission/kvadmission.go#L327)):* > ```Go > } > var err error > admitted, err = kvflowHandle.Admit( > ``` This can block in Quorum Replication Flow Control. We will need to plumb stuff into `rac2.WaitForEval` where the actual waiting happens. We can reuse `WorkAdmission` for that too instead of adding to the `WorkEventType` enum.

commits.message:
obs/ash: plumb workload identity fields and fix tenantID defaulting

Adds AppNameID and GatewayNodeID fields to admission.WorkInfo so that
ASH samples flowing through admission and colflow paths carry full
workload identity. Fixes tenantID defaulting in work_state.go to use
SystemTenantID when the provided tenant is zero-valued. Populates the
new fields in NewFlowBase so that Inbox receives them, and plumbs
WorkloadID through MVCCGetOptions and kvadmission.

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

kv: instrument KV execution paths with ASH work states

Adds ASH instrumentation to KV execution paths including batch request
transport, lock table waiter, span latch manager, replica backpressure,
evaluation, rate limiting, send, write, and admission queue waiting.
Each call site sets a work state that the ASH sampler captures to
provide visibility into where time is spent in the KV layer.

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

sql: instrument SQL execution paths with ASH work states

Adds ASH instrumentation to SQL execution paths including processor
base, parallel unordered synchronizer, colrpc inbox/outbox, and plan
optimization. Fixes a bug where the Outbox metadata send path
hardcoded roachpb.SystemTenantID instead of using the flow context's
tenant. Adds appNameID() and gatewayNodeID() helpers to Outbox and
populates full WorkloadInfo (WorkloadID, AppNameID, GatewayNodeID) in
both Inbox and Outbox send/receive paths.

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

obs/ash: add ASH instrumentation tests and benchmarks

Adds TestASHLockContention to verify lock contention produces ASH
samples with the correct work event type and event name. Uses
testutils.SucceedsSoon to poll for LockWait samples instead of a
hard-coded sleep, avoiding flakiness under load. Adds BenchmarkASH to
measure ASH sampling overhead on SQL workloads, and a lint test to
track the number of ASH call sites.

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

kv: plumb ASH workload identity into rac2 WaitForEval

Plumb `ash.WorkloadInfo` through the replication admission control
call chain so that the ASH sampler can attribute time spent waiting
for replication flow control tokens to the correct workload.

The `TenantID` needed for `ash.SetWorkState` is sourced from the
existing `rangeController.opts.TenantID` rather than being plumbed
through the call chain, since the correct range controller is already
selected by the time we reach `WaitForEval`.

The instrumentation is placed in `token_counter.WaitForEval` around
the `reflect.Select` loop, which is where the goroutine actually
blocks waiting for quorum token availability. The call chain is:

  kvadmission.AdmitKVWork
    → kvflowcontrol.ReplicationAdmissionHandle.Admit
      → replica_rac2.Processor.AdmitForEval
        → rac2.RangeController.WaitForEval
          → rac2.WaitForEval (token_counter.go)
            → ash.SetWorkState(..., WorkAdmission, "ReplicationFlowControl")

Epic: none
Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

obs/ash: replace lint test with generated inventory file

Replace the `TestASHInstrumentationCount` lint test (which checked a
hardcoded count of `ash.SetWorkState` call sites) with a generated
inventory file at `pkg/obs/ash/ash_integration_points.txt`. This gives
a readable artifact listing all ASH integration points, CODEOWNERS-based
notification when instrumentation changes, and a better developer
experience via `./dev generate ash-inventory`.

The generator walks all non-test `.go` files under `pkg/`, finds
`ash.SetWorkState(` calls that aren't comment lines, and writes sorted
`file:line` entries. CI enforces the file is up to date via
`check-generated-code.sh`.

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

obs/ash: fix data race in _clearWorkState

_clearWorkState wrote `state.prev = nil` on a WorkState struct that
the sampler goroutine could be concurrently copying via rangeWorkStates
(`*value` dereference). Although the sampler does not read the prev
field from copied WorkStates, the struct copy constitutes a read of
all fields, triggering the race detector.

Remove the nil assignment. This is safe because:
- The sampler never uses prev from the copied WorkState.
- The prev WorkState is independently reachable via activeWorkStates
  (stored back into the map on the next line), so the dangling pointer
  does not keep any extra objects alive.
- reclaimRetiredWorkStates zeroes the entire struct (`*s = WorkState{}`)
  before returning it to the pool, clearing prev at that point.

Fixes TestASHLockContention under the race detector.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

logtestutils: extract telemetry stubs to break import cycle

The logtestutils package contained telemetry test stubs (StubTime,
StubQueryStats, StubTracingStatus) that import pkg/sql/execstats.
This created a dependency cycle when pkg/obs/ash tests imported
logtestutils for the log spy utilities:

  ash_test → logtestutils → execstats → ... → admission → ash

Move the telemetry stubs into a new sub-package
logtestutils/telemetrylogtestutils so that importing logtestutils
for LogSpy/StructuredLogSpy no longer transitively pulls in
execstats.

Epic: CRDB-44217

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: workload: add security_invoker view option to schemachange workload
body:
Stacked PR. Only review the top commit.

The security_invoker option is added to CRDB views in 26.2. This
change enables testing it with the random schema worklaod.
The test will create views with security_invoker unset (default),
false, and true. It also tests `ALTER VIEW SET/RESET (security_invoker)`.

Fixes: #165253

Release note: None
author: shghasemi

workload: add security_invoker view option to schemachange workload

The security_invoker option is added to CRDB views in 26.2. This
change enables testing it with the random schema worklaod.
The test will create views with security_invoker unset (default),
false, and true. It also tests `ALTER VIEW SET/RESET (security_invoker)`.

Fixes: #165253

Release note: None

title: schemachanger: support infer_rbr_region_col_using_constraint storage param
body:
Previously, the declarative schema changer did not support the `infer_rbr_region_col_using_constraint` storage parameter for REGIONAL BY ROW tables, falling back to the legacy schema changer via a NotImplementedError panic. This commit adds full support for SET and RESET of this parameter in the declarative schema changer.

The implementation includes:
- A new `SetTableRBRUsingConstraint` immediate mutation op that sets or clears the `RBRUsingConstraint` field on the table descriptor.
- Build-phase logic in `setRBRUsingConstraint` that validates the table is RBR, resolves the named constraint, validates it is a suitable FK (includes region column, has >1 column, region col is not computed, no computed cols reference region col), and emits the appropriate element. Both validated and unvalidated FK constraints are supported.
- Build-phase logic in `resetRBRUsingConstraint` that drops the existing `TableLocalityRegionalByRowUsingConstraint` element.
- Opgen transitions for toPublic and toAbsent, with an `rbrUsingConstraintAppliedLater` check to handle element replacement ordering when the constraint is being swapped.
- The `inferRegionUsingConstraintEnabled` cluster setting is moved to `sqlclustersettings` to be accessible from the schema changer build phase without circular imports.

Informs: #150946

Release note: None
author: rafiss

reviews.body: Great work! Is it worth adding an end-to-end test to confirm the schema change plan too? > @shghasemi reviewed 8 files and all commit messages, and made 2 comments. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165239#-:-OnYKS1pAFGd_yaUG6gb:bu5w74j)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on rafiss). ___ *[`pkg/sql/schemachanger/scplan/internal/opgen/opgen_table_locality_regional_by_row_using_constraint.go` line 30 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165239#-OnYKiByEKN1L8v2mO9N:-OnYKiByEKN1L8v2mO9O:b-vqvpny) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/4e01462e84d432f39b20531449a6104c8405eb9b/pkg/sql/schemachanger/scplan/internal/opgen/opgen_table_locality_regional_by_row_using_constraint.go#L30)):* > ```Go > to(scpb.Status_ABSENT, > emit(func(this *scpb.TableLocalityRegionalByRowUsingConstraint, md *opGenContext) *scop.SetTableRBRUsingConstraint { > if rbrUsingConstraintAppliedLater(this, md) { > ``` This is a nice way of handling the swap without rules!

i added end to end tests, good suggestion! it caught an issue i had to fix in rewrite.go > @rafiss made 2 comments. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165239#-:-OnZ5H3t7gUY1yLlMzT5:bsakvyq)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (and 1 stale) (waiting on shghasemi). ___ *[`pkg/sql/schemachanger/scplan/internal/opgen/opgen_table_locality_regional_by_row_using_constraint.go` line 30 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165239#-OnYKiByEKN1L8v2mO9N:-OnZ56njBuJ7-PId8PTr:b69023a) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/4e01462e84d432f39b20531449a6104c8405eb9b/pkg/sql/schemachanger/scplan/internal/opgen/opgen_table_locality_regional_by_row_using_constraint.go#L30)):*
Previously, shghasemi wrote…
This is a nice way of handling the swap without rules!
hmm, actually thinking about this more, i think we should use rules here to be more consistent with other schema changes. i've updated it

commits.message:
schemachanger: support infer_rbr_region_col_using_constraint storage param

Previously, the declarative schema changer did not support the
`infer_rbr_region_col_using_constraint` storage parameter for REGIONAL
BY ROW tables, falling back to the legacy schema changer via a
NotImplementedError panic. This commit adds full support for SET and
RESET of this parameter in the declarative schema changer.

The implementation includes:
- A new `SetTableRBRUsingConstraint` immediate mutation op that sets or
  clears the `RBRUsingConstraint` field on the table descriptor.
- Build-phase logic in `setRBRUsingConstraint` that validates the table
  is RBR, resolves the named constraint, validates it is a suitable FK
  (includes region column, has >1 column, region col is not computed, no
  computed cols reference region col), and emits the appropriate element.
  Both validated and unvalidated FK constraints are supported.
- Build-phase logic in `resetRBRUsingConstraint` that drops the existing
  `TableLocalityRegionalByRowUsingConstraint` element.
- Opgen transitions for toPublic and toAbsent, with a
  `SameStagePrecedence` dep rule to ensure the old element is dropped
  before the new one is added when swapping constraints.

- A fix in `pkg/sql/catalog/rewrite/rewrite.go` to clear
  `RBRUsingConstraint` during backup/restore if the referenced FK
  constraint was dropped because its referenced table is not being
  restored.
- The `inferRegionUsingConstraintEnabled` cluster setting is moved to
  `sqlclustersettings` to be accessible from the schema changer build

title: roachtest/task: add Cancel method to Group and ErrorGroup
body:
Groups previously had no way to cancel their tasks directly.
Callers that needed to cancel a group had to manually create a
cancelable context and thread it through via task.WithContext,
resulting in boilerplate:

```
groupCtx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
g := t.NewGroup(task.WithContext(groupCtx))
```

Add a Cancel() method to both the Group and ErrorGroup interfaces.
Cancel delegates to the existing cancelAll mechanism, which cancels
each task individually and sets expectedContextCancellation so that
errors from context cancellation are not reported as test failures.
Callers can simply call `group.Cancel()` to cancel the `group`.

Release note: None
Epic: None
author: herkolategan

reviews.body:
Thanks for getting this change in! I did feel like there was some unnecessary boilerplate in the API and this cleans it up nicely.

Left a couple of comments that I was wondering about.
LGTM!

commits.message:
roachtest/task: add Cancel method to Group and ErrorGroup

Groups previously had no way to cancel their tasks directly.
Callers that needed to cancel a group had to manually create a
cancelable context and thread it through via task.WithContext,
resulting in boilerplate:

    groupCtx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
    g := t.NewGroup(task.WithContext(groupCtx))

Add a Cancel() method to both the Group and ErrorGroup interfaces.
Cancel delegates to the existing cancelAll mechanism, which cancels
each task individually and sets expectedContextCancellation so that
errors from context cancellation are not reported as test failures.
Callers can simply call g.Cancel() or pass g.Cancel as a func()
value (e.g., return g.Cancel from a setup function, or pass it to
time.AfterFunc).

Release note: None

title: roachtest: jepsen - ignore analysis timeouts
body:
During its run, Jepsen can accumulate a very large operation history (e.g., due to transaction retries that Jepsen does not handle at the client level).

This causes the post-run linearizability analysis to exceed the 40 min. roachtest timeout, generating numerous issues that are test flakes.

This patch detects scenarios in which the test times out, but the Jepsen workload succeeded (indicated by "Run complete" in jepsen.log). This situation means the timeout happened during the analysis phase, which is known to be flaky. We return a `TransientFailure` to file the failure as an X-Infra-Flake.

Epic: none
Fixes: #164919
Release note: None
author: golgeek

commits.message:
roachtest: jepsen - ignore analysis timeouts

During its run, Jepsen can accumulate a very large operation history
(e.g., due to transaction retries that Jepsen does not handle at the
client level).

This causes the post-run linearizability analysis to exceed the 40 min.
roachtest timeout, generating numerous issues that are test flakes.

This patch detects scenarios in which the test times out, but the Jepsen
workload succeeded (indicated by "Run complete" in jepsen.log).
This situation means the timeout happened during the analysis phase,
which is known to be flaky. We return a `TransientFailure` to file the
failure as an X-Infra-Flake.

Epic: none
Fixes: #164919

title: sql/regions: respect num_replicas zone config ext. for super region
body:
Previously, `AddConstraintsForSuperRegion` computed `numReplicas` independently (e.g. 4 for a 2-region super region with zone survival) and generated constraints totaling that value. When a zone config extension subsequently overrode `num_replicas` to a lower value (e.g. 3), `ExtendZoneConfigWithRegionalIn` inherited the constraints from the first step (still totaling 4) but set `num_replicas` to 3. Zone config validation then rejected the config because constraints (4) exceeded num_replicas (3).

This commit fixes `AddConstraintsForSuperRegion` to check the Regional and RegionalIn zone config extensions for a `num_replicas` override before generating constraints. If the override is at least `numVoters`, it is used as the effective `numReplicas` for constraint distribution, ensuring the generated constraints are consistent with the final `num_replicas` after extensions are applied.

Closes: #165851
Epic: CRDB-58694
Release note: None
author: spilchen

reviews.body: > @rafiss made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165120)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on spilchen). ___ *[`pkg/ccl/multiregionccl/multiregion_test.go` line 741 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165120#-OnuMnNH5QE8AgYixeBC:-OnuMnNH5QE8AgYixeBD:b-msodib) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/1c5e18db60d5769876e53249ca5d0355eee430ab/pkg/ccl/multiregionccl/multiregion_test.go#L741)):* > ```Go > > // Create super regions. > tdb.Exec(t, `SET enable_super_regions = 'on'`) > ``` nit: we shouldn't need to set this now
> @spilchen resolved 1 discussion. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/165120)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on rafiss).

commits.message:
sql/regions: respect num_replicas zone config extension in super region constraints

Previously, `AddConstraintsForSuperRegion` computed `numReplicas`
independently (e.g. 4 for a 2-region super region with zone survival)
and generated constraints totaling that value. When a zone config
extension subsequently overrode `num_replicas` to a lower value (e.g. 3),
`ExtendZoneConfigWithRegionalIn` inherited the constraints from the
first step (still totaling 4) but set `num_replicas` to 3. Zone config
validation then rejected the config because constraints (4) exceeded
num_replicas (3).

This commit fixes `AddConstraintsForSuperRegion` to check the Regional
and RegionalIn zone config extensions for a `num_replicas` override
before generating constraints. If the override is at least `numVoters`,
it is used as the effective `numReplicas` for constraint distribution,
ensuring the generated constraints are consistent with the final
`num_replicas` after extensions are applied.

Informs: #164531
Epic: CRDB-58694
Release note: None

title: obs/ash: write ASH reports with goroutine dumps and CPU profiles
body:
When the environment sampler triggers a goroutine dump or CPU profile, also write an aggregated ASH (Active Session History) report alongside it. This provides immediate context about what workloads were active at the time of interest, aiding diagnosis of performance issues.

Two output formats are produced per trigger:
- `.txt` — human-readable table for debug zip inspection
- `.json` — machine-readable for tooling/log aggregation

To support this, `GoroutineDumper.MaybeDump()` and `CPUProfiler.MaybeTakeProfile()` now return `bool` indicating whether a dump/profile was successfully taken. The env sampler checks these return values to decide when to trigger an ASH report.

New cluster settings:
- `obs.ash.report.lookback_window` (default 60s): aggregation window
- `obs.ash.report.total_dump_size_limit` (default 32MiB): GC size limit

Resolves: #164383

Release note: None
author: alyshanjahani-crl

commits.message:
profiler,goroutinedumper,ash: return success from dump/profile methods

Add bool return values to MaybeDump, MaybeTakeProfile, and
maybeTakeProfile so callers can tell whether a dump or profile
was actually written. Also expose GetGlobalSampler() in the ash
package for callers that need to pass a reusable buffer.

These changes are prerequisites for triggering ASH reports
alongside goroutine dumps and CPU profiles.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

obs/ash: add ASH sample aggregation and report formatting

Add AggregateSamples to bucket ASH samples by (WorkEventType,
WorkEvent, WorkloadID) over a configurable lookback window,
and WriteTextReport / WriteJSONReport to serialize the
aggregated data for debug zips.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

profiler: add ASHReportProfiler and wire into env sampler

Introduce ASHReportProfiler, which writes aggregated ASH reports
to disk whenever a goroutine dump or CPU profile is triggered.
Both text and JSON reports are produced so debug zips contain
machine-readable ASH data alongside the dumps.

The profiler cleans up partial files on write failure and checks
Close errors to avoid silently losing buffered data.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: sql: add AST stubs for SHOW USERS provisioning clauses
body:
Previously, the SHOW USERS statement accepted no options or filters. To support user provisioning workflows—such as identifying and deprovisioning dormant LDAP-provisioned users—we need filtering clauses on SHOW USERS.

This commit adds the AST types required for the new syntax:

  SHOW USERS WITH SOURCE = 'ldap:...', LAST ACCESS TIME OLDER THAN <ts> LIMIT n

Specifically, it introduces ShowUsersOptions (with Source and LastAccessOlderThan fields), along with Format, CombineWith, and IsDefault methods following existing patterns (e.g. ShowFingerprintOptions). The ShowUsers struct is extended with Options and Limit fields.

The grammar and delegation layers are not yet wired up; this is the first of three parts for the full feature.

Epic: CRDB-52460
Resolves: #150604

Release note: None
author: souravcrl

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview Adds AST support in `tree` for upcoming `SHOW USERS` provisioning filters, enabling formatted output and option merging while grammar/delegation wiring will follow in later PRs. **Changes:** - Introduce `ShowUsersOptions` (Source, LastAccessOlderThan) with `Format`, `CombineWith`, and `IsDefault`. - Extend `ShowUsers` AST with `Options` and `Limit`, and update formatting accordingly. - Add unit tests for formatting and option-combination behavior. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | pkg/sql/sem/tree/show.go | Adds `ShowUsersOptions` and extends `ShowUsers` with formatting for WITH-clause options and LIMIT. | | pkg/sql/sem/tree/show_users_test.go | New tests covering `SHOW USERS` formatting, `CombineWith`, and `IsDefault`. | | pkg/sql/sem/tree/BUILD.bazel | Registers the new test file in the Bazel `go_test` target. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
sql: add AST stubs for SHOW USERS provisioning clauses

Previously, the SHOW USERS statement accepted no options or filters.
To support user provisioning workflows—such as identifying and
deprovisioning dormant LDAP-provisioned users—we need filtering
clauses on SHOW USERS.

This commit adds the AST types required for the new syntax:

  SHOW USERS WITH SOURCE = 'ldap:...', LAST ACCESS TIME OLDER THAN <ts> LIMIT n

Specifically, it introduces ShowUsersOptions (with Source and
LastAccessOlderThan fields), along with Format, CombineWith, and
IsDefault methods following existing patterns (e.g.
ShowFingerprintOptions). The ShowUsers struct is extended with Options
and Limit fields.

The grammar and delegation layers are not yet wired up; this is the
first of three parts for the full feature.

Epic: CRDB-52460
Resolves: #150604

title: sql: disable buffered writes for implicit txns by default
body:
We expect that vast majority of implicit serializable txns don't benefit from the buffered writes feature while we've seen some regressions around blind writes, so this commit disables the buffered writes for all implicit txns. As an escape hatch, a new session variable `buffered_writes_implicit_txns_enabled` is introduced which can be set to `true` to get the previous behavior (to increase test coverage the default value for the session variable is metamorphically randomized in test builds).

One notable case is worth calling out. In the PG wire protocol, entering the extended protocol starts an implicit transaction which is then upgraded to explicit if BEGIN is executed as a prepared statement. See 9fdb39b49e95320561ea58b8cac087afec62014d for more context.

Fixes: #154553.
Release note: None
author: yuzefovich

reviews.body: Nice! Glad we will be metamorphically enabling it. > @michae2 reviewed 14 files and all commit messages, and made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/164885#-:-OnsMiH62RjyQT_o-xzZ:b-ycqb32)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on stevendanna).

commits.message:
sql: disable buffered writes for implicit txns by default

We expect that vast majority of implicit serializable txns don't benefit
from the buffered writes feature while we've seen some regressions
around blind writes, so this commit disables the buffered writes for all
implicit txns. As an escape hatch, a new session variable
`buffered_writes_implicit_txns_enabled` is introduced which can be set
to `true` to get the previous behavior (to increase test coverage the
default value for the session variable is metamorphically randomized in
test builds).

One notable case is worth calling out. In the PG wire protocol, entering
the extended protocol starts an implicit transaction which is then
upgraded to explicit if BEGIN is executed as a prepared statement. See
9fdb39b49e95320561ea58b8cac087afec62014d for more context.

Release note: None

title: backup: add test for online restore pre-splitting with slow external IO
body:
Add TestOnlineRestoreDistFlowSplitScatter which verifies that online
restore's dist flow path uses a fine-grained span target size (384MB)
rather than the goroutine path's coarse target (16GB). With the coarse
target, all files merge into one span, producing an oversized range.
When external IO is slow, the split queue cannot find a split key in
time, and write backpressure blocks further linking — a deadlock.

The test inflates backup file stats by 100,000x via a new
BackupProcessFileOverride testing knob and blocks all reads of data
SSTs at the storage layer. After each LinkExternalSSTable, a
TestingResponseFilter enqueues the range into the split queue so that
backpressure's MaybeAddCallback finds it. With proper pre-splitting,
each range stays small and backpressure never triggers. Without it,
the test deadlocks.

Epic: none
Release note: None
author: dt

reviews.body: LGTM!

Add a variant of ReplaceNodeLocalForTesting that accepts a read

Log elapsed time for slow splits (>5s), slow file links (>5s), and

rather than the goroutine path's coarse target (16GB). With the coarse
target, all files merge into one span, producing an oversized range.
When external IO is slow, the split queue cannot find a split key in
time, and write backpressure blocks further linking — a deadlock.

The test inflates backup file stats by 100,000x via a new

SSTs at the storage layer. After each LinkExternalSSTable, a
TestingResponseFilter enqueues the range into the split queue so that
backpressure's MaybeAddCallback finds it. With proper pre-splitting,

title: changefeedccl: deprecate legacy span-level checkpoint
body:
**changefeedccl: deprecate span-level checkpoint** 

This patch fully deprecates the legacy span-level checkpoint mechanism
in favor of the persisted frontier. Changefeeds have been dual-writing
and dual-reading both span-level checkpoints and persisted frontiers in
most code paths since `V25_4` and recent changes have made it so that we
dual-write in all code paths as of `V26_2_Start`.

To perform the full migration, we introduce the following version gates:

* `V26_2_ChangefeedsStopReadingSpanLevelCheckpoints`: includes a
  migration that backfills persisted frontiers from existing span-level
  checkpoint data. After this gate, changefeeds read only the persisted
  frontier.

* `V26_2_ChangefeedsStopWritingSpanLevelCheckpoints`: after this gate,
  changefeeds no longer write span-level checkpoints to job progress.

* `V26_2_ChangefeedsNoLongerHaveSpanLevelCheckpoints`: includes a
  migration that purges any remaining span-level checkpoint data from
  job progress.

The read and write version gate checks are done transactionally using
the system database schema version to ensure consistency between the
migrations and concurrent changefeed operations.

This patch also adds a `LoadClaimedJobWithTxn` helper to the jobs
registry so that reloading the changefeed job progress can be done
transactionally with version gate assertions.

Fixes #164598
Fixes #164599

Release note: None

---

**roachtest: update mixed-version CDC checkpointing test** 

This patch updates `cdc/mixed-version/checkpointing` to do frontier
persistence more frequently.

Release note: None
author: andyyang890

reviews.body: Exciting! Overall it looks great. My only concern is the change to the post flow job reconciliation.
Thanks for the review! Ready for another look.
Ready for another look!

LGTM

Nice work!

commits.message:
changefeedccl: deprecate span-level checkpoint

This patch fully deprecates the legacy span-level checkpoint mechanism
in favor of the persisted frontier. Changefeeds have been dual-writing
and dual-reading both span-level checkpoints and persisted frontiers in
most code paths since `V25_4` and recent changes have made it so that we
dual-write in all code paths as of `V26_2_Start`.

To perform the full migration, we introduce the following version gates:

* `V26_2_ChangefeedsStopReadingSpanLevelCheckpoints`: includes a
  migration that backfills persisted frontiers from existing span-level
  checkpoint data. After this gate, changefeeds read only the persisted
  frontier.

* `V26_2_ChangefeedsStopWritingSpanLevelCheckpoints`: after this gate,
  changefeeds no longer write span-level checkpoints to job progress.

* `V26_2_ChangefeedsNoLongerHaveSpanLevelCheckpoints`: includes a
  migration that purges any remaining span-level checkpoint data from
  job progress.

The read and write version gate checks are done transactionally using
the system database schema version to ensure consistency between the
migrations and concurrent changefeed operations.

This patch also adds a `LoadClaimedJobWithTxn` helper to the jobs
registry so that reloading the changefeed job progress can be done
transactionally with version gate assertions.

title: roachtest: overhaul operations runner observability
body:
Add comprehensive Prometheus metrics instrumentation to the roachtest operations runner to improve observability of operation executions in DRT environments. The metrics cover:

- Active operations and worker utilization by state
- Pending cleanup gauges

Refactor Datadog event emission into a dedicated operationEventEmitter struct in a separate file, enriching error events with failure details. Using datadog event stream following widgets will be created:
- Total Operation Executed, succeeded, failed, panicked.
- Event stream with logs of failure.

The operations runner now exposes a /metrics HTTP endpoint via --prom-port, scraped by the OpenTelemetry collector on the workload node and forwarded to Datadog. The wrapper script no longer retries in a loop since the runner is a long-lived process.

Fixes: #163590

Release note: None
author: shailendra-patel

reviews.body: LGTM!

struct in a separate file, enriching error events with failure details.
Using datadog event stream following widgets will be created:
- Total Opeartion Executed, succeeded, failed, panicked.
- Event stream with logs of failure.

The operations runner now exposes a /metrics HTTP endpoint via

title: cli: merge encode-uri into convert-url
body:
This commit merges the `cockroach encode-uri` command into the `cockroach convert-url` command so that we have a unified interface for generating connection URLs. `cockroach encode-uri` has been deprecated and will be removed in a later release.

Resolves: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/154384

cli: merge encode-uri into convert-url

This commit merges the `cockroach encode-uri` command into the
`cockroach convert-url` command so that we have a unified interface for
generating connection URLs. `cockroach encode-uri` has been deprecated
and will be removed in a later release.

Resolves: #154384

Release note (cli): The `cockroach encode-uri` command has been merged
into the `cockroach convert-url` command and `encode-uri` has been
deprecated. The PCR/LDR docs referencing `encode-uri` should be updated
to use `convert-url` instead. As part of this port, additional flags
`--inline`, `--database`, `--user`, `--password`, `--cluster`,
`--certs-dir`, `--ca-cert`, `--cert`, and `--key` have been added to

cli: add --format option to convert-url

This commit adds the `--format` option to `cockroach convert-url`, which
allows the user to specify a format type of the URL to output. If
provided, only the URL of that type will be provided instead of the
labeled list of all URL types.

users to specify the specific type of URL that should be outputted.

title: oidcauth, httputil: add tls_insecure_skip_verify for OIDC
body:
Local/dev setups (for example, Keycloak inside minikube with a
host-managed DNS zone) can make TLS verification for the OIDC
provider impossible to satisfy, blocking login. This change
adds an explicit opt-in cluster setting
`server.oidc_authentication.tls_insecure_skip_verify.enabled` and wires it to
the OIDC HTTP client TLS config. Docs and explain-bundle output are
updated accordingly.

Release note (server): add an opt-in OIDC setting to skip TLS
certificate verification for provider connections.

Epic: None
author: eXist-FraGGer

reviews.body:

After rebasing, you'll need to regenerate the settings documentation. Run:
./dev generate

commits.message:
oidcauth, httputil: add tls_insecure_skip_verify for OIDC

Local/dev setups (for example, Keycloak inside minikube with a
host-managed DNS zone) can make TLS verification for the OIDC
provider impossible to satisfy, blocking login. This change
adds an explicit opt-in cluster setting
server.oidc_authentication.tls_insecure_skip_verify.enabled and wires it to
the OIDC HTTP client TLS config. Docs and explain-bundle output are
updated accordingly.

Release note (ops change): add an opt-in OIDC setting to skip TLS
certificate verification for provider connections.

title: added sig* handling for all operations
body:
## Summary

Ensures roachtest operations always run their cleanup handlers, even when the process receives signals (SIGINT, SIGTERM, SIGHUP, SIGQUIT) during execution.

## Problem

Signals during operation execution left DRT clusters in dirty states (orphaned indexes, expired licenses, iptables rules, leaked nodes) because:
1. Process exited after 5 seconds, before cleanup could finish
2. `runOperation` skipped cleanup on context cancellation
3. `o.Fatal` panics prevented returning cleanup handlers

## Solution

**Runner (`run_operation.go`):**
- Always run cleanup, even on operation failure or context cancellation
- Moved panic recovery to attempt cleanup if handler was set
- Clear run-phase failures before cleanup so `op.Failed()` only reflects cleanup status

**Signal handling (`run.go`):**
- Extended grace period from 5 seconds to 2 minutes for operation mode
- Added handlers for SIGTERM, SIGHUP, SIGQUIT

**Operations:**
- Short operations: Use `context.Background()` with timeout for state-changing calls
- Long operations: Replace `o.Fatal` with `o.Errorf` after state changes
- Infrastructure operations: Named returns + deferred recovery
- Cleanup handlers: Use `IF EXISTS` for idempotency

Fixes: #163587
Release Note: None
Epic: None
author: cpj2195

reviews.body:
Thanks for working this.

I left a few comments on specific areas. The high-level suggestion is: I think we can get the same safety guarantees with a simpler approach by keeping `o.Fatal` and unifying cleanup into a single defer in `runOperation`. That would avoid the `Fatal → Errorf` conversions and all the `context.Background()` workarounds, which make up the bulk of the diff.

The parts I think should definitely be kept regardless of approach:
- Signal handling in run.go (SIGTERM/SIGHUP/SIGQUIT + extended shutdown window for operations)
- IF EXISTS in cleanup handlers for add_column and add_index
- The resize.go change to assign the cleanup handler early after Grow
 - Proceeding to cleanup instead of returning early when context is canceled during the wait period

Happy to discuss further if I'm missing something about why the Errorf approach was chosen.
LGTM

commits.message: added sig* handling for all operations

title: metric/aggmetric: strip out eviction strategy from high cardinality metrics
body:
This commit strips out the LRU-based, insertion-time cache eviction from high-cardinality metrics.
The old approach tightly coupled eviction logic with the cache's `ShouldEvict`/`OnEvictedEntry`
callbacks, making it impossible to accumulate evicted values or control eviction timing independently
of cache operations.

This commit replaces the LRU eviction with a `CacheNone` policy, deferring eviction to an explicit
external mechanism (added in #164720). Specifically:

- Remove `ShouldEvict`/`OnEvictedEntry` callbacks from cache initialization
- Remove `RetentionTimeTillEviction` from `HighCardinalityMetricOptions` and its corresponding
  environment variable
- Separate `Metadata` out of `HighCardinalityMetricOptions` into a distinct constructor parameter
  for Counter, Gauge, and Histogram
- Rename `CreatedAt()` to `UpdatedAt()` on child metrics and track last-mutation time atomically
  (updated on `Inc`, `Dec`, `Update`, `RecordValue`)
- Set default `MaxLabelValues` to `10000` (previously `0`)
- Remove obsolete test data files for the old eviction approach

Follow-up: #164720 (periodic eviction of stale high-cardinality metrics)

Epic: CRDB-59105

Release note: None
author: aa-joshi

reviews.body: Mostly consistency issues.
> @aa-joshi made 6 comments. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/164125)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on dhartunian).
LGTM. You have some lint failures.

commits.message:
metric/aggmetric: remove LRU-based cache eviction from high cardinality metrics

Previously, high cardinality metrics used an LRU cache with ShouldEvict
and OnEvictedEntry callbacks to evict stale child metrics at insertion
time. This approach was tightly coupled with the cache policy and made
it difficult to implement more sophisticated eviction strategies like
accumulating evicted values into a dedicated child.

This commit strips out the LRU eviction logic and replaces it with a
CacheNone policy, deferring eviction to an explicit external mechanism
(added in a subsequent commit). Specifically:

- Remove ShouldEvict/OnEvictedEntry callbacks from cache initialization
- Remove RetentionTimeTillEviction from HighCardinalityMetricOptions
  and its corresponding environment variable
- Separate Metadata out of HighCardinalityMetricOptions into a distinct
  constructor parameter for Counter, Gauge, and Histogram
- Rename CreatedAt() to UpdatedAt() on child metrics and track
  last-mutation time atomically (updated on Inc, Dec, Update, RecordValue)
- Set default MaxLabelValues to 10000 (previously 0)
- Remove obsolete test data files for the old eviction approach

Part of: CRDB-59105

Release note: None

title: drtprod: add AWS chaos testing cluster configuration
body:
Add drt_chaos_aws.yaml, the AWS equivalent of drt_chaos.yaml for running chaos testing on AWS. Key differences from the GCE configuration:

- Uses AWS Auto Scaling Groups (aws-managed: true) instead of GCE managed instance groups
- Uses m6i.4xlarge (16 vCPU, 64GB) for cluster nodes and m6i.2xlarge (8 vCPU, 32GB) for workload nodes
- Uses 4x EBS gp3 volumes (375GB each) instead of local SSDs
- Deploys in us-east-1a/b/c availability zones

The instances use IAM instance profile `drt-testing` which provides:
- Read access to AWS Secrets Manager for fetching datadog-api-key
- Read/write access to S3 buckets for backups and artifacts
- EC2 describe permissions for roachprod cluster management

Also update roachtest_operations_run.sh to support multi-cloud secret fetching. The script now accepts cluster, workload_cluster, and cloud parameters, fetching secrets from AWS Secrets Manager or GCP Secret Manager based on the cloud parameter.

Informs: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/156499

Release note: None
author: shailendra-patel

commits.message:
drtprod: add AWS chaos testing cluster configuration

Add drt_chaos_aws.yaml, the AWS equivalent of drt_chaos.yaml for running
chaos testing on AWS. Key differences from the GCE configuration:

- Uses AWS Auto Scaling Groups (aws-managed: true) instead of GCE
managed instance groups
- Uses m6i.4xlarge (16 vCPU, 64GB) for cluster nodes and m6i.2xlarge
(8 vCPU, 32GB) for workload nodes
- Uses 4x EBS gp3 volumes (375GB each) instead of local SSDs
- Deploys in us-east-1a/b/c availability zones

The instances use IAM instance profile `drt-testing` which provides:
- Read access to AWS Secrets Manager for fetching datadog-api-key
- Read/write access to S3 buckets for backups and artifacts
- EC2 describe permissions for roachprod cluster management

Also update roachtest_operations_run.sh to support multi-cloud secret
fetching. The script now accepts cluster, workload_cluster, and cloud
parameters, fetching secrets from AWS Secrets Manager or GCP Secret
Manager based on the cloud parameter.

Informs: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/156499

Release note: None

title: security: make SAN authN cluster setting publicly available
body:
* Previously, the security.client_cert.san_required.enabled cluster setting was marked as reserved (internal-only) while the SAN-based authentication feature was being developed and tested.
* This also changes WithReportable(false) → WithReportable(true), which means the setting value will now be included in diagnostic reports.
    
Epic: [CRDB-58928](https://cockroachlabs.atlassian.net/browse/CRDB-58928)
    
Resolves: #164136
    
Release note (security update): 
The new cluster setting
`security.client_cert.san_required.enabled` enables Subject Alternative Name (SAN) based authentication for client certificates. When enabled, CockroachDB validates client identities using SAN attributes (URIs, DNS names, or IP addresses) from X.509 certificates instead of or in addition to the certificate's Common Name field.
    
Key capabilities include:
- For privileged users (root and node): SAN identities are validated against values configured via the --root-cert-san and --node-cert-san startup flags, with automatic fallback to Distinguished Name validation when both methods are configured.
    
- For database users: SAN identities are extracted from client certificates and mapped to database usernames using Host-Based Authentication (HBA) identity mapping rules, allowing a single certificate with multiple SAN entries to authenticate as different database users based on context.
    
- Multiple identity attributes: A single certificate can contain multiple SAN entries (e.g., URI for service identity, DNS for hostname, IP for network location), providing flexible authentication options.
    
This authentication method works across both SQL client connections and internal RPC communication between cluster nodes, ensuring consistent identity verification throughout the system. Organizations using modern certificate management systems and service identity frameworks can now leverage their existing infrastructure for database authentication without requiring certificate reissuance or CN-based naming conventions.
author: sanchit-CRL

reviews.body:
LGTM. Two minor suggestions:

1. **Release note style**: The release note is thorough but quite long for a settings doc. Consider tightening the opening to lead with the user action rather than the implementation detail:
   ```
   Release note (security update): The new cluster setting
   `security.client_cert.san_required.enabled` enables Subject Alternative
   Name (SAN) based authentication for client certificates. When enabled,
   CockroachDB validates client identities using SAN attributes (URIs, DNS
   names, or IP addresses) from X.509 certificates instead of or in addition
   to the certificate's Common Name field.
   ```
   The key capabilities list is good — just the intro could be more direct.

2. **PR description**: Worth mentioning that this also changes `WithReportable(false)` → `WithReportable(true)`, which means the setting value will now be included in diagnostic reports. This is a minor but distinct behavior change from just making it public.

commits.message:
security: make SAN authN cluster setting publicly available

* Previously, the security.client_cert.san_required.enabled
cluster setting was marked as reserved (internal-only)
while the SAN-based authentication feature was
being developed and tested.

Epic: CRDB-58928

Resolves: #164136

Release note (security update): The new cluster setting
`security.client_cert.san_required.enabled` enables Subject
Alternative Name (SAN) based authentication for client
certificates. When enabled, CockroachDB validates client
identities using SAN attributes (URIs, DNS names, or
IP addresses) from X.509 certificates instead of or in
addition to the certificate's Common Name field.

Key capabilities include:

- For privileged users (root and node): SAN identities
are validated against values configured via the
--root-cert-san and --node-cert-san startup flags,
with automatic fallback to Distinguished Name
validation when both methods are configured.

- For database users: SAN identities are extracted from
client certificates and mapped to database usernames
using Host-Based Authentication (HBA) identity mapping
rules, allowing a single certificate with multiple SAN
entries to authenticate as different database users
based on context.

- Multiple identity attributes: A single certificate can
contain multiple SAN entries (e.g., URI for service
identity, DNS for hostname, IP for network location),
providing flexible authentication options.

This authentication method works across both SQL client
connections and internal RPC communication between
cluster nodes, ensuring consistent identity verification
throughout the system. Organizations using modern
certificate management systems and service identity
frameworks can now leverage their existing infrastructure
for database authentication without requiring certificate
reissuance or CN-based naming conventions.

title: mmaprototype: balance CPU on utilization
body:
Before, the CPU load summary compared absolute cpu-nanos to the mean even when
capacity was known. That caused heterogeneous core counts to converge to equal
absolute usage and left higher-core nodes underutilized. Compare CPU load to
the mean utilization instead. This keeps the existing thresholds and
homogeneous behavior.

x-ref https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/153777

Epic: CRDB-56265
author: tbg

Previously, sumeerbhola wrote…
I do think we need to use meanUtil being low enough as a gating condition, i.e., bring back the `meanUtil*1.1 < fractionUsed`. When the whole cluster is overloaded, we don't want to cause more rebalancing activity.
My view on this is that the code here is supposed to classify the stores, not only for future load decisions, but also for metrics. Let's say we do have all stores at 95% CPU. I think MMA should see these as overloaded and expose that status correspondingly. Yes, it will also try to shed, but this won't work since everybody is overloaded. Adding a "consider me overloaded only if the mean indicates that someone else probably isn't overloaded" is possible, but also feels inconsistent and like we're trying to do some load balancing decisions in this method. I added a test `rebalance_stores_all_overloaded` that demonstrates the behavior change here. The behavior as of this comment (no meanUtil check) still makes sense to me.

commits.message:
mmaprototype: remove denom= from load summary logging

Remove the `denom=` field from the load summary log output. This field
logged the effective denominator used by fractionAbove when the
significance floor clamped it. It's noisy and will be replaced by a more
compact annotation in a follow-up commit.

This is a mechanical change — no behavioral impact.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

mmaprototype: add test for uniformly saturated cluster

Add a TestClusterState test where all four nodes are at 95% CPU
utilization. s1-s3 share replicas, s4 has none but is equally
overloaded.

Baseline behavior: all stores are classified as loadNormal despite
being at 95% utilization. Because every store is at the mean, the
relative comparison (fractionAbove ≈ 0) results in loadNormal, and the
old utilization gate (meanUtil*1.1 < fractionUsed) also doesn't fire
since no store is above the mean. The cluster looks "normally loaded"
when it is clearly saturated.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

kv/kvserver/allocator: balance CPU on utilization

Before, the CPU load summary compared absolute cpu-nanos to the mean even when
capacity was known. That caused heterogeneous core counts to converge to equal
absolute usage and left higher-core nodes underutilized. Compare CPU load to
the mean utilization instead. This keeps the existing thresholds and
homogeneous behavior.

Additionally, generalize this utilization-based comparison to all resources
with a known capacity (ByteSize in addition to CPURate), not just CPURate.

Simplify the overload check: since fractionAbove is now utilization-based
whenever capacity is known, the multi-tiered intermediate checks (75%, 1.5x
meanUtil, 1.75x meanUtil) are redundant with the general
meanFractionSlow/meanFractionLow classification. Keep only the >90% absolute
utilization check as a safety net for nearing full capacity.

Test changes fall into the following categories:

1. rebalance_replica.txt: adds an explicit store-load-msg for s2 to give it a
   known capacity. Without this, s2 has UnknownCapacity and the
   utilization-based comparison can't work. The seq numbers bump accordingly.

2. Reason string changes from the removed multi-tiered overload block. The
   old code classified stores through the utilization override (reasons like
   "fractionUsed > 75%", "fractionUsed < 75% and >1.75x meanUtil", etc.).
   Now, the same classification happens through the general fractionAbove
   logic (reason: "load is >10% above mean"). The load summary level
   (overloadSlow, loadNormal, etc.) is unchanged in most cases. This affects
   rebalance_stores_capacity_mismatch, all rebalance_stores_cpu_* tests,
   rebalance_stores_nls_sls_mismatch, and skip_range_invalid_config.

3. rebalance_stores_cpu_replica_refusing_target.txt: all stores are at 100%
   utilization (load=1000, capacity=1000). Previously classified as
   loadNormal (since fractionAbove=0 and the utilization override didn't fire
   because meanUtil*1.1 > fractionUsed). Now correctly classified as
   overloadUrgent via the >90% check. The failure reason changes from
   "not-overloaded" to "no-cand-load" — same outcome (no rebalancing), but
   the classification is more accurate.

4. rebalance_stores_capacity_mismatch.txt: in addition to reason strings,
   some candidate evaluation blocks are removed. These were intermediate
   steps where the old code tried lease transfers between equally-loaded
   stores. With utilization-based fractionAbove, s1 at 90%/83.2% meanUtil
   now classifies as loadNoChange (fractionAbove=0.08) instead of
   overloadSlow, so it correctly skips trying to shed to equally-loaded
   peers earlier.

5. asim simulation testdata (fulldisk, high_write_uniform_cpu,
   skewed_cpu_skewed_write, one_voter_skewed_cpu_skewed_write, etc.): hash
   and metric value changes from different rebalancing decisions. The
   fulldisk test notably shows ByteSize now benefiting from
   utilization-aware balancing.

6. rebalance_stores_cpu_replica_lateral.txt: the test setup is adjusted to
   make this a homogeneous CPU deployment (s3/s4 capacity changed from 1000
   to 500, so n3 with two stores totals 1000 CPU capacity, matching n1 and
   n2). Previously, the utilization override ("fractionUsed < 75%") caused
   s1 and s2 to be incorrectly classified as overloadSlow for CPURate even
   though their CPU utilization was only 10%, because the override didn't
   account for how far from the mean utilization the store actually was. This
   triggered a full lease-shedding cycle from s1 (which failed, since all
   replica-holding stores had the same false-positive overload) before
   finally reaching s3's write-bandwidth overload. Now, with
   utilization-based fractionAbove, s1 and s2 are correctly classified as
   loadNormal (10% utilization vs 10% mean utilization), so only s3 is
   identified as overloaded (on WriteBandwidth). The rebalance moves the
   replica from s3 to s4 directly without the spurious detour through CPU
   lease shedding.

x-ref https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/153777

Release note: None

Epic: CRDB-56265

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

mmaprototype: annotate fractionUsed with * when clamped

When the significance floor clamps the denominator used to compute
fractionAbove, annotate `fractionUsed=x%` as `fractionUsed=x%*` in the
load summary log. This replaces the removed `denom=` field with a more
compact signal that the clamping was active.

Note: `†` (U+2020) was the preferred annotation character but the
cockroachdb/redact package strips non-ASCII characters from safe values,
so `*` is used instead.

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

mmaprototype: extract computeSummaryUpperBound

Factor out the summaryUpperBound computation from loadSummaryForDimension
into a standalone function with documentation explaining how it differs
from significance floor clamping. Both mechanisms prevent unnecessary
shedding at low load, but they address different concerns: clamping
handles statistical significance (small differences / small mean), while
the upper bound handles prioritization (don't bother shedding a
dimension whose absolute utilization is negligible).

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: roachtest: add 1M table introspection benchmark variant
body:
Add a new variant of the large-schema-benchmark roachtest that creates 1 million tables and benchmarks introspection query performance. Unlike the existing benchmark variants, this test:

- Uses minimal data import (warehouses=1) instead of full TPCC data
- Skips the TPCC workload and runs only ORM introspection queries
- Tests at a much larger scale (1M tables vs max 40K)
- Single-region only configuration
- 48-hour timeout to accommodate the larger scale

This serves as a smoke test to determine how introspection queries perform with 1 million tables, as part of our efforts to improve introspection query performance.

Run with:
```
# Also pass in --debug-always if you want the cluster to remain alive afterwards for testing.
roachtest run '^large-schema-benchmark/multiregion=false/tables=100000$' --cloud gce --parallelism=1
```

Resolves: #159557
Epic: CRDB-54999

Release note: None
author: rafiss

it's used here in session-based leasing too: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/d9663244d61468620bd9503f36b10a6db5fe46bb/pkg/sql/catalog/lease/lease.go#L3359-L3362 i remember i had issues and had to add this, but i forget what happened. let me see if i can confirm ___ *[`pkg/cmd/roachtest/tests/large_schema_benchmark.go` line 469 at r3](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/160595#-OnKe4A_EOtA8Cq9TpsP:-OnOUXH08jYFX9ABTfWJ:bm9mkhl) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/f27c7098b786d1d9f0e3da71018638fc52100a2a/pkg/cmd/roachtest/tests/large_schema_benchmark.go#L469)):*

commits.message:
workload: add schema-only data loader for benchmarks

Add a SchemaOnlyDataLoader that creates tables without loading any row
data. This is useful for benchmarks that only need the schema structure.
The loader batches CREATE TABLE statements in transactions of up to 5000
tables and distributes batches across nodes round-robin when multiple
URLs are provided.

Also add a --data-loader=none CLI option to use the new loader, fix an
off-by-one in fixture.go's table creation loop, and add progress logging
to the split and post-load phases.

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

workload/tpcc: improve multi-db schema creation reliability

Improve the tpccmultidb PreCreate hook in several ways:

- Batch database/schema creation in groups of 5000 with progress
  logging, using crdb.ExecuteTx for automatic transaction retries.
- Wrap PreCreate in sync.Once to prevent duplicate database creation
  when multiple init workers call it concurrently.
- Replace quotapool with ctxgroup.SetLimit for PostLoad concurrency
  control.

These changes make large-scale schema creation (10K+ databases) more
reliable and observable.

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

roachtest: add large-schema introspection benchmark

Add a new roachtest benchmark that creates large numbers of empty tables
(10K and 1M variants) and measures introspection query performance. This
benchmark uses the tpccmultidb workload with --data-loader=none to
create only table schemas without loading data, then runs ORM
introspection queries via the querybench workload on the workload node.

Key design choices:
- Tables are distributed across databases with increasing schemas per
  database (up to 72) to exercise realistic catalog structures.
- The querybench workload runs on the workload node rather than
  in-process on the roachtest runner, so runner hiccups don't affect
  benchmark results.
- querybench's sql.DB pools connections across all provided pgurl URLs,
  distributing load across CRDB nodes.
- INSPECT and descriptor post-validation are skipped because they would
  take too long with this many tables.

Also register the querybench workload in the cockroach binary so that
it can be used via `cockroach workload run querybench` without requiring
the deprecated workload binary.

Also add an InitNodes option to tpccOptions so init can distribute table
creation across all CRDB nodes.

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: release-26.1: kvserverbase: allow MMA cluster setting in production builds
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #159881 on behalf of @tbg.

----

Previously, the `kv.allocator.load_based_rebalancing` cluster setting could only be set to "multi-metric only" or "multi-metric and count" in test builds or when the `COCKROACH_ALLOW_MMA` environment variable was set.

Remove this restriction to allow MMA (multi-metric allocation) to be enabled in production. This is not recommended yet. Also remove references to the now-unnecessary `COCKROACH_ALLOW_MMA` env var from roachtests and drtprod configs.

Fixes: #159587
Epic: CRDB-55052

----

Release justification: allow toggling a feature gate for an experimental feature in CC deployments.
author: tbg

reviews.body: LGTM!

commits.message:
kvserverbase: allow MMA cluster setting in production builds

Previously, the `kv.allocator.load_based_rebalancing` cluster setting
could only be set to "multi-metric only" or "multi-metric and count"
in test builds or when the `COCKROACH_ALLOW_MMA` environment variable
was set.

Remove this restriction to allow MMA (multi-metric allocation) to be
enabled in production. Also remove references to the now-unnecessary
`COCKROACH_ALLOW_MMA` env var from roachtests and drtprod configs.

Fixes: #159587
Epic: CRDB-55052

title: sql: enforce statement_timeout on descriptor waits
body:
Previously, when a schema change was execute with a statement_timeout, we would enforce any statement timeouts on the job waits. However, there was also additional logic to wait for descriptors, that could apply afterwards. This patch, ensures that any extra logic complies with the statement_timeout, which will generate a similar query timeout error.

Fixes: #158272

commits.message:
sql: enforce statement_timeout on descriptor waits

Previously, when a schema change was execute with a statement_timeout,
we would enforce any statement timeouts on the job waits. However, there
was also additional logic to wait for descriptors, that could apply
afterwards. This patch, ensures that any extra logic complies with the
statement_timeout, which will generate a similar query timeout error.

Fixes: #158272

Release note: None

title: kvserverbase: allow MMA cluster setting in production builds
body:
Previously, the `kv.allocator.load_based_rebalancing` cluster setting could only be set to "multi-metric only" or "multi-metric and count" in test builds or when the `COCKROACH_ALLOW_MMA` environment variable was set.

Remove this restriction to allow MMA (multi-metric allocation) to be enabled in production. This is not recommended yet. Also remove references to the now-unnecessary `COCKROACH_ALLOW_MMA` env var from roachtests and drtprod configs.

Fixes: #159587
Epic: CRDB-55052
author: tbg

commits.message:
kvserverbase: allow MMA cluster setting in production builds

Previously, the `kv.allocator.load_based_rebalancing` cluster setting
could only be set to "multi-metric only" or "multi-metric and count"
in test builds or when the `COCKROACH_ALLOW_MMA` environment variable
was set.

Remove this restriction to allow MMA (multi-metric allocation) to be
enabled in production. Also remove references to the now-unnecessary
`COCKROACH_ALLOW_MMA` env var from roachtests and drtprod configs.

Fixes: #159587
Epic: CRDB-55052
