title: roachprod/cli: honor COCKROACH_ROACHPROD_INSECURE for ephemeral clusters
body:
Previously, clusters in `cockroach-ephemeral` always defaulted to insecure mode, ignoring the `COCKROACH_ROACHPROD_INSECURE` env var. The only way to force secure mode was passing `--secure` explicitly.

Now, if `COCKROACH_ROACHPROD_INSECURE` is explicitly set, it takes precedence over the ephemeral project default. Setting it to `false` forces secure mode, `true` forces insecure. CLI flags still have highest precedence.

Release note: None
Epic: None
author: shailendra-patel

reviews.body: LGTM modulo the commit msg which should mention the region change.

commits.message:
roachprod/cli: honor COCKROACH_ROACHPROD_INSECURE for ephemeral clusters

Previously, clusters in `cockroach-ephemeral` always defaulted to
insecure mode, ignoring the `COCKROACH_ROACHPROD_INSECURE` env var.
The only way to force secure mode was passing `--secure` explicitly.

Now, if `COCKROACH_ROACHPROD_INSECURE` is explicitly set, it takes
precedence over the ephemeral project default. Setting it to `false`
forces secure mode, `true` forces insecure. CLI flags still have
highest precedence.

Release note: None
Epic: None

title: release: add update-workflow-branches command and TeamCity scripts
body:
This PR automates the creation of PRs like https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pull/154038

Adds the `update-workflow-branches` command and companion scripts to automatically add the latest release branch to `.github/workflows/update_releases.yaml` when cutting a new release branch.

This script will be triggered by this TeamCity build:
- [Update workflows update_releases](https://teamcity.cockroachdb.com/buildConfiguration/Internal_Cockroach_Release_Publish_UpdateWorkflowsUpdateReleases?branch=159216&buildTypeTab=overview&focusLine=NaN&mode=builds)

## Changes

- **New command**: `release update-workflow-branches`
  - Auto-detects the latest release branch via `git ls-remote`
  - Uses `gopkg.in/yaml.v3` to parse and update the workflow YAML (structured node navigation, preserves formatting and comments)
  - Adds the branch to `.github/workflows/update_releases.yaml` matrix if not present
  - Sorts branches (master first, then release branches in semantic version order)
  - Idempotent — no-ops if the branch is already present
  - `--print-branch` flag: emits only the detected branch name to stdout (used by the shell script to capture the branch without grepping progress output)

- **TeamCity scripts**:
  - `update_workflow_branches.sh` — wrapper script
  - `update_workflow_branches_impl.sh` — implementation that runs the command and creates PRs

- **Tests** (`update_workflow_test.go`):
  - `TestWorkflowFileStructure` — validates the live workflow file has the YAML structure the code depends on; catches structural regressions at the source
  - `TestFindBranchNode_ExtractsValues` — verifies branch values are correctly extracted from the parsed YAML node
  - `TestSortBranches` — table-driven; covers master-first ordering, semantic version order (not lexicographic), unparseable fallback, and the degenerate two-master case
  - `TestSelectLatestBranch` — table-driven; covers RC branch filtering, highest-version selection, all-unparseable error, and empty-input error
  - `TestAddBranchToWorkflowFile` — exercises the full mutation path (read, parse, append, sort, re-encode, write) on a temp copy of the real workflow file; includes an idempotency case

Epic: None
Release note: None
author: celiala

reviews.body:
[Claude's review via [/review-crdb](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/master/.claude/skills/review-crdb/SKILL.md)]

This automates a previously manual step (adding a new release branch to the CI workflow matrix). The structured YAML navigation approach (`yaml.Node` API) is the right choice, `TestWorkflowFileStructure` as a live-file regression guard is genuinely useful, and the error messages in `findBranchNode` include actionable fix instructions.

**Test coverage gap**: The core logic functions are not exercised by any test:

- `findLatestReleaseBranch` calls `listRemoteBranches` (network I/O), so its filtering+selection logic can't be unit-tested without a refactor. Extract a pure `selectLatestBranch(branches []string) (string, error)` and add table-driven tests. Key untested paths: RC branch filtering (a regression silently adds an RC branch to the CI matrix), wrong sort direction (picks oldest instead of newest), error paths.

- `addBranchToWorkflow` hardcodes the `workflowFile` global constant — see inline. The entire mutation path (append, sort, re-encode YAML, write) has zero coverage.

- `sortBranches` is completely untested — see inline for the correctness issue.

The two existing tests only validate `findBranchNode` against the live workflow file, which is valuable but not sufficient.

commits.message:
release: add update-workflow-branches command and TeamCity scripts

This commit adds automation to update the GitHub Actions workflow file
(.github/workflows/update_releases.yaml) when new release branches are created.

Changes:
- New command: `release update-workflow-branches` that auto-detects the latest
  release branch via git ls-remote and adds it to the workflow matrix
- TeamCity wrapper and implementation scripts to run this command and create
  PRs
- Command preserves YAML formatting and is idempotent

The command can be run manually or via TeamCity automation to keep the workflow
file in sync with active release branches.

Epic: None
Release note: None

release: replace manual YAML parsing with yaml.v3 library

Address feedback from #159216 by switching update_workflow.go from
manual line-by-line YAML parsing to gopkg.in/yaml.v3 structured node
navigation. Also:

- Use strings.HasSuffix(branch, "-rc") instead of strings.Contains
  for more precise RC branch filtering (Rail's feedback)
- Add update_workflow_test.go to validate workflow file structure
- Add yaml.v3 dependency to BUILD.bazel; yaml.v2 remains for existing
  update_releases.go (consolidation is a follow-up TODO)
- Export workflows/update_releases.yaml from .github/BUILD.bazel for
  use in tests

Epic: None
Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

release: suppress yaml.Unmarshal lint for yaml.v3 Node API usage

The codebase lint rule requires yaml.Unmarshal callers to use
yamlutil.UnmarshalStrict instead. However, yamlutil uses go.yaml.in/yaml/v4
which has an incompatible Node API. Since update_workflow.go uses the yaml.v3
Node API for structured YAML tree navigation, add nolint:yaml suppressions
on the Unmarshal calls.

Epic: None
Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

release: address review feedback on update-workflow-branches

- Extract selectLatestBranch as a pure, testable helper (mirrors
  findVersions/findPreviousRelease split in git.go)
- Fix sortBranches comparator: return 0 when both a and b are "master"
  (was violating strict weak ordering)
- Add --print-branch flag to emit only the detected branch name to
  stdout, allowing the shell script to capture it without grepping
  progress messages
- addBranchToWorkflow now delegates to addBranchToWorkflowFile(branch,
  path) so tests can operate on a temp file
- Move GH_TOKEN check to top of impl script, before any build or
  network operations
- Use --print-branch in impl script instead of grepping fmt.Printf output
- Add table-driven tests: TestSortBranches, TestSelectLatestBranch,
  TestAddBranchToWorkflowFile (including idempotency)
- Replace tautological TestAddBranchToWorkflow_NoOp with
  TestFindBranchNode_ExtractsValues
- Lowercase phase comments per CockroachDB convention

Epic: None
Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: drtprod: changes for 300 node provisioning
body:
For provisioning a 300 node cluster we use a remote node. That node needs memory for executing the gcloud commands. So, a new drt_remote_300.yaml file is created. Also, the dmsetup was failing as the script was hard-coded for local ssd. This PR changes that logic to read the device by running "df -k" and getting the device name for /data1.

Release: None
Epic: None
author: nameisbhaskar

For provisioning a 300 node cluster we use a remote node. That node needs memory for executing the gcloud commands. So, a new drt_remote_300.yaml file is created. Also, the dmsetup was failing as the script was hard-coded for local ssd. This PR changes that logic to read the device by running "df -k" and getting the device name for /data1.

title: drtprod/scripts: run tpcc continuously to avoid w_ytd overflow
body:
After running TPCC for extended periods (e.g., a week), the workload fails with "DECIMAL(12,2) overflow" errors because the accumulated w_ytd balance grows indefinitely and exceeds 10^10.

Setting `--duration 0` in `generate_tpcc_run.sh` to run the workload continuously instead of restarting in a loop. This allows the workload's built-in long-duration mode to periodically reset w_ytd values, preventing overflow.

Also clean up log files after successful runs in generate_tpcc_drop.sh.

Epic: none

Release note: None
author: shailendra-patel

reviews.body: LGTM! Thanks for the change!

commits.message:
drtprod/scripts: run tpcc continuously to avoid w_ytd overflow

After running TPCC for extended periods (e.g., a week), the workload fails
with "DECIMAL(12,2) overflow" errors because the accumulated w_ytd balance
grows indefinitely and exceeds 10^10.

Setting `--duration 0` in `generate_tpcc_run.sh` to run the workload
continuously instead of restarting in a loop. This allows the workload's
built-in long-duration mode to periodically reset w_ytd values,
preventing overflow.

Also clean up log files after successful runs in generate_tpcc_drop.sh.

Or how frequently does it get run? > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/158021#-:-OeT1Zrk5nNyx2fiyFz0:b-5sfxu1)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on @nameisbhaskar and @shailendra-patel) ___ *[`pkg/cmd/roachtest/operations/inspect.go` line 37 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/158021#-OeSyu0aAtbVJpHcDkoO:-OeSyu0aAtbVJpHcDkoP:bg60o9o) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/46c490b0e8817bbcb79fe2c54dfe56cd2ab564a7/pkg/cmd/roachtest/operations/inspect.go#L37)):* > ```Go > if err != nil { > var pqErr *pq.Error > if errors.As(err, &pqErr) && pgcode.MakeCode(string(pqErr.Code)) == pgcode.FeatureNotSupported { > ``` Do we ever emit feature not support for a session variable? I think if we are on a really old build that didn't have any inspect code, then it would output `pgcode.UndefinedObject` if the session variable didn't exist. But then it's not clear to me if we would be running new drt code. Maybe worthwhile to ask about about having versioning code in the drt operations. I don't see any versioning code in other operations. ___ *[`pkg/cmd/roachtest/operations/inspect.go` line 44 at r1](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/158021#-OeT0auE36E0i7Xeb9bQ:-OeT0auE36E0i7Xeb9bR:b2rvmm2) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/46c490b0e8817bbcb79fe2c54dfe56cd2ab564a7/pkg/cmd/roachtest/operations/inspect.go#L44)):* > ```Go > } > > rows, err := conn.QueryContext(ctx, "SELECT database_name FROM [SHOW DATABASES]") > ``` I wonder if we should just call `PickRandomDB` and inspect one database for the entire operation. There is another operation that add/drops database. If that is interleaved with this command, then you may try to inspect a non-existent database.

It depends on the cluster. For `drt-chaos`, it spins up 3 parallel workers that randomly picks an operation as long as it: 1. Satisfies all preconditions, e.g. can run concurrently, has no unavailable ranges, et.c 2. Hasn't been run too recently (more than `cadence` duration has passed). For `drt-chaos` this is 5 minutes, which might be confusing since it sounds like it would run operations with a 5 minute candence? > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/158021#-:-OeXWBZF0K-81V4Vf-JY:b6bfanb)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on @bghal, @nameisbhaskar, and @shailendra-patel)

title: drtprod: add Slack notification support and YAML progress tracking
body:
This PR builds the capability in the YAML processor to send slack updates if the slack token and a slack channel is passed as a flag. This enables us to provide status of YAML execution which becomes handy when we are running scale tests. Currently, we manually put an update on the channel with the status.

To enable notification, we need to pass the slack-token and slack-channel flags and also, ensure that "notify_progress" flag is set to true for all targets and steps that requires slack notification.

Epic: None
Release note: None
author: nameisbhaskar

LGMT! Just a few small failures on CI that needs to be addressed.

commits.message:
drtprod: add Slack notification support and YAML progress tracking

This PR builds the capability in the YAML processor to send slack updates if the slack token and a slack channel is passed as a flag. This enables us to provide status of YAML execution which becomes handy when we are running scale tests. Currently, we manually put an update on the channel with the status.

To enable notification, we need to pass the slack-token and slack-channel flags and also, ensure that "notify_progress" flag is set to true for all targets and steps that requires slack notification.

One more change was done to use a variable function for fmt.Printf. This was done as the tests were failing due to HEAP full because of too much data in stdout.

Epic: None

title: sql: add descriptions for all session vars
body:
Add a `Description` field to the `sessionVar` struct and populate it for
every non-hidden session variable. An init-time check panics if any
non-hidden var is missing a description, ensuring new vars get documented.

Descriptions are centralized in a separate `session_var_descriptions.go`
map for maintainability.

Add a `ClusterSettingName` field to indicate which cluster setting
provides the default value for a session variable, when applicable. An
init-time check validates that each `ClusterSettingName` refers to a real
registered cluster setting.

Add a docgen command (`docgen session-vars`) that generates a markdown
reference table at `docs/generated/sql/session_vars.md` with name,
description, default value, read-only status, and associated cluster
setting.

Epic: None
Informs: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/101715
Release note: None

body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166112 on behalf of @mw5h.

----

## Summary

- Exclude lineitem (the largest TPC-H SF-100 dataset) from the
  `import/nodeShutdown/worker` and `import/nodeShutdown/coordinator`
  roachtests, matching the fix applied to `import/decommissioned` in
  d901a9a1910.
- These tests shut down 1 of 4 nodes, and importing lineitem with

Resolves: #166071

author: blathers-crl

reviews.body: > @yuzefovich reviewed 1 file and all commit messages. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166298)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on DrewKimball).

commits.message:
roachtest: exclude lineitem from import/nodeShutdown tests

The import/nodeShutdown roachtests have been timing out at the 10-hour
limit. Like the decommissioned test (fixed in d901a9a1910), these tests
shut down one of 4 nodes then import a random TPC-H SF-100 dataset.
When lineitem (the largest dataset) is selected, the reduced cluster
capacity (~3 active nodes) combined with metamorphic encryption and
expiration leases pushes the import + fingerprint validation past the
timeout.

Exclude lineitem from the dataset pool for the nodeShutdown/worker and
nodeShutdown/coordinator tests, keeping the remaining 5 datasets which
complete comfortably within the time budget.

Resolves: #166071

Release note: None

title: github-action-poller: detect and follow workflow reruns
body:
When a workflow fails, Blathers may re-run failed jobs for the same SHA. The poller now handles this by waiting (default 3 minutes, configurable via --rerun-wait) after a failure and checking if any required check has a new incomplete run. If so, it restarts polling from scratch. This is attempted up to 3 times.

The original RunE logic is extracted into `pollChecks`, and the single-shot GitHub API check is renamed to `checkStatus`. A shared `fetchCheckRuns` helper handles API pagination for both `checkStatus` and the new `hasNewCheckRuns` function.

Epic: None
Release note: None
author: rail

commits.message:

a new incomplete run. If so, it restarts polling from scratch. This is
attempted up to 3 times.

The original RunE logic is extracted into `pollChecks`, and the single-shot
GitHub API check is renamed to `checkStatus`. A shared `fetchCheckRuns`
helper handles API pagination for both `checkStatus` and the new

author: dhartunian

commits.message:
server/status: fix TestStructuredEventLogging flake

Replace the fixed `time.Sleep` approach with `testutils.SucceedsWithin`
polling. Under CI load the 500ms sample timer can jitter significantly,
and with external process tenants the server startup delay can push
`testStartTs` past the first few timer firings, causing the test to
find no `runtime_stats` entries within its 1-second sleep window.

title: cli: strip license NOTICE before collapsing SQL data retrieval lines
body:
Strip `NOTICE: No license is installed...` messages in `RunWithCapture` **before** the `<dumping SQL tables>` collapsing regex runs

Previously, NOTICE messages injected by the SQL driver could split `retrieving SQL data for ...` lines across multiple lines, breaking the collapsing and leaving orphaned fragments in test output

Also broadens the safety-net NOTICE regex in `eraseNonDeterministicZipOutput` to catch inline occurrences (not just line-start)

Simplifies the `<dumping SQL tables>` regex by removing the now-unnecessary `|.*NOTICE.*` alternative

This fixes `TestPartialZip` failures on release-25.2 where the NOTICE stripping didn't exist, and hardens master/26.x against the same issue when NOTICE appears inline.

Resolves: #165776
Resolves: #166149

Release note: None
author: dhartunian

commits.message:
cli: strip license NOTICE before collapsing SQL data retrieval lines

The SQL driver can inject `NOTICE: No license is installed...` messages
into output nondeterministically. When these appear inline within a
`retrieving SQL data for ...` line, they split the output across multiple
lines, preventing the `<dumping SQL tables>` collapsing regex from
matching. This leaves orphaned fragments (`<dumping SQL tables>`,
retrieval lines with embedded NOTICE, standalone NOTICE lines, and
` done` on its own line) in the test output.

Fix this by stripping NOTICE messages in `RunWithCapture` before the
`<dumping SQL tables>` collapsing runs. This ensures retrieval lines
remain intact and collapse correctly. Also simplify the collapsing regex
by removing the now-unnecessary `|.*NOTICE.*` alternative, and broaden
the safety-net regex in `eraseNonDeterministicZipOutput` to catch inline
NOTICE (not just line-start).

Resolves: #165776
Resolves: #166149

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: cli: strip ash_report files from zip test output comparison
body:
Strip nondeterministic ASH report files from `trimNonDeterministicZipOutputFiles` in zip tests

The ASH report profiler generates timestamped files (`ash_report.<ts>.goroutine_dump.json/txt`) in the heapprof directory that cause `TestZipIncludeAndExcludeFilesDataDriven` to fail when comparing against static testdata

Follows the same pattern already used for goroutine_dump, memprof, and cpuprof files

Resolves: #166244
Resolves: #166292

Release note: None
author: dhartunian

commits.message:
cli: strip ash_report files from zip test output comparison

The ASH (Active Session History) report profiler generates files with
timestamps in their names (e.g., `ash_report.<timestamp>.goroutine_dump.json`)
in the heapprof directory. These nondeterministic filenames cause
`TestZipIncludeAndExcludeFilesDataDriven` to fail when comparing zip
contents against static testdata.

Add a regex to `trimNonDeterministicZipOutputFiles` to strip ash_report
file entries, matching the existing pattern used for goroutine_dump,
memprof, and cpuprof files.

Resolves: #166244
Resolves: #166292

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: release-26.2: sqlstats: fix TestSQLStatsDiscardStatsOnFingerprintLimit flake
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166684 on behalf of @dhartunian.

----

Replace hardcoded 5s timeout in `waitForStatementStatsRows` and `waitForTransactionStatsRows` with `testutils.SucceedsSoonDuration()`, which scales to 225s under race builds.

Remove `skip.UnderRace` from `TestSQLStatsDiscardStatsOnFingerprintLimit` since the timeout fix makes it safe to run under race.

Fixes #166668

Release note: None

----

Release justification: test-only change
author: dhartunian

commits.message:
sqlstats: fix TestSQLStatsDiscardStatsOnFingerprintLimit flake

The `waitForStatementStatsRows` and `waitForTransactionStatsRows` helpers
used a hardcoded 5s timeout, which is insufficient under race builds
where the race detector significantly slows execution. Replace with
`testutils.SucceedsSoonDuration()` which automatically scales to 225s
under race (matching the standard `SucceedsSoon` behavior).

With the timeout fix in place, remove the `skip.UnderRace` from
`TestSQLStatsDiscardStatsOnFingerprintLimit` so the test runs under
race as intended.

Fixes #166668

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: release-26.2: security,acceptance: refactor certAuth for explicit SAN/DN/CN paths and add integration tests
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166289 on behalf of @sanchit-CRL.

----

* The PR refactors certAuth function to separate SAN, DN, and CN validation paths which includes:
- Extract SAN validation from ValidateUserScope into UserAuthCertHook, making the three auth methods
(SAN, DN, CN) explicit sequential checks rather than interleaved logic in a single function
- SAN check runs first when clientCertSANRequired is set, a matching SAN succeeds immediately (with
scope validation for root/node/debug users)
- DN check runs next when roleSubject is set, a matching DN succeeds immediately without requiring CN-based scope validation. Which is the exact behaviour as it was before, so this PR doesn't modify what was already present for DN (proof is all test case
for DN successfully passing)
- CN-based scope validation is the final fallback, blocked when clientCertSANRequired is set to prevent bypassing SAN auth
- Simplify ValidateUserScope signature by removing roleSubject, certSubject, clientCertSANRequired, and certSANs parameters, since this function should
only focus on user scopes
- Fix: disallowRootLogin and allowDebugUser checks now correctly apply in the SAN auth path (previously
short-circuited), as it was trying to follow what had been done for subject mapping

* Additionally it add SAN certificate authentication integration tests, which include:
- SAN flags require the san_required cluster setting to enforce
- Root SAN validation (matching, wrong, missing, cross-user, superset)
- Multiple SAN types (DNS, URI, IP) with subset enforcement
- SAN + DN OR logic (SAN short-circuits, DN fallback)
- SAN identity mapping via HBA (positive, missing SANs, wrong user)

Epic: CRDB-60660
Resolves: #164138

Release note: None

----

Release justification: This contains some code change and important acceptance test for the SAN auth feature which is releasing in 26.2
author: sanchit-CRL

- Extract SAN validation from ValidateUserScope into

- SAN check runs first when clientCertSANRequired is
set, a matching SAN succeeds immediately (with

a matching DN succeeds immediately without requiring
CN-based scope validation. Which is the exact behaviour
as it was before, so this PR doesn't modify what was

for DN successfully passing)
- CN-based scope validation is the final fallback,

title: operations: resume longest-paused changefeed instead of random
body:
Previously, `resume-changefeed-job` picked a random paused changefeed to resume. When multiple changefeeds were paused, this could leave one paused indefinitely while newer ones got resumed first, causing `protected_age_sec` to grow unboundedly and blocking MVCC GC.

Sort paused jobs by `high_water_timestamp` ascending when resuming so the longest-paused changefeed is always picked first. Other actions (PAUSE, CANCEL) continue to select randomly.

Resolves: #166034
Epic: CRDB-61724

Release note: None
author: shailendra-patel

commits.message:
operations: resume longest-paused changefeed instead of random

Previously, `resume-changefeed-job` picked a random paused changefeed
to resume. When multiple changefeeds were paused, this could leave one
paused indefinitely while newer ones got resumed first, causing
`protected_age_sec` to grow unboundedly and blocking MVCC GC.

Sort paused jobs by `high_water_timestamp` ascending when resuming so
the longest-paused changefeed is always picked first. Other actions
(PAUSE, CANCEL) continue to select randomly.

Resolves: #166034
Epic: CRDB-61724

- Fix: disallowRootLogin and allowDebugUser checks now correctly apply in the SAN auth path (previously
short-circuited), as it was trying to follow what had been done for subject mapping

* Additionally it add SAN certificate authentication integration tests, which include:
- SAN flags require the san_required cluster setting to enforce
- Root SAN validation (matching, wrong, missing, cross-user, superset)
- Multiple SAN types (DNS, URI, IP) with subset enforcement
- SAN + DN OR logic (SAN short-circuits, DN fallback)
- SAN identity mapping via HBA (positive, missing SANs, wrong user)

Epic: CRDB-60660
Resolves: #164138

Release note: None

acceptance: Integration test for SAN auth in cert

* The PR refactors certAuth function to separate
SAN, DN, and CN validation paths which includes:
- Extract SAN validation from ValidateUserScope into
UserAuthCertHook, making the three auth methods
(SAN, DN, CN) explicit sequential checks rather than
interleaved logic in a single function
- SAN check runs first when clientCertSANRequired is
set, a matching SAN succeeds immediately (with
scope validation for root/node/debug users)
- DN check runs next when roleSubject is set,
a matching DN succeeds immediately without requiring

as it was before, so this PR doesn't modify what was
already present for DN (proof is all test case

blocked when clientCertSANRequired is set to prevent
bypassing SAN auth
- Simplify ValidateUserScope signature by removing

certSANs parameters, since this function should
only focus on user scopes
- Fix: disallowRootLogin and allowDebugUser checks now
correctly apply in the SAN auth path (previously

integration tests, which include:

title: workflows: instruct autosolver to read issue comments
body:
The issue autosolver only read the issue title and body, missing
valuable context that is frequently posted in comments such as
follow-up reproduction steps, stack traces, clarifications, and
root cause analysis. Instruct both the assessment and implementation
stages to also fetch issue comments via `gh issue view --comments`.

Resolves: #166772

title: sql: add TRUNCATE privilege for tables
body:
Previously, CockroachDB required DROP privilege to execute TRUNCATE,
diverging from PostgreSQL which has a dedicated TRUNCATE privilege.
Users could not GRANT or REVOKE TRUNCATE on tables.

This commit adds TRUNCATE as a first-class privilege kind (Kind = 45)
on tables. The TRUNCATE statement now accepts either the TRUNCATE or
DROP privilege for backward compatibility. The has_table_privilege()
builtin is updated to map 'TRUNCATE' to the new privilege kind
instead of DELETE.

Both the legacy and declarative schema changer paths are updated to
check TRUNCATE first, falling back to DROP.

Resolves: #111529

Release note (sql change): Added a TRUNCATE table privilege,
matching PostgreSQL's privilege model. Users can now GRANT TRUNCATE
and REVOKE TRUNCATE on tables. The TRUNCATE statement accepts either
the new TRUNCATE privilege or the existing DROP privilege for backward
compatibility.
author: rafiss

reviews.body: > @fqazi partially reviewed 16 files and made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166083#-:-OofjPvMEfuP_0prwo9K:bnfp4nl)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on shghasemi).

commits.message:
sql: add TRUNCATE privilege for tables

Previously, CockroachDB required DROP privilege to execute TRUNCATE,
diverging from PostgreSQL which has a dedicated TRUNCATE privilege.
Users could not GRANT or REVOKE TRUNCATE on tables.

This commit adds TRUNCATE as a first-class privilege kindon tables.
We use the bitmask of the existing RULE privilege, since that was a
pseudo-privilege that never is persisted anywhere, and it's helpful to
avoid using up more bit positions.

The TRUNCATE statement now accepts either the TRUNCATE or
DROP privilege for backward compatibility. The has_table_privilege()
builtin is updated to map 'TRUNCATE' to the new privilege kind
instead of DELETE.

Both the legacy and declarative schema changer paths are updated to
check TRUNCATE first, falling back to DROP.

Resolves: #111529

Release note (sql change): Added a TRUNCATE table privilege,
matching PostgreSQL's privilege model. Users can now GRANT TRUNCATE
and REVOKE TRUNCATE on tables. The TRUNCATE statement accepts either
the new TRUNCATE privilege or the existing DROP privilege for backward
compatibility.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

sql/schemachanger: make CheckPrivilege accept multiple privileges with OR semantics

The declarative schema changer's CheckPrivilege now accepts variadic
privileges, returning nil if the user holds any one of them. This
consolidates the "check A or B" pattern used in truncate
(TRUNCATE/DROP) and zone config (CREATE/ZONECONFIG) privilege checks.

The privilege cache is changed from map[privilege.Kind]error to
map[privilege.Kind]bool, using HasPrivilege internally. When no
privilege is held, the error message lists all acceptable privileges.

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: release-26.1: logictestccl: fix as_of flake by increasing closed timestamp target
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166111 on behalf of @mw5h.

----

The TestCCLLogic_as_of test has been repeatedly flaking (deflaked 6+
times) because follower_read_timestamp() returns now()-4.2s by default,
which in race builds can land mid-bootstrap — after database creation
but before table creation — producing "relation t does not exist"
instead of the expected "database test does not exist".

Rather than continuing to widen error regexes (whack-a-mole), this
patch increases kv.closed_timestamp.target_duration to 1m for the
follower read portion of the test. This makes follower_read_timestamp()
go back ~62s, reliably predating cluster bootstrap even in slow builds.
The setting is reset to 3s before the bounded staleness tests that
follow.

Fixes: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/166055

Epic: none
Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

----

Release justification: Test only fix.
author: blathers-crl

reviews.body: > @michae2 reviewed 1 file and all commit messages, and made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166300#-:-OoBeSDX-RfKcW0pwRJO:bnfp4nl)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on mw5h).

commits.message:
logictestccl: fix as_of flake by increasing closed timestamp target

The TestCCLLogic_as_of test has been repeatedly flaking (deflaked 6+
times) because follower_read_timestamp() returns now()-4.2s by default,
which in race builds can land mid-bootstrap — after database creation
but before table creation — producing "relation t does not exist"
instead of the expected "database test does not exist".

Rather than continuing to widen error regexes (whack-a-mole), this
patch increases kv.closed_timestamp.target_duration to 1m for the
follower read portion of the test. This makes follower_read_timestamp()
go back ~62s, reliably predating cluster bootstrap even in slow builds.
The setting is reset to 3s before the bounded staleness tests that
follow.

Fixes: #166055

Epic: none
Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: roachtest: retry transient errors in cdc/multiple-schema-changes
body:
The cdc/multiple-schema-changes roachtest used ExecMultiple to run ALTER TABLE DROP col statements, which fatals on any error with no retry. A transient "connection refused" error on any ALTER causes the test to never reach the highwater check, leading to a confusing timeout.

Replace ExecMultiple with individual SucceedsSoon retry loops for each ALTER statement. Also handle the UndefinedColumn error case where a previous attempt succeeded at the KV level but the connection dropped before the response was returned.

Fixes: #155830
Fixes: #163154

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

Release note: None
author: rharding6373

reviews.body: LMK if you'd prefer to close this PR (and the related issues) as not enough info.
LGTM

commits.message:
roachtest: separate ALTER TABLE statements in cdc/multiple-schema-changes

The cdc/multiple-schema-changes roachtest used ExecMultiple to run ALTER
TABLE DROP col statements. ExecMultiple uses context.Background(), so
if a query hangs (e.g. due to a temporarily unavailable node), it blocks
indefinitely and cannot be cancelled. The test's 1-hour timeout fires on
the runner goroutine while the test goroutine is still stuck, so the
failure is reported as a timeout with no useful error. The actual error
(e.g. "connection refused") only surfaces later as a suppressed
failure_2 log entry.

Replace ExecMultiple with individual sqlDB.Exec calls so each ALTER
TABLE statement is executed separately. Use DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS for
idempotency.

Fixes: #155830
Fixes: #163154

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: release-26.2: opt: allow `ST_AsMVT` to be used as a window function
body:
Backport 3/3 commits from #166779 on behalf of @michae2.

----

### Revert "optbuilder: prohibit usage of st_asmvt as a window function"

This reverts #165757. The next commit in this branch fixes the
underlying panic that motivated PR #165757, so the prohibition is no
longer needed.

Regarding PG compatibility, It's true that in PostGIS / PostgreSQL,
`ST_AsMVT` cannot be used as a window function. But this restriction is
due to the implementation of `ST_AsMVT` in PostGIS, not some fundamental
logical problem with using it as a window function. PostGIS has other
aggregation functions that cannot be used as window functions in
PostgreSQL which can be used as window functions in CockroachDB
(e.g. `ST_Union`, `ST_Collect`, `ST_MakeLine`) so I don't think we need
to match this limitation of PostGIS for `ST_AsMVT`.

Furthermore, the Cockroach optimizer assumes that all aggregation
functions can work as window functions, and occasionally transforms them
into window functions, so I don't think we should introduce a
prohibition on this usage if we can help it.

Informs: #165687
Informs: #166382

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

---

### opt: allow `ST_AsMVT` to be used as a window function

The only reason we couldn't use `ST_AsMVT` as a window function was
because the NULL-padding in `constructAggregate` was breaking
`buildWindow`, which expected VariableExpr for all columns.

Fix this by padding `ST_AsMVT`'s optional arguments with typed NULL
defaults in `buildAggregateFunction`, so the default value columns are
added to the scope before the projection is built. In the
`buildAggregationAsWindow` path, the arguments are also padded before
`buildWindowArgs` so they get projected into columns and become
VariableExpr nodes in the memo.

Informs: #57829
Informs: #165687
Fixes: #166382

Release note (sql change): Aggregation function `ST_AsMVT` can now also
be used as a window function.

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

---

### sql: add logic tests for `ST_AsMVT` as a window function

Add tests for using `ST_AsMVT` as a window function with various
configurations (OVER (), PARTITION BY, ORDER BY, custom layer/extent).
Also add a regression test for the panic that occurred when `ST_AsMVT`
with NULL arguments was combined with ordered aggregates.

Informs: #57829
Informs: #166382

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

----

Release justification: fix newly introduced aggregate function to prevent internal errors during optimization.

title: release-26.2: sql: disable test tenant for tests using TestingDisableTableLeases
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166108 on behalf of @mw5h.

----

## Summary

Several tests directly manipulate table descriptors via KV writes and rely on
`lease.TestingDisableTableLeases()` to make those changes immediately visible
to the SQL layer. This process-global atomic bool only takes effect within the
test process; when an external process virtual cluster is metamorphically
injected, the external SQL server's lease manager still caches descriptors
normally and never sees the direct KV writes, causing intermittent failures.

This PR disables the default test tenant for all tests that combine
`TestingDisableTableLeases` with direct KV descriptor manipulation:

- `descriptor_mutation_test.go`: 5 tests
- `index_mutation_test.go`: 1 test
- `delete_preserving_index_test.go`: 3 test sites
- `crdb_internal_test.go`: 2 tests

Fixes: #165980

Release note: None

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

----

Release justification: Test only fix.
author: blathers-crl

reviews.body: > @yuzefovich reviewed 4 files and all commit messages. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166694)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on mw5h and ZhouXing19).

commits.message:
sql: disable test tenant for tests using TestingDisableTableLeases

Several tests directly manipulate table descriptors via KV writes and
rely on `lease.TestingDisableTableLeases()` to make those changes
immediately visible to the SQL layer. This process-global atomic bool
only takes effect within the test process; when an external process
virtual cluster is metamorphically injected, the external SQL server's
lease manager still caches descriptors normally and never sees the
direct KV writes. This causes intermittent failures where the SQL layer
uses stale descriptors.

The reported failure in TestOperationsWithIndexMutation manifests as a
missing row after UPSERT: the previous subtest's direct KV descriptor
writes (8+ version bumps) leave stale lease state in the external
process, and after the table is dropped and recreated, the first UPSERT
can silently target stale state.

Fix this by setting `DefaultTestTenant` to disable external process
virtual cluster injection in all tests that combine
`TestingDisableTableLeases` with direct KV descriptor manipulation.
Since shared-process mode works fine (same process, same atomic bool),
we use `TestDoesNotWorkWithExternalProcessMode` rather than disabling
all secondary tenant modes.

These tests were exposed when the package-wide tenant injection override
was removed in 3f64b0eb656 (Oct 2025). Since injection is metamorphic,
failures are intermittent.

Fixes: #165980
Informs: #166311

Release note: None

title: release-26.2: importer: reduce TestImportData runtime to fix timeout
body:
Backport 2/2 commits from #166303 on behalf of @mw5h.

----

## Summary

Two commits to fix importer test timeouts:

**Commit 1: Parallelize TestImportData subtests and reduce coverage**

`TestImportData` was timing out at the 15-minute limit with ~89 sequential
subtests at ~7-10s each. Two changes:
- Parallelize subtests by replacing the shared `mockRecorder` with a
  `sync.Map` keyed by per-subtest URL paths, giving each subtest its own
  DB connection.
- Only run `save_rejected=true` for test cases that actually verify rejected
  row output (`tc.rejected != ""`).

**Commit 2: Disable elastic CPU control in remaining import tests**

Follow-up to #165443 which disabled elastic CPU control in a few import
tests. Extends the fix to all remaining high-risk tests: TestFailedImport,
TestImportIntoCSVCancel, TestImportDefault, TestUniqueUUID,
TestImportDefaultNextVal, TestImportComputed, TestImportWorkerFailure,
TestImportDelimited, TestImportPgCopy.

Resolves: #166199
Resolves: #166130

Release note: None

----

Release justification: Test only fix.

commits.message:
importer: split TestImportData into per-format tests to fix timeout

TestImportData had ~70 sequential subtests, each taking ~7-10s,
totaling ~13+ minutes which regularly exceeded the 15-minute shard
timeout. This also re-enables the test after it was skipped via
skip.WithIssue in 38f856010f3.

Split the monolithic test into separate top-level tests by import
format (TestImportDataCSV, TestImportDataDelimited, TestImportDataPgCopy,
TestImportDataMysqloutMultiple). Each runs independently against its own
server and naturally distributes across test shards. The shared test
table and execution loop are extracted into importDataTests and
runImportDataTests.

Resolves: #166199
Resolves: #166130

importer: disable elastic CPU control in remaining import tests

Elastic CPU throttling (bulkio.import.elastic_control.enabled) was
recently enabled by default and can cause import tests to run
significantly slower, pushing them past the 15-minute shard timeout.

Disable it in all remaining import tests that don't already have it
disabled: TestFailedImport, TestImportIntoCSVCancel, TestImportDefault,
TestImportDefaultNextVal, TestImportComputed, TestImportWorkerFailure,
TestImportDelimited, TestImportPgCopy.

Resolves: #166130

title: tree: prevent double type annotation for FuncExpr in AnnotateTypeExpr
body:
When a FuncExpr with AmbiguousReturnType (e.g., statement_timestamp with preferred overloads) is wrapped in an AnnotateTypeExpr (:::TYPE) and has been type-checked, both FuncExpr.Format and AnnotateTypeExpr.Format add :::TYPE annotations under FmtParsable, producing a double annotation like `statement_timestamp():::TIMESTAMP:::TIMESTAMP`. This grows on each serialize/deserialize round-trip and can cause type-check errors when types don't match.

Fix AnnotateTypeExpr.Format to skip its annotation when the inner FuncExpr already provides one, following the same pattern already used for Array expressions. Also fix NewFunctionDefinition to copy the input FunctionProperties before mutating AmbiguousReturnType, preventing mutation of shared pointers like cdcFnProps.

Fixes: #147573
author: rharding6373

reviews.body: > @mw5h reviewed 3 files and all commit messages, and made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/164751#-:-OmpwsdbCPNA6_KTQ-Fi:bnfp4nl)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on aerfrei).
Looks good, I left a comment with a test to add.

commits.message:
tree: prevent double type annotation for FuncExpr in AnnotateTypeExpr

When a FuncExpr with AmbiguousReturnType (e.g., statement_timestamp with
preferred overloads) is wrapped in an AnnotateTypeExpr (:::TYPE) and has
been type-checked, both FuncExpr.Format and AnnotateTypeExpr.Format add
:::TYPE annotations under FmtParsable, producing a double annotation like
`statement_timestamp():::TIMESTAMP:::TIMESTAMP`. This grows on each
serialize/deserialize round-trip and can cause type-check errors when
types don't match.

Fix AnnotateTypeExpr.Format to skip its annotation when the inner
FuncExpr already provides one, following the same pattern already used
for Array expressions. Also fix NewFunctionDefinition to copy the input
FunctionProperties before mutating AmbiguousReturnType, preventing
mutation of shared pointers like cdcFnProps.

Fixes: #147573

title: storage: fix AES-256-CTR-V2 JWK algorithm string typo
body:
A typo error in EncryptionType_AES_256_CTR_V2.JWKAlgorithm() caused it to return "cockroach-aes-192-ctr-v2" instead of "cockroach-aes-256-ctr-v2". This meant that V2 256-bit store keys were written to JWK files with the wrong algorithm string, and when loaded back via EncryptionTypeFromJWKAlgorithm(), they were interpreted as AES-192. As a result, data keys generated under these store keys were 24 bytes (192-bit) instead of the expected 32 bytes (256-bit).

The fix is inspired by BastienClement's report in #160004 and has three parts:

1. Fix the typo in JWKAlgorithm() so newly written keys get the correct algorithm string.

2. Add a backward-compatibility cross-check in LoadKeyFromFile: after parsing a JWK file, if the algorithm string maps to AES_192_CTR_V2 but the actual key material is 32 bytes, correct the EncryptionType to AES_256_CTR_V2. This ensures existing JWK files written with the buggy algorithm string continue to work after upgrade without manual intervention.

3. Fix SetActiveStoreKeyInfo to detect EncryptionType changes for the same key ID. Previously the early-return check only compared KeyId, so when the load-time correction changed the type from 192 to 256, the function would short-circuit and never rotate the data key to the correct size. The fix adds EncryptionType to the comparison and relaxes the inactive-key reuse guard to allow the current active key to be re-registered with a corrected type.

Tests added:

- TestJWKAlgorithmRoundtrip: verifies JWKAlgorithm() and EncryptionTypeFromJWKAlgorithm() are inverses for all encryption types, directly catching the original typo.

- TestGenEncryptionKey: extended to verify that the EncryptionType loaded back from a generated key file matches the requested size, exercising the full write-then-read path.

- TestDataKeyManager: new data-driven test section simulating the upgrade path where a store key is re-registered with a corrected EncryptionType. Confirms that the fix is backward compatible and seamless: the type correction triggers a data key rotation with the correct type, old data keys remain in the registry and accessible via GetKey (so existing encrypted data can still be decrypted), and no errors occur during the transition.

Co-authored-by: @BastienClement <BastienClement@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Code <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fixes #160004

Release note: None
Epic: None

commits.message:
storage: fix AES-256-CTR-V2 JWK algorithm string typo

A typo error in EncryptionType_AES_256_CTR_V2.JWKAlgorithm() caused
it to return "cockroach-aes-192-ctr-v2" instead of
"cockroach-aes-256-ctr-v2". This meant that V2 256-bit store keys
were written to JWK files with the wrong algorithm string, and when
loaded back via EncryptionTypeFromJWKAlgorithm(), they were
interpreted as AES-192. As a result, data keys generated under these
store keys were 24 bytes (192-bit) instead of the expected 32 bytes
(256-bit).

The fix is inspired by BastienClement's report in #160004 and has
three parts:

1. Fix the typo in JWKAlgorithm() so newly written keys get the
   correct algorithm string.

2. Add a backward-compatibility cross-check in LoadKeyFromFile:
   after parsing a JWK file, if the algorithm string maps to
   AES_192_CTR_V2 but the actual key material is 32 bytes, correct
   the EncryptionType to AES_256_CTR_V2. This ensures existing JWK
   files written with the buggy algorithm string continue to work
   after upgrade without manual intervention.

3. Fix SetActiveStoreKeyInfo to detect EncryptionType changes for
   the same key ID. Previously the early-return check only compared
   KeyId, so when the load-time correction changed the type from
   192 to 256, the function would short-circuit and never rotate
   the data key to the correct size. The fix adds EncryptionType to
   the comparison and relaxes the inactive-key reuse guard to allow
   the current active key to be re-registered with a corrected type.

Co-authored-by: BastienClement <BastienClement@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Code <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fixes #160004

Release note (bug fix): Fixed a bug where store encryption keys
generated with `cockroach gen encryption-key --version=2 --size=256`
were written with an incorrect JWK algorithm string, causing them to
be misclassified as AES-192-CTR-V2 on load. This resulted in data
keys being generated at 24 bytes (AES-192) instead of the intended
32 bytes (AES-256). Existing key files with the wrong algorithm
string are now automatically corrected on load, and data keys are
rotated to the correct size on the next node startup.

title: release-26.2: sql,workload: fix aclitem usage in mixed-version schemachange workload
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166750.

/cc @cockroachdb/release

---

The schemachange workload used RandSortingType to pick random column types, which could select aclitem even in mixed-version clusters where older nodes don't know about it. This caused "function does not exist" errors when resolving function signatures via pg_catalog.pg_type on nodes that lack the aclitem type entry.

Fix this by switching the workload to use RandColumnType, which respects IsLegalColumnType (which already excludes aclitem).

Additionally, remove redundant oidext.T_aclitem and oidext.T__aclitem constants in favor of the upstream oid.T_aclitem and oid.T__aclitem that are now available in lib/pq.

Resolves: #166032
Resolves: #166220

Release note: None

Release justification: bug fix for mixed-version schemachange workload errors
author: rafiss

reviews.body: > @fqazi reviewed 20 files and all commit messages, and made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166826#-:-OoezNhP42q0P3EJONX4:bnfp4nl)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on cpj2195, golgeek, and ZhouXing19).

commits.message:
sql,workload: fix aclitem usage in mixed-version schemachange workload

The schemachange workload used RandSortingType to pick random column
types, which could select aclitem even in mixed-version clusters where
older nodes don't know about it. This caused "function does not exist"
errors when resolving function signatures via pg_catalog.pg_type on
nodes that lack the aclitem type entry.

Fix this by switching the workload to use RandColumnType, which
respects IsLegalColumnType (which already excludes aclitem).

Also add a version gate in ValidateColumnDefType so that aclitem
columns cannot be created until the cluster is fully upgraded to v26.2.

Additionally, remove redundant oidext.T_aclitem and oidext.T__aclitem
constants in favor of the upstream oid.T_aclitem and oid.T__aclitem
that are now available in lib/pq.

Resolves: #166032
Resolves: #166220

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: ui: migrate Statements page from Redux to SWR
body:
The Statements page currently fetches data through Redux sagas
(sqlStats, statementDiagnostics) with manual refresh calls on
mount and in response to user actions. This requires connected
components to thread numerous data and dispatch props through
multiple layers.

Add SWR hooks that replace the Redux data-fetching layer:
- useCombinedStatementStats: wraps getCombinedStatements with
time/limit/sort params encoded in the SWR key for automatic
refetching on change.
- useStatementDiagnostics: fetches diagnostic reports with
adaptive polling (30s when pending requests exist, disabled
otherwise).
- useCreateDiagnosticsReport / useCancelDiagnosticsReport:
call the create/cancel APIs then revalidate the diagnostics
SWR cache on completion.
- useResetSQLStats: calls the reset API then invalidates all
statement, transaction, and statement detail SWR caches using
a key filter.
- useDatabasesList: wraps getDatabasesList for the databases
dropdown.

-----

The Statements page receives data through ~13 Redux-connected
props (statementsResponse, statementDiagnostics, nodeRegions,
role flags, etc.) and dispatches ~6 refresh actions on mount and
in response to user interactions. This creates tight coupling
between the component and the Redux store shape.

Replace all data-fetching props with direct SWR hook calls inside
StatementsPage: useCombinedStatementStats, useStatementDiagnostics,
useCreateDiagnosticsReport, useCancelDiagnosticsReport, useNodes,
useUserSQLRoles, useDatabasesList, and useResetSQLStats. The
component now auto-fetches on mount via SWR and refetches when the
committed search criteria (time scale, limit, sort) change. Manual
refresh calls in mount/update effects are removed. isTenant is read
from ClusterDetailsContext instead of being threaded as a prop.

The connected components in both cluster-ui and db-console are
simplified to only provide UI state (filters, sort, columns) and
analytics callbacks.


-----

After migrating the Statements page to SWR, several Redux
artifacts are no longer dispatched or consumed by any production
code.

Remove createOpenDiagnosticsModalAction and its
OPEN_STATEMENT_DIAGNOSTICS_MODAL action constant, which were
the sole dispatcher for the trackOpenStatementsDiagnostics
analytics saga handler. Remove the dead saga handler and its
takeEvery registration from analyticsSagas. Delete two broken
storybook files that referenced the removed statementsResponse
prop.

Epic: CRDB-58145
Release note: None
author: jasonlmfong

we can skip the slow recursive permission change. We can check the gosdk build artifact
since it should exist to see if it is writable or not as an indicator.

While I'm here: the pruning can run on linux/ext4 just fine; only dedupe requires a
fancy filesystem like apfs (or zfs/btfs). Indeed we could even fallback to link on
linux if we want in the future.

Add and Wait calls. The fix removes the WaitGroup pattern
entirely (avoiding the race) and splits the test into two
focused tests for better isolation of future failures:

- TestChangefeedIdlenessWithWorkload: tests non-idle
  transitions with a throttled workload.

title: release-26.2: ccl/multiregionccl: deflake TestMultiRegionDataDriven_global_tables
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166247 on behalf of @cockroach-teamcity.

----

The test was flaky because the cluster setting
`kv.closed_timestamp.lead_for_global_reads_auto_tune.enabled` uses a
metamorphic constant that can randomly enable auto-tuning during tests.
When enabled on a localhost test cluster (near-zero inter-node latency),
auto-tuning selects a lower-latency policy bucket, reducing the closed
timestamp lead time. This reduced lead time provides insufficient buffer
for follower reads to be served consistently, causing the closed timestamp
to be too low (~27ms) when the test asserts that a GLOBAL table read is
served via follower read.

Explicitly disable auto-tuning in the test's cluster setup to ensure a
stable closed timestamp lead time.

Resolves: #165405
Resolves: #165266

title: release-26.2: sql: fix TestTrace flake from statement hints spans
body:
Backport 2/2 commits from #166334 on behalf of @mw5h.

----

## Summary

Fixes a flake in `TestTrace` caused by the statement hints feature introducing
internal query spans that leak into the session trace.

- **Commit 1**: Force `distsql=off` for statement hint queries
  (`CheckForStatementHintsInDB`, `GetStatementHintsFromDB`). These are simple
  point lookups that don't need distributed execution.

- **Commit 2**: Add `get-plan-hints` and `prepare stmt` to the always-optional
  spans list in `TestTrace` to handle the case where the hints cache rangefeed
  hasn't initialized yet and the internal query runs.

The root cause: before the `StatementHintsCache` rangefeed initializes
`hintedHashes` on a node, every statement falls through to querying
`system.statement_hints` via the internal executor. This internal query was
being distributed (due to `ORDER BY` with no stats), producing DistSQL spans
(`/cockroach.sql.distsqlrun.DistSQL/SetupFlow`) that the test didn't expect
when `distsql=off`.

Fixes #166212

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

----

Release justification: Test only fix.
author: blathers-crl

reviews.body: > @michae2 reviewed 2 files and all commit messages, and made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166482#-:-OoTAJ-805B3-BteYaAn:bnfp4nl)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on DrewKimball and ZhouXing19).

commits.message:
sql/hints: force local execution for statement hint queries

Statement hint queries (`CheckForStatementHintsInDB` and
`GetStatementHintsFromDB`) are simple point lookups on
`system.statement_hints` by hash. These should not incur the overhead
of distributed execution. Force `distsql=off` via a session data
override so the internal executor runs them locally.

Previously, when the statement hints cache rangefeed had not yet
initialized on a node, the cache would fall through to querying the
database. On multi-node clusters, the DistSQL planner could decide to
distribute this internal query (e.g. due to `ORDER BY` with no stats),
producing DistSQL-related trace spans that leaked into the session
trace.

Informs #166212

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

sql: add statement hints spans to optional list in TestTrace

Add `get-plan-hints` and `prepare stmt` to the always-optional spans
list in `TestTrace`. Before the statement hints cache rangefeed
initializes, the cache falls through to a database query whose spans
leak into the session trace and cause unexpected span name mismatches.

Fixes #166212

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: release-26.2: logictestccl: fix as_of flake by increasing closed timestamp target
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166111 on behalf of @mw5h.

----

The TestCCLLogic_as_of test has been repeatedly flaking (deflaked 6+
times) because follower_read_timestamp() returns now()-4.2s by default,
which in race builds can land mid-bootstrap — after database creation
but before table creation — producing "relation t does not exist"
instead of the expected "database test does not exist".

Rather than continuing to widen error regexes (whack-a-mole), this
patch increases kv.closed_timestamp.target_duration to 1m for the
follower read portion of the test. This makes follower_read_timestamp()
go back ~62s, reliably predating cluster bootstrap even in slow builds.
The setting is reset to 3s before the bounded staleness tests that
follow.

Fixes: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/166055

Epic: none
Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

----

Release justification: Test only fix
author: blathers-crl

reviews.body: > @michae2 reviewed 1 file and all commit messages, and made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166301#-:-OoBeNtuC1cgj3wLVsZ2:bnfp4nl)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on mw5h).

commits.message:
logictestccl: fix as_of flake by increasing closed timestamp target

The TestCCLLogic_as_of test has been repeatedly flaking (deflaked 6+
times) because follower_read_timestamp() returns now()-4.2s by default,
which in race builds can land mid-bootstrap — after database creation
but before table creation — producing "relation t does not exist"
instead of the expected "database test does not exist".

Rather than continuing to widen error regexes (whack-a-mole), this
patch increases kv.closed_timestamp.target_duration to 1m for the
follower read portion of the test. This makes follower_read_timestamp()
go back ~62s, reliably predating cluster bootstrap even in slow builds.
The setting is reset to 3s before the bounded staleness tests that
follow.

Fixes: #166055

Epic: none
Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: kvserver,server/status: handle disk stats counter wraps per-counter
body:
On s390x, /proc/diskstats counters are 32-bit unsigned and wrap after ~2^32 (~49.7 days of cumulative IO time). When this happens, the raw value drops to a small number, causing Counter.Update() to panic in test builds with "Counters should not decrease".

Store-level path (pkg/kv/kvserver/metrics.go):
Add an updateDiskCounter helper that detects per-counter wraps by comparing the new OS value against the current counter value. When a wrap is detected, the specific counter is cleared and the event is logged before updating.

Node-level path (pkg/server/status/runtime.go):
Refactor subtractDiskCounters to handle wraps per-counter instead of resetting all counters when any single one wraps. Each counter is now independently checked: wrapped counters have their baseline reset and result zeroed, while non-wrapped counters subtract normally.

Different counters accumulate at different rates and wrap independently, so per-counter handling preserves accuracy for counters that haven't wrapped.

Resolves: #162625
See also: #162624, #153048

Release note: None
author: dhartunian

commits.message:
kvserver,server/status: handle disk stats counter wraps per-counter

On s390x, /proc/diskstats counters are 32-bit unsigned and wrap after
~2^32 (~49.7 days of cumulative IO time). When this happens, the raw
value drops to a small number, causing Counter.Update() to panic in
test builds with "Counters should not decrease".

Store-level path (pkg/kv/kvserver/metrics.go):
Add an updateDiskCounter helper that detects per-counter wraps by
comparing the new OS value against the current counter value. When a
wrap is detected, the specific counter is cleared and the event is
logged before updating.

Node-level path (pkg/server/status/runtime.go):
Refactor subtractDiskCounters to handle wraps per-counter instead of
resetting all counters when any single one wraps. Each counter is now
independently checked: wrapped counters have their baseline reset and
result zeroed, while non-wrapped counters subtract normally.

Different counters accumulate at different rates and wrap
independently, so per-counter handling preserves accuracy for counters
that haven't wrapped.

Resolves: #162625
See also: #162624, #153048

title: storage: return error from SyncWait instead of panicking
body:
`writeBatch.SyncWait()` panicked on any error from the underlying Pebble `SyncWait`, including environmental failures like EPERM or EIO on WAL writes. This caused an ungraceful process crash, bypassing the `log.Fatalf` handler in `SyncWaiterLoop.waitLoop` that provides structured logging, crash reporting, and graceful shutdown.

Change SyncWait to return the error instead. Document the error semantics on the `WriteBatch.SyncWait` interface: an error means the WAL write or fsync failed, the data is already applied to the memtable but durability is not guaranteed, and the caller should treat it as fatal.

Fixes #163906

Release note: None
author: xxmplus

reviews.body: Looks good. AFAICT the only caller that would see this error is the `SyncWaiterLoop` which would fatal.

commits.message:
storage: return error from SyncWait instead of panicking

writeBatch.SyncWait() panicked on any error from the underlying Pebble
SyncWait, including environmental failures like EPERM or EIO on WAL
writes. This caused an ungraceful process crash, bypassing the
log.Fatalf handler in SyncWaiterLoop.waitLoop that provides structured
logging, crash reporting, and graceful shutdown.

Change SyncWait to return the error instead. Document the error
semantics on the WriteBatch.SyncWait interface: an error means the WAL
write or fsync failed, the data is already applied to the memtable but
durability is not guaranteed, and the caller should treat it as fatal.

Fixes #163906

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: release-26.2: security: removing curve preference of FIPS
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166290 on behalf of @sanchit-CRL.

----

* Previously, when running in FIPS mode, we restricted TLS curve preferences to only P-256. This was both redundant and overly restrictive: Go 1.25.5 already filters curves to FIPS-approved values internally (crypto/tls/defaults_fips140.go)
* Remove the explicit curve preference and let Go handle FIPS curve selection natively.
    
Release note (security update): Removed an overly restrictive TLS curve preference that limited FIPS mode to P-256. CockroachDB now uses Go's native FIPS curve selection, improving interoperability with clients that prefer other Go's native FIPS curves.

Epic: [CRDB-61940](https://cockroachlabs.atlassian.net/browse/CRDB-61940)
Fixes: #166494

----

Release justification: This removes an overly restrictive TLS curve preference limiting FIPS mode to P-256 only. The explicit curve restriction was redundant since Go's crypto/tls already handles FIPS curve filtering natively. This is required for 26.2 FIPS release
author: sanchit-CRL

commits.message:
security: removing curve preference of FIPS

* Previously, when running in FIPS mode, we restricted
TLS curve preferences to only P-256. This was both
redundant and overly restrictive: Go 1.25.5 already
filters curves to FIPS-approved values internally
(crypto/tls/defaults_fips140.go)
* Remove the explicit curve preference and let Go
handle FIPS curve selection natively.

Release note (security update): Removed an overly
restrictive TLS curve preference that limited FIPS mode
to P-256. CockroachDB now uses Go's native FIPS curve
selection, improving interoperability with clients that
prefer other Go's native FIPS curves.

Epic: CRDB-61940
Fixes: #166494

title: security: add telemetry and metrics for SAN-based cert auth
body:
* Add telemetry counter to track when the SAN authentication cluster setting is enabled, with ops-level logging on setting toggle.
* Add tenant-specific Prometheus counters for total and successful SAN-based certificate authentication attempts.
* Enhance certificate authentication failure errors with SAN details when SAN authentication is enabled

Epic: [CRDB-58866](https://cockroachlabs.atlassian.net/browse/CRDB-58866)

Resolves: #164137
    
Release note (ops change): Added two new metrics, auth.cert.san.conn.total and auth.cert.san.conn.success, to track SAN-based certificate authentication attempts and successes.
author: sanchit-CRL

reviews.body:
A few issues with the PR:

1. **Release note should not be `None`** — this PR adds two new user-visible metrics (`auth.cert.san.conn.total`, `auth.cert.san.conn.success`) and a telemetry counter (`auth.cert.san.enable`). These should be documented in a release note, e.g.:
   ```
   Release note (ops change): Added telemetry metrics for SAN-based certificate
   authentication: `auth.cert.san.conn.total` and `auth.cert.san.conn.success`
   track connection attempts and successes when the `security.client_cert.san_required.enabled`
   cluster setting is active.
   ```

2. **PR description mentions `FormatSANsForLog` helper** but this function does not appear anywhere in the diff. Either it was dropped or the description is stale — please update.

3. **No unit tests for the new metrics** — there are no tests verifying that `AuthCertSANConnTotal` is incremented on SAN auth attempts, `AuthCertSANConnSuccess` is incremented on success, or that the telemetry counter fires when the cluster setting is toggled. The existing `mockAuthConn.GetTenantSpecificMetrics()` in `auth_internal_test.go:54` returns `nil`, which means exercising the `clientCertSANRequired=true` path in tests would panic at line 448 (`c.GetTenantSpecificMetrics().AuthCertSANConnTotal.Inc(1)`). The mock needs to return a real `*tenantSpecificMetrics`, and a test should verify the metric counters.

Suggested test to add in `auth_internal_test.go`:
```go
func TestAuthCertSANMetrics(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	// Verify MarkUsedCertSANAuth / UsedCertSANAuth tracking
	b := &AuthBehaviors{}
	require.False(t, b.UsedCertSANAuth())
	b.MarkUsedCertSANAuth()
	require.True(t, b.UsedCertSANAuth())

	// Verify metric counters are properly initialized and incrementable
	metrics := newTenantSpecificMetrics(
		sql.MakeMemMetrics("test", time.Hour), time.Hour,
	)
	require.NotNil(t, metrics.AuthCertSANConnTotal)
	require.NotNil(t, metrics.AuthCertSANConnSuccess)

	metrics.AuthC

> @souravcrl made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/164277)* status: :shipit: complete! 0 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on sanchit-CRL). ___ *[`pkg/sql/pgwire/auth.go` line 292 at r2](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/164277#-Oocn_VVB3tNoYPUyrs9:-Oocn_VVB3tNoYPUyrsA:b-zdmkl3) ([raw file](https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/blob/f5ccb396eb65f02c52008c1938cb718529bd73ed/pkg/sql/pgwire/auth.go#L292)):* > ```Go > } > > // If SAN-based authentication was used, increment the SAN success counter > ``` nit: // If SAN-based authentication was successful, increment the SAN success counter _Code quote:_ ```Go // If SAN-based authentication was used, increment the SAN success counter ```

title: release-25.4: roachtest: fix gopg flake by disabling assertions
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166685 on behalf of @cockroach-teamcity.

----

The gopg roachtest was failing intermittently due to COPY operations
timing out. The root cause is latch contention from bulk intent
resolution blocking COPY TO operations on a single-node test cluster,
where all user tables share one range. The gopg client has a 10-second
ReadTimeout that gets exceeded when latches are held for several
seconds during intent cleanup.

This change disables runtime assertions for the
gopg test, as the slower assertion-enabled builds widen the timeout
window and make the flake more likely.

Resolves: #166379

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 1 files changed, 1 insertions(+), 0 deletion(-)

*Please review carefully before approving.*

Release justification:
author: blathers-crl

commits.message:
roachtest: fix gopg flake by ignoring flaky COPY tests and disabling assertions

The gopg roachtest was failing intermittently due to COPY operations
timing out. The root cause is latch contention from bulk intent
resolution blocking COPY TO operations on a single-node test cluster,
where all user tables share one range. The gopg client has a 10-second
ReadTimeout that gets exceeded when latches are held for several
seconds during intent cleanup.

This change adds `v10.TestBigColumn` and the CopyTo hook tests to the
gopg ignore list, matching the CopyFrom tests already ignored for the
same reason (#160602). It also disables runtime assertions for the
gopg test, as the slower assertion-enabled builds widen the timeout
window and make the flake more likely.

Resolves: #166379

title: ui: migrate Active Statements pages from Redux to SWR
body:
## Summary

- Extract the lock-wait combiner logic (`selectActiveExecutionsCombiner`) from `activeExecutionsCommon.selectors.ts` into `activeStatementUtils.ts` as `getActiveExecutionsWithLockWaits`, making it usable as a plain function outside Redux selectors.
- Add a `useLiveWorkload()` SWR hook that fetches sessions and cluster locks data, combines them using `getActiveExecutionsWithLockWaits`, and returns active statements/transactions plus metadata (loading, error, lastUpdated, refresh).
- Migrate the Active Statements page (`ActiveStatementsView`) and Active Statement Details page (`ActiveStatementDetails`) from Redux `connect`/`mapStateToProps`/`mapDispatchToProps` to the new SWR hook.
- Simplify connected components in both cluster-ui and db-console — data fetching is now colocated in the components.
- The sessions and clusterLocks Redux stores are **not deleted** — they are shared by Active Transactions, Sessions page, and terminate actions.

### Commits

1. **ui/cluster-ui: extract lock-wait combiner into activeStatementUtils** — mechanical refactor: moves `selectActiveExecutionsCombiner` → `getActiveExecutionsWithLockWaits`, deletes old selectors file, updates all references.
2. **ui/cluster-ui: add useLiveWorkload SWR hook** — creates the hook with two `useSwrWithClusterId` calls (sessions + cluster locks), combines results via `getActiveExecutionsWithLockWaits`, accepts `refreshInterval` and `immutable` options. Includes unit tests.
3. **ui: migrate Active Statements list page from Redux to SWR** — converts `ActiveStatementsView` to use the hook internally, manages sort/filters/columns as local state, deletes Redux selectors and connected wiring in both packages. Includes view tests.
4. **ui: migrate Active Statement Details page from Redux to SWR** — converts `ActiveStatementDetails` to use the hook with `immutable: true` (reads SWR cache without refetching), simplifies both connected wrappers to re-exports. Updates fixture and tests.

commits.message:
ui/cluster-ui: extract lock-wait combiner into activeStatementUtils

Move the `selectActiveExecutionsCombiner` function from
`activeExecutionsCommon.selectors.ts` into `activeStatementUtils.ts`
as `getActiveExecutionsWithLockWaits`. This makes the combiner logic
available as a plain function (not a selector), which is needed by
the upcoming `useLiveWorkload` SWR hook that combines session and
lock data outside of Redux.

The old selector file is deleted and all references (cluster-ui and
db-console selectors) are updated to use the new function.

Epic: CRDB-58145
Part-of: CRDB-61194
Release note: None

ui/cluster-ui: add useLiveWorkload SWR hook

Add a `useLiveWorkload()` hook that fetches sessions and cluster
locks data via SWR and combines them into active statements and
transactions using `getActiveExecutionsWithLockWaits`. The hook
returns loading/error state, a last-updated timestamp, and a
`refresh()` callback.

Options:
- `refreshInterval`: polling interval in ms (0 disables).
- `immutable`: when true, disables all SWR revalidation so the
  hook reads from cache without refetching. This is used by
  detail pages where active statements are ephemeral and a
  refetch would likely return different results.

Epic: CRDB-58145
Part-of: CRDB-61194
Release note: None

ui: migrate Active Statements list page from Redux to SWR

Convert `ActiveStatementsView` from a Redux-connected component to
a self-contained functional component that uses the `useLiveWorkload`
hook for data fetching. Sort, filters, selected columns, and
auto-refresh state are now managed as local component state
initialized from URL params, removing the need for Redux
local-storage actions.

This deletes `mapStateToActiveStatementsPageProps`,
`mapDispatchToActiveStatementsPageProps`, and the db-console
`activeStatementsSelectors.tsx` file. The `StatementsPageRoot` and
its connected wrapper no longer thread active-page props; the
`ActiveStatementsView` is rendered without props.

The sessions and clusterLocks Redux stores are **not deleted** —
they are still used by Active Transactions, Sessions page, and
terminate actions.

Epic: CRDB-58145
Part-of: CRDB-61194
Release note: None

ui: migrate Active Statement Details page from Redux to SWR

Convert `ActiveStatementDetails` from a Redux-connected component to
a self-contained functional component that uses the `useLiveWorkload`
hook with `immutable: true`. This reads from the SWR cache populated
by the list page without revalidating, since active statements are
ephemeral and a refetch would likely return different results.

The component now uses `useRouteMatch` to extract the execution ID,
computes contention details locally via `useMemo`, and fetches admin
role status via `useUserSQLRoles`. This removes the need for Redux
`connect`, `mapStateToProps`, and `mapDispatchToProps`.

Both the cluster-ui and db-console connected wrappers are simplified
to re-export the component directly.

Part-of: CRDB-61194
Release note: None

title: sql,workload: fix aclitem usage in mixed-version schemachange workload
body:
The schemachange workload used RandSortingType to pick random column types, which could select aclitem even in mixed-version clusters where older nodes don't know about it. This caused "function does not exist" errors when resolving function signatures via pg_catalog.pg_type on nodes that lack the aclitem type entry.

Fix this by switching the workload to use RandColumnType, which respects IsLegalColumnType (which already excludes aclitem).

Additionally, remove redundant oidext.T_aclitem and oidext.T__aclitem constants in favor of the upstream oid.T_aclitem and oid.T__aclitem that are now available in lib/pq.

Resolves: #166032
Resolves: #166220

Release note: None
author: rafiss
