> @fqazi made 1 comment. > *[Reviewable](https://reviewable.io/reviews/cockroachdb/cockroach/166750#-:-OoaogWP6V8i_vFfWzLm:bnfp4nl)* status: :shipit: complete! 1 of 0 LGTMs obtained (waiting on cpj2195, golgeek, and mw5h).

commits.message:
sql,workload: fix aclitem usage in mixed-version schemachange workload

The schemachange workload used RandSortingType to pick random column
types, which could select aclitem even in mixed-version clusters where
older nodes don't know about it. This caused "function does not exist"
errors when resolving function signatures via pg_catalog.pg_type on
nodes that lack the aclitem type entry.

Fix this by switching the workload to use RandColumnType, which
respects IsLegalColumnType (which already excludes aclitem).

Also add a version gate in ValidateColumnDefType so that aclitem
columns cannot be created until the cluster is fully upgraded to v26.2.

Additionally, remove redundant oidext.T_aclitem and oidext.T__aclitem
constants in favor of the upstream oid.T_aclitem and oid.T__aclitem
that are now available in lib/pq.

Resolves: #166032
Resolves: #166220

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: release-26.1: roachprod: address ibm quota and image deprecation, and optimize clusters list
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166415.

/cc @cockroachdb/release

---

This PR contains four changes for IBM cloud in roachprod:

1. roachprod: use more ibm AZs to reduce capa issues
  In order to avoid impacting IBM customers, roachprod was only using two IBM Cloud regions per our agreement with IBM.
  With the deprecation of s390x in IBM Cloud, we can no longer request for quota increases in these two regions, and as the number of customers decrease, we now can scale the number of regions roachprod uses.
  This patch grows from two regions to six in order to load balance the cluster creations during roachtest operations and reduce quota issues.

2. roachprod: use latest ubuntu 22.04 image in ibm
  Previously, roachprod was using the s390x  Ubuntu 22.04 rev. 5 image in IBM Cloud.
  As this image was deprecated a few months ago and replaced by rev. 7, this patch switches to the latest image.

3. roachprod: fix ibm clusters list
  Previously, listing clusters in IBM was creating two many requests in parallel, leading to very long list/create operations and, sometimes, the API returning HTTP error code 429.
  This patch adds a ctxgroup with a limit fixed to 30 concurrent requests to avoid spamming the IBM API when listing clusters.
  Also, some code path were leading to querying each instance details multiple times. This patch fixes this by only loading instance data when the creation date is unknown. This will reduce the total number of queries per instance from two to one.

4. roachprod: improve clusters cleanup for ibm
  Previously, after a failed cluster creation, roachtest was attempting to destroy the partially created cluster. This process relied on listing the cluster and destroy it via `provider.DestroyCluster()`.
  In IBM, this is subject to failures because of implicit rate limitings, so this patch attempts to call `provider.DeleteCluster()` (by name) in case no clusters were returned during listing.
  This should reduce the number of dangling resources cleaned by the garbage collector.


Epic: none
Release note: none

Release justification: Test only change
author: golgeek

commits.message:
roachprod: address ibm quota and image deprecation, and optimize clusters list

This PR contains four changes for IBM cloud in roachprod:

1. roachprod: use more ibm AZs to reduce capa issues
  In order to avoid impacting IBM customers, roachprod was only using
  two IBM Cloud regions per our agreement with IBM.
  With the deprecation of s390x in IBM Cloud, we can no longer request
  for quota increases in these two regions, and as the number of
  customers decrease, we now can scale the number of regions roachprod
  uses.
  This patch grows from two regions to six in order to load balance the
  cluster creations during roachtest operations and reduce quota issues.

2. roachprod: use latest ubuntu 22.04 image in ibm
  Previously, roachprod was using the s390x Ubuntu 22.04 rev. 5 image in
  IBM Cloud.
  As this image was deprecated a few months ago and replaced by rev. 7,
  this patch switches to the latest image.

3. roachprod: fix ibm clusters list
  Previously, listing clusters in IBM was creating two many requests in
  parallel, leading to very long list/create operations and, sometimes,
  the API returning HTTP error code 429.
  This patch adds a ctxgroup with a limit fixed to 30 concurrent
  requests to avoid spamming the IBM API when listing clusters.
  Also, some code path were leading to querying each instance details
  multiple times. This patch fixes this by only loading instance data
  when the creation date is unknown. This will reduce the total number
  of queries per instance from two to one.

4. roachprod: improve clusters cleanup for ibm
  Previously, after a failed cluster creation, roachtest was attempting
  to destroy the partially created cluster. This process relied on
  listing the cluster and destroy it via `provider.DestroyCluster()`.
  In IBM, this is subject to failures because of implicit rate
  limitings, so this patch attempts to call `provider.DeleteCluster()`
  (by name) in case no clusters were returned during listing.
  This should reduce the number of dangling resources cleaned by the
  garbage collector.

Epic: none
Release note: None

title: release-26.2: tochar: fix float8 to_char post-decimal digits; fix sign placement for FMS format
body:
Backport 2/2 commits from #166714 on behalf of @rafiss.

----

### sql: limit float8 to_char post-decimal digits to DBL_DIG 

PostgreSQL's float8_to_char adjusts the post-decimal digit count
(Num.post) to fit within DBL_DIG (15) total significant digits
before generating the overflow '#' fill string. CockroachDB was
not performing this adjustment, causing overflow strings with too
many '#' characters in the decimal portion when the format had
many post-decimal digit specifiers.

Add Float8DecimalToChar which applies the DBL_DIG adjustment
before formatting, matching PostgreSQL's behavior.

Skipping a release note since this seems like a minor bug.

Resolves: #166635

### sql: fix to_char sign placement with FMS and predec space 

The sign-writing condition in numProcessorToChar was checking the
character at the current number position (numberIdx) instead of the
character at the last_relevant position. This caused the sign to be
placed after the digit instead of before it when using FMS format
with values like 0 that have a predecimal space.

For example, `to_char(0, 'FMS9999.999')` produced `0+.` instead of
the correct `+0.`.

Fix by matching PostgreSQL's condition which checks
`*Np->last_relevant == '.'` (the character at the last_relevant
pointer) rather than the character at the current number position.

No release note since this bug seems pretty minor.

fixes #166634

---

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

----

Release justification: bug fix in new 26.2 feature
author: rafiss

commits.message:
sql: limit float8 to_char post-decimal digits to DBL_DIG

PostgreSQL's float8_to_char adjusts the post-decimal digit count
(Num.post) to fit within DBL_DIG (15) total significant digits
before generating the overflow '#' fill string. CockroachDB was
not performing this adjustment, causing overflow strings with too
many '#' characters in the decimal portion when the format had
many post-decimal digit specifiers.

Add Float8DecimalToChar which applies the DBL_DIG adjustment
before formatting, matching PostgreSQL's behavior.

Skipping a release note since this seems like a minor bug.

Resolves: #166635

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

sql: fix to_char sign placement with FMS and predec space

The sign-writing condition in numProcessorToChar was checking the
character at the current number position (numberIdx) instead of the
character at the last_relevant position. This caused the sign to be
placed after the digit instead of before it when using FMS format
with values like 0 that have a predecimal space.

For example, `to_char(0, 'FMS9999.999')` produced `0+.` instead of
the correct `+0.`.

Fix by matching PostgreSQL's condition which checks
`*Np->last_relevant == '.'` (the character at the last_relevant
pointer) rather than the character at the current number position.

No release note since this bug seems pretty minor.

fixes https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/166634

Release note: None

title: release-26.2: sql: add cluster setting to block auto-unlock of schema_locked tables
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166246 on behalf of @rafiss.

----

The `schema_locked` storage parameter was designed as a changefeed optimization. The declarative schema changer automatically unlocks schema-locked tables, performs DDL, then re-locks them — meaning `schema_locked` doesn't actually prevent user-initiated DDL.

This commit adds a new cluster setting `sql.schema.auto_unlock.enabled` (default `true`) that controls whether the declarative schema changer is allowed to auto-unlock schema-locked tables. When set to `false`, DDL on schema-locked tables is blocked unless the user manually unlocks the table first via `ALTER TABLE t SET (schema_locked = false)`.

The legacy schema changer already blocks all DDL on schema-locked tables regardless of this setting, so no changes were needed there.

Resolves: #166242

Release note (sql change): Added a new cluster setting `sql.schema.auto_unlock.enabled` that controls whether DDL operations automatically unlock `schema_locked` tables. When set to `false`, DDL on schema-locked tables is blocked unless the user manually unlocks the table first. This allows customers using LDR to enforce `schema_locked` as a hard lock preventing user-initiated DDL. The default is `true`, preserving existing behavior.

----

Release justification: low-risk, opt-in change requested by customer

title: ui: refactor useClusterSettings SWR hook and migrate 5 consumers from Redux
body:
## Summary

- Refactor the `useClusterSettings` SWR hook in cluster-ui to fetch all cluster settings in a single request and return them as `Record<string, ClusterSetting>`. The request parameter is now optional: when a `{ names: [...] }` filter is provided only those settings are returned, when omitted all settings are returned. All callers share the same SWR cache entry.
- Migrate 5 React component consumers in db-console from Redux `refreshSettings` dispatching to the refactored hook: `TimezoneProvider`, `MetricsDataProvider`, `NodeGraphs`, `KeyVisualizerPage`, and the Settings report page.
- Remove 7 now-unused Redux selectors from `clusterSettings.selectors.ts`.
- `alertDataProvider` in `alerts.ts` is left on Redux because it's a store subscriber, not a React component.

### Commits

1. **ui/cluster-ui: refactor useClusterSettings to fetch all settings** — reworks the hook to fetch all settings in one request with an optional name filter, adds `public` field to `ClusterSetting` type, exports from public API.
2. **ui/db-console: migrate 5 consumers from refreshSettings to useClusterSettings** — replaces Redux patterns in all React component consumers, updates tests, removes unused selectors.

Epic: CRDB-58145
Release note: None
author: kyle-a-wong

commits.message:
ui/cluster-ui: refactor useClusterSettings to fetch all settings

Rework the useClusterSettings hook to fetch all cluster settings in a
single request and return them as a typed Record<string, ClusterSetting>.
All callers now share the same SWR cache entry regardless of which
settings they request.

The hook's request parameter is now optional. When provided with a
{ names: [...] } filter, only the requested settings are returned
(with missing entries filled with defaults). When omitted, all settings
are returned. This supports both consumers that need specific settings
(e.g. TimezoneProvider) and consumers that need the full set (e.g. the
Settings report page).

Also adds a `public` field to the ClusterSetting type, which was
previously only available on the raw protobuf response. This allows
consumers to distinguish between public and reserved settings without
accessing the proto directly.

The single consumer of the old hook (databasesPageRoot) now uses the
new signature with the names filter, resulting in no behavior change.

Epic: CRDB-58145
Release note: None

ui/db-console: migrate 5 consumers from refreshSettings to useClusterSettings

Replace Redux-based refreshSettings dispatching and cachedData selectors
with the new useClusterSettings SWR hook in all React component consumers:

- TimezoneProvider: reads ui.default_timezone and ui.display_timezone
- MetricsDataProvider: reads timeseries storage TTL settings for
  resolution computation
- NodeGraphs: reads timeseries storage TTL settings
- KeyVisualizerPage: reads keyvisualizer.enabled and sample_interval
- Settings report page: renders all cluster settings; removes
  connect()/mapStateToProps/mapDispatchToProps entirely

The alertDataProvider in alerts.ts still uses refreshSettings because it
is a Redux store subscriber (not a React component) and cannot use hooks.
The settingsReducerObj and its two consumed selectors
(selectClusterSettings, selectClusterSettingVersion) are retained for
this reason.

Remove 7 now-unused Redux selectors from clusterSettings.selectors.ts:
selectTimezoneSetting, selectResolution10sStorageTTL,
selectResolution30mStorageTTL, selectAutomaticStatsCollectionEnabled,
selectIndexRecommendationsEnabled, selectIndexUsageStatsEnabled, and
selectDropUnusedIndexDuration.

Update MetricsDataProvider tests to mock useClusterSettings and provide
metricsTime-shaped data matching the refactored component internals.

Epic: CRDB-58145
Release note: None

title: sqlstats: fix TestSQLStatsDiscardStatsOnFingerprintLimit flake
body:
Replace hardcoded 5s timeout in `waitForStatementStatsRows` and `waitForTransactionStatsRows` with `testutils.SucceedsSoonDuration()`, which scales to 225s under race builds.

Remove `skip.UnderRace` from `TestSQLStatsDiscardStatsOnFingerprintLimit` since the timeout fix makes it safe to run under race.

Fixes #166668

Release note: None
author: dhartunian

commits.message:
sqlstats: fix TestSQLStatsDiscardStatsOnFingerprintLimit flake

The `waitForStatementStatsRows` and `waitForTransactionStatsRows` helpers
used a hardcoded 5s timeout, which is insufficient under race builds
where the race detector significantly slows execution. Replace with
`testutils.SucceedsSoonDuration()` which automatically scales to 225s
under race (matching the standard `SucceedsSoon` behavior).

With the timeout fix in place, remove the `skip.UnderRace` from
`TestSQLStatsDiscardStatsOnFingerprintLimit` so the test runs under
race as intended.

Fixes #166668

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: release-25.4: sql: wait for LEARNER replicas to clear before disabling queues in relocate tests
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166674 on behalf of @cockroach-teamcity.

----

TestRelocateVoters, TestExperimentalRelocateVoters, and
TestExperimentalRelocateNonVoters all share a race condition where
LEARNER replicas from ongoing replication may still exist when the test
disables replicate queues. This freezes the LEARNER in place, causing
subsequent RELOCATE commands to fail with "trying to add to a store
that already has a LEARNER".

The same fix was already applied to TestRelocateNonVoters in e0e333a7be9
(for #161363) — add a testutils.SucceedsSoon loop after
WaitForFullReplication that queries SHOW RANGES WITH DETAILS and waits

title: release-25.4: sql/catalog/lease: fix test hang in TestModificationTimeTxnOrdering
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166673 on behalf of @cockroach-teamcity.

----

The test spawns two goroutines that call `t.Fatal`/`t.Fatalf` on
error. In Go, calling `t.Fatal` from a non-test goroutine invokes
`runtime.Goexit()`, which terminates the goroutine immediately.
Because `wg.Done()` was placed at the end of each goroutine body
(not deferred), it was skipped when `t.Fatal` triggered, causing
`wg.Wait()` in the main test goroutine to block forever. The test
then hung until the overall timeout killed it.

Move `wg.Done()` to `defer` statements at the top of each goroutine
so it always executes, even when `t.Fatal` calls `runtime.Goexit()`.

Resolves: #166380

Release note: None

Generated by Claude Code Auto-Solver
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: release-24.3: roachtest: fix gopg flake by disabling assertions
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166685 on behalf of @cockroach-teamcity.

----

The gopg roachtest was failing intermittently due to COPY operations
timing out. The root cause is latch contention from bulk intent
resolution blocking COPY TO operations on a single-node test cluster,
where all user tables share one range. The gopg client has a 10-second
ReadTimeout that gets exceeded when latches are held for several
seconds during intent cleanup.

This change disables runtime assertions for the
gopg test, as the slower assertion-enabled builds widen the timeout
window and make the flake more likely.

Resolves: #166379

title: release-26.2: sql/sqlstats: fix TestTransactionLatencies flake
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166554 on behalf of @dhartunian.

----

## Summary

- Replaces the flaky wall-clock comparison (`InDelta` with 5% tolerance) in `TestTransactionLatencies` with an invariant chain: `expectedIdle <= idle <= service <= txnTime`.
- This validates the latency accounting without comparing against external wall-clock time, which varies significantly in CI environments.
- This test is a chronic flake with 5+ prior closed issues, most recently #166479. The latest failure showed a 21% delta.

Fixes #166479

Release note: None

----

Release justification:
author: dhartunian

commits.message:
sql/sqlstats: fix TestTransactionLatencies flake

Replace the wall-clock comparison assertion (InDelta with 5% tolerance)
with an invariant chain that is not timing-sensitive. The test now
verifies the relationship: expectedIdle <= idle <= service <= txnTime.
This validates the latency accounting without comparing against external
wall-clock time, which varies significantly in CI environments.

Fixes #166479

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: release-26.2: backup: add redaction markers to online restore URIs
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166332 on behalf of @andrew-r-thomas.

----

This patch adds redaction markers to URIs passed to pebble in online
restore. Specifically, all query params in the URIs are marked as unsafe
and as such will be redacted in logs and error messages.

Informs: #161169

Release note: None

----

Release justification: Security-sensitive bug fix. Online restore passes cloud storage URIs containing credentials to Pebble without redaction markers, meaning sensitive fields can appear in plaintext in logs and error output. This change wraps URIs with redaction markers before passing them to Pebble, so that log and error output redacts credentials while still allowing Pebble to access the full unredacted URI at runtime.
author: blathers-crl

reviews.body: approving to test if non TL approval allows merge

commits.message:
backup: add redaction markers to online restore URIs

This patch adds redaction markers to URIs passed to pebble in online
restore. Specifically, all query params in the URIs are marked as unsafe
and as such will be redacted in logs and error messages.

Informs: #161169

Release note: None

error. In Go, calling `t.Fatal` from a non-test goroutine invokes
`runtime.Goexit()`, which terminates the goroutine immediately.
Because `wg.Done()` was placed at the end of each goroutine body
(not deferred), it was skipped when `t.Fatal` triggered, causing
`wg.Wait()` in the main test goroutine to block forever. The test
then hung until the overall timeout killed it.

Move `wg.Done()` to `defer` statements at the top of each goroutine
so it always executes, even when `t.Fatal` calls `runtime.Goexit()`.

Resolves: #166380

Release note: None

Generated by Claude Code Auto-Solver
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: release-26.2: catalog/lease: use normal priority for descriptor version fetching
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166746 on behalf of @fqazi.

----

getDescriptorsFromStoreForInterval is used to fetch historical descriptor versions for foreground SQL traffic. Previously, it was using BulkNormalPri, which meant it could be throttled by the elastic CPU limiter. Throttling these requests delays foreground work, which can lead to query timeouts and test failures (like
backup-restore/small-ranges).

This change sets the priority to NormalPri, ensuring these requests are treated as foreground work and not subject to elastic CPU control.

Fixes: #166263

Release note (bug fix): Fixed a bug where descriptor version fetching could be incorrectly throttled by the elastic CPU limiter, potentially leading to increased query latency or timeouts under high CPU load.

----

Release justification: fix in new code
author: fqazi

title: release-26.2: kvserver: fix TestVirtualizedIntentResolution flake
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166696 on behalf of @miraradeva.

----

The test writes a transactional intent via `SendWrappedWith` and then immediately calls `executeReadOnlyBatch` to read it. `CanAckBeforeApplication` returns true for transactional intent writes, so the proposal can be ack'd
before the engine batch is applied. This means the read can open a snapshot that doesn't yet contain the intent, causing it to return the original value instead of a `LockConflictError`.

Fix by setting `DisableCanAckBeforeApplication` on the test's store config.
Also add named subtests for easier debugging.

Fixes: https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/166600

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

----

Release justification: Testing only.
author: blathers-crl

commits.message:
kvserver: fix TestVirtualizedIntentResolution flake

The test writes a transactional intent via SendWrappedWith and then
immediately calls executeReadOnlyBatch to read it. CanAckBeforeApplication
returns true for transactional intent writes, so the proposal can be ack'd
before the engine batch is applied. This means the read can open a snapshot
that doesn't yet contain the intent, causing it to return the original
value instead of a LockConflictError.

Fix by setting DisableCanAckBeforeApplication on the test's store config.
Also add named subtests for easier debugging.

Fixes: #166600

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: roachdev-claude <roachdev-claude-bot@cockroachlabs.com>

title: release-26.2: roachtest: fix gopg flake by disabling assertions
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166685 on behalf of @cockroach-teamcity.

----

The gopg roachtest was failing intermittently due to COPY operations
timing out. The root cause is latch contention from bulk intent
resolution blocking COPY TO operations on a single-node test cluster,
where all user tables share one range. The gopg client has a 10-second
ReadTimeout that gets exceeded when latches are held for several
seconds during intent cleanup.

This change disables runtime assertions for the
gopg test, as the slower assertion-enabled builds widen the timeout
window and make the flake more likely.

Resolves: #166379

Release note: None

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Release justification:
author: blathers-crl

commits.message:
roachtest: fix gopg flake by ignoring flaky COPY tests and disabling assertions

The gopg roachtest was failing intermittently due to COPY operations
timing out. The root cause is latch contention from bulk intent
resolution blocking COPY TO operations on a single-node test cluster,
where all user tables share one range. The gopg client has a 10-second
ReadTimeout that gets exceeded when latches are held for several
seconds during intent cleanup.

This change adds `v10.TestBigColumn` and the CopyTo hook tests to the
gopg ignore list, matching the CopyFrom tests already ignored for the
same reason (#160602). It also disables runtime assertions for the
gopg test, as the slower assertion-enabled builds widen the timeout
window and make the flake more likely.

Resolves: #166379

Release note: None

Generated by Claude Code Auto-Solver
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: release-26.1: sql: fix ALTER FUNCTION RENAME duplicate detection in non-public schemas
body:
Backport 1/1 commits from #166570 on behalf of @shghasemi.

----

Previously, renaming a function or procedure to a name that already existed with the same signature in a non-public schema was incorrectly allowed. This happened because the duplicate check did not set ExplicitSchema on the routine name, causing matchRoutine to search the session search path instead of the specific schema. If the schema was not on the search path, the duplicate was not found and the rename succeeded, leaving two functions with the same name and signature that could not be dropped.

Fix by setting SchemaName and ExplicitSchema on the lookup object.

Fixes #166361

Release note (bug fix): Fixed a bug where ALTER FUNCTION ... RENAME TO and ALTER PROCEDURE ... RENAME TO could create duplicate functions in non-public schemas.

----

Release justification: Fixes a serious issue to avoid customer escalations.

title: tidy up some correctness issues reported by go vet
body: I use go vet (http://golang.org/cmd/go/#hdr-Run_go_tool_vet_on_packages) as a presubmit hook on almost all of my go code. It definitely helps with finding correctness problems.
author: andybons

commits.message: tidy up some correctness issues reported by go vet
